name: A-Z Auto Sydney
description:
A-Z Auto Sydney is a verified Filipino-owned and operated automotive service centre located in St Peters. Led by Glenn and his highly skilled team, the workshop specialises in logbook servicing, general repairs, and advanced diagnostics for European, Japanese, and performance vehicles.

Local drivers highly recommend the workshop for its transparent communication and tidy environment. Customers appreciate that Glenn explains mechanical issues clearly and provides fully itemised quotes before any work begins. The team frequently shares photo updates and screenshots of repairs to keep clients informed throughout the process.

A-Z Auto Sydney is well-known for its prompt, same-day service and reliable workmanship. Whether you need an urgent battery replacement, brake repairs, a clutch upgrade, or a last-minute pink slip, the team delivers professional care with no hidden costs.

Conveniently located on Princes Highway near Sydenham Station, the workshop is open from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM on weekdays, and 8:00 AM to 1:00 PM on Saturdays. The friendly and welcoming atmosphere makes it a popular choice for the local community.

name: AAA Migration Services
description:
AAA Migration Services Pty Ltd is a registered migration agency located in Penrith, Sydney. The agency is operated by Maria Fe Alcover Peffer, who is a registered migration agent under migration agent registration number 1574492. She specialises in providing visa and immigration assistance for clients aiming to move to or stay in Australia.

The agency supports clients through various stages of their immigration journey, helping to navigate the complex Australian visa application processes. Maria and her team provide professional guidance on family visas, business and work visas, and student applications. They offer reliable and clear advice to ensure the application requirements are met correctly.

Conveniently located at 26 Coombes Drive in Penrith, AAA Migration Services helps the local community in the Greater Western Sydney region and beyond. Clients can contact the firm via email at mpeffer@aaamigrationservices.com or call them on (02) 4722 0711 to discuss their migration needs and options.

name: A-Team's Kitchen
description:
A-Team's Kitchen was a beloved family-run Filipino eatery nestled in Rooty Hill, Western Sydney, that earned a loyal following for its authentic home-style cooking. The restaurant specialised in traditional Filipino breakfast favourites, including a range of silog dishes such as tapsilog, bangsilog, and tocilog, all served with garlic fried rice and egg.

Customers particularly praised the sizzling sisig, widely regarded as one of the best in the area, alongside generous servings of Batangas lomi, crispy pata, and tender BBQ pork skewers. The menu also featured comforting bowls of arroz caldo, kare-kare, and nostalgic treats like ice scramble, taho, and halo-halo. For larger groups, the restaurant offered boodle fight dining experiences.

The atmosphere was casual and unpretentious, with a self-serve setup where diners would queue at the register, collect their own cutlery, and help themselves to a condiment station stocked with banana ketchup and Mang Tomas sauce. Regular visitors appreciated the warm, welcoming service from the hosts, who were known for making patrons feel right at home.

Situated in the heart of Rooty Hill's Filipino food corridor, A-Team's Kitchen was a go-to destination for anyone craving genuine Filipino flavours in a relaxed, community-oriented setting.

Please note: A-Team's Kitchen has permanently closed.

name: ACI Central (Philippine-Australian Arts, Culture and Innovation Central)
description:
ACI Central (Philippine-Australian Arts, Culture and Innovation Central) is a vibrant community hub and cultural centre located in Campbelltown, Sydney. Jointly acquired in 2016 by the NARRA Co-operative Ltd and Community Association Plaza Filipino Inc., the centre serves as the physical home for preserving and celebrating Filipino heritage in the Macarthur region. It provides a welcoming space where community members can gather to bridge generational gaps and foster deep cultural connections.

The hub is highly regarded for hosting a diverse range of activities, including art exhibitions, educational workshops, and community fundraisers. Notable initiatives staged at the centre include the prestigious Ambassador for Culture and the Arts recognition night, youth mental health advocacy programmes like 'From Heritage to Healing', and various leadership forums. ACI Central is powered by the passion of over 120 dedicated volunteers and members who collaborate to keep the spirit of bayanihan alive.

Situated at Artisan Apartments on Warby Street, this multi-functional venue offers a vital platform for Filipino-Australian creatives and community groups. Whether visiting for a cultural workshop or participating in a community forum, guests appreciate the warm, inclusive atmosphere that makes the centre a true pillar of the local community.

name: ALiN Cargo Express
description:
ALiN Cargo Express is a dependable door-to-door sea cargo and logistics service provider based in Blacktown, Sydney. They specialise in sending balikbayan boxes and commercial cargo from Australia to various destinations across the Philippines. The company is highly regarded by the local Filipino-Australian community for its secure handling and transparent communication throughout the shipping process.

Customers consistently praise the team's exceptional customer service and clear notifications. They provide text updates, including a convenient one-hour arrival window for driver pick-ups. To ensure packages are well-protected during transit, the business supplies high-quality, sturdy shipping boxes that keep items safe and intact. Senders can also easily monitor their shipments through an online tracking portal, offering peace of mind from dispatch to delivery.

Whether you are a first-time sender needing guidance or a regular customer shipping to family, the friendly administration team is always ready to assist and explain the entire procedure. With reasonable freight charges and efficient delivery networks, ALiN Cargo Express is a trusted choice for the community's logistics needs.

name: ALiN Cargo Express Australia
description:
ALiN Cargo Express Australia is a trusted logistics partner for the Filipino-Australian community in Sydney, specialising in secure and reliable door-to-door shipping to the Philippines. Operating out of Blacktown, they offer both sea cargo and air cargo freight services, specifically catered to sending balikbayan boxes and personal shipments. Local clients frequently praise the service for its high-quality, thick, and durable shipping boxes that ensure items arrive at their destination intact and undamaged.

What makes ALiN Cargo Express stand out is their outstanding customer service and clear communication. The team is highly responsive, keeping senders informed throughout the process and offering a user-friendly portal for easy package tracking. Customers also appreciate their efficient local pick-up service, which includes helpful SMS notifications with a precise one-hour arrival window for the driver. Whether you are sending a balikbayan box to Davao City or coordinating an air freight shipment to Manila, ALiN Cargo Express provides a professional, stress-free shipping experience backed by a friendly and helpful team.

name: APCO Australia
description:
The Philippine Australian Multicultural Association Incorporated (PAMAI), formerly known as the Alliance of Philippine Community Organisations (APCO), is a key non-profit community group in New South Wales. The association has supported the community for over fifteen years. It collaborates with local organisations to advocate for the Filipino-Australian community and preserve their cultural heritage.

Community members recognise the organisation for hosting the annual Pista sa Nayon festival at The Granville Centre. This vibrant celebration commemorates Philippine Independence Day with traditional food stalls, dance competitions, and cultural performances. Additionally, the group leads meaningful projects like the Generations in Motion program, which runs creative workshops to connect youth and seniors. They also partner with groups like the Kapit-bahayan Cooperative and the Multicultural Assistance Task Force to support families during difficult times.

Under the leadership of President Ronna Guzman and other dedicated volunteers, PAMAI remains an active hub for cultural connection and community support. The organisation welcomes volunteers and partners to strengthen multicultural relations across Western Sydney and the broader state.

name: AVC Migration
description: AVC Migration (also known as Australian Visa Centre) is a MARA-registered boutique migration and visa consultancy located in Blacktown, Western Sydney. Led by Registered Migration Agent Rajwant Singh, the consultancy specialises in providing clear, reliable guidance for parent, partner, student, family visas, and Administrative Review Tribunal (ART) appeals. Clients frequently praise Mr. Rajwant Singh and his team for their deep knowledge of Australian immigration law and their patient, reassuring approach during what is often a highly stressful process. Customers appreciate the clear explanations of complex visa pathways, prompt communication, and meticulous preparation of documentation. The agency has a strong reputation for handling urgent and sensitive cases efficiently, guiding families step-by-step from their initial consultation to successful visa grants and citizenship ceremonies. Located on Patrick Street in the multicultural hub of Blacktown, AVC Migration plays an active role in supporting the local community, including many Filipino families, with personalised migration solutions tailored to their unique circumstances.

name: Adhika
description:
Adhika is a cosy, nostalgic Filipino-inspired cafe and cantina located in Rushcutters Bay, Sydney. Launched in September 2025 by owners Aileen Aguirre and Francis Dela Cruz, this charming neighbourhood spot welcomes visitors with a rustic 1960s and 70s decor reminiscent of a traditional Filipino grandmother's or lola’s house, making breakfast feel comforting and familiar.

The menu specialises in unique sweet and savoury breakfast and brunch options. Local diners highly recommend the embutido pandesal, which features soft, freshly baked bread rolls filled with well-seasoned savoury meat and pickles. The avocado on ensaymada is another popular favourite. For beverages, the cafe brews quality Campos coffee and offers creative specialty drinks like the ube matcha, the decadent leche latte, and Milo lattes that highlight a beloved cultural tradition.

With its exceptionally friendly service and relaxed atmosphere, Adhika provides a welcoming space for the local community to gather. It is a wonderful centre for anyone looking to enjoy fresh, beautifully prepared food in a cosy setting.

name: Agape Christian Church
description:
Established in February 1995, Agape Christian Church is a welcoming Pentecostal congregation situated on the Great Western Highway in Colyton. Known for its landmark 'JESUS IS THE ANSWER' sign, the church has served the Western Sydney community for over three decades under the leadership of Senior Pastors Rowell and Rubi Corpuz.

Members and visitors value the church for its warm fellowship, caring community, and strong focus on faith. The church provides various services, including Sunday worship at 10:00 am, youth services on Friday evenings, and regular small group meetings. These programs help local families grow together in a supportive environment.

Outreach is a central part of the church’s activities. The congregation organises a food distribution program on the first Saturday of each month to support local families. They also visit residents in nearby nursing homes every second Saturday. Globally, the church supports child education sponsorships in the Philippines. Whether you are looking for a spiritual home or community connection, this local church offers a supportive space to connect and grow.

name: Aguman Capampangan Australia Inc.
description:
Aguman Capampangan Australia Inc. (ACAI) is a vibrant Filipino-Australian community organisation established in 1990. The association serves as a vital bridge to unite cabalens. These are individuals from the Pampanga and Tarlac provinces now living in Australia. It focuses on preserving the rich Kapampangan cultural heritage and language. The group also promotes traditions and fosters close friendships among its members.

Based in Sydney, the group is widely recognised for organising social and fundraising events. Their regular calendar includes golf tournaments, family cherry-picking outings, and anniversary celebrations. They also participate in major multicultural events, such as the Grand Philippine Fiesta Kultura in Sydney.

Beyond social connection, the organisation makes a significant humanitarian impact. They run educational programmes to provide tuition assistance to scholars in the Philippines. The group also coordinates medical and dental missions to Pampanga and Tarlac. These missions are run in partnership with the Philippine-Australian Medical Association (PAMA).

Led by a passionate team of volunteers, Aguman Capampangan Australia welcomes new members. Whether you want to reconnect with your roots or support charity outreach, the association offers a warm, supportive space.

name: All Hallows Catholic Church
description:
All Hallows Catholic Church is a welcoming and active parish located in Five Dock. The church is known for its friendly and supportive community, led by dedicated parish staff and clergy who offer helpful services for key events like baptisms and family services. Parishioners and visitors frequently praise the warm, devout atmosphere of the services and the parish's accommodating approach to community needs.

The church building features a unique semi-circular design with a large foyer that serves as an excellent space for people to gather and socialise. The venue is fully wheelchair-accessible, making it open to everyone, and convenient street parking is available nearby.

For the Filipino-Australian community in Sydney's Inner West, All Hallows hosts a regular monthly Tagalog Mass on the fourth Sunday of each month at 11:00 am. This service provides a wonderful opportunity for the local community to connect, worship, and share in their shared cultural and spiritual heritage.

name: All Saints Catholic Church
description:
All Saints Catholic Church is a welcoming and active parish located at 48 George Street in Liverpool, Sydney. As a vibrant spiritual centre within the Catholic Archdiocese of Sydney, the church serves a diverse multicultural congregation. It is particularly known for serving the local Filipino-Australian community by hosting a monthly Tagalog Mass on the fourth Sunday of the month at 11:00 am.

Parishioners appreciate the warm, devout atmosphere and the solid formation of the services. The community is highly active, featuring youth groups and a dedicated choir that has performed for over forty years. The parish also organises valuable outreach programmes, including morning tea and companionship in the Parish Hall on Tuesdays and Fridays. Additionally, a well-stocked piety stall offers Bibles, rosaries, and spiritual books.

Sacraments are celebrated regularly, with reconciliation services held on Saturday afternoons. The church also runs healing masses, Alpha seminars, and an annual Christmas lunch for those spending the day alone. While visitor parking inside the school grounds can become crowded during peak times, the welcoming community makes everyone feel at home. Whether attending the regular services or participating in parish festivals, visitors will find a caring space for spiritual reflection and connection.

name: Allan Miranda (VisaEnvoy)
description:
Allan Miranda (VisaEnvoy) is a professional immigration law and migration practice located in Miranda, Sydney. The firm specialises in tailored immigration services for the Filipino-Australian community, helping clients navigate the complex Australian visa system. The practice is led by senior immigration lawyer Allan Miranda. As a registered migration agent and former Philippine Certified Public Accountant, he offers expert advice on business, skilled, and family visas.

Clients highly recommend the dedicated team, particularly highlighting migration agents Nadia Zdielnik and Voya. Feedback consistently praises their meticulous preparation, clear and timely updates, and deep empathy during stressful applications. They have successfully guided clients through various pathways, including permanent residency, subclass 190 skilled nominated visas, and partner points calculations.

The Sydney office is located at Level 4, 29 Kiora Road, Miranda, and is open from 8:00 am to 8:00 pm daily. For professional assistance with visa enquiries and migration applications, you can contact the team on (02) 9161 7253 or email them at team@visaenvoy.com.

name: Alviz Accounting
description:
Alviz Accounting is a professional accounting firm based in Winston Hills, Sydney, specialising in tax, bookkeeping, accounting, and payroll services. They cater to a wide range of clients, from local small businesses to individuals, and have earned a strong reputation for their supportive and client-focused approach.

Clients frequently praise Melanie and the team for making the tax return process simple, smooth, and stress-free. The team is particularly valued by international students for their affordable rates and patient guidance. They take the time to explain deductions, visa status impacts, and specific details like the Medicare Levy Exemption in plain English, helping clients feel confident at every step.

Whether you need help lodging a straightforward individual tax return, managing business bookkeeping, or handling payroll needs, they provide reliable and efficient service. Located on Old Windsor Road, they are a convenient and highly recommended choice for anyone seeking dedicated and approachable financial support in the Sydney area.

name: Andre Automotive
description:
Andre Automotive, also affectionately known to locals as The Handsome Mechanic, is an award-winning, family-owned workshop in Mount Druitt. Established in 2014 by a Filipino owner, this community favourite specialises in comprehensive mechanical repairs, tyre services, and logbook servicing.

Customers frequently praise the team for their absolute transparency, fair pricing, and clean, spotless workshop. Whether you need a regular pink slip, a quick blue slip inspection, or complex clutch and rear main seal repairs, the mechanics are known for doing a thorough job. They are always happy to walk customers through their vehicle diagnostic reports and explain the work needed. The friendly staff make everyone feel welcome and looked after, like family.

For convenience, the workshop is open from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM on weekdays, and from 8:00 AM to 12:30 PM on Saturdays. Located on Kurrajong Avenue, it is a highly trusted destination for reliable and stress-free car care in Western Sydney.

name: Alliance of Philippine Community Organisations (APCO) Inc.
description:
The Alliance of Philippine Community Organisations (APCO) Inc., which rebranded to the Philippine Australian Multicultural Association (PAMAI) in 2025, is a prominent state federation in New South Wales. Established to support the Filipino-Australian community, this non-profit group provides vital advocacy, social welfare, and cultural programmes.

The association specialises in bringing people together by coordinating major local festivals. Its most notable event is the annual Pista sa Nayon, a vibrant celebration of Philippine Independence that showcases traditional music, dance, and food. The organisation also hosts the annual Freedom Day Ball and collaborates on community initiatives like Generations in Motion, which connects seniors with local youth to foster social cohesion.

Led by active community advocates, the federation focuses on empowering culturally and linguistically diverse seniors, international students, and vulnerable sectors. Through leadership and collaborative partnerships, the group preserves Filipino heritage while helping new migrants settle and connect in Sydney. It operates as a welcoming community hub, encouraging active participation in multicultural life across New South Wales.

name: Angel's Filipino Barbeque
description:
Angel's Filipino Barbeque was a popular dining spot located in the busy Dining Precinct of Westpoint Shopping Centre in Blacktown. It served as a key local venue for authentic Filipino street food and comfort classics in Greater Western Sydney.

The eatery specialised in traditional charcoal-grilled dishes, with customers particularly enjoying the well-marinated pork skewers and grilled chicken. Other highlights on the menu included sizzling sisig, sinigang prawn soup, and crispy pata, which was a deep-fried pork knuckle served alongside sweet and tangy achara salad. For those seeking classic breakfast-style meals, the restaurant offered tapsilog, featuring seasoned beef served with fragrant garlic rice and a fried egg. Sweets like the traditional halo-halo dessert were also a popular choice to finish a meal.

Diners regularly highlighted the genuine flavours that reminded them of home, as well as the welcoming smiles of the staff. While some guests experienced longer wait times during peak hours or noted smaller portion sizes on certain dishes, it was widely appreciated for bringing traditional tastes to the local community.

This business has permanently closed.

name: Antig Studios
description:
Antig Studios is a professional, high-end wedding, portrait, and lifestyle photography and videography studio specialising in capturing authentic stories for the Filipino-Australian community. With a deep commitment to preserving genuine emotions, they create beautifully crafted visual narratives that couples will treasure for a lifetime.

Clients rave about their exceptional storytelling and cinematic wedding films, frequently highlighting the team's professionalism, warmth, and fun approach on the day. Couples particularly value how natural and emotionally resonant the films are, even years down the track, bringing back cherished memories of their special day with tears and goosebumps. Working closely with their clients, the talented team is celebrated for becoming trusted friends and teammates, aligning perfectly with any preferred theme or aesthetic to deliver outstanding results.

They specialise in pre-wedding (prenup) photo shoots, event documentation, and comprehensive videography services that turn life's milestones into premium keepsakes. While based in Melbourne, Antig Studios operates widely across Victoria, Sydney, and all of Australia, ensuring their premium visual storytelling is accessible to families and couples nationwide.

name: Asia Pacific Groceries Pty ltd (Filipino)
description:
Asia Pacific Groceries is a popular Asian grocery store located on Nelson Street in Wallsend, serving the Newcastle community. The shop specialises in authentic Filipino staples and products, making it a favourite destination for those seeking a taste of home.

Customers highly appreciate the extensive product range and the weekly arrival of fresh items. The store stocks a wide selection of goods, including bulk rice, taro, green bananas, noodles, and various regional sauces and marinades. For those with a sweet tooth, the shelves feature popular items like ube candies, cheese powder, and fish crackers. Additionally, the shop provides homemade hot food, fresh sweet bread, and a selection of African hair products. Practical amenities such as Ria Money Transfer and MoneyGram services are also available on-site.

The store is well-known for its welcoming atmosphere, thanks to the friendly and accommodating staff. Visitors frequently highlight the excellent customer service and the helpfulness of the owners, Lorna and her team, who ensure a pleasant shopping experience. Whether you are looking for specific ingredients for traditional cooking or simply browsing for unique snacks, this centre offers a convenient and friendly local hub.

name: Asian Fresh Food Market Rooty Hill
description:
Asian Fresh Food Market in Rooty Hill is a popular community hub specialising in Filipino and Asian groceries. Located in Western Sydney, this one-stop shop provides a wide selection of fresh fruits, vegetables, meat, and seafood at very affordable prices.

Local shoppers frequent the store to stock up on their favourite Pinoy pantry staples, including sauces, rice, snacks, and popular noodle brands. The fresh meat section is particularly well-known, offering various cuts for traditional dishes, though it can get quite busy during peak hours. For those looking for a quick bite, the onsite eatery serves authentic Cebuano cuisine, including pork skewers with house-made barbecue sauce.

Customers appreciate the helpful and friendly service from local staff members like Mae and Maegan, who go out of their way to assist with product enquiries. While the shop does not provide trolleys for the car park, the bargain prices and extensive range make it a worthwhile trip. Whether you are cooking a traditional family meal or looking for unique ingredients, this market is a central destination for the local Filipino community in Rooty Hill.

name: Association of Filipino Australian in Sydney Inc.
description:
The Association of Filipino-Australian in Sydney (AFAS) is a dedicated non-government community organisation focused on promoting, preserving, and perpetuating Filipino heritage in the Greater Sydney region. Through a vibrant calendar of cultural, educational, and charitable activities, AFAS provides a highly supportive environment where community members can connect and celebrate their shared history.

As a key community hub, the association is active in organising local events, fundraisers, and educational initiatives that support community integration while keeping rich cultural traditions alive for younger generations. Known for its friendly, approachable, and welcoming atmosphere, AFAS is run by a team of passionate volunteers who assist newcomers with settling in and foster strong connections within the broader Filipino-Australian community in New South Wales.

Whether hosting traditional festival celebrations, coordinating charitable outreach programs, or offering guidance to families, the group plays an essential role in bringing people together. By encouraging cultural pride and mutual support, the association continues to build a strong, inclusive, and connected network for the Filipino diaspora across Sydney.

name: Association of Golden Australian Pilipinos Inc.
description:
The Association of Golden Australian Pilipinos Inc. (AGAPI) is a registered not-for-profit community organisation active since 2005 in the Greater Western Sydney region, with strong ties to the Blacktown area. Dedicated to supporting the older generation of Filipino-Australians aged 55 and over, the association plays an important role in promoting senior welfare. It actively fosters camaraderie, fights social isolation, and helps members preserve their rich cultural customs and values in their new home.

AGAPI is well-known in the local area for its vibrant calendar of social gatherings and celebrations. The group hosts regular birthday luncheons, Mother's Day celebrations, Melbourne Cup events, and United Nations Day costume parades. Members also participate in broader community initiatives, including the Blacktown City Festival, the Multicultural Seniors Festival, and traditional religious observances like Flores de Mayo. Through these varied activities, the organisation provides a supportive network and a friendly environment where seniors can share experiences, stay physically and socially active, and maintain strong links to their Filipino heritage.

name: Association of Golden Australian Pilipinos Inc. (AGAPI)
description:
The Association of Golden Australian Pilipinos Inc. (AGAPI) is a dedicated community organisation based in Rooty Hill. It supports Filipino-Australian seniors aged 55 and over in the Greater Western Sydney region. The group focuses on promoting camaraderie and brotherhood. It helps members stay connected while preserving beloved Filipino customs, cultural traditions, and values.

Members and their families appreciate the warmth and active fellowship that the group provides. AGAPI regularly organises social and cultural gatherings to mark key milestones. They celebrate traditional Filipino festivals, such as Flores de Mayo and Santacruzan. The organisation also hosts popular events like Mother's Day luncheons and regular fellowship meetings.

These activities are held at local community venues, including the Rooty Hill School of Arts and St Mary MacKillop Hall. Led by President Dorothy Del Villar, the group actively encourages seniors to participate in the broader multicultural Australian society. At the same time, it maintains strong links to their cultural heritage. It offers a welcoming space for older community members to socialise, share experiences, and integrate smoothly into local community life.

name: Association of Pinoy Students in Australia
description:
The Association of Pinoy Students in Australia (APSA) is a dedicated, volunteer-led community organisation established in 2014. The group's primary focus is on supporting and assisting Filipino international students and temporary migrants transitioning to life in Australia.

APSA plays a vital role in student welfare through targeted programmes and support networks. A key initiative is the Feed-A-Student programme, which distributes food packs and grocery assistance to students in need. The organisation also partners with other community bodies like the Alliance of Philippine Community Organisations (APCO). Together, they run vital projects such as winter relief distributions and job fairs in Western Sydney hubs like Blacktown.

In addition to welfare support, APSA organises regular social gatherings and bicultural support networks. These activities help students connect, share experiences, and build lasting friendships to combat homesickness and foster camaraderie. By providing both material assistance and a welcoming social community, APSA helps Filipino international students feel supported and valued during their educational journey in Australia.

name: Ate Maria Cleaning Services
description:
Ate Maria Cleaning Services was a dedicated domestic and commercial cleaning provider that served households and corporate offices throughout Liverpool and the wider Western Sydney region. Highly regarded within the local community, this family-focused business specialised in delivering thorough, reliable, and high-quality cleaning solutions tailored to the unique needs of busy households and local workplaces. The team committed themselves to ensuring spotless and hygienic environments, establishing a reputation built on trust, care, and consistent attention to detail.

Clients frequently turned to the service for comprehensive solutions, which ranged from regular home maintenance and deep seasonal cleaning to commercial workspace tidying. By focusing on friendly customer service and utilising efficient cleaning methods, the provider helped families maintain healthy, comfortable, and beautifully organised living spaces. Their flexible offerings allowed residents to enjoy the comfort of a pristine home without the stress of performing the chores themselves.

Please note that Ate Maria Cleaning Services has permanently closed its operations and is no longer accepting bookings or providing services in the Sydney area.

name: Atienza Taxation and Accounting Services
description: Atienza Taxation and Accounting Services is a family-run accounting practice based in Plumpton, Western Sydney. Operated by a dedicated mother-and-daughter duo, the practice specialises in individual, sole trader, partnership, and company tax returns, as well as business setups and BAS services. Clients highly value their professional, welcoming, and down-to-earth approach. The team excels at translating complex Australian tax regulations into clear, easy-to-understand guidance. They pay close attention to detail to help clients legally maximise their tax claims and refunds, making tax time smooth and stress-free. Whether managing employment income or helping sole traders set up their businesses, they provide personalised, reliable support. The practice operates from a comfortable home office space with convenient street parking. Appointments are preferred to ensure tailored attention, though walk-ins can be accommodated. Operating from Monday to Saturday, they are a trusted choice for local residents and small businesses looking for friendly and competent taxation expertise.

name: Atienza Taxation and Accounting Services
description: Atienza Taxation and Accounting Services is a family-owned accounting firm in Plumpton, specialising in personalised taxation services for the Western Sydney community. The practice assists individual tax filers, sole traders, and small business owners with their financial compliance and business planning. Known for their approachable and down-to-earth manner, lead tax consultants Maria Atienza and Vanessa are highly regarded by locals for making tax season simple and stress-free. Clients appreciate the firm's clear explanations and prompt communication. The team takes time to help clients understand their tax obligations and identify deductions they might otherwise miss. They manage complex financial situations, such as multiple income streams, sole trader accounts, and overdue tax lodging, with transparency and honesty. The office provides a homey environment with convenient street parking. While the practice prioritises appointments to ensure dedicated client attention, they can also accommodate walk-ins. Whether you are in Plumpton, Rooty Hill, Mount Druitt, or Blacktown, this local firm offers reliable and friendly professional accounting support.

name: Atin Ito Filipino Finest Cuisine
description:
Atin Ito Filipino Finest Cuisine is a welcoming, family-owned restaurant located in Cringila, a short drive from Wollongong. Established in July 2023 by pastor and restaurateur Roger Lingal, the venue specialises in authentic, home-style Filipino cooking served with legendary Pinoy hospitality. Inside, the cosy dining room features homely decorations that create a warm, family-friendly atmosphere.

Diners can explore an extensive menu filled with traditional favourites. Popular dishes include the signature pork sisig, crispy bagnet pinakbet, and slow-roasted Cebu lechon with perfectly crackled skin. The restaurant also serves classic sweets and desserts, such as refreshing halo-halo, banana Q, and sweet glutinous rice cakes like biko. For mid-week dining, the affordable lunch specials, such as chicken tocino served with garlic rice, are a local favourite.

The restaurant is open for lunch and dinner from Wednesday to Saturday. On the last Saturday of each month, the team hosts a highly popular buffet night that showcases a broad range of classic dishes. Whether you are dining in or picking up takeaway, the friendly staff make everyone feel like part of the family.

name: Augustinian Recollect Sisters' Convent
description:
The Augustinian Recollect Sisters' Convent is a welcoming Catholic church convent located in the suburb of Doonside, New South Wales. This religious centre serves as a vital spiritual hub for the Filipino-Australian community in Western Sydney. The convent hosts a regular monthly Filipino Catholic Mass, which takes place on the third Sunday of each month at 1:30 p.m., drawing families together for worship and community connection.

In addition to the monthly Filipino Mass, the convent schedules regular service times, including Masses on Friday evenings at 5:30 p.m. and Saturday evenings at 6:00 p.m. on the first, third, and fourth Saturdays of the month. Members of the local congregation value the peaceful atmosphere of the convent for silent prayer, reflection, and spiritual support.

Managed by the Congregation of the Augustinian Recollect Sisters, the convent actively organises and supports various community and pastoral initiatives, such as marriage enrichment seminars. This active involvement helps strengthen cultural and spiritual ties among local residents. The convent's dedicated sisters offer warm guidance, fostering a sense of belonging and mutual support in the community.

name: Ausphin
description:
Ausphin Group is a hospitality recruitment, training, and skilled migration services provider with its main Sydney office located in Parramatta. The group specialises in bridging Australia's skills gap. They connect talented international hospitality professionals, particularly from the Filipino community, with leading employers. They provide comprehensive career pathways through vocational education, traineeships, and Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL) assessments.

Clients frequently praise the dedicated team at Ausphin for their structured, step-by-step guidance through complex visa applications. Many candidates credit the staff with helping them transition successfully from subclass 407 training visas to subclass 482 and 494 sponsorship visas. Reviewers highlight the team's patience and thoroughness in checking documents and organising job interviews or trial shifts. By offering personalised support, the agency helps reduce the stress associated with migration and employment processes.

Ausphin Group operates from Monday to Friday, between 8:30 am and 5:30 pm. Their Sydney office is situated on Level 49 at 8 Parramatta Square, Parramatta, making it highly accessible for consultations. With additional offices in the Philippines, New Zealand, and the UAE, they offer a trusted global network for both job seekers and hospitality businesses.

name: Aussie-Link Visa Services
description:
Aussie-Link Visa Services is a professional migration agency based in Bankstown, Sydney, specialising in Australian visa and migration assistance. Led by registered migration agent Hana, the team helps clients navigate the complex immigration process with ease. They provide dedicated support for various visa pathways, including partner, student, work, training, visitor, and tourist visas, as well as citizenship applications.

Clients highly appreciate Hana's supportive, professional, and patient guidance throughout their migration journeys. She is known for her prompt communication and thorough attention to detail, which helps make stressful situations feel manageable and straightforward. The agency has a strong reputation for successfully handling complex cases, including bridging visas and cases with previous refusals.

With a focus on delivering positive outcomes, Aussie-Link Visa Services assists applicants from the Philippines and other countries in securing their Australian visas. The office operates Monday to Friday from 9 am to 5 pm, and on Saturdays from 9 am to 1 pm, providing a reliable and trusted service for the local community.

name: Australia Philippine Chamber of Commerce
description:
The Australia Philippine Chamber of Commerce (APCCI) is a dedicated non-profit organisation based in St Clair, Sydney. The group focuses on strengthening trade, investment, and business links between Australia and the Philippines. Acting as a vital support network, APCCI assists small and growing enterprises, as well as individual professionals, who are navigating business opportunities in both countries.

By fostering strategic partnerships and organising regular networking events, the chamber aims to create sustainable avenues for mutual economic growth. They advocate for the needs of their members, providing them with a platform to voice their business concerns and ideas. Members can connect with industry peers and share expertise to help build a stronger local business presence.

If you are looking to expand your business network, access practical advice, or collaborate on cross-border projects, APCCI provides a friendly and professional community to support your goals. Their office is contactable from Monday to Friday, serving as a key resource for the Filipino-Australian business community in New South Wales.

name: Australian Devotees of Our Lady of Peñafrancia, Inc.
description:
The Australian Devotees of Our Lady of Peñafrancia (ADOLP) Inc. is a church-based community organisation in Greater Sydney. Established in 2006, the group is dedicated to propagating the devotion to Our Lady of Peñafrancia, affectionately known as 'Ina'. Based at St Nicholas of Myra Parish in Penrith, the association serves as an important spiritual and cultural hub for the Filipino-Australian community, particularly for those from the Bicol region.

Devotees gather at the Penrith parish for regular novena services and masses on the first Saturday of each month. The highlight of their calendar is the annual feast day celebration, held on the third Saturday of September. This popular event features a beautiful fluvial procession on the Nepean River, bringing traditional Filipino water-borne processions to Western Sydney.

The organisation is highly regarded for its positive impact on the local community. It has received formal recognition from local government and the New South Wales Parliament for fostering social inclusion, assisting new migrants, and preserving rich cultural traditions. The warm fellowship and vibrant celebrations make it a welcoming centre for anyone looking to connect with Filipino heritage.

name: Australian Filipino Healthcare Alliance
description: The Australian Filipino Healthcare Alliance was founded as a community organisation in Sydney, New South Wales, to support and connect healthcare workers of Filipino heritage. Based along the prominent Macquarie Street medical precinct in the Sydney central business district, the association focused on fostering professional development, resource sharing, and networking opportunities for Filipino-Australian nurses, doctors, and allied health professionals. By encouraging collaboration and cultural connection, the alliance aimed to help its members navigate the Australian healthcare sector, share career guidance, and participate in community health initiatives. The group served as a central hub for members to socialise, discuss industry developments, and collaborate on professional goals. It provided a valuable platform for healthcare practitioners seeking to build their professional network while maintaining strong cultural ties to the Filipino community in New South Wales. Members could access peer support and share insights about practice standards and clinical education. This organisation has permanently closed.

name: Australian Filipino Women Support Group Inc. (AFWomen)
description:
The Australian Filipino Women Support Group Inc. (AFWomen) is a dedicated non-profit organisation based in Blacktown. Formed by a passionate group of Filipino women, the group campaigns for meaningful change regarding domestic and family violence within the community.

AFWomen focuses on the safety and well-being of Filipino-Australian women and their children. They provide a safe and supportive network where individuals can build resilience and recover from traumatic experiences. The organisation connects women with essential services through referrals to community education, counseling, and training programs.

Operating as a community-focused advocacy group, they collaborate closely with government agencies, health centres, and other local support services. While they do not have a physical storefront or public office, they remain highly active throughout Greater Western Sydney. They support local residents through community partnerships, informative workshops, and public events.

If you need to contact the group for resources or advice, you can reach out via email at afwomen21@gmail.com or by calling 0412 269 930. If you are in immediate danger, please always dial Triple Zero (000) for emergency services.

name: Australian Immigration Law Services (Imelda Argel)
description:
Australian Immigration Law Services (AILS) is a prominent Sydney-based legal and migration practice that has been supporting the Filipino-Australian and international student communities since 1994. Conveniently located in Tower One Barangaroo, the firm specialises in providing honest, clear, and comprehensive advice for complex visa pathways, including 189 permanent residency, 485 graduate visas, citizenship, bridging visas, and ministerial interventions.

Under the guidance of principal solicitor Imelda Argel and founder Karl Konrad, the dedicated team—including senior agents Han and Zoe—is widely praised for their professional, meticulous, and compassionate approach. Clients appreciate their realistic assessments and dedication to maximising success, particularly for challenging cases that other agencies might decline. AILS also maintains an active public presence, producing informative YouTube guides to keep applicants updated on changing immigration regulations. Whether you require guidance on an independent application or full case management, AILS offers a reliable, supportive partner for your journey to making Australia your home.

name: Australian Visayan Multicultural Communities Inc.
description:
Australian Visayan Multicultural Communities Inc., commonly known as AUSVISAYAN, is a dedicated Filipino-Australian community organisation based in Sydney, New South Wales. Established to support and represent the interests of Filipino migrants from the Visayas region of the Philippines, the group plays an active role in preserving and promoting Visayan cultural heritage, language, and traditions within the multicultural landscape of Australia.

As a registered affiliate organisation of the Philippine Community Council of New South Wales Inc. (PCCNSW), AUSVISAYAN collaborates closely with other regional bodies, including the Luzon and Mindanao community groups. Together, they organise and participate in major cultural celebrations, such as the annual Philippines-Australia Friendship Day, the Philippine Christmas Festival, and Australia Day events. These activities showcase traditional music, regional dances, and community gatherings that help keep cultural ties alive for the diaspora.

Beyond cultural advocacy, the organisation focuses on fostering social, sports, and community development. It serves as an important support network for newly arrived Visayan-Filipinos, offering outreach programmes and helping them integrate into Australian society while maintaining their regional identity. Through collaborative events and advocacy, AUSVISAYAN ensures that the unique heritage of the Visayas region is celebrated and passed down to future generations of Filipino-Australians.

name: Auxilium Migration Services
description:
Auxilium Migration Services is a boutique Australian migration agency based in Wolli Creek, Sydney. Led by Registered Migration Agent Mayumi Bautista (MARN 1801614), the practice specialises in offering professional and compassionate immigration advice. They support individuals, families, and organisations navigating the complex Australian visa system.

Clients appreciate their transparent and heart-led approach. Testimonials highlight how easy the team is to deal with, particularly when coordinating partner visas, family reunions, and permanent residency pathways. Mayumi and her colleague, Ms Jen, receive warm praise for their thoroughness, clear communication, and dedicated support during difficult applications.

Auxilium Migration Services provides guidance across a wide range of visa categories, including temporary visas, citizenship pathways, skilled migration, and appeals for visa refusals. The boutique agency focuses on delivering honest, candid assessments grounded in migration law. Operating from Wolli Creek, they assist local clients in Sydney and offer online consultations to support people worldwide. Whether you are reuniting with family or seeking long-term settlement, the team aims to make your migration journey clear and manageable.

name: B.A.R. Cafe and Resto
description:
B.A.R. Cafe and Resto was a popular dining spot located in the coastal suburb of Dee Why, Sydney, that specialised in serving authentic Filipino cuisine. Nestled in the Dee Why Village Plaza along Oaks Avenue, this establishment offered a warm, welcoming, and relaxed atmosphere for locals looking to experience traditional Filipino flavours on the Northern Beaches.

The menu featured a range of classic dishes prepared with care, including crowd favourites like crispy lechon, savoury sisig, garlic rice, and various pork delicacies. The kitchen also prepared unique, highly delightful savoury Bicol dishes made to order, showcasing the diverse regional culinary traditions of the Philippines. Beyond their regular dine-in and takeaway options, the cafe was highly regarded for providing excellent catering services, making it a popular choice for local family events, special celebrations, and community gatherings. Visitors frequently praised the restaurant for its friendly staff, prompt service, and reasonable prices. It served as a wonderful hub for the local Filipino-Australian community and food enthusiasts alike.

This business has permanently closed.

name: BISA Education Consultancy
description:
BISA Education Consultancy is a dedicated education and student visa agency based in Merrylands, Sydney. They specialise in guiding Filipino students through the entire process of starting their educational journey in Australia. The agency assists clients with course selection, school enrolments, scholarship opportunities, and student visa applications.

Students highly praise the consultancy for its hands-on approach, transparency, and expert guidance. Many clients report that the team makes the application process feel fast and hassle-free, reducing the stress commonly associated with moving abroad. The team guides applicants step-by-step, ensuring all documents are correctly prepared before submission.

To help prospective students start their journey, BISA Education Consultancy provides free consultations and initial eligibility assessments. They work with a wide range of accredited partner schools, colleges, and universities across Australia. Whether you are a senior high school graduate, a current college student, or a working professional seeking career progression, this friendly agency offers personalised support. You can contact their Sydney office or reach them via their active social media profiles to start planning your study pathway.

name: BM Express
description: BM Express is a long-standing cargo shipping and money remittance service provider that has specialised in serving the Filipino-Australian community in Sydney for over thirty years. Based in Minto, this dependable business is highly regarded for its door-to-door balikbayan box shipping services to the Philippines, offering both sea and air cargo options. Local customers regularly praise the accommodating and polite team, noting that the booking and pickup processes are smooth, punctual, and supported by helpful SMS notifications. Over the decades, many families have trusted BM Express to deliver their parcels safely and in original condition, appreciating the care taken with each shipment. Alongside their secure logistics management, they offer straightforward money transfer options to help residents support their families back home. Operating daily from 9:00 am to 5:00 pm, the branch makes it easy for clients to drop off parcels or enquire about tracking. Whether you are sending cargo boxes, gifts, or transferring funds, BM Express provides a reliable and friendly connection between Sydney and the Philippines.

name: BRD Accounting & Taxation Services
description:
BRD Accounting & Taxation Services is a Filipino-owned accounting firm located in Granville, New South Wales. Formerly known as Aquino & Associates, this local practice has supported the community with tax preparation and business advisory services for over thirty years. The team offers personal and business tax return lodgments, business registration, setup, and Business Activity Statements (BAS). 

Clients can receive assistance in both English and Filipino, making their services highly accessible. Loyal patrons appreciate the convenient location, which is situated just a short walk from Granville train station. Many long-term clients highlight the friendly assistance they receive from team members like Ms Alyn when filing their annual claims.

While the practice has experienced leadership changes following the departure of its founder, Cesar Aquino, it remains a popular choice for local residents and small business owners. The firm focuses on tailoring its tax solutions to meet individual needs, helping clients navigate the lodging process with convenience. Whether you are setting up a new business structure or lodging a straightforward tax return, this accessible office provides reliable local support.

name: Bakbakan Martial Arts Centre
description:
Bakbakan Martial Arts Centre, operating under Power Core MMA in Marrickville, is a welcoming, family-run gym specialising in a diverse range of disciplines. Under the guidance of Head Coach Rahneer Fabi, the Australian Chief Instructor of Bakbakan International, the centre offers top-tier training in Mixed Martial Arts (MMA), Muay Thai, Boxing, Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu, and Filipino Martial Arts (Kali).

Students frequently praise the supportive and friendly community vibe, noting that coaches like Rahner and Ronan provide highly detailed, personalised instruction that feels like a private lesson. The gym is well-equipped with modern facilities and air conditioning, ensuring a clean and comfortable training space. 

Programs cater to all skill levels, from absolute beginners to advanced competitors looking to hone their technique. The centre also features a dedicated kids' program designed to build self-confidence, coordination, and discipline in a safe environment, complete with fun games like dodgeball. With a strong emphasis on mutual respect, older students actively support and role-model positive behaviours for the younger ones. Located in the heart of Marrickville, it is a perfect spot for locals looking to learn self-defence, improve fitness, and join a tight-knit fitness family.

name: BakeFresh
description:
BakeFresh is a welcoming Filipino bakeshop located at 45 Oxford Road in Ingleburn, Sydney. Specialising in traditional Filipino breads and sweets, this local bakery has become a favourite neighbourhood spot for authentic treats.

Customers highly recommend their signature ube cake, which features a moist, light sponge with creamy frosting and a delicious layer of coconut filling. Other popular sweet treats include ensaymadas, yema cakes, flan, and tropical smoothies. For those seeking classic savoury options, the shop bakes soft pandesal and Spanish bread fresh daily, alongside meat-filled buns. They also serve a popular bacon and egg roll with a perfectly cooked egg, which pairs wonderfully with their smooth coffee and iced coffee.

The bakeshop is known for its clean, inviting atmosphere and friendly, accommodating staff who are always happy to help. Whether you are stopping by for a morning coffee, picking up a birthday cake, or satisfying a craving for traditional Filipino sweets, BakeFresh provides a lovely slice of community flavour in Sydney's south-west.

name: Baker's Avenue
description:
Baker's Avenue is a popular, family-run Filipino bakery situated in Blacktown, Sydney. This local favourite specialises in traditional Filipino pastries and breads, offering a wide array of freshly baked goods that are well priced and generous in size.

Customers particularly enjoy the classic pan de sal, asado buns, and savoury pork empanadas. The bakery is also highly regarded for its sweet treats, including cheese rolls, ube cheese pandesal, and their popular ensaymadas, which feature a classic coating of butter, sugar, and cheese. For those seeking recommendations, the friendly staff are happy to suggest popular options like the ube Spanish bread or the sweet long johns.

With its convenient location in Blacktown's vibrant community hub, Baker's Avenue serves as an accessible spot for the local Filipino diaspora and visitors wishing to experience authentic Pinoy baked goods. The shop operates on a first-come, first-served basis, ensuring that customers receive fresh bakes throughout the day.

name: Bankstown District Uniting Church (Filipino Fellowship)
description:
The Bankstown District Uniting Church is a vibrant and welcoming multi-ethnic Christian congregation located in Bankstown, New South Wales. This active church community is dedicated to serving the diverse local population, offering specialised worship fellowships including a dedicated Filipino Worship Service held every Sunday from 11:30 AM to 12:30 PM.

Members of the community appreciate the warm and lively atmosphere of the church, noting the friendly and encouraging environment during fellowship gatherings. Beyond its regular worship services, which also include Cook Island, Tongan, and Arabic groups, the church is widely recognised for its extensive community outreach and newcomer support initiatives. A particular highlight for many is the free English conversation (ESL) classes taught by enthusiastic and supportive instructors. The congregation also provides youth programs, family ministries, and has historically organised local food relief initiatives to support vulnerable residents. Located at 72 Kitchener Parade, close to the heart of Bankstown, this church serves as a vital spiritual centre and community hub for the local Filipino-Australian community and anyone seeking spiritual connection, friendship, or peace.

name: Bankstown Filcos Association Incorporated
description:
Bankstown Filcos Association Incorporated is a dedicated community organisation that supports the spiritual, social, and cultural needs of the Filipino-Australian community in the Canterbury-Bankstown area. Established to foster a strong sense of unity, the group focuses on preserving and enriching Christian values among local Filipino families.

The organisation works closely to assist and support the Spiritual Director in various ministry and pastoral works. Through this partnership, they help run programmes designed to address the spiritual, social, intellectual, and moral needs of the community. These initiatives provide valuable support structures for kababayans, helping them navigate life in Australia while maintaining their rich cultural heritage.

In addition to faith-based activities, the association actively promotes cultural preservation and social integration. They encourage members to participate in local multicultural events, strengthening connections with the broader Australian society. By fostering both community spirit and cultural pride, Bankstown Filcos Association Incorporated serves as an important support network for Filipinos in Sydney's south-west.

name: Bar-B-Skew
description:
Bar-B-Skew is a popular Sydney-based food truck and stall specialising in traditional Filipino charcoal-grilled street food. Bringing the vibrant flavours of Pinoy grilling to local markets and festivals, this mobile vendor has built a dedicated community following.

Customers consistently praise the generous, chunky portions of their expertly marinated meat. The chicken and pork skewers are particularly popular, offering tender cuts with a rich, smoky flavour. For a complete meal experience, these grilled skewers are typically paired with classic Java rice and tangy home-made atchara, which cuts through the richness of the meat. Beyond their signature skewers, the team also serves delicious burgers that fuse traditional Filipino marinades with modern styles.

Operating at major community events across the region, Bar-B-Skew has showcased its menu at prominent locations like Parramatta Lanes, the Sydney Cherry Blossom Festival in Auburn, and Canberra’s Spilt Milk festival. Whether you are catching them at a local weekend market or a larger cultural food event, they offer a friendly, approachable dining experience that highlights authentic Filipino barbecue culture.

name: Barong Tagalog Filipiniana
description:
Barong Tagalog Filipiniana is a boutique retail shop located in Blacktown, Sydney, specialising in traditional and formal Filipino clothing. Situated in Shop 3 of the Station Arcade on Main Street, this store serves as a helpful resource for the local community seeking authentic garments for cultural events, weddings, and other special occasions.

Customers appreciate the friendly and attentive assistance provided by the owners, who focus on helping individuals find the perfect outfit. The store offers a range of attire, including the classic Barong Tagalog for men and elegant Filipiniana garments for women. These traditional items are crafted with attention to detail, preserving Filipino heritage and design.

The shop is open from 10:00 am to 5:00 pm from Wednesday through Monday, and is closed on Sundays. It provides a convenient physical location in Greater Western Sydney where patrons can browse options in person. For enquiries or assistance with fitting and availability, customers can contact the boutique directly on 0425 306 583.

name: Barong at Bestida Australia
description:
Barong at Bestida Australia is a specialised clothing boutique in Campbelltown, Sydney, that offers authentic, high-quality traditional and modern Filipino formal wear. Inspired by the rich cultural heritage of the Philippines, the store features a stunning collection of custom-designed Barong Tagalog (the national formal shirt) and Filipiniana dresses (bestida) for men, women, and children.

Local customers highly recommend the shop for its exceptional customer service and warm hospitality. Visitors appreciate the helpful guidance from the passionate owners, Vanessa and her team, who often offer warm coffee to guests during fittings. Reviewers highlight the prompt responsiveness of their communication, particularly when helping clients prepare for significant events like weddings, citizenship ceremonies, or cultural celebrations.

The boutique is known for its beautiful craftsmanship, attention to detail, and soft, thick fabric choices like satin. In addition to formal wear, they also supply traditional items such as sungka game boards. They accommodate custom garment requests and coordinate reliable, fast shipping services locally and internationally. The shop is located at 274 Queen Street and is open from Wednesday to Sunday.

name: Bay Predo’s Lechon Cebu
description:
Bay Predo’s Lechon Cebu is a vibrant, family-friendly Filipino restaurant located in the homewares centre car park in Warwick Farm, Sydney. Specialising in Cebu-style roast pig, they serve authentic, spit-roasted lechon with incredibly crispy crackling and juicy, flavourful meat.

Beyond their signature roast pork, the extensive menu features a range of classic Filipino favourites. Diners can enjoy the sizzling lechon sisig seasoned with chilli and garlic, char-grilled pork skewers accompanied by a fresh mango salad, and traditional pancit and longanisa. For dessert, the popular halo-halo and leche flan offer a perfect, sweet finish.

The venue has a laid-back, cosy atmosphere reminiscent of a seaside diner, complete with warm, hospitable service. Adding to the joyful and social dining experience, Bay Predo’s regularly hosts live music and karaoke sessions where guests are encouraged to request songs or take the stage themselves. With a fully licensed bar stocking Filipino beers like San Miguel and Red Horse, it is a fantastic spot to enjoy a relaxed lunch or a lively dinner with friends and family.

name: Bayani Imports Pty Ltd
description:
Bayani Imports is a dedicated importer and supplier of authentic Filipino products based in Minchinbury, Sydney. The business specialises in distributing dried and frozen food, alongside health and beauty products, to Asian supermarkets throughout Australia. They work closely with local businesses to ensure a steady supply of popular household brands.

Customers highly appreciate their accommodating team and friendly service, noting that their selections offer an impressive taste of home. They ensure all products, from staple pantry goods to unique specialty items, are fresh, high quality, and well-packaged. The friendly staff are widely praised for being helpful and accommodating to all client needs.

By making popular brands accessible, Bayani Imports helps connect the local community with their favourite products from the Philippines. Located at 18 Eddie Road in Minchinbury, the business operates on weekdays, providing reliable logistics and distribution support. They are committed to helping local stores bring high-quality products directly to Filipino-Australian families.

name: Bayanihan News
description:
Bayanihan News was a prominent Filipino-Australian community newspaper and news portal based in Sydney, New South Wales. Established in the late 1990s under the leadership of publisher and editor Nonoy Perdon, the publication served as a vital media hub for the Filipino diaspora across Australia. It provided comprehensive coverage of local community events, immigration updates, cultural stories, and business developments, alongside news from the Philippines.

The publication featured articles in both English and Filipino, specialising in keeping readers informed about issues affecting the community while celebrating Filipino-Australian achievements. As a long-standing ethnic media outlet, it played a key role in fostering connections and preserving cultural heritage. The news portal also acted as a digital resource directory, offering guidance on migration, taxation, and community support programmes.

Following many years of service to the Filipino-Australian community, the publication ceased operations, and the associated corporate entity was voluntarily deregistered. This business has permanently closed.

name: Bethel Bible Baptist Church
description:
Bethel Bible Baptist Church is a warm, independent Baptist congregation located in Ingleburn, South West Sydney. Established as a close-knit community of about 45 regular attendees, the church provides a welcoming, family-like environment for everyone. Pastor Alfredo (Freddie) Arellano has led the congregation since 1997, welcoming a diverse membership with a strong Filipino presence.

Attendees appreciate the friendly atmosphere, where ushers greet visitors to ensure they feel comfortable. Long-term members value the church’s focus on biblically grounded preaching and traditional godly music. The church offers several weekly services, including Sunday School at 9:30 am, followed by main worship services at 10:30 am and 1:30 pm. There is also a mid-week service on Wednesdays at 7:00 pm and a youth group meeting on Fridays at 6:30 pm. Services are also broadcast online via YouTube for those unable to attend in person.

Families are well supported with nursery services for children aged six and under, managed by checked volunteers. A foyer equipped with video and audio feeds allows parents with newborns to watch the service comfortably. There is no dress code, making it easy for newcomers to join.

name: Beverly Simmons-Hurtado (Your Celebrant & Ceremony)
description:
Beverly Simmons-Hurtado, operating as Your Celebrant & Ceremony, is a professional bilingual marriage celebrant and wedding master of ceremonies based in Maroubra, Sydney. Fluent in English and Tagalog, she specialises in crafting warm, culturally inclusive ceremonies that weave in traditional Filipino wedding customs.

Beverly is highly regarded by local couples for her calm, reassuring presence and positive energy. She is extremely organised and adaptable, helping to make the planning process smooth and stress-free. Clients regularly praise her ability to manage timelines, coordinate with other vendors, and handle last-minute schedule changes with confidence. In addition to officiating, she offers complete reception master of ceremonies services to keep the entire event flowing naturally.

Her experience includes officiating on Married At First Sight and being named an ABIA Marriage Celebrant finalist. Based in Sydney's eastern suburbs, Beverly is happy to travel for weddings across New South Wales, including the Hunter Valley and the South Coast. She provides a supportive, professional service to ensure every couple's unique story is beautifully celebrated.

name: Bff Amanda Hair And Beauty Expert
description:
Bff Amanda Hair and Beauty Expert is a dedicated Filipino-owned hair salon located in Blacktown, Sydney. Registered under Antonio Lorenzo Agustin, the salon has built a strong reputation in the local community, attracting clients from across the Sydney region, including Ryde.

The salon specialises in advanced hair transformations and restorative treatments. Clients particularly praise the signature home-brand Kerabond hair straightening treatment, which leaves hair smooth, soft, and naturally manageable. The salon also offers professional hair colouring, balayage, keratin smoothing, nanoplasty, and precision haircuts.

Visitors frequently highlight the warm, welcoming, and relaxed atmosphere of the salon. Owner Amanda is highly regarded for her consistent, professional, and patient approach. She takes the time to offer genuine hair care advice and tailored recommendations rather than rushing through services. To help clients maintain their results, the salon offers a custom line of aftercare products, including specialist shampoos, conditioners, hair masks, and serums. It is a trusted choice for those seeking high-quality hair care in a friendly local setting.

name: Bhajune Hair & Makeup
description:
Bhajune Hair & Makeup is a welcoming, Filipino-owned and operated hair salon situated on Level 3 of Westfield Parramatta. The salon specialises in a broad range of hair and beauty services, including precision haircuts, custom styling, professional makeup application, keratin treatments, and vibrant hair colouring. Led by June and his attentive team of stylists, such as Amore, the salon is known for tailoring cuts and styles to suit each client's individual features and preferences.

Regular visitors highly appreciate the friendly and caring nature of the staff, with some clients travelling from far across Sydney to receive services here. Customer reviews frequently highlight the excellent quality of their blowdries, which are noted for being long-lasting, smooth, and shiny. The stylists are also praised for their expertise in creating custom bangs, colouring, and styling that boost client confidence.

Conveniently located within the Westfield Parramatta shopping centre, Bhajune Hair & Makeup is easily accessible by public transport and has ample parking. It is a popular spot for the local Filipino-Australian community and anyone seeking professional styling in a warm environment. Clients are encouraged to request a detailed quote before their styling session to ensure their expectations are fully met.

name: Bible Baptist Church (Prestons)
description:
Bible Baptist Church in Prestons is a welcoming, independent Baptist congregation located in the Liverpool region of Western Sydney. Operating as a King James Bible-believing fellowship, the church has built deep connections with the local Filipino-Australian community. It actively supports a global network of pastors and missionaries, reflecting its strong community focus.

Members and visitors appreciate the warm, friendly atmosphere and the inspiring worship services. A highlight of the weekly assemblies is the focus on traditional, God-honouring music and sound biblical preaching. The church also runs a dedicated ministry designed specifically for teens and young adults, helping them grow in their faith.

The congregation meets regularly for worship and study throughout the week. Weekly gatherings include Sunday School from 9:30 am to 10:30 am, followed by the main Sunday Morning Service from 10:30 am to 12:30 pm. An afternoon service is also hosted on Sundays from 4:00 pm to 6:00 pm. For midweek encouragement, prayer and Bible study are held on Wednesdays from 7:00 pm to 8:30 pm. Located at Lyn Parade, it provides a supportive spiritual centre for individuals and families in Western Sydney.

name: Bicol Communities League Inc.
description:
Bicol Communities League Inc. (BCLI) is a vibrant Filipino community organisation established in 2006 in Sydney, New South Wales. The group specialises in bringing together Filipino-Australians with ancestry from the Bicol region of the Philippines. By promoting Bicolano heritage, culture, and tourism, the league helps members preserve their regional identity, language, and customs in Australia.

The organisation uses an equitable governance model where leadership roles rotate among representatives of the six Bicol provinces: Albay, Camarines Norte, Camarines Sur, Catanduanes, Masbate, and Sorsogon. Members and supporters regularly gather for major cultural celebrations, trade exhibitions, and community support initiatives. The league celebrated its twentieth anniversary in March 2026 with a major anniversary ball at the Wentys Leagues Club in Wentworthville. This landmark event featured cultural performances, traditional Bicolano cuisine, and speeches by distinguished guests, including the Philippine Ambassador to Australia. BCLI is a prominent member of the broader multicultural network in Western Sydney and continues to foster strong ties between Australia and the Philippines.

name: Bicol Communities League Inc. (BICOL Inc.)
description:
Bicol Communities League Inc. (BCLI or BICOL Inc.) is a registered non-profit community organisation serving Filipino-Australians of Bicolano heritage in Sydney, New South Wales. Established in March 2006, the group has spent two decades promoting cultural heritage, unity, and support for the local Bicolano diaspora.

The organisation is highly regarded for its unique and equitable leadership model. It rotates executive roles among the six provincial constituencies of the Bicol region—Albay, Camarines Norte, Camarines Sur, Catanduanes, Masbate, and Sorsogon. This democratic structure ensures fair representation and collaborative decision-making.

Community members and delegates appreciate BCLI's active role in organising cultural celebrations and trade exhibitions that showcase regional specialties, such as pili nuts and traditional abaca crafts. The group regularly hosts major events like the annual anniversary ball, which has attracted distinguished guests, including the Philippine Ambassador to Australia.

BCLI has received formal recognition in the New South Wales Parliament for its dedicated service and outstanding contribution to the multicultural fabric of Western Sydney. The organisation remains an important pillar of community support and cultural preservation.

name: 7000 Islands® Drinks
description:
7000 Islands® Drinks is a women-owned, Sydney-based craft beverage brand that brings the flavours of the Philippine archipelago to Australia. Founded by Siegfrid "Siggy" Bacani, the company specialises in importing and distributing premium Filipino-inspired spirits, wines, and beers from its office at Workit Spaces in Alexandria.

Their flagship Ube Cream Liqueur, crafted by Destileria Barako, has earned international recognition as the World's Best Cream Liqueur at the World Drinks Awards. Customers consistently praise its indulgent, smooth texture and unique earthy sweetness. The range also includes Kanto Salted Caramel Vodka, Agimat Black Gin, Pandan Liqueur, and Hotel Lobby Rum — each drawing on distinct Filipino flavour profiles that reviewers describe as "purely exceptional."

The brand is known for outstanding customer service, with next-day delivery on online orders. Their "Eat Drink Kwento" blog celebrates Filipino cocktail culture at venues across the country, and their products feature in over 300 bars and restaurants nationwide. 7000 Islands actively participates in Filipino community events, including the Grande Finale Filipino Festival in Western Sydney and the "Ube After Dark" trail showcasing ube-inspired drinks and desserts at participating venues.

You can find 7000 Islands products at major retailers including Dan Murphy's, Costco, Porter's Liquor, and Valore locations throughout Sydney, or order directly from their online shop at 7000islands.co.

name: Bm Asian Food Import Pty Ltd
description:
BM Asian Food Import Pty Ltd is a specialised retail shop and importer situated in Bow Bowing, in Sydney's south-west. The business serves the local community with a range of imported goods and is particularly celebrated as a destination for authentic Filipino desserts.

Local visitors frequently praise the shop for its refreshing treats, especially their famous halo-halo. Customers appreciate the generous portions, noting that the dessert is exceptionally large and filled with a fresh, delicious variety of traditional mix-ins. It has earned a reputation as one of the finest spots in the region to enjoy this classic cold treat, making it a perfect choice for hot summer days.

Whether you are looking to source hard-to-find grocery items from the Philippines or simply want to treat yourself to a sweet snack, this friendly local store provides a welcoming atmosphere. You can find BM Asian Food Import Pty Ltd located at 11 Wiltshire Street in Bow Bowing, offering a convenient stop for authentic flavours and community warmth.

name: Bruno's Filo Street Foods
description:
Bruno's Filo Street Foods is a popular food stall that brings the vibrant flavours of Filipino street food to Western Sydney. Operating every Sunday from 7:00 am to 1:00 pm at the bustling Blacktown Markets, located at the Skyline Drive-In in Prospect, this stall is a local favourite for authentic Pinoy comfort food.

The menu features a wide range of classic street eats and hearty meals prepared with care. Visitors can savour traditional charcoal-grilled barbecue skewers and silog breakfast plates, including tapsilog, chic silog, hotsilog, and bangsilog served with garlic rice and fresh egg. For main dishes, the stall offers tender pork adobo, kilayin, and a slow-cooked, savoury balbacua beef broth.

Snack lovers will appreciate the selection of traditional street food items such as fishballs, kikiam, kwek-kwek, and squidballs served with classic dipping sauces. Other popular options include crispy lumpiang shanghai, vegetable okoy fritters, prawn siomai, and comforting chicken macaroni soup. Whether you are looking for a quick market snack or a hearty Sunday lunch with the family, this stall provides a welcoming atmosphere to experience the rich culinary heritage of the Philippines in Sydney.

name: Burger Point (Circular Quay)
description: Burger Point, located on George Street near Circular Quay, is a popular dining spot specialising in American-style burgers with a distinct Filipino influence.
