The business is run by Myra, who grew up working in one of the top wedding styling and floral companies in the Philippines. She brings over ten years of industry experience to help couples design their perfect day. Operating from Shop 2, 1420 Canterbury Road, the company specialises in creating custom backdrops, exquisite floral arrangements, and personalised setups for both wedding ceremonies and receptions.

Clients highly recommend Myra for her friendly approach, patience, and excellent communication. Reviewers frequently praise her ability to take a couple's ideas and transform them into a cohesive reality. Whether styling a first birthday milestone or a beautiful wedding, the team is known for its meticulous attention to detail and professional delivery. Customers appreciate her reasonable prices, lovely personality, and fast service. From designing custom pastel flower backdrops to delivering quality event chair hires, the business works hard to make every celebration feel extra magical. Dreamy Weddings & Events by Myra is a fantastic local choice for stress-free event design and styling across Sydney.

name: ELAINA LOVE HAIR & BEAUTY SALON
description:
Elaina Love Hair & Beauty Salon is a welcoming, family-run hair salon located at Shop 10/24-30 Flushcombe Road in Blacktown, Sydney. Led by highly experienced hairdressers Love and Yana, the salon specialises in a broad range of professional hair services. These include precision cuts, colour regrowth, keratin smoothing, and rejuvenating hair botox and nanoplasty treatments designed to restore hair health and shine.

Clients consistently praise the team for their attentive, warm, and friendly service, noting that stylists listen carefully to their preferences to achieve the exact desired style. The roomy, clean salon provides a relaxing atmosphere where visitors feel well looked after. Many loyal clients have been visiting the salon regularly for years, valuing the team's professional expertise and honest styling advice.

Conveniently located near the local train station, the salon operates from Wednesday through Tuesday with variable hours, including Sunday opening. Whether you are seeking a fresh new look, a subtle trim, or a specialised hair treatment, Elaina Love Hair & Beauty Salon offers reliable, high-quality care for the whole family in both English and Filipino.

name: EUCA Gelato
description:
EUCA Gelato is an artisanal dessert café situated in a quiet courtyard at The Stables, a beautifully restored sandstone heritage building in Parramatta. Created by renowned Filipino pastry chef Miko Aspiras, this boutique venue specialises in blending traditional Italian gelato-making techniques with unique Australian native botanicals and classic Filipino fusion flavours.

Local visitors love the serene, calm atmosphere, describing the experience as walking back in time away from the busy city centre. The menu features creative, multidimensional gelato offerings designed to look and taste like fine-dining plated desserts in a cup. Highly recommended choices include their signature eucalyptus-infused gelato, Matcha Black Forest, and pepperberry Basque cheesecake. In addition to their unique gelatos, the café serves expertly brewed cold brew coffee and fresh, beautifully presented cakes.

With warm hospitality from the chef and staff, this charming spot has quickly become a favourite destination for dessert lovers. EUCA Gelato is open for quick visits on Monday and Tuesday mornings, with extended hours from Wednesday to Friday, and regular opening times on Saturdays and Sundays.

name: El Pinoy Asian Shop
description:
El Pinoy Asian Shop was a local retail store situated in St Marys, New South Wales. The shop specialised in offering a diverse selection of Filipino and Asian groceries. It served as a helpful local hub for the community to purchase imported ingredients, traditional condiments, and everyday household items.

The store's shelves were stocked with a wide variety of product offerings. These included popular Filipino snacks, sweets, canned goods, and key cooking ingredients like vinegar, soy sauce, and shrimp paste. Customers visited the shop to source authentic items that are often difficult to find in larger supermarkets, helping them prepare traditional home-cooked meals.

Centrally located in the Queen Street commercial precinct of St Marys, the shop was easily accessible for residents in the Western Sydney region. It provided a welcoming environment for the local Filipino-Australian community to connect with their favourite brands and flavours from home.

This business has permanently closed.

name: El Pinoy Care Services
description:
El Pinoy Care Services is a registered NDIS and aged care provider based in Campbelltown, serving families across Greater Sydney. Founded in 2020 by Maria De La Cruz during the COVID-19 pandemic, the organisation was born out of her personal journey caring for her father. This experience shapes their core philosophy of providing support that truly feels like family. Led by Maria and Operations Manager Alexander Maniquis, the dedicated team specialises in tailored care plans that promote independence, dignity, and wellbeing.

The provider offers a comprehensive range of support services, including personal care, domestic assistance, and daily life support. They also run a vibrant community participation program featuring regular social outings, creative workshops, and group activities to help participants stay active and connected.

Local clients and support workers highly praise the organisation for its compassionate and professional approach. Reviews frequently highlight the accommodating nature of the director and administrative staff. People appreciate how the team goes above and beyond, offering reliable, cost-efficient, and highly personalised services. Whether you require home care support or NDIS assistance, this family-oriented provider offers a welcoming and dedicated environment.

name: El Shaddai DWXI PPFI - El Shaddai House
description: El Shaddai DWXI PPFI Australia is a prominent Filipino Catholic Charismatic movement with a dedicated sanctuary and administrative centre based in Western Sydney. Established locally in September 1992 during a visit by founder Bro Mariano 'Mike' Velarde, the organisation has grown to support numerous cell groups and prayer fellowships across the state. The administrative house in Mt Druitt serves as the central hub for coordination, seminars, and spiritual support for the community. The movement's primary prayer meetings, or gawain, are held in nearby Rooty Hill and Blacktown under the spiritual direction of Father Raning Creta, the Filipino chaplain of the Parramatta Diocese. These gatherings offer local Filipino-Australian families a welcoming space for fellowship, worship, and community connection. While visitors appreciate the spiritual guidance and supportive community network, the centre also coordinates outreach programs and religious activities. With a focus on faith, love, and unity, the church remains an essential spiritual sanctuary for many Filipino Catholics in Sydney.

name: El Shaddai DWXI PPFI Mount Druitt
description:
El Shaddai DWXI PPFI Mount Druitt is a vibrant Filipino Catholic charismatic community that has supported the spiritual lives of local believers since the Sydney Chapter was established in September 1992. Located at El Shaddai House on Ophir Grove, the centre serves as a welcoming place of worship, prayer, and connection for the Filipino-Australian community in Western Sydney.

Members gather regularly for prayer meetings, bible sharing, and intercessory prayers to build faith, love, and unity. The chapter hosts weekly Bible Sharing from 10:00 AM to 10:00 PM on Mondays and Wednesdays through Fridays, alongside Tuesday evening Intercessions from 7:00 PM to 10:00 PM. On Sundays, the main prayer meeting takes place at the Rooty Hill School of Performing Arts from 1:00 PM to 4:30 PM, featuring Mass on the first and third Sundays of the month. Additionally, the group conducts Sunday evening services at the Blacktown Senior Citizen Centre from 5:30 PM to 7:30 PM.

With a dedicated focus on community support and spiritual growth, this fellowship offers a warm and friendly environment for kababayans looking to connect with their faith and culture.

name: El Shaddai DWXI-PPFI Sydney Chapter
description:
The El Shaddai DWXI-PPFI Sydney Chapter is a vibrant Catholic charismatic movement that has supported the local Filipino-Australian community since its inauguration in September 1992. Focusing on spiritual growth, fellowship, and evangelisation, the chapter brings people together to share their faith and participate in active community worship.

The congregation meets regularly on Sundays, organising prayer meetings and fellowship services that encourage spiritual development. The gatherings feature praise and worship sessions, sermons, and community prayer, creating a welcoming space for both long-term members and newcomers. Standard Sunday services are hosted at the Senior Citizen Centre in Blacktown from 5:30 pm to 7:30 pm, while additional prayer meetings and Tagalog masses are held at the Rooty Hill School of Performing Arts on Sunday afternoons.

With its administrative office, El Shaddai House, situated in Mt Druitt, the chapter serves as a central spiritual hub for the Filipino community in Western Sydney. The chapter is dedicated to fostering a supportive environment where individuals can deepen their faith and build strong community connections.

name: Eleganz Hair & Beauty
description:
Eleganz Hair & Beauty is a boutique salon located in Castle Hill, Sydney, specialising in premium hair styling, colour transformations, and advanced beauty treatments. Managed by owner Marivic, who is highly regarded by locals for her passionate and hospitable approach, the salon delivers custom styling experiences tailored to each client's individual needs.

A particular highlight of their service is the nanoplasty smoothing treatment, which is designed to tame unruly hair, leaving it silky, smooth, and completely tangle-free. Marivic takes the time to explain every procedure thoroughly, ensuring clients feel comfortable and informed. Beyond specialised hair care, the salon offers a complete suite of services including precision haircuts, makeup application, facials, laser hair removal, and lip tattooing.

Clients consistently praise the salon for its reasonable pricing, attention to detail, and personalised consultations. Whether you are looking for a fresh new hair colour or a rejuvenating facial treatment, Eleganz Hair & Beauty provides a welcoming local space where quality and client satisfaction are the top priorities.

name: Elite NSW Basketball
description:
Elite NSW Basketball is a youth basketball academy operating at the Auburn Basketball Centre in Sydney. Founded by experienced Australian-Filipino coaches Mel Gile and Jeson Delfinado, the academy specialises in year-round player development, teamwork, and building a supportive community culture. They offer structured programs tailored to different skill levels. The beginner classes run on Sundays from 12:00 pm to 1:00 pm, helping young athletes learn core fundamentals like dribbling, passing, and defence. The intermediate training follows from 1:00 pm to 2:00 pm, designed to refine skills and boost game understanding. For serious players chasing competitive pathways, representative teams, or overseas scholarships, the academy runs high-performance skills sessions on Saturdays from 8:00 am.

A highlight of the program is the opportunity to participate in international tours, such as the Manila Tour, connecting players with the vibrant basketball culture of the Philippines. Local families highly recommend the academy, praising the friendly, professional coaches and the encouraging environment they create. Elite NSW Basketball focuses on teaching more than just sports skills; they place a strong emphasis on accountability, discipline, and respect, helping to shape great people both on and off the court.

name: Bayanihan Sydney
description:
Bayanihan Sydney is a vibrant grassroots community collective and youth group dedicated to supporting the Filipinx diaspora in Sydney. The group focuses on mental health, wellbeing, and celebrating Filipino heritage in Australia. It provides a welcoming and safe space where members can connect, share lived experiences, and build a strong sense of belonging.

The group organises a diverse range of activities, including social meetups, cultural activations, guest speaker events, and food workshops. A key highlight is the monthly Bayanihan Walking Group, which brings community members together for walks along the scenic Nepean River and other locations. Additionally, the collective curates 'Bayanihan Reads', a dedicated database of books and educational resources that explore Filipino culture and history.

Through active collaborations with local organisations, such as the Fairfield City Museum and Gallery, the group facilitates meaningful programs like the 'Bread and Bayanihan' event. This collective makes it easy for young Filipino-Australians to find support, celebrate their rich heritage, and build lasting friendships in a supportive environment.

name: Ensaymada etc
description:
Ensaymada etc is a popular Filipino food stall and bakery located at the Chatswood Mall Market in Sydney, specialising in traditional Filipino baked goods and desserts. This local favourite is particularly famous for its handcrafted treats, which are made in small batches using premium ingredients.

Local customers highly recommend their creamy ube Basque cheesecake, noting its rich texture and delightful flavour. Other popular sweet treats include their silky egg pie, ube chiffon cake, and traditional fluffy ensaymadas. The bakery also specialises in classic Filipino favourites such as bibingka, silvanas, and cashew cookies, offering an authentic taste of home-style Philippine baking.

You can find their stall set up at the Chatswood Mall Market every Thursday, Friday, and Saturday from 10:00 am to 7:00 pm. For those planning a special celebration or looking to order ahead, they can be contacted directly via mobile phone or email. Whether you are craving a slice of traditional ube pie or looking to try a selection of authentic pastries, this market stall provides a welcoming experience for the local community.

name: Estelita's Filipino Kusina
description:
Estelita's Filipino Kusina was a cosy traditional Filipino restaurant located on Church Street in Parramatta, Sydney, specialising in authentic home-style dishes with a Kapampangan influence. It was widely known for its warm hospitality, welcoming staff, and lively dining atmosphere, which featured a raised stage hosting live musical performances on Saturday nights.

The menu showcased classic Filipino favourites, including crispy pata, pancit, bicol express, and the highly praised tinola manok—a flavourful ginger-infused chicken soup celebrated for its soothing warmth. Diners also enjoyed sweet treats like halo-halo, banana fries, and traditional cassava cake. While many visitors appreciated the generous portions and authentic recipes, some reviews noted inconsistencies in dish preparation, card payment issues, and occasionally long wait times during busy service periods. The kitschy, home-like decor of the dining room offered a unique and nostalgic spot for local families and groups to gather, socialise, and share large plates.

This business has permanently closed.

name: FASTBOX PH
description:
Fastbox PH is a specialised cargo and freight company based in Smithfield, Sydney, providing international shipping and logistics solutions between Australia and the Philippines. Originally established in 1999 as Fastpak Express Australia, the business rebranded in recent years to modernise its operations and expand into digital-first logistics.

For the Filipino-Australian community, the team specialises in door-to-door balikbayan box services via both sea and air freight. They also provide comprehensive commercial cargo solutions, including less-than-container load (LCL) and full container load (FCL) shipping, customs clearance, and pick-and-pack services. Their local operations in Australia are powered by LC Carriers to ensure secure and efficient last-mile delivery.

Customers highly appreciate their role in facilitating cross-border trade, including e-commerce fulfilment services and partnerships with shipping platforms. The company has also supported significant community milestones, such as facilitating agricultural exports from the Philippines.

You can find their Sydney office at Unit 40, 191 McCredie Road in Smithfield. They operate from Monday to Friday, between 10:00 AM and 6:00 PM, helping individuals and businesses manage their shipping needs with ease.

name: FCF Life Centre
description:
FCF Life Centre is a welcoming multicultural church and community hub located in Minchinbury, Western Sydney. Originally established in the early 1980s as a Filipino Christian Fellowship Bible study group, the church has grown into a vibrant congregation affiliated with the Foursquare Church of Australia. Historically led by the late Pastor Rudy Tan for over three decades, the church maintains its close-knit family feel while welcoming members from diverse backgrounds. The centre offers weekly Sunday morning worship services at 9:30 am, alongside prayer meetings held via Zoom and ministries tailored for all age groups.

Congregants highly value the warm welcome, strong scripture-based teaching, and the supportive network of members who help one another grow in faith. The church has a long-standing history of community service, famously hosting collaborative fundraising events like the Selfless Love Concert to support international students and families in need. With its rich legacy of pastoral care and outreach, the centre continues to serve as an encouraging home for the Filipino-Australian community and other local residents in the Western Sydney region.

name: FILCOS NORTH SHORE Inc.
description:
FILCOS North Shore Inc. (Filipino Catholic Organisation of Sydney, North Shore) is a vibrant community group and religious association dedicated to supporting Filipino Australians on the North Shore of Sydney. Founded in 1981, this non-profit organisation has spent over four decades fostering unity, preserving cultural heritage, and supporting spiritual life.

The organisation is highly regarded for its close partnership with local Catholic parishes, particularly within the Diocese of Broken Bay. A central part of its service includes facilitating monthly Filipino Masses celebrated in Tagalog at St Kevin’s Catholic Church in Dee Why. In addition to spiritual services, the group organises key cultural events that celebrate Filipino culture. These include the annual Fiesta and the Philippine Fiesta Experience, which showcase traditional music, folk dance, and authentic Filipino food to the wider community.

To engage younger generations, the FILCOS Youth Committee runs a popular basketball tournament over a twenty-week season, promoting teamwork and active lifestyles. Whether joining for spiritual connection, community support, or cultural celebrations, local families value how this group preserves their heritage and creates a welcoming home away from home.

name: FILCOS North Shore (Filipino Catholic Organisation of Sydney)
description:
FILCOS North Shore, also known as the Filipino Catholic Organisation of Sydney (North Shore), is a registered charity and community organisation. Established in 1981, the group has supported Filipino-Australian families across Sydney's North Shore and Northern Beaches for over four decades. The organisation works closely with local parishes, such as the Parish of Warringah, within the Catholic Diocese of Broken Bay to nurture Christian values.

The group is dedicated to cultural preservation, community connection, and pastoral support. It provides a warm place of belonging for newly arrived migrants and established families alike. Throughout the year, the organisation hosts and participates in various religious and cultural celebrations. These include traditional Filipino dance presentations, the Sinulog Festival, and the popular Philippine Fiesta Experience.

Through these events, the group shares the rich heritage of the Philippines with the wider Australian community. It fosters strong bonds among members and promotes multicultural harmony. As a non-profit association, the group continues to serve as a vital support network for the Filipino Catholic community.

name: FILOFOODS ROCKDALE
description:
FILOFOODS ROCKDALE is a welcoming family-run hub in Rockdale that combines an authentic Filipino eatery with an Asian grocery store. Located at 89 Railway Street, this local favourite specialises in casual, homestyle dining and hard-to-find grocery essentials.

Visitors love the warm hospitality and the diverse menu of freshly prepared meals. The kitchen is highly regarded for its ready-to-eat dishes, including crispy Ilocos empanadas, savoury dinakdakan, and traditional longganisa. Regular diners also appreciate the customisable palabok, which the friendly staff gladly adapts to suit specific dietary preferences. The cosy, no-fuss dining space is perfect for a quick lunch, family gatherings, or small community celebrations featuring a buffet-style setup.

In addition to hot meals, the shop offers a convenient grocery section stocked with popular imported products from the Philippines. Shoppers can find staples like Argentina corned beef and Magnolia ice cream in classic flavours such as ube macapuno. With a wheelchair-accessible entrance, free street parking, and takeaway and delivery options, FILOFOODS ROCKDALE makes enjoying Filipino flavours easy and accessible for the Rockdale community.

name: FLAGCOM and Friends
description: FLAGCOM and Friends (Filipino Lesbian and Gay Community and Friends) is a prominent Sydney-based community group that supports Filipino LGBTQIA+ individuals and their allies. Co-founded in 2010 by Albie Prias and Charles Chan, the association has built a reputation for cultivating an inclusive, welcoming space where people can express themselves freely and showcase their talents. Members highly appreciate the strong, family-like support system within the group, noting that the founders actively encourage individuals to pursue their goals and stand by them during difficult times. The organisation is widely celebrated for its participation in the annual Sydney Gay and Lesbian Mardi Gras, where it showcases vibrant, culturally rich entries that celebrate Filipino heritage. Over the years, the group has earned significant accolades for its community contribution. These achievements include the Ron Muncaster Award in 2020 and being named the FILCCA NSW Best Community Organisation in 2021. Through advocacy, cultural pride, and community outreach, the group continues to promote social cohesion and health education across Sydney's diverse multicultural community.

name: Faith in Christ Fellowship
description:
Faith in Christ Fellowship Macarthur is a welcoming Filipino Christian church located in Minto, Sydney. Led by Reverend Nomer Velasco, the congregation focuses on building a supportive community, helping members grow in their faith, and spreading the teachings of Jesus Christ. Weekly Sunday services are held at 10:00 AM at their main venue on Lincoln Street.

Members of the church frequently highlight the warm, family-oriented atmosphere, describing it as a loving family in Jesus Christ. The fellowship organises regular community programmes, youth activities led by dedicated coordinators, and seasonal celebrations such as Easter baptisms and Mother's Day breakfasts, fostering strong local connections in the Macarthur region. They also maintain an active digital ministry, livestreaming sermons and updates via their YouTube channel and social media profiles.

Located in South West Sydney, Faith in Christ Fellowship serves as a key spiritual hub for the local Filipino community, offering both in-person fellowship and virtual outreach. Visitors and new members are always welcome to join the congregation for worship and community events.

name: Eletax
description:
Eletax is a professional accounting and tax preparation practice located in Quakers Hill, Sydney. Established in 1993 by the late Dante Reyes (CPA) and currently led by Chartered Accountant Redmond (Red) Reyes, the friendly team specialises in managing tax and financial affairs for local families, sole traders, and small businesses. They assist clients with individual tax returns, rental properties, GST, BAS, and general business advisory.

Clients appreciate the practice's thorough approach, clear communication, and honest advice. Reviews highlight their quick turnaround times and competitive pricing. Many clients find the process simple and stress-free, often completing their tax preparation in just a few emails. Eletax has built a strong reputation in the local community, with many families trusting them as their go-to accountants for over a decade.

The office is open Monday to Friday from 9:00 am to 5:00 pm, and is closed on weekends. For convenience, clients can easily contact the team via email or phone to organise their next tax return.

name: Family Express Pty Ltd
description:
Family Express Australia, located in Hornsby, Sydney, is a trusted financial services provider specialising in online money transfers and remittance services from Australia to the Philippines and Vietnam. Established in 2019, this AUSTRAC-registered company offers a secure, reliable, and convenient platform for the Filipino-Australian community to support their loved ones back home.

Senders can make transfers 24/7 using modern digital payment methods such as PayID and Poli Payments, ensuring that funds can be sent at any time of the day or night. For recipients in the Philippines, Family Express offers a wide variety of payout options, including bank deposits, cash pickup at popular partner locations like Cebuana Lhuillier and M Lhuillier, and even special food delivery services from selected local restaurants.

As a sister company of Family Express Canada Ltd, the business brings international remittance expertise to its Hornsby headquarters. Local customers appreciate the user-friendly FAMEX Remit mobile app and the flexible payout channels that keep families closely connected across borders.

name: Fernandez Lawyers
description:
Fernandez Lawyers is a professional legal practice situated in the heart of Blacktown, Sydney. They specialise in providing a broad range of legal services. These include family law, immigration, property law, conveyancing, commercial leasing, and wills and estates. Led by Florante 'Butch' Fernandez and Charleen Lim Ventura, the team is uniquely qualified as both Australian and Philippine lawyers. This makes them a valuable resource for cross-border legal needs.

To ensure legal services are accessible to the local community, the firm offers multilingual support. They are capable of communicating in English, Filipino, Pangasinan, Ilocano, and Bisaya. Local clients have highlighted their helpful services and responsiveness in positive feedback. However, reviews also note that clients should prepare to be proactive with communication and follow-ups during stressful property transactions.

Conveniently located on Main Street, Fernandez Lawyers assists the Filipino-Australian community with legal guidance. Whether you need help with a visa appeal, buying a home, or planning an estate, they are available to guide you through the process.

name: Fiesta Avenue
description:
Fiesta Avenue is a friendly, casual carinderia-style Filipino restaurant located in Blacktown, Sydney, conveniently situated right opposite Blacktown Station. The eatery specialises in bringing authentic, home-cooked Filipino flavours to the local community. It serves a wide selection of traditional dishes, including tender beef kare-kare, savoury pork and chicken adobo, classic dinuguan, sisig, pinakbet, and bicol express.

Customers particularly love the delicious, freshly prepared all-day silog meals, such as tapsilog and tosilog, which come served with garlic rice and egg. Another popular highlight is their classic halo-halo, highly praised as a refreshing, generous dessert perfect for cooling down.

The dining space is fully air-conditioned, making it a comfortable spot for lunch or a quick bite. Although some visitors have noted occasional service wait times and price updates, the warm and approachable atmosphere keeps local diners returning for their favourite comfort foods. Whether you choose to dine in or grab a takeaway, Fiesta Avenue provides a genuine taste of the Philippines in a welcoming suburban setting.

name: Fil-Mart
description:
Fil-Mart is a friendly Filipino grocery and general store situated at Shop 5, 220 George Street in Liverpool, Sydney. The shop specialises in bringing a wide range of basic Filipino groceries, essential ingredients, and traditional kitchen supplies to the local area, making it a very handy spot for community members to stock up on their favourite home-style products. Visitors particularly appreciate the warm, welcoming staff who consistently go out of their way to provide friendly, attentive, and helpful customer service.

In addition to its retail grocery selection, the store is well known for offering a range of freshly cooked, authentic Filipino takeaway dishes. Customers regularly praise the delectable Cebu ngohiong, fresh lumpia, and flavourful pork barbecue options that are prepared daily in the kitchen. Beyond daily shopping and takeaway meals, the business also supports local gatherings and special family events through its smooth and stress-free catering services.

Please note that this business is currently temporarily closed.

name: Fil-Oz Liverpool and Districts, Inc.
description:
Fil-Oz Liverpool and Districts, Inc. is a registered not-for-profit community association based in South West Sydney. The organisation specialises in preserving and promoting Filipino cultural and religious heritage in New South Wales. Community members highly value the group for its dedicated efforts in passing down traditional values and cultural practices to the younger generation.

One of the organisation's major annual highlights is hosting the vibrant Santacruzan and Flores de Mayo festivals at All Saints Catholic Parish in Liverpool. These colourful events feature traditional religious processions and bring the local multicultural community together. In addition to religious celebrations, the group organises performing arts programs, including traditional Filipino folk dances, which are recognised and regularly showcased at multicultural events like the Children's Festival of Sydney.

Historically, the association has also been active in humanitarian work, fundraising for disaster relief efforts to support communities in the Philippines. As an active affiliate of the Alliance of Philippine Community Organisations (APCO) and the Visayan Association of Australia (VAA), the association has a long history of community leadership under dedicated presidents such as Cora Bojarski and Josephine Maynard. It remains a vital social and cultural centre for Filipino-Australians in the Liverpool area.

name: Filipiniana Food Mart
description:
Filipiniana Food Mart, widely known among locals as Fil-Mart, is a specialty grocery store situated on George Street in the heart of Liverpool, Sydney. This store serves as a popular community hub for the Filipino-Australian diaspora, offering a comprehensive range of imported pantry staples, frozen goods, classic snacks, and traditional cooking ingredients.

Patrons frequently praise the friendly, welcoming staff who provide fabulous and helpful customer service. The store is highly regarded for stocking much-needed supplies that allow home cooks to recreate authentic family recipes. In addition to retail groceries, they provide an array of daily cooked Filipino takeaway dishes. Among the delectable items, customers particularly favour Cebu-style ngohiong, fresh lumpia, and skewered pork barbecue. The establishment also collaborates with popular catering services that locals highly recommend for bringing authentic flavours to community events and family gatherings.

Whether you are searching for hard-to-find ingredients or looking to enjoy freshly prepared home-style meals, this centre offers a wonderful taste of the Philippines.

Please note that this business is currently temporarily closed.

name: Filipino Apostolate of Holy Family Parish
description:
The Filipino Apostolate of Holy Family Parish is a dedicated community association of Filipino Catholics based in Emerton, Western Sydney. Working within the vibrant and diverse Holy Family Catholic Parish on Luxford Road, this association helps connect and support the local Filipino diaspora through shared faith, religious activities, and cultural preservation.

Members of the local community appreciate the exceptionally warm, friendly, and welcoming atmosphere of the parish. The church features active groups and programs, including local support services like a men's health initiative called The Shed, youth programs, and tutoring services. The community frequently hosts social gatherings, such as barbecues after Sunday services, to foster connection and friendship among parishioners of all backgrounds. 

Religious services are held regularly at the parish. The schedule features a Saturday Vigil Mass at 5:30 PM, as well as Sunday morning Masses at 9:00 AM and 10:30 AM. The parish also coordinates weekday services, holy hour adoration, and outreach programs. Located at the Holy Family Centre in Emerton, this apostolate serves as a vital spiritual and social hub for Filipino-Australian families in the Mount Druitt region.

name: Filipino Australian Diabetes Support Network
description:
The Filipino Australian Diabetes Support Network (FADSN) is a volunteer-led community health group in Western Sydney. Established in 2021 and incorporated in late 2022, the group works to prevent and manage type 2 diabetes among Filipino Australians. It brings together bicultural health professionals, including dietitians, diabetes educators, nurses, and local leaders, to offer culturally sensitive health education.

FADSN works closely with Western Sydney Diabetes (WSD) and Blacktown Council to run community events. These activities include healthy cooking demonstrations, educational forums, and group walking events like 'Step it up for Diabetes'. The group focuses on the relationship between traditional diets and diabetes management, showing how to adjust classic Filipino dishes for healthier lifestyles.

Participants and professionals highly value the network. In feedback surveys, 97 per cent of participants rated the group's resources as helpful or very helpful. FADSN also shares educational articles and videos through social media to help families stay informed. The network provides a supportive space that respects both cultural traditions and health needs in the community.

name: Filipino Australian and Multicultural Community Association
description:
Based in Rhodes, the Filipino Australian and Multicultural Community Association (FAMCA) supports the Filipino-Australian and wider multicultural community across Sydney. This dedicated non-profit organisation actively helps local residents, temporary migrants, and international students through a range of community initiatives.

Community members praise the association for its hands-on assistance and genuine care. During the COVID-19 pandemic, FAMCA gained widespread recognition for distributing food packages and relief goods to international students in need. They also offer job search assistance and host useful migration information sessions. Beyond crisis relief, FAMCA coordinates community projects, such as Clean Up Australia Day events, to foster local engagement and care for the environment.

With its office at Level 3, 9-11 Blaxland Road, FAMCA services diverse suburbs including Blacktown, Doonside, Strathfield, and the Ryde area. Local residents express great pride in being associated with the organisation, highlighting its positive impact on cultural preservation and social connection. Whether you want to participate in community activities, access support, or volunteer, FAMCA provides a welcoming space for everyone in the multicultural community.

name: FilOz Triathlon Club
description:
FilOz Triathlon Club is a community-focused sporting group based in Western Sydney, specialising in representing Filipino-Australian heritage through multisport activities. Established in 2013 and officially incorporated in 2015, the club holds the distinction of being the first culture-based triathlon club affiliated with Triathlon Australia and Triathlon New South Wales.

Members and participants appreciate the club's extremely welcoming and inclusive environment, which caters to athletes of all backgrounds and fitness levels, from complete beginners to experienced competitors. The club focuses on encouraging healthy, active lifestyles by organising group training rides, clinics, and mentoring programs.

The highlight of the club's calendar is the popular annual Happy Triathlon event, hosted at the Sydney International Regatta Centre in Penrith. This unique event is highly regarded for its festive 'fiesta' atmosphere, featuring Filipino food and a vibrant community spirit that sets it apart from traditional race meets. For those looking to join or train in the Parramatta, Blacktown, and Penrith areas, the club offers support and camaraderie to help members achieve their personal fitness goals.

name: Filipino Carer Support Group
description:
The Filipino Carer Support Group is a peer support community based in Cabramatta, Sydney. Designed for carers of Filipino heritage, the group is facilitated by Anglicare Sydney. It provides a welcoming and safe space for individuals looking after family members or friends. These friends or family may live with dementia, intellectual or physical disabilities, autism, or brain injury. The group also supports those caring for people with sensory impairments or aged-related frailty.

This network focuses on reaching 'hidden carers' who are not yet connected to formal services. By bringing members of the local Filipino community together, the group helps to reduce social isolation. It also encourages the sharing of personal experiences and practical advice. Members can learn about respite care options and access other Anglicare support services.

Meetings are held monthly on the last Thursday at 40 Cumberland Street, Cabramatta. Carers looking for connection can contact the coordinator, Ronna Guzman, on (02) 9728 0200. She can provide further details about the upcoming sessions.

name: Filipino Cenacle Group - St Michael's Hurstville
description:
The Filipino Cenacle Group is a vibrant and welcoming community prayer and fellowship ministry based at St Michael's Catholic Church in Hurstville. Functioning as a key ethnic ministry within the parish, this active group brings together community members for shared prayers, rosary devotions, and warm fellowship. Gatherings are typically held in the Fr Ron Harden Hall, offering a space for spiritual reflection, mutual encouragement, and cultural connection.

Beyond their prayer meetings, the group plays an active role in parish life. Parishioners and visitors highly appreciate their hospitality services, particularly the coffee and tea gatherings they organise after the 10:00 am Sunday Mass. The group also contributes to the church through donations and by helping with seasonal displays, such as setting up the Christmas crib and other parish decorations.

St Michael's Catholic Church is located at 10 Croydon Road, Hurstville. Those interested in joining or learning more about the Filipino Cenacle Group can contact the St Michael's parish office directly via phone or email.

name: Filipino Chaplaincy - Catholic Community of North Harbour
description:
The Filipino Chaplaincy - Catholic Community of North Harbour is a vibrant migrant ministry. It provides pastoral care, chaplaincy services, and fellowship for the Filipino-Australian community in Sydney's Northern Beaches. Serving within the Catholic Community of North Harbour, the ministry holds gatherings at St Kieran's Catholic Church in Manly Vale. The community is under the pastoral care of the Augustinian Fathers, who maintain strong ties with the Australian Filipino Augustinian Solidarity partnership. 

Parishioners and visitors frequently praise the church for its warm, inclusive, and friendly environment. The congregation is known for its welcoming community spirit, beautiful singing, and supportive clergy. Regular services are led by various priests who alternate running the sermons, offering diverse and engaging spiritual perspectives. The parish centre and church hall also host community functions, funerals, and wakes, with local volunteers offering respectful hospitality. 

For those seeking a supportive spiritual home, this chaplaincy offers a light, airy, and comfortable space to celebrate faith and culture together. Regular parish office hours run from 9:00 am to 4:00 pm from Tuesday to Friday.

name: Filipino Chaplaincy - Diocese of Parramatta
description:
The Filipino Chaplaincy of the Catholic Diocese of Parramatta is a vibrant, faith-based organisation established in 2003. Centred at St Michael’s Church Presbytery in Blacktown, the chaplaincy specialises in providing pastoral care, spiritual guidance, and social support to the large Filipino-Australian community across Western Sydney and the Blue Mountains. It acts as a warm and welcoming home away from home, helping newly arrived migrants settle in Australia while keeping their cultural heritage and faith alive.

A focal point of the community's spiritual life is the rotation of monthly Tagalog Masses. These services are celebrated at 3:00 PM on the first Sunday of the month at Our Lady of the Rosary Parish in Kellyville, followed by 11:00 AM Masses on the second Sunday at Winston Hills and the fourth Sunday in Blacktown. A third Sunday Mass is also held at 12:00 PM at Emu Plains. Beyond regular worship, the chaplaincy coordinates major cultural celebrations such as the Feast of Santo Niño, the Feast of Our Lady of Peñafrancia, and the traditional Simbang Gabi dawn novenas leading up to Christmas. Community members cherish the deep sense of unity, spiritual comfort, and cultural connection fostered by these gatherings.

name: Filipino Chaplaincy Chatswood Parish
description: The Filipino Chaplaincy of Chatswood Parish, based at Our Lady of Dolours Catholic Church, is a vibrant community group providing pastoral and cultural support to Filipino Catholics in Chatswood and the broader Broken Bay area. Parishioners warmly praise the welcoming, peaceful, and beautifully maintained church grounds, including the quiet courtyard and library. The community highly values the dedicated, passionate priests, as well as the active and friendly congregation. The parish is well regarded for its reverent atmosphere and inclusive environment that brings diverse families together. Key community offerings include the monthly Filipino Culture Mass, which is celebrated on the first Sunday of each month at 12:00 pm and followed by warm social fellowship. They also host traditional seasonal celebrations such as Simbang Gabi Christmas novena masses, playgroups, youth groups, and Philippine Independence Day cultural festivals. Conveniently located within a ten-minute walk from Chatswood Metro station, this welcoming community offers a serene, structured, and supportive spiritual home for visitors and local families alike.

name: Filipino Community Chatswood Parish
description: Our Lady of Dolours Catholic Church in Chatswood serves as a central place of worship and a welcoming spiritual home for the local community, including many Filipino-Australian families. Located just a ten-minute walk from Chatswood shopping centre and metro station, the parish is highly regarded for its peaceful atmosphere, beautiful architecture, and exceptionally maintained grounds. Visitors and parishioners frequently appreciate the warm and inclusive environment. The church features beautiful historical relics, a parish library, and comfortable facilities. The active parish community organises a wide range of ministries and groups for all ages, including children, youth, and seniors. This includes a monthly youth group for young adults and fellowship gatherings with tea and coffee after Sunday services. For the Filipino-Australian community, the parish serves as a vibrant cultural and spiritual hub. It hosts a monthly Filipino Culture Mass on the first Sunday of every month at 12:00 PM, which is a focal point for community gathering, traditional music, and fellowship. The parish also coordinates special liturgical events and cultural celebrations throughout the year.

name: Filipino Fiesta
description:
Filipino Fiesta is a welcoming restaurant and grocery store located in Kogarah, offering a slice of the Philippines in the heart of Sydney's southern suburbs. Situated at 9 Regent Street, this local favourite serves as a convenient one-stop shop for authentic Filipino dining and retail.

Diners can enjoy a range of classic, flavourful dishes including crispy pata, lumpiang shanghai, and savoury sisig. The venue is particularly popular for its boodle fight experiences and all-you-can-eat buffet options, which showcase the rich culinary traditions of the community. In addition to main meals, visitors highly recommend their selection of fresh pastries and breads, with the sweet Spanish bread being a Sunday favourite.

Beyond the dining experience, Filipino Fiesta features a clean and well-stocked grocery section. Here, home cooks can browse a comprehensive range of imported Filipino ingredients, snacks, and cooking products. The friendly owners provide helpful service, creating a warm, community-focused atmosphere that makes locals feel right at home. Whether you are popping in to stock up your pantry, picking up a fresh loaf of bread, or enjoying a hearty dinner with friends, Filipino Fiesta provides an authentic and satisfying experience.

name: Filipino Food Movement Australia
description:
Filipino Food Movement Australia is a passionate, volunteer-run community organisation dedicated to celebrating and elevating Filipino cuisine, culture, and culinary talent across Australia. Operating as a registered non-profit, the collective champions local businesses and helps food lovers discover the rich flavours of the Philippines.

Community members and supporters highly value how the organisation actively promotes local venues and fosters a strong sense of Bayanihan, the traditional Filipino spirit of communal unity. The group is well-known for organising major public events, most notably the popular FiloFomoFest in Centenary Square, Parramatta. This festive celebration features a wide array of food stalls, live music, cultural dance performances, and fun community activities like the Lumpia Legends Challenge. Alongside large-scale festivals, the movement runs smaller gatherings such as Meryenda, Kape, at Tsaa to showcase traditional afternoon tea and coffee culture.

Through their website and active social media platforms, the group maintains a helpful national directory connecting diners with Filipino eateries, caterers, and food producers. By bridging the gap between local food businesses and the wider public, the movement continues to shine a bright spotlight on Filipino culinary heritage in Sydney and beyond.

name: Filipino Homes Australia
description:
Filipino Homes Australia specialises in connecting the Filipino-Australian community with premium real estate and property investment opportunities across the Philippines. Managed by Ms. June Myrrh Dueñas-Aldeguer, a licensed real estate broker and unit manager at Filipino Homes, the agency provides dedicated support for clients looking to secure their future back home.

The service helps buyers navigate the Philippine property market, offering a comprehensive selection of residential sales, pre-selling condominiums, house and land packages, and rental management. Clients can access professional guidance throughout the entire process, including assistance with bank financing, property resale, and asset management.

By partnering with major developers throughout the Philippines, the agency ensures access to secure and high-yield investments. Whether you are looking for a retirement home, a holiday retreat in Boracay, or a passive income property, Ms. Dueñas-Aldeguer offers personalised advice tailored to individual financial goals. This service bridges the gap for overseas workers and families in Sydney and across Australia, allowing them to build a lasting legacy with peace of mind. The business operates with an always-open schedule online, making it highly accessible for enquiries at any time.

name: Filipino Imports
description:
Filipino Imports is a dedicated grocery supplier and importer located in North Strathfield, Sydney. Situated directly opposite the North Strathfield railway station on Queen Street, this local business specialises in sourcing and distributing authentic food products from the Philippines. It serves as a vital connection for the local community, bringing favourite home-style ingredients and pantry staples to the Inner West region.

The shop offers a wide variety of imported goods, ranging from popular canned items like Century Tuna to classic snacks, sauces, and seasoning mixes. Whether you are looking to prepare traditional dishes at home or stock up on familiar brands, the business provides a convenient and accessible source for essential Filipino items. The store plays an important role in supporting local residents and families who want to enjoy the diverse flavours of the Philippines.

With its convenient location and focused range of products, Filipino Imports makes it easy for locals to find high-quality imported groceries. Visitors can contact the business directly at 0450 145 821 to enquire about current stock or specific product arrivals.

name: Filipino Nursing Diaspora (FiND) Network
description:
The Filipino Nursing Diaspora (FiND) Network is an outstanding professional organisation headquartered in Brighton-Le-Sands, Sydney. It serves as a global association that unites, supports, and empowers Filipino nurses in Australia and across seventeen countries worldwide. The network specialises in fostering professional growth, mentorship, and advocacy for healthcare professionals.

Members highly value the organisation for its active community engagement and educational programmes. FiND Network regularly hosts professional development webinars and training sessions to help nurses advance their careers. They also produce the insightful Kalinga podcast, sharing stories and resources tailored to the nursing diaspora. Through partnerships with global institutions like the Daisy Foundation, they recognise excellence in nursing practice.

Whether you are a newly arrived nurse in Sydney looking for guidance or an experienced practitioner seeking professional connection, this network offers a welcoming space. The group helps members build strong professional relationships and navigate their healthcare careers. It remains a vital pillar for cultural connection and professional advocacy within the Filipino-Australian community.

name: Filipino Sports Arts & Recreational Club (FILSPARC)
description: FILSPARC (Filipino Sports, Arts & Recreational Club) is a vibrant community organisation based in Blacktown, Sydney, dedicated to promoting health, wellness, and unity among the Filipino-Australian community. The club encourages members of all ages to actively participate in sports, cultural, arts, and recreational activities. One of their popular regular activities is the weekly casual pickleball session held on Monday mornings at Chifley College's Bidwill Campus, alongside various cultural programmes including parol-making contests during festive seasons. Known for its strong community spirit, the organisation has been highly active in local welfare, such as coordinating grocery donation drives to support international students. Beyond organizing social events, FILSPARC also provides practical support for the community by offering a community transport van service available for hire. Backed by local government support and led by dedicated community figures, the club serves as a crucial social hub in Western Sydney. It fosters a warm sense of camaraderie and belonging, making it an excellent space for individuals to connect, stay active, and support one another in a friendly environment.

name: Filipino Student Association of UTS
description:
The Filipino Student Association (FSA) of the University of Technology Sydney (UTS), also known as FiloSoc, is a vibrant, student-run club established in 2019. Located at the UTS campus in Ultimo, the society serves as a welcoming home and support network for domestic and international students, staff, and alumni who share a love for Filipino culture and the Philippines.

As a highly inclusive, multicultural group, the association welcomes members from all backgrounds. The club is renowned for organising engaging social gatherings and cultural initiatives. Key activities include traditional boodle fights, Tagalog language lessons, and lively karaoke nights. The society also collaborates with other university clubs across New South Wales to host inter-university celebrations. Through these programmes, the club bridges connections between students and the broader Filipino-Australian community.

Whether you want to connect with fellow students, practice Tagalog, or experience authentic Filipino hospitality, the association provides a warm space to socialise and belong. You can easily connect with the club through their active social media profiles and community forums.

name: Filipino Student Council of New South Wales
description:
The Filipino Student Council of New South Wales (FSC NSW) is the peak student-led body representing Filipino student associations and international students across the state. Officially accredited by the Philippine Consulate General in Sydney, this active organisation promotes student welfare. It also strengthens cultural ties and fosters leadership among the youth.

Members and supporters highly value the group for its engaging, well-organised events. A major highlight is the annual Tulong Liga charity sports tournament. This showcase brings together students from major institutions, including the University of Sydney, UNSW, and UTS. Participants play friendly basketball and volleyball matches at the Blacktown Leisure Centre to raise funds for underprivileged youth in the Philippines. The council also hosts formal social gatherings, such as the Gunita Year-End Ball at Parramatta Town Hall, to celebrate student achievements.

By coordinating with university societies from Sydney to Wollongong, the council creates a welcoming support network for international students. Through sports, social balls, and community advocacy, they help students build lasting connections and maintain their cultural heritage in Australia.

name: Filipino Student Society - WSU
description:
The Filipino Student Society of Western Sydney University (FSSWSU), also known as SINAG, is a vibrant cultural student club. It brings students together to celebrate and share Filipino culture. Operating across Western Sydney University campuses, the society focuses on cultivating lifelong friendships and fostering a supportive community for both local and international students.

Members can participate in a wide variety of activities, including social gatherings like the annual Salu-Salo and university O-Week events. FSSWSU is well-known for hosting collaborative events with other university societies in New South Wales. These include popular music and karaoke nights, such as Tambayan Tunes. These are run alongside student groups from Macquarie University and the University of New South Wales. The society also places a strong emphasis on community service and sports, organising events like the Tulong Liga charity sports tournament. This student-led tournament raises funds for charitable organisations such as Bantu Philippines through friendly basketball and volleyball matches.

Whether you want to connect with your heritage, make new friends, or participate in campus activities, the society offers a welcoming space. You can easily get in touch with the team via email at wsufilosoc@gmail.com. You can also stay updated by following their active social media handles, @fsswsu.

name: Filipino Student Society of Macquarie University
description:
The Filipino Student Society of Macquarie University is an affiliated cultural organisation at Macquarie University in Sydney. It provides a welcoming and friendly space for both Filipino and non-Filipino students, including domestic and international members, to connect and immerse themselves in the vibrant culture of the Philippines.

Members of the society appreciate the inclusive environment and the active social calendar. The organisation hosts popular events throughout the academic year to celebrate Filipino traditions and values. Key activities include cultural showcases, the annual Kalye Race, and social gatherings such as the Tambayan Tunes karaoke nights, which are often organised in collaboration with other university societies. They also run stalls during O-Week and participate in student integration programmes to welcome new students to the campus.

In addition to social connections, the group supports charity initiatives and fundraising drives for local communities in the Philippines. It serves as a vital network for students seeking to make friends and build leadership skills during their time at university. The society is based on the Wallumattagal Campus of Macquarie University, making it a central hub for student life and cultural exchange in Sydney's north-west.

name: Filipino Student Society of UNSW
description:
The Filipino Student Society of UNSW, also known as FiloSoc, is an active student organisation at the University of New South Wales in Sydney. Based on the Kensington campus, this vibrant club offers a welcoming space for both international and local students. It aims to bridge the Filipino-Australian experience by celebrating and sharing the rich heritage of the Philippines.

Students appreciate the warm, cheerful and family-like 'kapamilya' atmosphere fostered by the group. FiloSoc has a proud history on campus, having been recognised as the Arc Club of the Year. The club is well-regarded for organising engaging cultural and social events throughout the academic year. Highlights include traditional boodle fights, karaoke nights (such as Tambayan Tunes at the UNSW Roundhouse), and festive halo-halo socials. Additionally, members can participate in sports days, including basketball and volleyball tournaments, as well as collaborative study sessions and membership programs.

Whether you want to connect with your cultural roots or simply enjoy delicious Filipino food and friendly community support, the society encourages anyone interested to get involved. You can find their team on campus or follow their active social media channels for the latest event updates.

name: Filipino Student Society of WSU
description:
The SINAG Filipino Student Society of Western Sydney University (WSU) is a vibrant, student-run organisation dedicated to supporting the Filipino student community across Sydney. As an officially recognised cultural group under the university's cultural and religious listings, the society plays a key role in promoting Filipino culture, heritage, and language throughout the various campuses of WSU.

Members and participants appreciate the club's focus on fostering an inclusive and welcoming environment where students can build strong social networks. The society regularly organises a range of social activities, cultural celebrations, and collaborative gatherings, often partnering with other university-based student groups in New South Wales to strengthen community ties.

For students looking to engage with the club, the society maintains an active presence on the university's student engagement platform, WESTERNlife. This platform serves as a central hub where members can easily discover upcoming events, check community announcements, and find contact information to get involved. By offering both peer support and cultural activities, the society provides an invaluable space for students to share their heritage and enrich their overall university experience.

name: Filipino Student Society of the University of Sydney (FSS USYD)
description:
The Filipino Student Society of the University of Sydney (FSS USYD) is a registered, student-run organisation established in 2017 to connect students, staff, and alumni who share an appreciation for Filipino culture. The community operates on the Camperdown campus of the University of Sydney, promoting cultural exchange and offering a welcoming support network for both domestic and international students.

FSS USYD is guided by the core values of Pakikisama (camaraderie), Pagsusulong (advancement), and Pagtataguyod (advocacy). To foster this spirit, the group hosts several popular social and cultural gatherings throughout the academic year. These include Salubong welcome events at the start of each semester, karaoke sessions, sports days, and collaborative charity events like the Tulong Liga sports initiative. Members particularly enjoy the society's food-focused events, featuring traditional favourites such as lechon, halo-halo, and boodle fights. The group also organises peer buddy systems and informal kapihan sessions to help international students settle into university life in Sydney.

The society actively collaborates with local businesses and other student groups, partnering with local icons like Taguan Cafe and Kariton Gelato for special events, and co-hosting fundraisers to assist communities in need. FSS USYD welcomes anyone keen to experience the warmth of Filipino hospitality on campus.

name: Filipino Walkers
description:
The Filipino Walkers is a free community walking group in Western Sydney that encourages physical activity and social connection. Operating in partnership with the Heart Foundation Walking program, this welcoming group is specifically designed for the local Filipino community in Doonside, Arndell Park, and neighbouring areas.

Led by volunteer walk organiser Imelda Provido, the group meets every Saturday morning at 8:00 AM. Walkers gather at the Dyanmila picnic shelter, located within Bungarribee Park in the Western Sydney Parklands, Arndell Park. Each weekly session runs for approximately 45 to 60 minutes, offering a medium-paced walk that is both wheel-friendly and dog-friendly.

With around 39 active participants, the group provides a supportive environment to help you stay active, improve your cardiovascular health, and build lasting friendships. It is a wonderful way for kababayan to socialise and keep fit together in a relaxed outdoor setting. Anyone interested in joining can easily register through the official Heart Foundation Walking website or contact the organiser directly.

name: Filipino Women Support Group (Macarthur)
description: The Filipino Women Support Group (Macarthur) was a dedicated community-led initiative based in Campbelltown and the broader Macarthur region of New South Wales. The group was established to offer vital settlement support, guidance, and social connection for newly arrived Filipino migrants, while providing a safe space and advocacy for women who had experienced domestic and family violence. Alongside its supportive network, the group was highly active in the local community, organising cultural performances, participating in collaborative diaspora festivals, and successfully fundraising for local health services, such as the Campbelltown Hospital Cancer Clinic. Through its close partnerships with regional multicultural organisations like Macarthur Diversity Services Inc. (MDSI) and peak bodies like the Philippine Community Council of NSW (PCCNSW), it played a key role in fostering social inclusion and helping members of the Filipino diaspora integrate into Australian society. The group provided a welcoming environment for women to share their experiences and build lasting friendships. This community support group has permanently closed.

name: Filipino native cuisine
description:
Filipino Native Cuisine is a home-style catering service and food producer operating in Riverstone, in Sydney's north-west. Specialising in traditional, made-to-order Filipino desserts and sweets, this local gem brings the authentic flavours of the Philippines directly to the Western Sydney community.

The kitchen is particularly known for creating classic Filipino delicacies such as kutsinta and mini sapin-sapin. Each batch is prepared fresh to order, making these colourful treats a popular choice for local family gatherings, community celebrations, or weekend desserts.

Customers appreciate the convenience of local pickup in Riverstone and the friendly, personalised service. Whether you are hosting an event or simply craving a taste of home, the menu offers a delightful range of sweets that showcase traditional Filipino cooking techniques.

The business operates throughout the week, opening from 9:00 am to 5:00 pm from Monday to Saturday, and from 9:00 am to 12:00 pm on Sundays. You can place your orders directly by calling the team or sending an email. For anyone looking to enjoy genuine Pinoy desserts in Sydney, Filipino Native Cuisine provides a wonderful option to experience classic sweets made with care.

name: Filo Lasa Blacktown
description:
Filo Lasa Blacktown is a popular casual eatery specialised in authentic Filipino comfort food. You can find this bustling takeaway stall on Level 1 inside the Westpoint Blacktown shopping centre. It offers a convenient dining spot for locals looking for a quick lunch or dinner.

Diners regularly praise the eatery for its genuine flavours and hearty home-style meals. The two-dish combo options are a local favourite, providing great value with generous portions of rice and mains. Standard menu highlights include the popular pork and chicken barbecue skewers, savoury pork sisig, and rich menudo served alongside classic garlic rice. For dessert, visitors highly recommend trying the crispy sweet banana spring rolls, known as turon, or the refreshing ube milkshakes and halo-halo.

As the stall is located within a busy shopping centre food court, finding nearby seating during peak hours can sometimes be a challenge. The eatery operates daily from 10:00 am to 5:00 pm, with late-night trading until 8:00 pm on Thursdays. Whether you are after a quick bite during a shopping trip or taking home a family meal, it is a reliable choice for authentic taste.

name: Filo Lasa Penrith
description:
Filo Lasa is a welcoming Filipino eatery situated in the Level 2 Food Terrace at Westfield Penrith. Specialising in authentic, home-style flavours, this popular food court spot is a favourite among locals seeking comforting and affordable classic dishes.

Diners highly recommend their pork skewers and barbecue pork, which are grilled to perfection. Other popular favourites include flavourful palabok, crispy sisig served alongside aromatic garlic rice, pancit, and a sweet-style spaghetti that brings back fond memories of home. For dessert, customers rave about the super-fresh, crispy turon, traditional halo-halo, and a light buko pandan drink made with coconut water and coconut pieces.

The helpful team at Filo Lasa, including Jerald, Jillian, and Yan, are known for their warm and accommodating service. Because the dishes are prepared fresh rather than pre-packaged, there may be a brief wait during busy periods. Whether you are grabbing a quick takeaway lunch or enjoying a casual dine-in combo meal with family, this eatery offers a satisfying taste of the Philippines right in Penrith.

name: Filo Pantry Mini Mart
description:
Filo Pantry Mini Mart is a welcoming Filipino grocery store located in the heart of Merrylands, Sydney. Situated just two hundred metres from Merrylands Station on McFarlane Street, this local supermarket specialises in authentic products imported directly from the Philippines. Customers can explore a wonderful selection of traditional Filipino snacks, spices, sweet treats, and essential cooking ingredients. It is a favourite destination for local home cooks who appreciate high-quality, genuine ingredients to prepare classic dishes.

Visitors highly value the friendly and warm service provided by the store's staff, which helps create a comforting, home-away-from-home atmosphere for those feeling a little homesick. In addition to its extensive grocery range, the shop serves very affordable yet excellent quality takeaway coffee and hot chocolate drinks. Filo Pantry Mini Mart operates daily from 9:30 am to 7:00 pm, making it highly convenient for community members to pick up their favourite household goods and groceries at any time of the week.

name: Filo Pride Asian Grocery, Eatery and Catering
description:
Filo Pride Asian Grocery, Eatery and Catering is a welcoming Filipino specialty store and eatery located in Marrickville, Sydney. The family-friendly venue serves as a popular community hub, offering a combined grocery and dining experience under one roof.

Diners consistently praise the authentic, traditional flavours of the freshly prepared food. The kitchen specialises in classic Filipino comfort meals, including flavoursome chicken and pork skewers, dinuguan, and stews. On weekends, the eatery hosts a popular buffet style option, alongside fresh desserts like halo-halo, leche flan, and ube halaya. Visitors appreciate the warm and accommodating hospitality from the staff, noting the clean dining space and friendly, homey atmosphere that makes them feel welcome.

In addition to the hot food counter, the store features a neatly organised grocery section. The shelves are well-stocked with a wide range of essential Filipino ingredients, snacks, and pantry staples, helping customers cook their favourite recipes at home. Situated in the heart of Marrickville, this local favourite is a convenient one-stop destination for both grocery shopping and authentic dining.

name: Filo Station
description: Filo Station, also known as Filo's Casa Cucina, was a beloved family-friendly Filipino restaurant located in Croydon Park, Sydney. It specialised in serving authentic Filipino comfort food prepared using traditional family recipes. Locals and visitors praised the eatery for its warm, welcoming atmosphere and friendly hospitality, with special mentions of the attentive service provided by Chester and Chef Diane. The cozy environment made guests feel right at home. The menu featured a wide range of traditional classics, including crispy pata, sizzling pork sisig, and crispy pork belly kare-kare served with a rich peanut sauce. Other popular customer favourites included savoury garlic fried rice, authentic chicken inasal, tinola, and special arroz caldo. For dessert, the restaurant offered traditional sweet treats like halo-halo, chewy caramel-coated banana-que, and freshly steamed puto bumbong. With its generous portion sizes and moderate pricing, it became a popular gathering spot for families and groups of friends looking to share a meal. This business has permanently closed.

name: Filoship Connect Seventh-day Adventist Church
description:
Filoship Connect Seventh-day Adventist Church is a welcoming Filipino-Australian congregation located in Prospect, New South Wales. Operating under the Greater Sydney Conference, this vibrant religious group focuses on shepherding and nurturing the local Pinoy community in Sydney's west. The church provides a warm, family-friendly environment that caters to all generations, from infants to seniors.

Members and visitors gather every Saturday morning for worship and fellowship. The day begins at 9:15 AM with Sabbath School, which features interactive Bible study groups for adults in English, Tagalog, and Bisaya. Children can participate in dedicated youth groups, including the Adventurers and Pathfinders Clubs, which offer songs, crafts, and fun activities. The main worship service follows at 11:00 AM and features inspiring sermon sessions, music, and community prayer.

A standout highlight of the weekly gathering is the popular potluck lunch, where the congregation shares home-cooked meals and enjoys social fellowship. Led by Pastor Frank Ropati, the passionate leadership team strives to offer spiritual and practical support to anyone in need. Whether you are a lifelong believer or a spiritual seeker, this active community offers a supportive space to connect, grow, and experience Filipino hospitality in Australia.

name: Filoz Asia Mini Mart
description:
Filo Pantry Mini Mart (historically known as Filoz Asia Mini Mart) is a specialty grocery store located at Shop 7, 215 Pitt Street in Merrylands, just a short two-hundred-metre walk from Merrylands Station. The shop specialises in sourcing and supplying a comprehensive range of Filipino food items, including popular snacks, authentic spices, sweet treats, and pantry staples. In addition to shelf groceries, customers can enjoy affordable, high-quality coffee and hot chocolate drinks served on site. 

Locals value the mini mart for its welcoming atmosphere and friendly, attentive customer service, which often provides a comforting slice of home for visitors feeling nostalgic. Those who love to cook traditional dishes appreciate the excellent variety of quality ingredients available on the shelves. Whether you are looking to stock up on everyday household essentials or grab a quick hot drink on your commute, the store serves as a helpful community hub. Filo Pantry Mini Mart is open seven days a week from 9:30 am to 7:00 pm, providing consistent access to favourite imports and specialty products in Western Sydney.

name: Filoz Entertainment Collective
description:
The Filoz Entertainment Collective is a vibrant creative hub in Campbelltown, Sydney. It operates from the Philippine-Australian Arts, Culture and Innovation Central (ACI Central). Launched in October 2025, the collective actively supports and empowers local Filipino-Australian talent across music, film, fashion, and performing arts.

As a non-profit community organisation, the group focuses on celebrating Filipino heritage. They work to amplify diverse voices within Australia’s multicultural entertainment scene. The collective plays an active role in organising fashion shows, live music concerts, and community celebrations. They often collaborate with local community groups, businesses, and international partners. They also focus on bridging generational gaps, encouraging participants from all age groups—from Baby Boomers to Gen Z—to connect and share their stories.

Located at Shop 4, 3–9 Warby Street, the collective provides a welcoming physical space for artistic collaboration, workshops, and networking. With Jimmy Lopez handling partnerships and media enquiries and Eman Recio overseeing bookings, the organisation proudly fosters community pride and creative growth in Western Sydney.

name: First Class Accounts - Blacktown
description:
First Class Accounts Blacktown, run by CPA member and registered BAS Agent Eileen Catindig, is a highly regarded bookkeeping service in Western Sydney. The business specialises in assisting small and medium-sized enterprises with their financial management needs. Clients can access professional bookkeeping, BAS lodgements, payroll management, and Single Touch Payroll (STP) compliance. Eileen also provides tailored software training for popular programs like Xero, MYOB, and QuickBooks.

Local clients highly appreciate Eileen's exceptional diligence, responsiveness, and supportive nature. She frequently goes above and beyond to help business owners get their accounts in order, reducing operational stress and ensuring complete tax compliance. Her clear communication style makes complex financial terms easy for business owners to understand.

First Class Accounts Blacktown operates from Flushcombe Road in Blacktown, serving clients across nearby Western Sydney areas including Horsley Park, Bossley Park, Rooty Hill, and Doonside. Eileen offers a free, one-hour initial consultation to assess each business's specific bookkeeping requirements and help keep their accounts in top order.

name: First One Education Migration Philippines
description:
First One Education Migration is a professional education and migration consultancy centre located in Town Hall Square, Sydney. The agency specialises in helping international students navigate the Australian study and migration pathways, offering support for student visas, graduate visas, training visas, partner visas, and skilled migration.

Clients regularly praise the team for their step-by-step guidance, prompt communication, and supportive approach. Many international students, particularly from the Philippines, appreciate how the staff simplifies the complex visa process with clear and honest explanations. A standout feature of their service is the provision of free initial consultations and free processing for student visas lodged within Australia.

The experienced consultants, including Jerry Rivera and Jierlyn, are highly regarded for their patience and dedication to client success. They assist students with course selection and visa transitions, helping them move smoothly from IT programs to kitchen management certifications while ensuring all visa requirements are meticulously met. Situated in the heart of Sydney's CBD, the centre provides a welcoming and trustworthy environment for individuals aiming to establish their careers in Australia.

name: Flaming Q
description:
Flaming Q is a popular mobile Filipino food truck and market stall specialising in authentic charcoal-grilled street food across Sydney. This family-focused business is renowned for stoking natural charcoal embers to create the traditional smoky flavours of Filipino barbecue.

Locals frequently gather around the food truck at community hubs like the Blacktown Night Markets and Castle Hill NightMarkets, drawn in by the mouth-watering aromas. The absolute favourite amongst regular customers is the spicy pork barbecue skewers, which are cooked to perfection. Diners rave about the tender, juicy meat and the signature spicy sauce that offers a proper heat. The chicken skewers also receive high praise for being hot, succulent, and full of flavour.

Beyond the delicious street food offerings, the team is highly regarded for their friendly hospitality. The owner and staff consistently provide superb customer service, making every visitor feel welcome and valued. As a mobile operator, Flaming Q regularly updates their social media profiles with schedules for upcoming pop-up appearances and market locations, ensuring local barbecue fans can easily track down their next meal.

name: Flat Planet
description:
Flat Planet is a professional talent outsourcing and recruitment agency located on Pitt Street in the heart of Sydney.
