Specialising in sea and air freight solutions, the depot primarily serves the Filipino-Australian community by delivering balikbayan boxes, parcels, and important documents from Australia to the Philippines.

The Western Sydney depot offers a spacious facility where customers can organise their shipments. Many clients appreciate the friendly service and straightforward transaction process when visiting the warehouse. The team is known for helping customers prepare their boxes for international transport, offering both drop-off options and scheduled home pickups.

While the service is popular for connecting families across borders, customer feedback suggests that transit times can vary significantly, with some shipments experiencing long transit delays. Clients also advise ensuring all items are securely packed to prevent damage during the long journey. LBC Express Australia remains a major link for the local community, facilitating cargo shipments and money remittances to the Philippines. The depot operates Monday to Friday from 9:00 am to 5:30 pm, and is closed on weekends.

name: LBC Express Sydney
description:
LBC Express Sydney is a specialised cargo forwarding and courier service provider catering to the Filipino-Australian community. Located in the multicultural hub of Blacktown, it has long served as a vital link for locals sending balikbayan boxes, parcels, and money remittances back home to their families in the Philippines.

Customers frequently praise the friendly and polite service of the in-branch staff, noting their helpfulness when processing transactions. The provider offers convenient money transfer options including PesoPak door-to-door delivery, direct bank deposits, and instant remittance services. However, some clients have experienced delays in delivery timelines and occasional communication challenges regarding pickup bookings and drivers.

The physical branch on Main Street in Blacktown has permanently closed. Despite this closure, community members can still access LBC Express shipping and money transfer services through the company's official Australian website or by visiting authorised local retail agents, such as Masagana Oriental Variety Store, to arrange their shipments and transfers.

name: LLT-Racing
description:
LLT-Racing, also known as LLT Racing and Mechanical Services, is a professional, family-owned automotive performance workshop located in Kings Park, Sydney. Founded by Luis Lovero (Lui), an experienced mechanic with roots in the Manila drag racing scene, this workshop specialises in Mitsubishi and Honda platforms. They offer a comprehensive range of services, including general mechanical repairs, logbook servicing, engine builds, transmission refreshes, and advanced ECU/dyno tuning.

Local car enthusiasts highly recommend LLT-Racing for their honest advice and welcoming atmosphere, with many customers noting they feel treated like family. The team, including Toshi, is praised for their great attention to detail, precision, and high-quality workmanship on both daily drives and track cars. Whether you need a standard logbook service, custom turbo installation, or complex performance modifications, Luis is recognised for delivering solid results at competitive rates compared to larger corporate workshops. LLT-Racing is open Monday to Friday from 10:30 am to 8:30 pm, providing Western Sydney with dedicated automotive expertise.

name: La Crema Cusina Penrith
description:
La Crema Cusina is a welcoming Filipino-fusion café and restaurant located at 12 The Crescent in the heart of Penrith. Known for its relaxed and cosy atmosphere, this local favourite specialises in creative fusion dishes that blend traditional flavours with modern twists.

Diners highly recommend the unique menu, which features standout options like the crispy pork belly bao buns, Portuguese chicken skewers served with traditional Java rice, and the chilli soft-shell crab burger. For those seeking breakfast or brunch, the Korean chilli scramble croissant and the eggplant katsu focaccia are popular choices. Visitors can pair their meals with expertly crafted specialty coffee, premium matcha lattes, or their signature ube matcha latte. There are also plenty of gluten-free and vegetarian options available to suit different dietary needs.

The café is family-friendly, offering a small toddler playground at the rear. Customers consistently praise the attentive, friendly staff and the quick service, with meals often arriving in under ten minutes. Whether you are catching up with friends or celebrating a family occasion, this venue provides a warm and relaxing environment. You can also download their mobile app to place orders and access loyalty points and discounts.

name: La Mesa Filipino Restaurant
description:
La Mesa was a highly regarded, family-owned Filipino restaurant that operated in the heart of Sydney's Haymarket on Goulburn Street. First established in Dee Why before relocating to Chinatown in 2012, it served as a beloved hub for authentic Philippine cuisine in the Sydney Central Business District.

The restaurant was celebrated for its warm, colonial Manila-style interior, featuring dark timber and wicker chairs that created a homely, kitchen-table atmosphere. Patrons regularly praised the kitchen's traditional flavours, with signature dishes such as the crispy pata, which was renowned for its tender pork and perfectly bubbled crackling. Other customer favourites included the sizzling sisig, comforting bowls of sinigang soup, and crispy sweet potato okoy fritters. To complete their dining experience, visitors often finished their meals with traditional desserts like biko and halo-halo.

Although it was a staple dining destination for the local Filipino community and city workers alike, this business has permanently closed.

name: La Star Asian Food Store
description:
La Star Asian Food Store is a local grocery supermarket located in Berala, positioned conveniently opposite Woolworths and right next to Berala station. It specialises in a wide selection of Asian groceries, providing essential ingredients for Chinese, Vietnamese, and Filipino cooking. Local shoppers appreciate the store as a handy spot for a quick grocery run, stocking everything from pantry staples to imported snacks, specialty sauces, and large bags of rice.

Customers frequently highlight the store’s convenient location and affordable pricing. The staff are known to be helpful, occasionally assisting shoppers by carrying heavy items like bulk rice packages directly to their cars. While the store offers a broad range of products, visitors should note its specific card payment policies, which include a minimum card purchase limit and a small card surcharge, alongside strict preferences regarding coin payments. Overall, it serves as a reliable neighbourhood centre for the Berala community to source their daily Asian ingredients and favourite imported brands.

name: Lakas Filipina Inc
description:
Lakas Filipina Inc is a dedicated not-for-profit community organisation that supports and empowers Filipino-Australian women. Based in Southwestern Sydney, the group focuses on helping women exercise their rights, participate in community leadership, and access essential services like healthcare and education.

Members and the wider community highly value the organisation for its active role in cultural preservation and social support. Lakas Filipina regularly organises major community events, including the vibrant annual Flores de Mayo festival, community fundraisers, and informative women's conferences. The group also collaborates with key bodies such as the Philippine Community Council of New South Wales (PCCNSW) and the Philippine Embassy to run educational and health workshops.

Led by passionate community figures like Elsa Collado and Dr. Elsa Ringor, Lakas Filipina provides a welcoming space for women to connect, share resources, and build support networks. Whether you are looking to volunteer, attend a workshop, or participate in local cultural celebrations, this organisation offers valuable opportunities to engage with the Filipino-Australian community in Sydney.

name: Larry's Unisex Salon
description:
Larry's Unisex Salon is a popular Filipino-owned hairdressing and barbering salon situated in the heart of Blacktown, Sydney. Known for its welcoming atmosphere and exceptional customer care, the salon specialises in a broad range of hair treatments, styling, and cuts for both men and women.

Local clients highly value the personalised attention and skill of the dedicated styling team, which includes Larry, Chantelle, Cheska, and Manchie. Visitors frequently praise the salon for its patience with complex styling requests, such as hair straightening, rebonding, colouring, and wedding day makeup. The salon is also noted for its affordable rates and the styling tips provided by the friendly staff. Whether you need a simple trim, highlights, or a complete makeover, the team takes pride in ensuring every visitor leaves feeling beautiful and satisfied.

Located at Unit 3a/24 Main Street, the salon is open Monday to Wednesday, Friday, and Saturday from 9:00 am to 5:00 pm, and has extended opening hours until 6:30 pm on Thursdays. The business is closed on Sundays. Appointments and enquiries can be made directly by calling 0433 971 108.

name: Lava Bistro & Grill
description:
Lava Bistro & Grill was a popular Filipino restaurant located on Artillery Crescent in Seven Hills, Sydney. It was widely known for its authentic, hearty comfort food and flame-grilled specialities. The eatery specialised in a range of traditional Pinoy dishes, such as the char-grilled marinated chicken inasal, BBQ pork belly, pork skewers, and sizzling pork sisig. Seafood lovers enjoyed their stuffed squid and char-grilled salmon, while breakfast options included classic silog meals like tocilog and longsilog. Visitors could also enjoy a variety of traditional dipping sauces, including mildly spicy vinegar and sweet BBQ sauce.

Locals loved the vibrant atmosphere and the casual dining experience, which frequently featured live bands playing classic Filipino pop music. The restaurant was a favourite gathering spot for families and friends celebrating special occasions or simply looking to connect over familiar dishes. Guests appreciated the convenient parking outside, as well as the generous portions and enticing flavours of the takeaway options. Visitors also frequently commended the friendly staff and the welcoming environment that made everyone feel right at home. This business has permanently closed.

name: Lazza Marrickville
description:
Lazza Marrickville is a welcoming Filipino restaurant located on the bustling Marrickville Road in Sydney. This cosy dining spot specialises in authentic sizzling platters and traditional festive dishes, bringing the rich flavours of the Philippines to the local community. It is a favourite gathering place for families and large groups looking for a warm and lively dining experience.

The menu features classic Filipino dishes cooked to perfection. Diners highly recommend the crispy pork sisig, which is served on a sizzling hot plate. Other popular choices include the crispy lechon kawali, deep-fried pork knuckle (crispy pata), and beef bulalo soup. For special celebrations, groups can pre-book a traditional Boodle Fight feast, where a variety of delicious dishes are served together on banana leaves.

To end the meal, the sweet turon rolls served with ube ice cream and the generous halo-halo desserts are popular selections. The friendly staff create a homey environment, ensuring everyone feels welcome. The restaurant also hosts live music performances on Friday and Saturday nights, adding to the festive atmosphere. Lazza is open for dine-in and takeaway, offering a true taste of home in Sydney's inner west.

name: Leenez Telopea Take Away Shop
description:
Leenez Telopea Take Away Shop is a popular local eatery in Telopea, Sydney, specialising in authentic Filipino dishes alongside classic takeaway favourites. The shop is run by a warm and welcoming team. They have earned a reputation for excellent customer service and generous portions.

Locals highly recommend trying the traditional Filipino plates, which showcase delicious flavours made with love. Customer favourites include the savoury pork adobo, crispy spring rolls (lumpia), and giniling, a classic minced meat dish. For those seeking classic takeaway fare, the Pinoy burger is also a standout choice. Customers frequently highlight the freshness of the meals, which are prepared quickly and packed carefully for delivery or takeaway.

Whether ordering a quick lunch or a satisfying dinner, visitors appreciate the reasonable prices and friendly atmosphere. The takeaway shop serves the surrounding Telopea community and offers both delivery and kerbside pickup options. It is a fantastic spot to experience genuine Filipino hospitality and delicious, home-style cooking.

name: Leoncia's Filipino Desserts & Yema
description:
Leoncia's Filipino Desserts & Yema is a warm and welcoming dessert shop and cafe located on Level 3 of Castle Towers Shopping Centre in Castle Hill. This family-run business specialising in authentic, homemade Filipino hot meals and sweet treats has quickly become a favourite spot for the local community.

Customers love the generous portions and the friendly hospitality of the owner, Leoncia. The menu features a delicious range of classic mains such as beef tapa, pork sisig, rich dinuguan, and beef kare-kare, alongside refreshing calamansi juice. For dessert, visitors can indulge in traditional Halo-Halo, chocolate champorado, and biko. The shop is also highly celebrated for its signature 'Yummi Yema' custard spreads and ube gelato, which are made with care and capture the comforting taste of home.

Whether you are seeking a nostalgic taste of the Philippines or wishing to try these unique dishes for the first time, this Castle Hill centre offers an excellent experience. The team serves every visitor with a smile, ensuring a bright and clean dining atmosphere.

name: Let’s Chon
description:
Let’s Chon is a cosy and popular Filipino restaurant located at 7 Gleeson Avenue in Sydenham, just a short walk from the train station. This quaint local eatery specialises in authentic Cebu lechon, a traditional savoury roast pig from the Visayas region of the Philippines. Local diners highly recommend their lechon pork belly, which features perfectly seasoned meat, delicious sauces, and a delightfully crispy crackling skin. 

Beyond their signature roast pig, the menu highlights other classic Filipino favourites. Guests can enjoy flavourful garlic fried rice, lechon sisig, stuffed bangus, and hearty beef bulalo soup. For dessert, the restaurant offers sweet treats such as turon with ice cream, halo-halo, and creamy mango or ube shakes. 

The dining space is quite small and intimate, creating a lively and bustling community atmosphere. Because it can get busy during peak hours, booking in advance is recommended for larger groups. Whether you are sharing a feast with friends or introducing newcomers to traditional Filipino flavours, Let’s Chon offers a warm and welcoming slice of Pinoy culture in Sydney's inner west.

name: Leumeah Baptist Church
description:
Leumeah Baptist Church is a welcoming independent Baptist church located at 78 Amundsen Street in Leumeah. Originally established under the ministry of Pastor Raymond Payne, the church is currently led by Pastor Sherwin Ramos. It serves as a vibrant gathering place for a multicultural congregation. It includes a warm and active Filipino fellowship group that is highly regarded for its community-focused work.

The church offers a variety of ministries designed to support everyone in the family. These include dedicated programs for men, ladies, and youth, alongside active outreach initiatives in the local area.

Members of the community and visitors are welcome to join the weekly services. On Sundays, the day begins with morning prayer at 9:00 am, followed by Sunday School at 9:30 am. The main morning worship service starts at 10:30 am, and an afternoon worship service is held at 3:00 pm. A midweek prayer service also takes place every Wednesday evening at 7:30 pm. For those unable to attend in person, the church regularly shares livestream links to their sermons online. It is a wonderful space to grow in faith, connect with friendly locals, and experience genuine Christian fellowship.

name: Life Choice Church
description:
Life Choice Church is a warm and welcoming Pentecostal Foursquare congregation based in Cronulla, Sydney. Led by Senior Pastors Rosel Joy and Irene Bona, this community-focused church is known for its friendly, family-oriented atmosphere where everyone feels valued and accepted from the moment they walk through the doors.

Attendees regularly praise the inspiring and practical Sunday messages, which connect faith to everyday life, alongside the uplifting worship experiences. Beyond spiritual growth, the church is deeply committed to supporting the local neighbourhood. A key initiative is their 'Heart to Cart' community outreach programme, which generously distributes free groceries to families and individuals in need. Additionally, the church offers dedicated children's ministries led by Liuven and Claudine, as well as pastoral care coordinated by Michael and Abby.

Weekly services are held every Sunday from 3:00 pm to 5:00 pm at their Gosport Street venue. Whether you are looking to build meaningful relationships, grow in your faith, or connect with a supportive local community, Life Choice Church provides a welcoming home for all.

name: Linda Natividad
description:
Linda Natividad is a highly experienced civil marriage celebrant based in Quakers Hill, Western Sydney. With over twenty-four years of service, she specialises in crafting beautiful and memorable ceremonies, including weddings, intimate elopements, vow renewals, and naming days. She is particularly popular within the Filipino-Australian community for her ability to seamlessly incorporate traditional customs such as the Veil, Cord, and Arrhae, bringing a touch of cultural authenticity to the special day.

Couples warmly appreciate her friendly, caring, and professional approach. She guides partners through every step of the legal process, from the initial consultation to receiving the marriage certificate, ensuring a stress-free experience. Clients highlight her meticulous attention to detail, punctuality, and ability to keep ceremonies organised, even under challenging conditions. Her ceremonies are known for being both solemn and cheerful, often featuring lighthearted jokes and anecdotes that put guests at ease. Whether planning a small gathering or a larger celebration, Linda provides dedicated guidance to help couples create a ceremony that truly reflects their unique love story.

name: Linda Natividad (Civil Marriage Celebrant)
description:
Linda Natividad is a highly experienced civil marriage celebrant and Justice of the Peace based in Quakers Hill, Western Sydney. Migrating from the Philippines in 1975, she has been an active leader in the local Filipino-Australian community for decades. Linda has spent nearly thirty years crafting personalised, meaningful ceremonies that beautifully blend modern and traditional elements.

Couples appreciate her friendly, warm, and professional approach. She guides them through the entire process, from the first consultation and legal paperwork to the final vows. She is particularly skilled in incorporating traditional Filipino wedding customs, such as the veil, cord, and coin ceremonies. Her ability to keep events highly organised, solemn, yet lighthearted with cheerful anecdotes makes her a popular choice.

Linda is dedicated to serving couples from all cultural backgrounds, officiating multicultural, interfaith, and same-sex unions. Whether planning a large traditional wedding or an intimate elopement, she ensures a stress-free and memorable experience. Based in the Blacktown region, she serves couples throughout Sydney and the wider New South Wales area.

name: LionLamb Cakes
description:
LionLamb Cakes is a boutique home bakery located in Riverstone, Sydney, specialising in custom-designed cakes, cupcakes and desserts for special occasions. Founded and operated by local baker Joanne, the business has built a stellar reputation in the community for creating beautiful and delicious custom creations, ranging from vintage-style vanilla cakes and gender reveal treats to themed birthday designs like Paw Patrol.

Customers consistently praise the outstanding taste and texture of Joanne's bakes. Her signature chocolate cupcakes are known for being incredibly moist and fluffy, paired with a smooth frosting that strikes the perfect balance of flavour without being overly sweet. Beyond her baking talent, Joanne is highly regarded for her accommodating customer service, clear communication during the design process and ability to work on short notice. She also shows meticulous care when catering to specific dietary requirements and allergen concerns.

Deeply valued for her community spirit, Joanne has previously supported charity events such as St Canice's Kitchen Christmas party by donating her labour to provide hundreds of cupcakes. Operating from Nicotera Avenue, LionLamb Cakes is the perfect choice for anyone looking for personalised, high-quality cakes that look as amazing as they taste.

name: Litrato
description:
Litrato is a professional photography and videography studio based in Doonside, Sydney, specialising in weddings, pre-wedding shoots, birthdays, and community events. Managed by lead photographer and creative director Michael Dela Cruz, the studio is known for capturing memorable moments with an artistic, cinematic, and candid approach.

Clients frequently praise the team's ability to put couples at ease on their big day, delivering vibrant and beautiful images that notice every detail, even in challenging conditions like rainy weather. In addition to photography and filmmaking services, Litrato offers popular photobooth packages complete with fun props, which add a lively and memorable touch to wedding receptions and parties. The studio is appreciated by the local Filipino-Australian community for its affordable rates and dedication to preserving special celebrations. While most couples report highly positive experiences with their final photo and video packages, some reviews suggest that booking timelines and post-production communication can occasionally experience delays. Despite these minor timing issues, local clients widely recommend Michael and his team for their creativity, patience, and professional talent. They provide a reliable option for couples seeking to document their love story or family milestones in Western Sydney.

name: Little Angel Cafe
description:
Little Angel Cafe is a cosy, community-focused eatery tucked away in a Rydalmere business park on Victoria Road, directly opposite Bunnings Warehouse. Sharing its space with a guitar shop and a music school, this unique cafe has become a favourite local spot for its friendly service and welcoming atmosphere.

The cafe specialises in a creative menu that blends traditional Filipino and Japanese flavours. Visitors highly recommend the signature beverage selection, particularly the authentic ube matcha, banana matcha, and oat matcha lattes. For food, the menu features delicious savoury bites including freshly made chicken katsu sandos served on soft shokupan bread, longganisa bagels, and tuna mayo or yuzu salmon onigiris. The avocado toast with miso cream cheese is another unique option popular with local workers.

The intimate space features a Polaroid photo fridge and 'We’re Not Really Strangers' card games to encourage warm conversation and community connection. The cafe operates from 7:30 am to 2:00 pm on weekdays, providing a perfect spot for breakfast, brunch, or a quick midday coffee break.

name: Lola Remy's
description:
Lola Remy's is a welcoming family-run business in Jordan Springs that serves the local community as both an authentic Filipino eatery and a well-stocked grocery shop. Situated right by the scenic lake on Lakeside Parade, this cosy local favourite offers a warm, friendly atmosphere where visitors are treated with genuine hospitality.

As a carinderia-style diner, it is highly regarded for serving delicious, traditional comfort food. Local favourites include crispy sisig, pork adobo, tender lechon pork belly, and savoury diniguan, alongside fresh spring rolls and sweet treats like cassava cake and leche flan. For those dining in or ordering takeaway, the popular two-choice combination with rice provides a satisfying and affordable taste of the homeland.

Beyond the hot food counter, the store specialises in essential Filipino groceries, featuring imported pantry items, frozen meats, sweet snacks, and ice cream. The owners actively support the community by displaying flyers for other local ventures. This outstanding contribution to the Londonderry region has even earned formal recognition in the New South Wales Parliament. It is a wonderful spot to grab a bite, stock up on specialty ingredients, and experience classic hospitality.

name: Lola Rizza's Filipino Eatery
description:
Lola Rizza’s Filipino Eatery is a warm and inviting café and restaurant located in Mount Druitt, Western Sydney. Conveniently situated just outside Westfield Mount Druitt and near the station, this family-owned eatery specialises in authentic, home-cooked Filipino comfort food with flavours originating from the Pampanga region.

Led by Rizzo and a hospitable team, the eatery is highly regarded for its friendly, engaging, and attentive service that makes diners feel right at home. The menu features a delicious selection of traditional favourites, including rich pork sisig, smoky charcoal-grilled chicken and pork skewers, crispy lechon kawali, and savoury dinuguan. Guests also rave about the refreshing halo-halo, making it the perfect sweet finish.

Diners appreciate the exceptional value, with generous portions and popular meal deals that include rice and a complimentary bowl of comforting hot soup. With its own cosy indoor seating area, Lola Rizza’s offers a delightful dine-in experience, alongside takeaway, delivery, and catering options. Whether stopping by for a casual lunch or ordering catering for a special event, this local gem provides a genuine taste of the Philippines in a hospitable community setting.

name: Lola's Table Filipino Mexican Bistro Wentworth Point
description:
Lola's Table Filipino Mexican Bistro is a unique, welcoming dining spot in Wentworth Point, located close to the water at Shop 1/18 Footbridge Boulevard. This family-friendly eatery specialise in a creative fusion of traditional Filipino comfort food and vibrant Mexican flavours.

Diners love the generous portions and authentic tastes. Among the standout menu items are the incredibly crispy pata (crispy pork knuckle), tender pork belly, sizzling sisig, and savoury barbecue skewers. The bulalo beef soup is huge and highly popular on cold days. Guests also enjoy a variety of Mexican options, such as massive spicy fish tacos, quesadillas, and elote (charred, juicy street corn). For dessert, the house-made ube halaya and refreshing halo-halo are absolute favourites that should not be missed.

The bistro offers a cosy, casual atmosphere suitable for both small and large groups, with indoor seating and an outdoor patio. They also provide popular catering services and traditional Filipino boodle fight feasts, which require booking two days in advance. Lola's Table operates for dinner from 5:00 pm to 9:00 pm Monday through Thursday, with both lunch and dinner services from 12:00 pm to 3:00 pm and 5:00 pm to 9:00 pm on Friday, Saturday, and Sunday.

name: Lovely Variety Store
description: Lovely Variety Store was a family-run Asian supermarket located on the library side of Hurstville in Sydney. The business specialised in sourcing a rich selection of authentic food products from the Philippines and other Asian nations. Locals frequented the shop to buy traditional ingredients for cooking classic dishes at home, including popular items like longanisa, tocino, and tapa. In addition to essential groceries, the store functioned as a specialty convenience outlet. It stocked unique international treats, such as Dubai chocolate with pistachio cream, Takis, and American chocolate bars. Customers regularly highlighted the warm, family-friendly environment and the helpfulness of the owners, who were always happy to chat and share knowledge about their products. The shop was highly regarded for its affordable pricing and welcoming service. To support the local community, the store also offered home delivery services across the St George area and the Sutherland Shire, allowing residents to easily receive their favourite items. Although it was a beloved local hub for the community to connect over a shared love of Filipino cuisine, this business has permanently closed.

name: Lugaw Queen Food Trailer
description:
Lugaw Queen Food Trailer is a popular mobile eatery in Sydney that specialises in authentic Filipino street food. Founded by Lyngel Malate, the venture began as a small home-cooking project for friends and local mechanics in Blacktown. Today, it has grown into a beloved community favourite, frequently popping up at markets and events across Western Sydney, including Prospect and Parramatta.

The food trailer is best known for serving warm, comforting bowls of traditional rice porridge, such as goto and lugaw. A typical serving of their goto is topped with boiled egg, corned beef, and crispy chicharon, making it a hearty choice for those craving classic Manila flavours. To accompany the meals, they also offer sweet, refreshing gulaman drinks.

Local patrons highly value the trailer for its authentic recipes and friendly hospitality. Many customers appreciate the warm atmosphere created by the owners, who are known to offer exceptionally welcoming service. While their schedule varies between permanent sites and regional community festivals, Lugaw Queen Food Trailer remains a go-to destination for anyone seeking a genuine taste of Filipino comfort food in the Sydney area.

name: Lutong Pinoy atbp.
description:
Lutong Pinoy atbp. is a beloved Filipino restaurant in Rooty Hill, Western Sydney, which now trades as Cucina de Manila. This dining spot specialises in authentic, homestyle Filipino comfort food served in a warm and welcoming setting.

Diners highly recommend visiting for their generous portions and authentic flavours. A crowd favourite is the crispy pata, which features perfectly crackly skin and tender, succulent pork knuckle. Other popular dishes include their rich peanut-based kare-kare, savoury pork sisig, pork dinuguan, and traditional lechon. They also offer classic sides like garlic fried rice and refreshing sago't gulaman to complete the meal.

Whether you choose to dine in with family or grab takeaway, this venue offers a casual and relaxed atmosphere that makes it a popular hub for the local Filipino community. It is conveniently located on Rooty Hill Road South, making it easily accessible for anyone looking to enjoy a hearty, traditional Pinoy feast in Sydney.

name: MARCELO TRANSPORT
description:
Marcelo Transport is a family-owned logistics and courier company based in Blacktown, Sydney, that has been specialised in cargo forwarding and transport since 1999. They are highly regarded by the local community for delivering reliable, timely, and professional freight services. 

Customers regularly praise the helpful team for their friendly, kind, and cooperative customer service. The company specialises in fragile freight and general cargo transportation, ensuring that every shipment is handled with the utmost care. Many clients highly recommend them for their excellent communication and trustworthy, professional drivers who ensure safe and timely deliveries. Whether you are sending cargo locally across the Sydney metropolitan area or require reliable transport services, Marcelo Transport prides itself on its high level of customer care and dedication. Their local operations in Western Sydney make them a convenient and favourite choice for residents and businesses needing professional transport, courier, and logistics support. They focus on delivering a stress-free experience for all their clients.

name: MD KITCHEN [ masarap dito ]
description:
MD Kitchen, also known as MD Kitchen [ masarap dito ], is a welcoming Filipino eatery specialising in authentic home-style dishes in Blacktown. Situated on Patrick Street, it has become a popular spot for locals seeking genuine Filipino flavours in the heart of Western Sydney.

The eatery is highly regarded for its delicious and traditional meals, which are prepared with care and served at very affordable prices. Customers particularly appreciate the brunch options and the welcoming service. The menu features a variety of classic dishes that capture the true essence of Filipino cuisine. To support the local community, the business also offers a special discounted price for pensioners.

Whether you choose to dine in and enjoy the friendly atmosphere or opt for a convenient takeaway meal, MD Kitchen provides a wonderful opportunity to experience traditional Filipino hospitality. The restaurant is open daily from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM, making it an ideal destination for a hearty lunch or a daytime treat.

name: MESA
description: Mesa is a boutique Filipino dining spot situated in the heart of Rooty Hill, in Sydney's vibrant western suburbs. Established by passionate chef and restaurateur Will Mahusay, who is also famous for founding Sydney Cebu Lechon, this restaurant specialises in serving authentic and comforting Filipino flavours. The unique dining experience operates on a booking-only basis and welcomes guests exclusively for dinner service on Saturdays and Sundays. Diners appreciate the welcoming atmosphere and professional service at the venue. The menu focuses on traditional Pinoy comfort food, which is prepared with premium ingredients that showcase the rich culinary heritage of the Philippines. Guests can enjoy their meals in a cosy setting, making it a perfect spot for family dinners or catching up with friends in the neighbourhood. The restaurant is conveniently located on Rooty Hill Road South, and visitors benefit from plenty of free parking space available in the surrounding area and streets. Please note that the restaurant is currently temporarily closed.

name: MIGASSIST
description:
MIGASSIST is a registered migration agency based in the suburb of Woodcroft in Western Sydney, New South Wales. Led by experienced migration agent Gilda Asoy Ponferrada, the practice specialises in offering tailored visa and immigration support services to the Filipino-Australian community. The agency assists clients with various immigration pathways, including skilled independent visas, employer-sponsored programs, student visas, and family reunion applications.

Local clients appreciate having a dedicated service in Western Sydney to help them navigate the complex Australian immigration system. Some clients have shared reviews regarding occasional scheduling delays during peak appointment hours and note that response times can vary when discussing initial enquiries. However, the agency continues to provide essential support for kinsfolk seeking to relocate or extend their stay in Australia.

The office is located in the residential area of Woodcroft. This location offers convenient access for local residents in the surrounding Blacktown and Western Sydney areas who prefer face-to-face consultations. The practice aims to deliver reliable guidance for individuals and families planning their future in Australia.

name: MANUY'S GRILL
description:
Manuy's Grill is a local Filipino eatery in Rooty Hill, known for serving authentic barbecue and comfort food in a cosy backyard-style setting. Operating beside a residential property with outdoor seating, the venue delivers a relaxed, casual dining experience reminiscent of the Philippines.

Visitors frequently praise the friendly, attentive staff and the extensive selection of classic dishes and street food treats. Popular menu highlights include their pork skewers, sizzling pork sisig, pares, and crispy chicharon bulaklak. For dessert or drinks, guests often enjoy the traditional sago't gulaman. The portions are generous and prices are highly reasonable, making it a popular choice for families and local food lovers looking for hearty Filipino meals. In addition to their main location, the team regularly hosts a busy stall at the Blacktown Markets pop-up events.

Please note that this business is temporarily closed.

name: MRT Pilipino Foods and Products
description:
MRT Pilipino Foods and Products is a welcoming Filipino grocery store and eatery in Cabramatta. Located inside the BKK Shopping Centre, it serves as a central hub for the local Filipino-Australian community. The shop stocks a wide variety of authentic imported groceries, pantry staples, and popular snacks. It is a reliable spot to find familiar products like Reno liver paste that are hard to find elsewhere.

Beside the grocery aisles, the venue runs a popular hot food bar. They prepare and serve a range of delicious, affordable Filipino dishes daily. Customers can enjoy warm cooked meals and traditional delicacies on-site.

Locals highly praise the store for its friendly customer service and warm hospitality. The helpful owners make every visitor feel right at home. It is a comforting space for homesick Filipinos and a welcoming place for others to discover new flavours. MRT Pilipino Foods and Products offers a genuine taste of the Philippines in South West Sydney.

name: Mabuhay Filipino Cuisine
description: Mabuhay Filipino Cuisine was a local eatery situated at Shop 1/137-139 Stephen Street, Blacktown, in the western suburbs of Sydney. This restaurant specialised in preparing and serving a variety of traditional and authentic Filipino comfort food dishes, catering to the local community's desire for genuine home-style flavours and hospitable service. Customers visiting the establishment could enjoy an array of classic Filipino favourites, including popular grilled meats, stews, and traditional desserts, highlighting the rich culinary heritage of the Philippines. The dining experience was designed to be welcoming and family-friendly, making it a popular choice for local residents looking to savour authentic dishes. It operated as a key takeaway and dine-in spot for those seeking comfort meals in the Blacktown area, contributing to the diverse multicultural dining scene of Western Sydney. The venue was particularly noted for offering these classic flavours in a friendly and casual atmosphere, serving as a hub for both the local Filipino diaspora and food enthusiasts eager to explore new tastes. This business has permanently closed.

name: Mabuhay HomeCare Services
description:
Mabuhay HomeCare Services is a dedicated provider of personal home care, disability support, and aged care services based in Leppington, New South Wales. Co-founded by two experienced Filipino nurses, the service operates with a focus on delivering high-quality, compassionate care that allows participants to live independently at home.

The team, composed largely of trained support workers and registered nurses, offers focused, one-on-one assistance tailored to each individual's needs. Key offerings include personal care, housekeeping, community participation, travel and transport assistance, and support coordination. A central feature of their service is the 24/7 onsite clinical oversight provided by registered nurses, ensuring continuous safety and professional medical assessments at any hour.

Mabuhay HomeCare Services supports residents across Greater Western Sydney, including Blacktown, Camden, Campbelltown, Liverpool, Fairfield, and Parramatta. The team works in close partnership with participants and their families to design personalised support plans that promote dignity, safety, and long-term well-being.

name: Mabuhey App - Online Filipino Groceries Food and Products
description:
Mabuhey App is a dedicated online Filipino grocery service based in Sydney, offering a convenient way to access authentic Philippine food and household products. The digital platform features a comprehensive range of imported groceries, traditional snacks, and essential cooking ingredients that are delivered directly to customers or made available for kerbside pickup. Based centrally at 710 George St in Haymarket, the service provides 24-hour access to online shopping, allowing customers to place orders at any time of the day or night.

Serving the local Filipino-Australian community, this mobile marketplace platform makes it simple to order favourite pantry staples from the comfort of home. Whether you are looking for classic sauces, sweet treats, or regional delicacies, the service specialises in connecting customers with the familiar tastes of the Philippines. By providing reliable delivery options and convenient pickup services in the heart of Sydney, Mabuhey App supports both busy shoppers and local suppliers, helping to preserve and celebrate Filipino culinary traditions.

name: Maganda Hair
description:
Maganda Hair is a welcoming, modern hair salon located in the heart of Cronulla, specialising in advanced colouring, cutting, and styling. The name 'Maganda' translates to 'beautiful' in Tagalog, reflecting the salon's mission to help every client look and feel amazing. Conveniently situated on Gerrale Street near the beach, it has become a favourite local destination for high-quality hair care.

Clients highly recommend the talented styling team, particularly praising Charlotte for her exceptional blonde foils, personalised advice, and friendly approach. Stylists like Krystal are celebrated for their detailed colour consultations, ensuring each cut and styling service is tailored to the client's needs. The salon is known for its warm, inviting vibe, where a skilled team of hairdressers delivers positive, relaxing experiences.

As part of their commitment to the community and the environment, Maganda Hair operates as a sustainable salon, implementing eco-friendly waste recycling practices. They offer a full range of services, including balayage, lived-in blondes, and hair extensions, with appointments available from Tuesday to Saturday. Whether you need a fresh new look or a premium styling session, the friendly team provides expert care in a comfortable coastal setting.

name: Mama's Mart
description:
Mama's Mart is a cosy Filipino grocery store located on Church Street in the heart of Parramatta, New South Wales. Serving as a popular local hub for the Filipino-Australian community in Greater Sydney, this shop offers a great selection of traditional food, snacks, and retail goods.

Locals frequent the shop to purchase popular packaged snacks like Boy Bawang and Clover Chips, as well as a wide range of Goldilocks polvoron varieties. The store also features a fridge section stocked with classic Filipino desserts, such as puto and kutsinta. Additionally, customers can find prepared dinners like traditional stews and noodles cooked by local home cooks, making it easy to enjoy a taste of home. Beyond groceries, Mama's Mart supports the community by offering essential services like sea cargo shipping, remittance, and balikbayan box cargo.

The friendly and helpful staff are highly appreciated by visitors, making it a welcoming place to find all your favourite Filipino grocery items and household goods. Please note that this business has permanently closed.

name: Mama Lor Restaurant & Bakery
description:
Mama Lor Restaurant & Bakery in Rooty Hill, Sydney, is a beloved family-owned and operated venue that specialises in traditional Filipino home cooking and freshly baked goods. The welcoming space functions as both a dine-in eatery and a bakery, offering a warm atmosphere that makes patrons feel at home.

Visitors frequently praise the charcoal-grilled meats, particularly the pork barbecue skewers and the crispy Cebu-style lechon pork belly. Other savoury highlights include the rich and creamy kare-kare, crispy pata, and hearty bowls of sinigang and bulalo. To complement the dishes, the in-house bakery provides an excellent selection of traditional treats, including soft pandesal, sweet Spanish bread, and the popular ube macapuno cake.

Known for its friendly service and hospitable staff, Mama Lor provides a comforting taste of the Philippines for the local community and visitors alike. Whether you are popping in for a family dinner, picking up fresh pastries, or organising catering for a special function, the venue delivers generous servings of authentic comfort food in a relaxed, local setting.

name: Mamma's House
description:
Mamma's House is a popular Filipino food truck and event caterer operating across Sydney, with a regular presence in Alexandria. Specialising in authentic, home-cooked style Filipino dishes, the mobile kitchen has developed a loyal local following for its rich, traditional flavours and generous portion sizes.

Local patrons highly recommend their signature BBQ pork rice, crispy chicken, and mouth-watering pork sandwiches, which are marinated and seasoned to perfection. Other crowd favourites include the unique cheese sticks and deep-fried chillies, all freshly prepared with consistent quality and care. Operating under the culinary direction of owner Karla, the service is exceptionally warm, friendly, and welcoming.

In addition to street food, Mamma's House specialises in catering for private events, community gatherings, and local pop-up collaborations. Whether you are a fan of classic Filipino cuisine or a curious newcomer, the team provides a wonderful culinary experience.

name: Mamma’s House Food Truck
description:
Mamma’s House Food Truck is a popular mobile eatery specialising in authentic Filipino street food. Located in Alexandria, this food truck has built a loyal local following over the years for its consistently fresh and flavourful offerings.

Customers highly recommend the signature pork sandwich, which features tender, juicy marinated pork served in a soft, fresh bun. Other crowd favourites include the beautifully charred barbecue pork, crispy chicken, and salt and pepper chicken. Diners love pairing these mains with garlic rice, which comes accompanied by savoury sauces, chilli, and a zingy salsa-style pickle. The menu also features popular sides like cheese sticks and deep-fried chilli.

With generous portions and friendly service, Mamma’s House Food Truck is a favourite choice for a casual lunch or takeaway meal. They also provide catering services for community events and private functions, bringing the vibrant flavours of the Philippines directly to gatherings across the Sydney area.

name: Manila Food
description:
Manila Food is a beloved Filipino eatery located on Main Street in Blacktown, situated directly opposite the train station. For over twenty years, this local favourite has specialised in bringing authentic Pinoy street food and home-style dishes to the Sydney community.

Patrons highly recommend their charcoal-grilled barbecue options and classic main dishes like laing, Bicol Express, and savoury pork soup. Other popular menu items include sweet potato spring rolls, traditional pork buns, and refreshing desserts such as halo-halo and gulaman jelly drinks. The menu also offers vegetarian-friendly options like pinakbet and ginataang langka.

The eat-in area is relatively small and clean, making it a cosy spot for a quick lunch or brunch. Because of the limited seating, many customers choose to order takeaway. The owner and staff are known for their warm hospitality and fast, accommodating service. For added convenience, the venue also hosts a Ria Money Transfer station, making it a handy multi-service hub for the local Filipino community. Whether you dine in or grab takeaway on your commute, it is a great spot to enjoy traditional flavours.

name: Manila Grind Co.
description:
Manila Grind Co. was a cosy and welcoming Filipino-style café situated in the heart of Eastlakes, Sydney. The local venue specialised in serving classic Filipino dishes alongside high-quality coffee. Visitors regularly praised the warm hospitality and helpful service provided by the dedicated staff, who were always ready to assist patrons with directions.

The café was a popular spot for the local community to gather and enjoy hearty meals, such as their well-regarded brunch options and traditional breakfast fare. Among the menu offerings, customer favourites included flavourful poultry dishes of excellent value and unique vegetarian choices like the sarsiado eggs. The coffee was consistently brewed to a high standard, making it a favourite stop for morning routines.

Conveniently located at Eastlakes Shopping Centre, across from Pattison's Bakery, the venue featured a family-friendly atmosphere with a secure area, spacious corridors, and floor-level access that ensured excellent wheelchair accessibility. The surrounding area also provided ample parking options in the nearby garage. This business has permanently closed.

name: MWP Care
description:
MWP Care (Manly Warringah Pittwater Care) is a dedicated not-for-profit organisation in North Narrabeen that specialises in supporting seniors and individuals with disabilities on the Northern Beaches and North Shore. Operating for over 57 years, this centre offers a welcoming, family-like environment where community members can connect and thrive.

Clients and volunteers highly value the service for its warmth and practical help. Social outings and bus trips are particular favourites, helping individuals build lasting friendships. The organisation also provides vital door-to-door transport services, shopping assistance, and individual social visits. For home safety, their handyman team can assist with maintenance tasks and home modifications, such as installing handrails.

A key highlight is the dedicated Filipino Culturally and Linguistically Diverse (CALD) group, which provides tailored support to the local Filipino community. MWP Care operates Monday to Friday from 9 am to 4 pm. To access these government-subsidised services, individuals should register through My Aged Care. The friendly team is always ready to assist locals in maintaining their independence at home.

name: Manila Mart West Ryde
description:
Manila Mart is a beloved Filipino grocery store and local hub located at 23 Ryedale Road in West Ryde, conveniently close to the train station. This shop specialises in providing the local community with an extensive range of popular Filipino groceries, import snacks, spices, and essential ingredients for home-style Pinoy cooking.

Beyond its fully stocked supermarket shelves, the store features its own in-house kitchen. Here, customers can purchase signature takeaway dishes and fresh, hot food, including the famous halo-halo dessert. The business also serves as a key community resource by operating as an outlet for Forex Cargo shipping services.

Run by friendly staff including Auntie Annie and Kuya Rommel, the shop is renowned for its warm hospitality and reasonable prices. It serves as an essential touchstone for the Filipino-Australian community, helping to ease homesickness by bringing authentic tastes and services to Northern Sydney. Whether you are searching for specific pantry staples or looking to enjoy a fresh, home-cooked meal, Manila Mart offers a welcoming atmosphere that makes visitors feel right at home.

name: Manila Sari-Sari Store
description:
Manila Sari-Sari Store is a dedicated Filipino grocery shop situated inside the Lemon Grove Shopping Centre in Chatswood. Serving as a convenient hub for the local Filipino-Australian community, the store specialises in imported groceries and pantry favourites that bring a taste of home to Sydney's North Shore.

Shoppers will find a wide range of specialty goods, including traditional frozen longganisa, pandesal, Spanish bread, and essential snacks like pancit canton. The shelves are well-stocked with popular sauces, ube jam, and flavourings perfect for authentic home cooking. While many appreciate the extensive product selection, visitors should note that items are often not individually priced, and pricing is on the premium side. The store also provides convenient services like money exchange.

Located close to Chatswood station, it is an easy stop for anyone seeking authentic ingredients. Keep in mind that opening times can sometimes vary slightly from the scheduled hours, so calling ahead is recommended if visiting early in the day.

name: Manila Sunset Convenience Store
description: Manila Sunset Convenience Store was a local Filipino supermarket located at 3/43 Broadarrow Road in Narwee, Sydney. Serving as a convenient hub for the local Filipino-Australian community, the shop specialised in importing and retailing a range of Filipino groceries, pantry staples, traditional treats, and specialised healthcare items. In addition to physical products, the store also offered essential local community services, including international remittance transfers via Western Union to help families send money home, and the sale of calling cards. Regular visitors and locals appreciated the convenience of finding familiar home products and the friendly customer service from the store's staff. While some visitors noted that the variety of merchandise was modest, many regarded it as one of the most complete Filipino grocers in the Narwee area. The business maintained its online presence through a blogspot website, which served as a simple contact point for phone inquiries. This business has permanently closed.

name: Maison Mornings
description:
Maison Mornings is a warm, bright, and welcoming café located in Mortlake, Sydney, that has become a popular local gem for its fusion of classic brunch options and exciting Filipino dishes. The café offers a cosy and relaxing atmosphere, making it a comfortable spot for a weekend brunch. It is a family-friendly venue featuring a convenient nook for prams, and also welcomes pets in its small outdoor seating area.

The menu stands out for its Filipino-inspired offerings, with patrons highly praising the pork adobo for being exceptionally tender, rich, and well-balanced in its vinegar and soy sauce ratio. Other popular choices include the juicy, seasoned burger steak served with a rich gravy, and the truffled benny, which is presented on thick brioche toast with hollandaise and crispy enoki mushrooms. The café is also known for serving high-quality, silky coffees and unique drinks like a warm black sesame latte. While some visitors have noted that the mushroom pasta can be quite heavy on the cream and the pancake syrup is sometimes pre-poured, the overall culinary experience is highly regarded. Service is consistently described as friendly, attentive, and accommodating, reflecting the hospitable nature of this local eatery.

name: Manila Town
description:
Manila Town is a planned cultural and commercial precinct in Doonside, Western Sydney, designed to celebrate Filipino heritage. Situated directly next to Doonside Station, the development is set to open in 2027. It aims to serve as Australia's first dedicated Filipino hub where families and visitors can gather.

The precinct will showcase the rich flavours and traditions of the Philippines. Visitors can look forward to authentic dining experiences, featuring classic dishes like lechon and adobo. A dedicated grocery store will offer imported ingredients, snacks, and daily essentials. Additionally, the development includes a spacious events centre for community celebrations and family gatherings.

The project organisers are actively welcoming expressions of interest from local business owners. They aim to feature diverse businesses such as cafes, bakeries, restaurants, and salons. This collaborative space will provide local entrepreneurs with marketing support and networking opportunities.

Ultimately, Manila Town seeks to create a welcoming home for the Filipino-Australian community. It invites everyone to share meals, tell stories, and experience vibrant cultural programs in a unified precinct.

name: Manly Freshwater Parish
description:
Manly Freshwater Parish is a welcoming Catholic community located on Sydney's Northern Beaches, serving the suburbs of Manly and Freshwater. The parish comprises two historic worship centres: Mary Immaculate Church on Raglan Street in Manly and St John the Baptist Church on the corner of Johnson and Oliver Streets in Freshwater. Known for its warm and inviting atmosphere, parishioners and visitors frequently praise the parish for its friendly people, exceptional preaching, and peaceful environment.

The parish is highly active in fostering community connections and is home to a dedicated Filipino Family Group. This group regularly organises social morning teas and community events, helping to build strong relationships among Filipino Catholic families in the region. Weekly services at Mary Immaculate Church in Manly include morning Masses from Tuesday to Friday at 9:15 am, with early morning Masses at 7:00 am on Tuesdays and Fridays. Weekend Masses are held on Saturday evenings at 6:00 pm, and Sunday services are scheduled at 8:30 am, 10:30 am, and 6:00 pm. With a strong commitment to charity and pastoral support, the parish provides a supportive space for both young and old to gather, reflect, and participate in community life.

name: Manuel's Kitchen
description:
Manuel's Kitchen is a cosy Filipino cafe located in Artarmon, directly across from the Artarmon train station. Known for its warm and homely atmosphere, this local favourite specialises in authentic Filipino comfort food that brings a taste of the Philippines to Sydney. Patrons highly recommend the signature Chicken Inasal, which is juicy, smoky, and full of traditional Bacolod-style flavour, especially when paired with their special spiced vinegar sauce. Other standout dishes include the popular pork BBQ skewers, tapsilog, longsilog, tender Pata Tim, and a very generous serving of halo-halo for dessert.

The welcoming vibe is a key highlight of the dining experience. Chef Manuel personally greets customers at their tables, sharing warmth and humour that makes everyone feel like part of the family. The attentive staff carry the same energy, ensuring exceptional service. Whether you are local to the area or travelling from further away, Manuel's Kitchen offers a relaxed, wholesome setting to enjoy a satisfying, authentic meal.

name: Marah Hair & Beauty Studio
description:
Marah Hair & Beauty Studio is a welcoming Filipino-owned boutique hair salon located in Doonside, Sydney. Run by passionate stylist Marah, this local studio specialise in custom haircutting, colouring, and advanced treatments such as keratin services. The salon has built a loyal following within the community, with clients frequently praising the relaxing and friendly vibe.

Visitors appreciate Marah's dedicated approach to hair care. She is known for taking her time to ensure every detail is perfect, making her a favourite for those with long, thick, or curly hair. From modern curtain bangs and face-framing layers to classic cuts for salt-and-pepper hair, she tailors each style to the individual. Customers also highlight the excellent value for money and the warm, accommodating service.

As Marah often works by appointment, booking ahead is highly recommended to secure a session. Located in the heart of Doonside, the studio provides a convenient and personal hair care experience for residents in Sydney's West.

name: Margie Hairdresser
description:
Conveniently situated just steps from Rockdale Train Station, Margie Hairdresser is a welcoming boutique salon that specialises in premium hair styling and custom colouring services. The salon is owned and run by Margie, a friendly Filipina expert who brings more than ten years of professional hairdressing experience to the community. Margie offers a full range of services for all genders, including precision haircuts, layering, styling, and bold hair colouring.

Local clients highly appreciate Margie for her warm, accommodating nature and professional expertise. Many long-standing customers satisfy their styling needs here and frequently recommend her services. The salon provides a relaxed, friendly environment where everyone is treated with genuine care, ensuring that visitors leave with a happy smile. In addition to high-quality results, the salon is known for its highly affordable prices.

Margie Hairdresser is located at Suite 3, 3 King Street in Rockdale. The salon operates from Monday to Saturday between 10:00 am and 6:00 pm, while Sunday sessions are available by appointment only. Whether you need a fresh new look or a simple trim, Margie provides dedicated care that caters to the local Filipino-Australian community and wider neighbourhood.

name: Marilyn Santiago
description:
Marilyn Santiago is a registered civil marriage celebrant based in Rooty Hill, Sydney. With more than seventeen years of experience, she specialises in officiating weddings, vow renewals, naming ceremonies, and other significant life celebrations. Her interest in celebrancy began after attending a wedding that inspired her to pursue the profession, completing her Certificate IV in Celebrancy in 2005 and receiving her registration in 2007.

She is widely recognised in the Western Sydney and Eastern Creek region for her warm, personal, and heartfelt approach. Rather than reading from a rigid script, she prides herself on delivering ceremonies by heart, ensuring an authentic and emotional connection with the couple and their guests.

Marilyn is dedicated to assisting the local Filipino-Australian community. She manages all necessary legal documentation and paperwork to ensure that each marriage is legally binding under Australian law. While she specialises in civil ceremonies, she is highly flexible and happy to accommodate personal requests such as Bible readings or prayers when ceremonies are conducted in secular settings like parks.

name: Marilyn Santiago - Civil Marriage Celebrant
description: Marilyn Santiago is an authorised civil marriage celebrant based in Rooty Hill, serving couples across Sydney. With over 17 years of professional experience, Marilyn is dedicated to creating warm and personalised wedding ceremonies. She began her path into celebrancy after feeling inspired by a wedding she attended, which motivated her to complete a Certificate IV in Celebrancy. Marilyn is known for delivering ceremonies "by heart", bringing a personal and heartfelt touch to each special day. As an authorised civil celebrant, she handles all legal documentation and officiates civil weddings with care. While she ensures that every ceremony meets official legal guidelines, she also offers great flexibility to suit the couple's wishes. Marilyn is happy to personalise the service by incorporating readings, prayers, or unique requests in non-religious settings like parks or gardens. Based in the suburb of Rooty Hill in Western Sydney, she is a trusted figure in the local Filipino-Australian community, offering welcoming services to couples as they take their next steps together.

name: Marvin's Catering Sydney
description:
Marvin's Catering Sydney is a local Filipino catering service based in Marayong, Blacktown. Led by Chef Marvin, a professional chef with over twenty years of experience, this catering business specialises in bringing authentic Filipino flavours to special events across the Sydney region.

Customers highly recommend their party trays and highlight the quality of their traditional dishes. Favourites include the succulent lechon, flavourful kare-kare, chicken inasal, chicken relleno, and their special sisig. For seafood lovers, they also prepare a delicious sweet and sour barramundi. From gender reveal parties to large family get-togethers, they customise event food trays to suit different community celebrations.

The business operates as a home-based catering service, preparing meals for pickup or delivery. They are particularly active during weekends, crafting fresh party trays and custom cakes for local families. With a focus on friendly service and generous portions, they make it easy to enjoy traditional Filipino comfort food at home.

name: Mary Immaculate Catholic Parish Eagle Vale
description:
Mary Immaculate Catholic Parish is a warm and welcoming religious community located in Eagle Vale, in South-Western Sydney. The parish is known for its friendly congregation and dedicated spiritual leaders, offering a comfortable space for worship and reflection. Parishioners often highlight the helpfulness of the priests and the supportive atmosphere during gatherings.

A key highlight for the local Filipino-Australian community is the monthly Filipino Mass, which is held on the second Sunday of each month at 4:00 pm. Additionally, the parish hosts a Holy Hour led by the Filipino group on the first Saturday of the month at 11:00 am. Regular weekly services also take place, including vigils and weekday Masses.

Located at 61 Emerald Drive, the parish features ample parking and a welcoming environment for all visitors. The church works closely with the local community and school to prepare families and children for sacraments and school transitions.

name: Mary Immaculate Parish
description:
Mary Immaculate Parish is a welcoming Catholic community located at 125 Barnier Drive in Quakers Hill, New South Wales. The parish serves as a vibrant spiritual hub for local families and features a particularly strong and active Filipino community. Parishioners regularly praise the friendly atmosphere, where staff and volunteers in the parish office are always ready to assist with services such as baptisms and home blessings.

Visitors appreciate the serene and well-organised environment of the church. The facility is fully air-conditioned and equipped with a high-quality sound system, ensuring that services are comfortable and accessible for everyone. There is also ample parking within the compound and excellent accessibility for all attendees.

The parish leadership, including the highly supportive resident priest Fr Oliver, is well-regarded for being supportive and available to the congregation. A variety of active programmes cater to different age groups, including dedicated youth ministries such as the Little Saints and young adult fellowship groups. Whether you are attending a regular mass or participating in community events, this parish offers a peaceful and supportive centre for spiritual growth and connection in Western Sydney.

name: Mary Immaculate Parish Bossley Park
description:
Mary Immaculate Parish in Bossley Park is a welcoming Roman Catholic church community that has been serving Western Sydney since at least 1980. The parish is highly regarded by locals for its warm, solemn services, active liturgy, and friendly, approachable clergy. It is particularly known for its strong connection to the local Filipino community, hosting a dedicated monthly Tagalog Mass on the second Sunday of each month at 11:00 am.

Visitors appreciate the solemn atmosphere of the modern church building, which features fantastic musicians, a dedicated choir, and beautifully organised parish groups. The parish offers a full range of sacramental services, including baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and funerals, with a reputation for smooth coordination. There is ample parking on-site, including designated mobility-impaired parking spaces, though it can get busy during large services. Located on Mimosa Road, the church is closely linked with the neighbouring Mary Immaculate Primary School, making it a central spiritual and educational hub for local families. Whether attending regular weekday services or the monthly Filipino gathering, parishioners and guests are sure to experience a loving, caring Christian community.

name: Mary Mother of the Church Catholic Parish
description:
Mary Mother of the Church Catholic Parish is a warm and active faith community located at 58 Edgar Street in Macquarie Fields. Served by the Divine Word Missionaries (SVD), this multicultural parish serves as a quiet and reflective sanctuary for local families and visitors alike. Parishioners frequently highlight the wonderful spiritual atmosphere, the deep sense of fellowship, and the dedicated choir that enhances the Sunday services. The parish priest is widely appreciated for being welcoming, approachable, and delivering engaging messages tailored to all age groups.

In addition to hosting regular sacraments and devotions, the parish offers a comprehensive schedule of weekday and weekend Masses, Reconciliation, Adoration, and monthly Indonesian services. It is particularly known for its strong connection to the local Filipino community, coming alive during the Christmas season with traditional dawn Masses (Simbang Gabi). With parking available on-site and early access accommodated for special parish events, it stands out as a friendly and caring centre of community life where people of all backgrounds can gather to pray, reflect, and celebrate the Eucharist together.

name: Mary, Queen of the Family Parish, Blacktown
description:
Mary, Queen of the Family Parish is a vibrant Catholic church in the heart of Blacktown, Western Sydney. The parish represents a welcoming and diverse community formed by the unification of the historical St Patrick’s and St Michael’s churches. It is a key spiritual hub for the local area. The parish works closely with schools like Nagle College and St Patrick's Blacktown.

Visitors and parishioners love the parish for its positive and holy atmosphere. The grounds feature plenty of parking. There is a spacious lobby and a quiet chapel for silent prayer. Regular attendees appreciate the warm hospitality and friendly staff. Community fellowship often includes sharing food after Sunday Masses. The church is also known for hosting the annual Good Friday Walks, a major local Easter tradition.

Mary, Queen of the Family Parish hosts the Diocesan Filipino Chaplaincy. This includes monthly Tagalog Masses and community fellowship. The main parish office opens from Monday to Friday between 9:00 AM and 4:00 PM. The community gathers regularly for daily Masses, special services, and fellowship, making it a supportive spiritual home for the Filipino-Australian community.

name: Masagana Asian Groceries
description:
Masagana Mini Mart & Filipino Groceries (also known as Masagana Oriental Variety Store) is a beloved family-run grocery shop located at Nardoo Street in the heart of Ingleburn. The store specialises in sourcing and supplying authentic Filipino groceries, pantry ingredients, and classic snacks directly to the local community in south-western Sydney. 

Locals particularly love visiting the shop on Saturday mornings, when the store serves delicious, hot homestyle cooked Filipino lunch meals and traditional kakanin desserts. Regular customers highly recommend trying their popular ube cake, pork skewers, and fresh lumpia. Alongside a wide variety of daily staples and imported products, the friendly and patient staff assist customers with convenient Ria money transfer services, making the mart a helpful hub for the Filipino-Australian community. With a warm, welcoming atmosphere where the team is always happy to chat in Tagalog, it offers a truly authentic shopping experience for both newly arrived families and long-term residents looking for a taste of home.

name: Masagana Mini Mart & Filipino Groceries
description:
Masagana Mini Mart & Filipino Groceries is a welcoming, family-run Asian grocery store located in Ingleburn, Sydney. This local business specialises in bringing authentic tastes of the Philippines to the local community, offering an extensive range of imported ingredients, pantry staples, and popular snacks.

Customers frequently praise the friendly and patient staff, who are always ready to assist and offer advice on product freshness. The shop is highly regarded for its Saturday morning cooked food offerings, featuring delicious homestyle meals, fresh lumpia, and traditional desserts like ube cake. In addition to groceries, the store serves as a helpful hub for Ria money transfer transactions, making it a key resource for the local diaspora.

Located in Nardoo Street, the mini-mart provides a warm, community-oriented atmosphere where customers can shop and chat in their own language. Whether you are looking for specific ingredients for a favourite Filipino dish or craving fresh weekend kakanin (rice cakes), this convenience store offers a genuine taste of home in Sydney's south-west.

name: Masagana Oriental Variety Store
description:
Masagana Oriental Variety Store is a long-standing Filipino grocery store located in Ashfield, Sydney. Operating since 1998, this local favourite specialises in authentic ingredients, packaged snacks, and imported merchandise from the Philippines. Customers highly appreciate the clean and well-organised shelves, decent prices, and the extremely helpful service from the friendly staff. 

The store features a popular hot food section offering freshly cooked meals such as bangus (milkfish) and ulam, alongside a bakery area that stocks fresh bread, pan de sal, and pastries from Goldilocks. Visitors can also find a wide range of frozen goods, traditional sweets, pork hopia, and the popular Manila St ice cream. In addition to groceries, Masagana provides vital community services including Palawan and Ria money transfer remittances and balikbayan shipping boxes. Situated right next to Ashfield train station, it offers a convenient and welcoming shopping experience with a warm, home-like feeling for both the local Filipino-Australian community and visitors. Whether you are shopping for daily essentials or looking for a taste of home, the helpful team makes everyone feel welcome.

name: Masagana Oriental Variety Store - Campsie
description:
Masagana Oriental Variety Store is a popular Filipino grocery shop located at Shop 6, 20-22 Anglo Road in Campsie. Established in 1998, the store specialise in offering a wide selection of authentic groceries and pantry staples. These items bring a familiar taste of home to the local community in Sydney. Customers regularly praise their range of snacks, frozen foods, and delicious treats like Spanish bread, mango float, and sweet leche flan. The store's savoury chicken empanadas are also a favourite choice for local shoppers.

Beyond groceries, the store serve as a helpful community hub by offering convenient services. Visitors can organise money transfers and remittances to the Philippines, including bank deposits and door-to-door options. They also assist with sending balikbayan boxes back home. While some customers mention waiting times during busy hours, many value the friendly service and family-friendly atmosphere. For extra convenience, they also run branches in Ashfield and Rockdale, and offer Australia-wide delivery through their online store.

name: Marvin Mobile Mechanic
description:
Marvin Mobile Mechanic is a trusted mobile vehicle servicing and repair business owned and operated by Filipino-Australian mechanic Marvin Del Valle. Based in Mount Druitt, Marvin provides convenient mobile mechanical services across Western Sydney, including Rooty Hill, Blacktown, and Emu Plains.

Regular customers highly value the business for its punctuality, honest pricing, and clear communication. Marvin is known for explaining mechanical issues simply without attempting to upsell unnecessary services. He specialises in a wide range of mobile services, including routine car maintenance, vehicle diagnostics, pre-purchase inspections, suspension repairs, CV joint replacements, and air-conditioning services. He also offers prompt assistance for emergency repairs such as flat batteries or minor roadside accident support.

With convenient operating hours from Monday to Friday between 8:00 am and 5:00 pm, and weekend service on Saturdays from 9:00 am to 1:00 pm and Sundays from 8:30 am to 1:00 pm, Marvin makes vehicle maintenance straightforward and stress-free. Whether you need a regular logbook service at your home or an urgent repair at your workplace, Marvin Mobile Mechanic delivers reliable, professional, and friendly local service you can trust.

name: Masagana Oriental Variety Store - Rockdale
description:
Masagana Oriental Variety Store in Rockdale is a welcoming Asian grocery shop that specialises in bringing authentic Filipino flavours and products to the local community. It serves as a go-to spot for those seeking imported Filipino ingredients, pantry staples, snacks, frozen foods, and other popular delicacies. Customers frequently appreciate the excellent variety of stock, fair prices, and the well-organised layout of the shop. The staff are widely described as friendly, helpful, and accommodating, offering warm customer service that makes shoppers feel right at home. 

In addition to its grocery offerings, the store provides valuable community services, including door-to-door and bank-transfer remittances, as well as cargo services for sending balikbayan boxes back to the Philippines. While most patrons experience exceptional service, visitors are advised to double-check their purchases. Conveniently located on Walz Street near Rockdale station, the shop is easily accessible for residents across Sydney's southern suburbs. It is open daily, with operating hours from 11:00 am to 7:00 pm on weekdays, 10:30 am to 6:30 pm on Saturdays, and 10:30 am to 5:00 pm on Sundays, making it a convenient stop for both quick snacks and major grocery trips.

name: Mate Burger
description:
Mate Burger is a casual fusion restaurant specialising in American-style burgers with bold Filipino flavours. Located in Mount Druitt, this local favourite began as a popular food truck before establishing its permanent storefront. Founded by Henry Paraan, the eatery is famous for its creative combinations and unique menu offerings.

Diners highly recommend the signature Ensaymada Burger, which features a juicy beef patty served on a sweet, buttery Filipino brioche bun with sriracha-maple bacon. Other popular choices include the crispy Filipino Fried Chicken (FFC) burger, the Lechon burger, and hearty rice bowls topped with crispy pork crackling. For sides, the loaded fries—such as the savoury Sisig fries, Adobo fries, and Cheese-cino fries topped with tocino bacon—are perfect for sharing. Guests can also enjoy traditional Filipino breakfast dishes like Longsilog, Tapsilog, and Tocsilog, before finishing their meal with a refreshing cup of creamy halo-halo.

Whether you are dining in or ordering takeaway, the friendly service and vibrant flavours make it a fantastic spot to experience modern Pinoy cuisine in Western Sydney. This business is temporarily closed.

name: Melchrishel Bakery
description:
Melchrisel Bakery is a long-standing Filipino bakery and café located in Rooty Hill, Western Sydney. For thirty-five years, this local icon has served the community with authentic Filipino breads, pastries, and custom celebration cakes. The bakery is now operating under new management, with the owners warmly greeting everyone with friendly smiles and accommodating service.
