Locals visit the shop daily for fresh, soft pandesal, sweet Spanish bread, and cheese-topped ensaymada. The display cases feature a broad range of classic treats, such as hopia, ube yema cake, and nostalgic mocha chiffon cakes reminiscent of traditional bakeries in the Philippines. Customers can also order custom cakes for weddings and birthdays, choosing from flavours like Black Forest, pistachio, and cassava suman.

Whether you are looking to enjoy a coffee and a sweet merienda treat in the cosy seating area or grabbing takeaway, the bakery offers a comforting taste of home. Located close to Rooty Hill station, it is a favourite gathering spot for the Filipino-Australian community.

name: Members Church of God International - MCGI Sydney
description:
The Members Church of God International (MCGI) Sydney is a welcoming Christian congregation that serves the Filipino-Australian community in St Marys and the greater Western Sydney area. Located at a convenient centre on the Great Western Highway in St Marys, this religious organisation centres its activities on bible-based teachings, regular community outreach, and charitable projects under the MCGI Cares initiative.

Members and visitors appreciate the powerful worship services and the focus on teaching the complete doctrines of Jesus Christ. Many describe the congregation as a safe, loving, and glorious environment that feels like a spiritual home. The church specialises in providing a supportive space for truth seekers who want to explore Christian doctrines.

The Sydney chapter hosts various regular activities, including weekly worship services, prayer meetings, and Bible studies. They also coordinate weekly online community prayers on Fridays via Zoom for the Asia Oceania district, complete with English translation services. Whether you are looking to join their Saturday thanksgiving gatherings or participate in local community work, MCGI Sydney offers a dedicated space for spiritual growth and fellowship.

name: Merienda AU
description:
Merienda AU is a registered home-based micro-bakery located in Sydney's South West, specialising in authentic Filipino bread rolls. Operating with a valid Food Safety Supervisor Certification, this family-oriented service shares the rich tradition of 'merienda'—the classic Filipino afternoon snack—with local communities.

The bakery specialises in baking soft, fluffy pandesal from scratch. Customers can choose from classic, ube, and pandan flavours. For a more indulgent treat, they offer stuffed versions, including cheese pandesal filled with cheddar or sweet cream cheese, and ube pandesal loaded with rich, homemade ube halaya. Their menu also features traditional Spanish bread, which are soft, buttery rolls filled with a sweet sugar paste.

As a delivery-only service, Merienda AU operates on a pre-order model with deliveries scheduled every Sunday. They deliver directly to doors across the Liverpool and Campbelltown council areas for a flat five-dollar delivery fee. To ensure a smooth process, ordering is done online through their official website, with cashless bank transfers as the only accepted payment method.

name: Merkado Filo Grocery
description:
Merkado Filo Grocery is a welcoming, family-owned Filipino and Asian grocery store located inside Campbelltown Mall in Campbelltown. Run by a first-generation Filipino migrant family, the shop aims to connect the local community with authentic tastes and crafts from home.

The store is neatly organised and well-stocked with a wide range of popular groceries, pantry staples, canned goods, frozen items, and snacks that can be difficult to find elsewhere in Sydney. During the festive season, they also supply traditional items like capiz parols to help families celebrate their heritage. Beyond grocery items, the shop provides valuable community services, including Western Union money transfers and efficient assistance with Philippine visa documents and NBI clearance renewals.

Locals highly appreciate the warm, attentive service provided by the owner, Karl, who is known for being extremely responsive and helpful. Customers also value the speed and reliability of the store's document processing services, which are handled with great professionalism. Transactions are smooth and straightforward, with convenient delivery and in-store pickup options available. The store is open Monday through Friday from 10:00 am to 6:00 pm, and on weekends from 10:00 am to 5:00 pm.

name: Meryenda Cafe
description:
Meryenda Cafe is a charming Filipino eatery nestled in Darlinghurst, Sydney. It has quickly become a beloved neighbourhood gem, offering a warm, relaxed, and welcoming atmosphere where locals can enjoy authentic Filipino comfort food alongside excellent coffee.

The menu features a delightful variety of traditional favourites. Visitors highly recommend the signature pork sisig roll, which is packed with tender, flavourful pork on a soft milk bun. Another popular breakfast choice is the longanisa roll, loaded with authentic Filipino sausage, egg, cheese, and a crispy hash brown. The cafe also specialises in classic dishes like chicken adobo, beef pares, and various silog plates served with garlic rice and egg.

For those with a sweet tooth, the dessert selection is a major highlight. The cafe is famous for its ube treats, with customers praising the ube cake and cupcakes as some of the best ube desserts in the area.

Whether you are popping in for a quick takeaway lunch or sitting down to savour a cup of coffee, the friendly owner and attentive staff ensure a wonderful dining experience. Meryenda Cafe brings a genuine taste of the Philippines to the heart of Darlinghurst at very reasonable prices.

name: Michael Quimson
description:
Michael Quimson is a professional financial and money transfer service based in the heart of Blacktown, Sydney. Located at Suite 1, 7-9 Patrick Street, this local business specialises in helping members of the Filipino-Australian community send money back home to the Philippines securely and reliably.

Customers highly appreciate the prompt and dependable service, noting that their money transfers consistently arrive on time. The business is well-regarded for its friendly and reliable customer support, building long-term trust with clients who have used their services for many years. The owner's dedication to customer satisfaction is evident in their positive responses and commitment to sending love back home through every transaction.

Whether you need to send funds to support your family or require assistance with local financial and taxation services, Michael Quimson offers a personal and high-quality experience. Conveniently situated near local transport links and shopping hubs in Blacktown, the office is easily accessible for residents across Western Sydney. You can contact them directly on 0452 112 592 to enquire about their services and transfer rates.

name: Migrant Workers Office (MWO) Australia - Sydney
description:
The Migrant Workers Office (MWO) Australia, formerly known as the Philippine Overseas Labor Office (POLO) Sydney, is the official labor and welfare arm of the Philippine Government's Department of Migrant Workers (DMW) in Australia. Physically situated within the Philippine Embassy in Canberra, the office operates to safeguard the welfare, verify employment contracts, and protect the rights of Overseas Filipino Workers (OFWs) across the nation, including the Sydney and New South Wales communities.

The office specialises in contract verification services, ensuring that employment terms comply with both Philippine and Australian labor standards, and coordinates closely with local community organisations to support vulnerable workers. MWO Australia is also responsible for facilitating programs for reintegration, holding regular consular and labor outreach missions, and providing counselling services for Filipino nationals facing workplace challenges or disputes. Led by dedicated labor attachés and welfare officers, the service serves as a vital bridge connecting kinsmen to government support, promoting fair treatment, safety, and empowerment within the Australian workforce.

name: Migration Mate
description:
Migration Mate is a professional migration agency based in Beecroft, Sydney, specialising in Australian visa and immigration services.

Led by registered migration agent Ruth Tabios and her supportive team, clients highly value the agency's exceptional integrity, transparency, and thorough approach to handling visa applications. Reviewers frequently praise their clear guidance and prompt responses, which help make highly complex and stressful migration processes feel smooth and manageable.

The agency provides strategic pathway planning for various visa types, including the Subclass 186 Employer Nomination Scheme, de facto partner visas, Subclass 485 graduate visas, skilled worker streams, and student visa transitions. They offer comprehensive document checklist management and realistic advice tailored to individual circumstances.

Rooted in the Filipino-Australian community, Ruth and her team have built a strong reputation for dedication, sometimes going above and beyond to assist clients through financial and personal challenges. The Beecroft-based consultancy maintains standard operating hours from Monday to Friday, helping potential migrants successfully navigate the steps to achieve their Australian dream.

name: Miguelito's Diner
description:
Miguelito's Diner is a popular Filipino fast-food eatery located in the Level 3 food court at Macarthur Square in Campbelltown. Bringing the nostalgic flavours of the Philippines to Sydney, this spot is a favourite for locals craving classic comfort food.

The diner specialises in crispy, golden fried chicken served with rich, buttery gravy, reminiscent of the famous Jollibee style. The menu also features classic Pinoy dishes such as sweet-style spaghetti, palabok, siomai, and burgers. For dessert, customers can enjoy traditional sweet treats including halo-halo, soft-serve ice cream, and warm taho.

Local diners appreciate the generous portions, particularly the large chicken pieces that are juicy and moist. While some visitors find the prices slightly high for a food court venue, the familiar home-style tastes bring comfort to the local Filipino community. The shop is tucked away in a corner of the food court, making it a hidden gem worth seeking out. Whether you want a quick lunch or a comforting taste of home, it offers a welcoming dining experience.

name: Miguelitos Diner Blacktown
description:
Miguelitos Diner is a welcoming, Filipino-owned eatery located in the Level 1 Food Court of Westpoint Blacktown. It specialises in authentic, budget-friendly Filipino comfort food and popular desserts that evoke a taste of home. Local patrons highly recommend visiting to satisfy cravings for Jollibee-style sweet and creamy spaghetti and crispy fried chicken, which is served in generous portions with savoury gravy.

The diner is also famous for its affordable soft-serve ice cream and traditional sweet treats. Regular favourites include the ube soft serve, mango supreme, halo-halo, taho, and rolled vanilla ice cream. While the diner is well known for its friendly staff and warm hospitality, visitors should note that wait times can occasionally be longer during peak weekend hours when lines form. Whether you are after a quick takeaway snack or dining in with family in the food court, it remains a popular local hub for the community to enjoy classic Filipino flavours.

name: Miguelitos Diner Campbeltown
description:
Miguelito's Diner is a popular Filipino-inspired fast-food eatery located in the bustling Macarthur Square shopping centre in Campbelltown. Tucked away in a cosy corner of the dining area, this local gem specialised in bringing the comforting flavours of the Philippines to the local community.

The diner is best known for its crispy fried chicken, which many patrons compare to the famous Chickenjoy from home. Customers frequently praise the exceptionally crispy skin and the juicy, well-seasoned meat, which is served alongside a rich, buttery gravy. For those looking for a complete fast-food experience, the menu also features Filipino-style sweet spaghetti, burgers, and chips.

To round off the meal, visitors can indulge in traditional Filipino desserts such as refreshing Halo-Halo and warm taho. While the diner sits in a busy retail hub, it offers a welcoming spot for families and shoppers to enjoy a quick bite. Whether you are a member of the local Filipino-Australian community looking for a nostalgic taste of home, or a newcomer eager to try classic Pinoy fast-food favourites, Miguelito's Diner provides a satisfying and flavourful dining option in Western Sydney.

name: Milestone Migration
description:
Milestone Migration is a professional migration agency based in Rockdale, Sydney, specialising in comprehensive Australian visa and immigration services. Led by Principal Migration Agent Susan Cayago (MARN 0801338), the dedicated team assists clients with various pathways, including Partner Visas (Subclass 801), Employer Sponsored Visas (Subclasses 482 and 186), skilled visas, and citizenship applications.

Clients highly recommend the agency for its clear communication, thorough guidance, and quick response times. The consultants, including Susan, Ryan, and Jamie, are widely praised for their exceptional patience and professionalism. They handle every stage of the visa journey with great care and precision, keeping applicants informed and confident throughout the process. This supportive approach helps reduce stress and ensures a smooth application experience.

Deeply connected to the local Filipino-Australian community, Milestone Migration regularly celebrates the successful residency stories of its clients. The office is located at 5 King Street, Rockdale, making it easily accessible for face-to-face consultations in the Sydney area, while they continue to support applicants from all over Australia.

name: MingYan Funerals
description:
MingYan Funerals is a professional and compassionate funeral service provider in Canterbury, Sydney. Owned and operated by Chinese Christian funeral directors, the team specialises in organising culturally sensitive services, burials, cremations, and international repatriations. Pastor Frank Kwok and his team work tirelessly to assist families. They handle all arrangements to alleviate stress and offer both Christian and non-religious services tailored to each family's wishes.

Their funeral home is situated at 51-53 Canterbury Road, Canterbury, just an eight-minute walk from the shopping centre. The facility features a comfortable chapel that can accommodate eighty to one hundred people, an on-site mortuary, and a hearse. Operating twenty-four hours a day, seven days a week, the team is always available to offer support when it is needed most.

Families highly recommend the service, praising Frank for his kind, patient, and organised approach. Clients appreciate his attention to detail, sincere care, and clear explanation of the entire process, alongside fair and affordable pricing. This dedicated provider brings reassurance and professional support to the local community in Sydney during moments of loss.

name: Miss Philippines Australia
description:
The Miss Philippines Australia Pageant is a long-standing and highly respected community event in Sydney, organised by the Philippine Australian Sports & Culture Inc. (PASC Inc.). Held annually as a key highlight of the Grand Philippine Fiesta Kultura, this pageant celebrates the cultural heritage, personal development, and charity work of young Filipino-Australian women.

Rather than acting as a standard commercial beauty search, the program focuses on building self-esteem, public speaking skills, and cultural awareness. Participants consistently value the supportive environment, which helps them connect with their Filipino roots and build confidence. Throughout the journey, candidates engage in various segments including cultural interviews, fun fashion displays, and community networking.

A central aspect of the pageant is the Charity Queen initiative. Through this platform, candidates fundraise for important causes, including mental health services, local charities, and children's shelters in the Philippines. Based in Sydney, this pageant provides a wonderful platform for the Filipino-Australian community to celebrate their shared identity, support vital philanthropic projects, and foster the next generation of leaders.

name: Mix Mix Co.
description:
Mix Mix Co. is a contemporary Filipino dessert parlour and food truck specialising in traditional Pinoy sweet treats in Rooty Hill. Founded by husband-and-wife culinary team Kim and Andy Ambrose, the business originally started as a popular food trailer before opening its welcoming physical storefront in Western Sydney.

Local dessert lovers frequent this cosy spot for its signature halo-halo, featuring shaved ice, a variety of toppings, and creamy soft-serve. Another favourite is the warm taho, a comforting silken tofu pudding served with sweet syrup and mixed sago pearls. The menu also features warm pocket pastries, including the savoury-sweet ube cheese pie and the classic buko coconut pie. For refreshing drinks, visitors can choose from sago't gulaman, melon coolers, and coconut pandan floats.

Customers appreciate the friendly, efficient service and the vibrant, photo-friendly interior design of the Rooty Hill shop. Mix Mix Co. provides a comforting taste of home for the local Filipino-Australian community while introducing classic street-food flavours to the broader public. The parlour opens from Tuesday to Sunday, offering a delightful community hub for anyone craving a unique sweet experience.

name: Mix Mix Co. Express
description:
Mix Mix Co. Express was a popular Filipino dessert spot and food trailer located at Westfield Liverpool, specialising in authentic and contemporary sweet treats. The business brought a nostalgic taste of traditional Filipino afternoon snacks, or merienda, to the local community in south-western Sydney.

Customers frequently visited for their signature desserts, especially the layered halo-halo and the warm, silky taho, which features soft sago pearls and rich brown sugar syrup. Other crowd favourites included their creme caramel soft serve, mais con yelo, and freshly prepared pastries like buko pie, peach mango pie, and crispy turon. Visitors consistently praised the warm, friendly customer service, reasonable prices, and the authentic flavours that transported them back to their childhood.

While this Liverpool location has permanently closed, the owners continue to operate their main headquarters at Unit 5, 52 Rooty Hill Road North in Rooty Hill, where they serve their full range of dessert specialities from Tuesday to Sunday. This business has permanently closed.

name: Mix Mix Co. Rooty Hill
description:
Mix Mix Co. is a popular Filipino dessert shop located at 5/52 Rooty Hill Road North in Rooty Hill, Sydney. Established in 2019, the business originally served locals from a small food trailer at Parklea Markets before opening its welcoming physical store. The shop specialises in classic and contemporary Filipino sweet treats and refreshing beverages.

Customers highly recommend the signature halo-halo, which features a creamy soft serve ice cream and an assortment of traditional toppings. Another favourite is the warm taho, a comforting silken tofu dessert served with sweet syrup and mixed pearls that brings back fond childhood memories. The menu also includes house-made ube cheese pies, buko pies, sago't gulaman, and chocolate scramble.

The cosy venue features a vibrant interior design that makes it a perfect spot for family gatherings and photos. Visitors frequently praise the generous portion sizes, balanced sweetness, and the friendly, efficient service provided by the team. Mix Mix Co. operates from Tuesday to Sunday, offering a delightful taste of the Philippines in Western Sydney.

name: Mix N Match Bakehouse
description:
Mix N Match Bakehouse is a family-owned Filipino bakery and cake shop located on Rooty Hill Road North in Rooty Hill, Sydney. This welcoming local favourite specialises in a wide variety of authentic Filipino breads, traditional pastries, and custom celebration cakes.

Regular visitors highly recommend their fresh pandesal and Spanish bread. These are popular choices for breakfast or a sweet snack. The bakery is also well-known for its delicious ube macapuno cake. Customers celebrate the cake for being exceptionally moist with a perfect ube flavour. In addition to the quality baked goods, the friendly and helpful staff are frequently commended for their warm service.

You can find a wonderful range of daily fresh breads, including hopia and ensaymada. They also create special custom cakes for family birthdays. The bakery serves as a key community hub for the Filipino-Australian community in Western Sydney. It offers a nostalgic taste of home in a friendly neighbourhood setting.

name: Mix N Match Bakehouse Doonside
description:
Mix N Match Bakehouse Doonside is a popular Filipino bakery located on Hill End Road in Doonside, Sydney. This welcoming local business specialises in a wide variety of traditional Filipino breads, pastries, and custom-designed cakes.

Locals and visitors love the bakery for its friendly staff, who are always happy to explain the different Filipino treats on offer. Among the favourites are the soft ube pandesal, sweet Spanish bread, and classic ensaymada. For those with a sweet tooth, the bakery provides delicious options like choco crinkles, turon, and ube cake. They also specialise in custom cakes for birthdays and other special events, prepared by their dedicated bakers.

As a popular hub in the Western Sydney community, the bakehouse can get quite busy. Customers planning bulk orders of bread or custom cakes are encouraged to pre-order in advance. Whether you want a daily treat or a special celebration cake, this bakehouse offers a delicious taste of Filipino baking traditions in a warm, community-focused environment.

name: Mock Manila Diner Sydney
description:
Mock Manila Diner Sydney is a welcoming restaurant that brings the traditional flavours of the Philippines to Sydney. This eatery specialises in serving classic Manila-style dishes that are popular with both locals and the Filipino community.

The menu highlights include their signature lechon, which features beautifully roasted pork with crispy skin, and a classic savoury chicken or pork adobo. These traditional dishes are prepared with care to offer a genuine dining experience. The restaurant provides a casual and friendly environment, making it a great place to enjoy a relaxed lunch or dinner with family and friends.

Conveniently located in Sydney, the diner is a lovely spot for anyone wanting to explore authentic Filipino cuisine or enjoy a comforting taste of home. Whether you are dining in or picking up a takeaway meal, this local eatery offers a delicious option for your next meal.

name: Mock Pinoy Brew Sydney
description:
Mock Pinoy Brew Sydney is a cosy and welcoming café located in the heart of Sydney, specialising in introducing traditional Filipino flavours to the local coffee scene. This friendly spot is a favourite among residents and visitors alike, offering a relaxed environment to enjoy a warm beverage or a light bite.

The café is particularly well-known for its specialty ube lattes, which combine rich espresso with the sweet, nutty taste of purple yam, creating a vibrant and delicious drink. To accompany your beverage, they serve fresh pandesal toast, a beloved Filipino bread roll that is toasted to perfection. It is a fantastic place to start your morning or drop in for an afternoon treat.

Situated conveniently at 45 Pitt Street, the café provides an easy stop for those working or exploring the Sydney CBD. With its approachable service and unique menu items, this café brings a comforting taste of the Philippines to the local community, making it a wonderful addition to the city's diverse dining landscape.

name: Mock Sari Sari Mart Sydney
description:
Mock Sari Sari Mart Sydney is a well-stocked grocery store located in the heart of Sydney, specialising in imported Filipino brand goods and ingredients. This convenience-style shop serves as a familiar spot for the local community, bringing authentic flavours of the Philippines to the city centre.

The store is highly appreciated for its diverse selection of traditional packaged goods, pantry staples, and regional snacks. Visitors can find a wide range of popular Filipino brands, making it simple to source ingredients for home-cooked meals. From sweet treats and savoury sauces to essential condiments, the shelves are filled with favourites that remind customers of home.

Situated at 88 Pitt Street, the shop is easy to access for anyone working, living, or shopping in the Sydney Central Business District. It operates daily with extended hours on Thursdays and Fridays to accommodate busy schedules. Whether you are searching for specific ingredients for a recipe or simply looking for a quick snack, this friendly grocery store provides a convenient shopping experience for all.

name: Mock Tagalog Fellowship Sydney
description:
Mock Tagalog Fellowship Sydney was a Christian organisation and faith-based community group located in Sydney, New South Wales, dedicated to serving the local Filipino-Australian community. The fellowship was established to provide a welcoming space for worship, shared prayer, and cultural connection, specialising in spiritual guidance and community support programs for Tagalog-speaking residents and their families across the metropolitan area.

As a central hub for social, religious, and spiritual activities, the group organised regular prayer meetings, bible study sessions, and fellowship events aimed at fostering strong relationships and maintaining cultural heritage within the diaspora. Through its various community activities, it encouraged members to support one another, share meals, and participate in local charity initiatives.

The fellowship aimed to serve as a home away from home for newly arrived migrants and long-term residents alike, helping them integrate into Australian society while preserving their language and faith traditions.

However, the organisation has ceased all operations and gatherings. This business has permanently closed.

name: Monalisa Style & Beauty
description:
Monalisa Style & Beauty is a welcoming, Filipino-owned beauty salon and hair clinic situated at 257 Doonside Crescent in Doonside, Sydney. Established by John Tamayo, the salon specialise in delivering high-quality hair styling, colouring, and personalised beauty treatments.

Local customers highly recommend the salon for its warm, friendly atmosphere and skilled staff. Visitors frequently praise their main stylist, Via, for her exceptional professionalism, warm conversation, and ability to recreate requested hairstyles perfectly. Clients appreciate that the staff take the time to understand personal needs and offer helpful advice on maintaining hair health. The clinic is also valued for its reasonable pricing and quality results, whether for a quick trim or full styling and make-up.

The salon is open seven days a week, making it convenient for the community. They operate from 10:00 am to 6:00 pm on weekdays, 9:00 am to 6:00 pm on Saturdays, and 10:00 am to 4:00 pm on Sundays. As the business operates on a phone-bookings-only basis, clients should contact the team directly at (02) 9671 3771 to secure their session.

name: Monseour Nikko Santos - Licensed Real Estate Agent
description:
Monseour Nikko Santos is a licensed real estate agent and NSW Justice of the Peace specialising in residential property sales, leasing, and management services across Sydney. Affiliated with the Real Estate Institute of New South Wales (REINSW) and working alongside Kaluh Real Estate, he offers comprehensive assistance to both buyers and sellers, with a particular focus on supporting first-home buyers.

Clients highly appreciate his patient, professional, and hands-on approach. He is known for providing clear guidance through every stage of the property journey, from the initial application process to final settlement. Whether answering questions, resolving concerns, or liaising with builders, he remains highly responsive and attentive to ensure a smooth, stress-free experience. His dedication and genuine care help clients navigate complex property markets with confidence.

Operating from Bexley North, Monseour Nikko Santos serves the local community with dedicated, round-the-clock availability to match the dynamic needs of Sydney property buyers and tenants.

name: Mr Wong Oriental (Filipino) Store
description:
Mr Wong Oriental (Filipino) Store is a welcoming, family-run Asian grocery shop located in the heart of Ashfield, New South Wales. Specialising in imported Filipino groceries and household essentials, the store serves as a key local hub for the Filipino-Australian community in Sydney.

Inside, shoppers can find a fantastic selection of classic Filipino ingredients, pantry staples, and beloved snacks, ranging from SkyFlakes crackers and traditional mayonnaise to freshly prepared regional delicacies. Beyond the grocery shelves, the store provides several essential community services. These include secure money remittance, dry cleaning, personal and small business tax services, and balikbayan cargo box shipping for sending parcels back home to families in the Philippines.

Local customers regularly praise the friendly, helpful service, often highlighting the kind welcome from the owner, whom they affectionately refer to as 'Tito'. Whether you are looking for specific ingredients to cook traditional dishes at home or need assistance with shipping and remittance, this local shop offers a warm and authentic experience.

name: Mt Druitt Asian Food Market
description:
Mt Druitt Asian Food Market is a popular grocery store located in Mount Druitt, Sydney, specialising in a wide variety of Asian and Filipino products. The market is highly valued by the local community for its competitive prices and excellent range of specialty ingredients.

Shoppers can find a diverse selection of fresh produce, including sweet potato leaves, banana leaves, fresh calamansi, bitter melon, and spinach. The store also stocks frozen items such as milkfish and soursop, alongside fresh meats, seafood, and essential pantry staples. Customers frequently praise the friendly and helpful staff who work hard to keep the shelves well-stocked with fresh goods every day.

Situated at a highly convenient location on Cleeve Close, it is a favourite shopping destination for students and families looking for authentic home-cooked flavours. The budget-friendly prices make it an easy choice for weekly grocery runs. Whether you are preparing a traditional Filipino meal or stocking up on daily essentials, this market offers a welcoming atmosphere and reliable service.

name: Mummy Inday's Catering
description:
Mummy Inday's Catering is a family-operated food service specialising in authentic traditional Filipino cuisine. Based in Macquarie Fields, this caterer serves private parties, family milestones, and community events across the Sydney region. They offer both pick-up and delivery options to make event coordination simple for hosts.

Customers frequently praise the fresh, hot food and the friendly, professional service. The menu features classic Pinoy dishes, with the Cebu-style lechon (whole roasted pig) and juicy lechon belly with crispy skin being major favourites. Other popular menu options include savoury pork barbecue skewers, crispy lumpia, well-seasoned fried chicken, and fresh chopsuey. They can cater for events ranging from small family dinners to large parties with over a hundred guests.

The staff are highly regarded for their clear communication and flexibility. They work closely with clients to plan portions and can often accommodate bookings on short notice. Whether organising a child's first birthday, a baptism, or a major milestone celebration, Mummy Inday's Catering provides a smooth and reliable dining experience for the local community.

name: Mx Filoz Asian Store
description:
Mx Filoz Asian Store, also known as MxJ Filoz Asian Store, was a local Filipino-Asian grocery shop situated on Burwood Road in Belmore, New South Wales. The shop specialised in bringing a wide range of imported Filipino pantry staples, traditional snacks, and essential cooking ingredients to the local neighbourhood. It served as a vital hub for residents seeking authentic flavours from home, stocking items that are often difficult to find in mainstream supermarkets.

In addition to its retail offerings, the store acted as a convenient drop-off point for cargo shipping services, assisting community members with sending balikbayan boxes back to the Philippines. Local customers frequently highlighted the shop's affordable prices and highly accessible location. The staff were particularly well-regarded for their warm, helpful, and polite customer service, creating a friendly and welcoming environment for the Filipino-Australian community in Sydney's south-west. While some visitors noted occasional inconsistencies with the daily opening times, the store was highly valued for its contribution to local cultural connection. This business has permanently closed.

name: My Home Living Care
description:
My Home Living Care is a professional support provider in Sydney that specialises in home care and supported independent living (SIL). Established in 2009, this registered NDIS provider serves individuals with disabilities under the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS). They focus on delivering person-centred services that respect each client's cultural background.

Clients and their families appreciate the patience, dedication, and professional standard of the care workers. Many highlight the high-quality home care and reliable domestic assistance, including tasks like house cleaning, vacuuming, and mopping. The team assists clients with daily living support and personal care. While some clients with complex physical needs have noted the importance of ensuring support workers are fully aligned with specific clinical care requirements, many families report that their loved ones are well looked after.

Based in Castle Hill, the organisation works across the Sydney metropolitan area. They offer services during weekdays from 9:00 AM to 5:30 PM, helping clients build independence in a supportive environment.

name: My Redeemer Lives Inc
description:
My Redeemer Lives is a dedicated Filipino-Australian Christian community organisation based in St Clair, New South Wales. This registered charity focuses on strengthening the community and providing vital support to vulnerable individuals, specifically targeting Filipino inmates, their families, and local seniors.

The group works closely with partners like Foodbank to coordinate and distribute essential food aid and groceries to jobless seniors and families needing assistance. Beyond direct aid, they organise educational seminars that address practical needs. These include helpful sessions on taxation, migration options, financial literacy, and domestic violence awareness to empower and educate community members.

Supporters and participants highly value their welcoming, multi-denominational Christian approach that embraces all backgrounds. By hosting active outreach programs and community forums across Western Sydney, they foster meaningful connections, encourage social inclusion, and help repair relationships. Their practical, compassionate programs offer a supportive hand to anyone navigating difficult life transitions or isolation.

name: Mykababayan
description:
Located near Fairfield station at 52 The Crescent, Mykababayan is a long-standing hub for the local Filipino community in Sydney's west. Operating as both a sari-sari mini-mart and a casual canteen-style eatery, this local favourite has been serving authentic tastes of home since the 1980s.

On the dining side, customers love the hot home-style dishes served fresh daily from the bain-marie. Popular favourites include rich dinuguan, pork adobo, paksiw na bangus, and beef caldereta, as well as traditional native desserts like halo-halo and fried bananas. The grocery shelves are fully stocked with a wide selection of imported snacks, native cooking ingredients, frozen specialities, and cool drinks directly from the Philippines.

Beyond food, Mykababayan serves as a helpful community centre by providing overseas money transfer and cargo parcel shipping services to the Philippines. The staff are widely appreciated for their warm, attentive welcome, making it a friendly and convenient destination to shop, eat, and connect with heritage.

name: Mykababayan Filipino Dishes & Sari-Sari Store
description:
Mykababayan Filipino Dishes & Sari-Sari Store is a beloved community hub located near Fairfield station in Sydney's west. Operating since the 1980s under its former name, Sans Rival Food Service, this multi-functional venue combines a traditional Filipino mini-mart (sari-sari store) with a casual dine-in and takeaway eatery.

Locals frequent the shop for its authentic, homestyle cooked dishes served daily from a classic bain-marie. Favourites include chicken and pork adobo, beef caldereta, paksiw na bangus, and the highly popular dinuguan (pork in rich blood jelly). For dessert, the store offers a delightful selection of native sweets like banana fritters and ube flan cake.

Beyond dining, Mykababayan specialises in imported Philippine groceries, including frozen foods, snacks, cooking ingredients, and cool drinks. It also supports the local diaspora by providing money remittance and parcel shipping services to the Philippines. The warm hospitality and friendly staff make this tiny shop a true hidden gem for anyone seeking genuine Filipino comfort food and community connection.

name: NARRA Co-operative Ltd
description:
NARRA Co-operative Ltd (National Affiliation of Respectable and Responsible Associations) is a registered Filipino-Australian charity and non-profit organisation based in Campbelltown, New South Wales. Conceived as a co-operative framework to unite the community, NARRA focus on preserving Filipino heritage, supporting local families, and empowering youth. Since its establishment in 2013, the co-operative has actively organised key cultural celebrations, youth mental health advocacy circles, and fundraisers like the annual Pamana Ball.

A central part of NARRA's vision is the establishment of ACI Central (Arts, Culture and Innovation Central), which operates as their community hub on Warby Street in Campbelltown. This active space hosts cultural workshops, art exhibitions, and community meetings. Looking ahead, NARRA is driving a long-term initiative known as PAACI (Philippine-Australian Arts, Culture and Innovation Centre) with the goal of constructing a permanent cultural landmark in Sydney by 2032. Through these initiatives, the co-operative creates a welcoming home away from home for the Filipino-Australian community, fostering belonging and creative collaboration.

name: NamNam BBQ
description: NamNam BBQ is a popular Filipino-style street food eatery operating in Macquarie Fields, Sydney. This local favourite specialise in bringing authentic Pinoy flavours to the community, serving freshly grilled barbecue skewers, hearty rice bowls, and refreshing juices. Diners can enjoy traditional specialities such as pork and chicken skewers coated in a signature sweet and savoury glaze, alongside classic silog dishes like tapsilog and porksilog which feature garlic fried rice and egg. The eatery operates with convenient evening hours throughout the week to cater to the local dinner crowd, offering delivery options via platforms like Uber Eats. On weekends, the business opens earlier in the day to serve customers looking for lunch and dinner options in the Macquarie Fields area. By focusing on simple, delicious comfort food, NamNam BBQ has become a go-to spot for those craving authentic Filipino street food. Whether you are ordering a quick weeknight meal or gathering for a weekend treat, this food stall offers a wonderful taste of the Philippines in Western Sydney.

name: National Affiliation of Respectable and Responsible Associations (NARRA) Co-operative Ltd
description:
The National Affiliation of Respectable and Responsible Associations (NARRA) Co-operative Ltd is a registered charity and cooperative that acts as an umbrella body for numerous Filipino-Australian organisations in New South Wales. Established in 2013, the co-operative focus is on promoting and preserving Philippine arts, culture, and community heritage.

A key highlight of their work is the operation of ACI Central (Arts, Culture and Innovation Central), a dedicated community centre located in Campbelltown. Acquired in 2016 in partnership with Plaza Filipino Inc., ACI Central provides a physical hub where volunteers, members, and local residents gather. The centre hosts cultural exhibitions, educational workshops, and community fundraisers.

Throughout the year, the cooperative organises significant events to celebrate the community, such as the annual Tribute to Mothers and Fathers and the Ambassador for Culture and Arts recognition nights. By bringing people together, the organisation helps to build a supportive network and strengthen ties between Australia and the Philippines.

Located at Shop 4, 3-9 Warby Street in Campbelltown, ACI Central is a welcoming space for anyone interested in experiencing or supporting Filipino culture. It runs purely on the dedication of volunteers and community supporters.

name: National Affiliation of Respectable and Responsible Associations Co-operative Ltd
description:
NARRA Co-operative Ltd is a dedicated Filipino-Australian community organisation based in Campbelltown, New South Wales. Established in 2013, this not-for-profit, member-driven group works to unite the local Filipino-Australian community, celebrate Philippine heritage, and foster shared progress.

A central focus of the co-operative is ACI Central (Arts, Culture and Innovation Central), a cultural hub located on Warby Street in Campbelltown. This centre serves as a welcoming home away from home, hosting various cultural workshops, exhibitions, and community gatherings. The co-operative organises popular annual events, including the Pamana Ball and Sto Niño Festival, and runs community programs such as the PAACI Next-Gen Circle for youth.

Through collaborative efforts and fundraising drives like their Buy a Brick campaign, the group aims to establish a permanent multicultural performing arts and community centre. The co-operative is run by active members and volunteers who support its charity initiatives and local programs. Open from Monday to Sunday, the team is always happy to assist with general, events, or membership inquiries.

name: New Life Sydney
description:
New Life Sydney is a welcoming Christian church community situated in North Rocks, in the north-western suburbs of Sydney. Meeting at Davies Hall on North Rocks Road, this active congregation focuses on providing a Christ-centred environment where individuals and families can grow in their faith and experience warm fellowship. While the church has a special commitment to supporting members of the Filipino-Australian community who have settled in Sydney, it extends a warm welcome to people from all cultural backgrounds.

Services are held every Sunday at 10:00 AM, offering a vibrant space for worship, prayer, and community connection. To help visitors attend, the church organises a convenient shuttle bus service between Epping Station and the church premises before and after the service. Attendees appreciate the friendly and supportive atmosphere, making it a great place to connect with others. Whether you are new to the area or looking for a local faith community to call home, New Life Sydney offers a supportive and inclusive environment to explore and practice your faith.

name: New Life Sydney Christian Church
description:
New Life Sydney Christian Church is a welcoming and vibrant faith community situated in Toongabbie, in the western suburbs of Sydney. Associated with New Life Philippines, this Christ-centred church focuses on supporting and welcoming individuals and families who have moved to Australia, helping them build a spiritual and relational home in Sydney.

The congregation gathers for its weekly Sunday worship service from 10:00 am to 11:30 am. These services are held at the Girls' Brigade NSW building, located at 3 Junia Avenue in Toongabbie. Sunday services are followed by a warm lunch fellowship, providing a wonderful opportunity for community members to connect and share together.

In addition to Sunday worship, New Life Sydney Christian Church hosts weekly prayer huddles and connect groups to strengthen members in their faith. The church provides a safe, encouraging space for fellowship, pastoral care, and spiritual growth. All visitors, neighbours, and friends are warmly invited to join, regardless of background. The community is dedicated to sharing the teachings of the Bible and making a positive, life-changing impact in the local area.

name: Nipa Hut Minimart
description:
Nipa Hut Minimart was a family-friendly mixed business and takeaway shop situated on Queen Street in St Marys, New South Wales. The store specialised in providing the local Filipino-Australian community in Western Sydney with a variety of authentic groceries and convenient food options. It was designed to function similarly to a traditional Philippine sari-sari store, offering customers easy access to essential ingredients for home-cooked meals.

The shop's shelves were stocked with popular pantry items, including soy sauces, vinegar, seasoning mixes, and canned goods. Customers could also browse a selection of dry goods, snacks, and sweets imported directly from the Philippines. In addition to grocery items, Nipa Hut Minimart offered hot takeaway food, providing convenient quick-meal options for busy residents. It served as a familiar gathering point for the local community to connect and find a taste of home. This business has permanently closed.

name: Noemis Cafe
description:
Noemis Cafe is a welcoming local spot in Padstow, Sydney, known for serving delicious Filipino breakfasts, pastries, and excellent coffee. Located on Gibson Avenue, this warm cafe opens very early in the morning. It is a favourite spot for early risers and commuters looking for a quick bite or a hearty start to their day.

Customers love the generous portions and budget-friendly prices. The menu features traditional breakfast options, including classic silog meals, alongside popular local choices like bacon and egg rolls. Visitors also rave about the smooth coffee, the clean environment, and the variety of food selections available for both dine-in and takeaway.

The outstanding customer service is a true highlight of the cafe. The owner, Noemi, is known for greeting everyone with a friendly smile, a kind word, and a warm conversation. The space is clean, well-organised, and offers wheelchair-accessible entry, making it comfortable and accessible for all guests. It is a fantastic spot to experience authentic Filipino flavours and a friendly neighbourhood atmosphere.

name: Northern Beaches Pentecostal Church
description:
Northern Beaches Pentecostal Church is a friendly, multicultural Christian congregation meeting in Forestville. Led by Pastor Don Modarelli, this active community focuses on sharing the life-changing gospel of Jesus Christ.

Visitors and regular attendees appreciate the warm, welcoming atmosphere and the supportive community. The church provides an engaging Sunday School program and creche for children, allowing families to participate in the Sunday morning worship and praise services. Beyond Sunday gatherings, the church specialises in teaching biblical doctrine through various mid-week activities. These include face-to-face Tagalog Bible studies on Tuesday evenings, Wednesday night Bible college courses, and adult Zoom study groups on Wednesday and Friday nights. The church also runs the Revolution Youth group on Friday nights, providing dynamic fellowship opportunities for younger members.

Many in the congregation speak of the spiritual support, healing, and guidance they receive. The leaders and members travel across the region to minister to others, fostering a close-knit environment. Whether you are looking for a place of worship in the Northern Beaches or seeking to connect with the local Filipino community, this church offers a supportive environment to grow in faith.

name: Oi Manila Food Hall
description:
Oi Manila Food Hall is an authentic Filipino dining spot and sari-sari grocery store located in Doonside, Sydney. Local food lovers highly recommend their signature charcoal-grilled pork barbecue skewers, flavoursome chicken inasal, and sizzling pork sisig. Guests frequently rave about the authentic dipping vinegar, which pairs perfectly with the grilled mains, as well as the refreshing calamansi juice made with real fruit. 

The menu also features popular Filipino comfort classics like hot sinigang soup, crispy liempo, and traditional street foods like pork intestines (isaw) and quail eggs on a stick. Along with serving hot meals, the food hall stocks an excellent selection of popular Filipino snacks, sweets, and pantry essentials for those looking to cook at home. 

With a small, cozy indoor dining area, this family-run business offers a warm and welcoming carinderia-style experience. The friendly, hospitable staff and nostalgic vibe make it a must-visit spot in Western Sydney for anyone craving a taste of the Philippines.

name: Ola, Marijo
description:
Ola, Marijo was a professional cleaning service provider based in North Strathfield, Sydney. Operating as a dedicated group of experienced Filipino cleaners, the team specialised in delivering high-quality residential and commercial cleaning solutions across the local area. Their services covered a wide range of property types and sizes, including general house cleaning, deep cleaning, and end-of-lease services for apartments, units, and offices.

The cleaners took great pride in their punctual, trustworthy, and detail-oriented approach to trade cleaning. They offered comprehensive support for everyday chores, such as sweeping, mopping, dusting, surface wiping, window cleaning, and trash removal. To ensure a seamless experience for their customers, the team supplied all their own professional equipment and cleaning products. They were also highly flexible, offering to adjust the strength of chemicals or avoid harsh agents entirely upon request to suit individual household needs and health preferences.

Their cleaning services were highly accessible, offering one-time bookings, same-day emergency cleans, and regular recurring maintenance schedules. This business has permanently closed.

name: One In Christ Church Sydney
description:
One In Christ Church (OIC) Sydney is a vibrant Christian fellowship in Emerton, serving the Filipino-Australian community in Greater Western Sydney. Members and visitors frequently praise the church for its hospitable, friendly, and joyful atmosphere, describing the congregation as a welcoming spiritual family that beautifully displays the heart of Jesus.

The fellowship offers a range of services designed to support spiritual growth, including Sunday worship services, Tagalog mass fellowships, regular prayer meetings, and interactive Bible studies. Led by Bishop Jessie Malibiran and Pastor Efren Lozano, the church focuses on promoting a positive Christian lifestyle and providing pastoral care to its members.

Conveniently located in a commercial centre on Popondetta Road in Emerton, the church provides an accessible space with wheelchair-friendly entry. Whether you are seeking a place for weekly worship or looking to connect with the local Filipino-Australian community, OIC Sydney offers a supportive environment to grow in faith. Services are held every Sunday starting at 9:30 AM and run until 2:00 PM.

name: Our Lady of Czestochowa Queen of Poland, Marayong
description:
Our Lady of Czestochowa, Queen of Poland Catholic Church is a vibrant parish centre located on Quakers Road in Marayong. Also known as the Polish War Memorial Chapel, this church features a unique, unconventional architectural design. It is highlighted by spectacular stained-glass windows and a serene interior for quiet reflection. Outside, the beautifully maintained grounds include a touching tomb monument under the flight path of local planes.

The church is renowned for its warm, welcoming atmosphere. Parishioners and local nuns are always happy to engage in friendly conversation with visitors. While the parish deeply cherishes its Polish heritage, it serves a multicultural congregation with services held in both English and Polish.

For the local Filipino-Australian Catholic community, the church hosts a monthly Filipino Mass. This is held on the third Friday of each month at 8:00 PM. It is a cherished gathering place that brings the community together. Visitors can also experience traditional events where Polish foods like pączki are shared, fostering a close-knit community spirit. Whether you visit for mass, prayer, or cultural connection, this chapel stands as a peaceful sanctuary in Western Sydney.

name: Our Lady of Dolours Catholic Parish Chatswood
description:
Our Lady of Dolours Catholic Parish in Chatswood is a vibrant and welcoming community hub in the Diocese of Broken Bay. Located just a short walk from Chatswood Metro station, the church features elegant architecture, a peaceful foyer, and a lovely courtyard. The active congregation is highly community-oriented, led by passionate priests who foster a warm and respectful atmosphere.

Parishioners and visitors appreciate the calm environment, which houses unique historical relics and a comforting statue of Our Lady of Dolours. The parish is known for its diverse ministries and groups, including a monthly youth group for young adults. A major highlight is the dedicated Filipino Cultural Mass, held on the first Sunday of every month at 12:00 pm. This special service is supported by the Filipino Catholic Chatswood Parish (FCCP), which hosts social activities and religious events.

The parish is well-organised, offering multiple Sunday services, children's liturgies, and midday weekday masses. Visitors often gather for tea and coffee in the courtyard after the 10:30 am Sunday mass, making it easy to connect.

name: Our Lady of Fatima Catholic Church
description: Our Lady of Fatima Catholic Church in Kingsgrove is a vibrant and welcoming parish that serves as a cornerstone for the local multicultural community, including many Filipino-Australian families. Parishioners highly value the warm, family-like atmosphere of the congregation, where everyone is made to feel welcome. The church has a strong connection to the Filipino community, notably through the historical guidance of its beloved Filipino parish priest, Father Hamilton 'Casey' Ureta, whose spiritual leadership and homilies have provided comfort and connection. The parish hosts regular liturgical services, including daily Mass, Eucharistic Adoration on Fridays, and Reconciliation services on Saturday mornings. Active community participation is central to the church, with members enjoying the dedicated choir and engaging parish events, such as the annual Feast of Our Lady of Fatima. Located in a highly accessible spot in Kingsgrove, the church is just a short walk from the train station and local bus stops. It features a spacious church building, well-maintained facilities, and a convenient car park situated directly at the rear. Whether attending Sunday Mass or participating in community events, visitors appreciate the peaceful setting and the diverse, friendly community that makes this parish a special place of worship.

name: Our Lady of Lourdes Catholic Church, Seven Hills
description:
Our Lady of Lourdes Catholic Church is a warm and welcoming parish centre located at 7 Grantham Road in Seven Hills. Part of the Diocese of Parramatta, the church serves as a key spiritual and community hub for local residents and the vibrant Filipino congregation in Western Sydney.

Parishioners highly value the friendly and supportive atmosphere of the church. Father Henry is particularly well regarded for celebrating engaging masses and taking the time to chat with attendees afterwards. The parish is known for its beautiful services and strong sense of community fellowship.

In addition to regular weekly services, the parish offers specialised groups. These include the Lourdes Luminate Young Adults and the Our Lady of Lourdes Junior Youth Group, which help connect the younger generation. The church also collaborates closely with the Sisters of the Holy Family of Nazareth to host Eucharistic Adoration and fellowship evenings. For families, the parish maintains a close relationship with the adjacent Our Lady of Lourdes Primary School, fostering a supportive environment for Catholic education and local childcare services.

name: Our Lady of Mount Carmel Catholic Church
description:
Our Lady of Mount Carmel Catholic Church in Wentworthville is a welcoming and diverse parish under the care of the Carmelite Fathers and Sisters. Located at the corner of Bennett and Garfield Streets, the church serves a vibrant, multicultural community that includes an active Filipino parishioner group. Warm volunteers and priests greet visitors at the door, fostering a family-like atmosphere where people feel at home.

Parishioners particularly appreciate the parish's serene environment, friendly community, and the spiritual leadership of the Carmelite clergy. The church building recently underwent interior improvements, featuring a renovated altar, a modern sound system, and comfortable padded kneelers. The parish is also known for its talented choir and uplifting music programme, which greatly enhances worship services. Beyond regular Masses, the church accommodates family sacraments such as Baptisms and maintains strong ties with the adjacent primary school. It remains a central place of worship, faith, and connection for local Catholic families in Western Sydney.

name: Our Lady of Mount Carmel Catholic Parish Varroville
description:
Our Lady of Mount Carmel Catholic Parish in Varroville is a welcoming church community located in South-West Sydney. Managed by the Carmelite Missionaries, this parish is known for its warm, prayerful atmosphere and strong multicultural engagement. It maintains an active connection with the local Filipino community by hosting devotional events, including an annual parish fiesta and the Flores de Mayo celebration.

Parishioners highly appreciate the beautiful grounds and excellent facilities. The church features an air-conditioned parish hall equipped with a kitchen and dry bar, which is popular for community gatherings, weddings, and funerals. There is also ample parking on-site and along nearby streets. Helpful parish staff are always on hand to assist with audio-visual equipment.

The parish offers a comprehensive weekly liturgical schedule. Weekday Masses are celebrated at 9:00 am on Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, and Friday, and at 7:30 pm on Thursday. Weekend services include a Saturday morning Mass at 9:00 am, a Vigil Mass at 6:00 pm, and Sunday Masses at 9:30 am and 5:00 pm. Special devotions also take place regularly, such as Saturday Reconciliation from 11:00 am, a monthly Healing Mass on the first Saturday, and the Alliance of the Two Hearts Mass and Vigil on the first Friday of the month.

name: Our Lady of Victories Horsley Park
description:
Our Lady of Victories is a welcoming Roman Catholic parish situated in Horsley Park, serving the local faithful under the Archdiocese of Sydney. The church building itself is widely appreciated for its beautiful, spacious interior, striking imagery, and excellent air conditioning, which provides comfort during large gatherings. The property also features a lovely forecourt and ample parking spaces for parishioners and guests.

Visitors and local families frequently select this scenic venue for significant life events, such as weddings and funerals. Many appreciate the supportive parish priest and accommodating office staff who assist in organising these celebrations. However, some wedding groups note that strict timing constraints are enforced by the on-day caretakers during ceremonies.

As a central hub for the multicultural local community, the parish hosts regular services throughout the week, including Rosary recitals and Holy Mass. A notable highlight is the dedicated monthly Filipino Mass, celebrated on the fourth Sunday of the month at 11:00 AM. This service provides a wonderful opportunity for the Filipino-Australian community to gather, pray, and connect in a shared spirit of faith.

name: Our Lady of the Rosary Cathedral (Hornsby Cathedral Parish)
description:
Our Lady of the Rosary Cathedral is the cathedral parish of the Catholic Diocese of Broken Bay, located in the leafy suburb of Waitara. Serving as a central spiritual home, the cathedral is renowned for its welcoming and peaceful atmosphere. The building itself features a unique modern design, featuring a large basalt stone wall that symbolises the diverse community coming together as living stones.

For the local Filipino-Australian community, the cathedral is a cherished gathering place. It hosts a dedicated Filipino (Tagalog) Mass on the second Sunday of every month at 3:30 pm. These monthly services, often referred to as Filipino Day, are known for their warm and intimate feel. Following the Mass, parishioners enjoy sharing free snacks and socialising, fostering strong community connections.

Visitors appreciate the serene surroundings, ample parking, and family-friendly amenities, including a dedicated toddler room for families with young children. Regular weekend Masses are held with a Saturday Vigil at 5:00 pm and Sunday services at 8:00 am, 9:30 am, 11:00 am, and 6:00 pm. Weekday Masses are also offered daily. Whether attending the Tagalog Mass or joining the regular services, the parish offers a supportive environment that serves as a true haven of light.

name: Our Lady of the Rosary Catholic Cathedral
description:
Our Lady of the Rosary Catholic Cathedral in Waitara is a vibrant parish and the seat of the Catholic Diocese of Broken Bay. Serving the Hornsby region, this welcoming centre hosts a variety of religious services, including weekday and weekend masses. The cathedral is well known for its warm and friendly environment, making it a peaceful sanctuary for personal reflection and community gathering.

The local community highly values the supportive atmosphere and the dedicated parish priests who lead the services. Visitors appreciate the beautiful architecture and serene surroundings, which feature convenient on-site parking. For families, the cathedral includes a dedicated children and toddler room, ensuring a comfortable space for parents with young kids. 

Our Lady of the Rosary has supported a strong Filipino community since 2001. A highlight for the local Filipino congregation is the monthly Tagalog mass, held at 3:30 pm on the second Sunday of each month. This popular gathering is followed by community fellowship and light refreshments, providing a wonderful opportunity for people to connect and share their heritage. Whether you are attending the regular schedules or the monthly community fellowship, this parish offers an inviting space for worship and connection.

name: Our Lady of the Rosary Church, St. Mary’s
description:
Our Lady of the Rosary Church is a welcoming and vibrant Catholic parish located in St Marys, New South Wales. Serving the local community since 1946, the parish is known for its warm, inclusive atmosphere and active congregation. Visitors often notice the church’s beautiful architecture, featuring striking stained-glass windows that fill the interior with light, and a peaceful prayer room situated at the back.

Parishioners appreciate the helpful parish staff and the friendly, approachable priests, led by Father Brendan. The community is highly active and accommodates people of all ages through various ministries and regular weekday and weekend liturgies. For the Filipino-Australian community, the parish holds a dedicated monthly Filipino Mass on the first Sunday of each month at 11:30 AM, fostering cultural and spiritual connection.

The parish is also highly regarded by visitors and organisers for its accommodating nature, offering support during special family milestones and community events. Whether you are a visitor looking to experience the local community spirit or a lifelong parishioner, this parish offers a supportive sanctuary for worship and fellowship.

name: Our Lady of the Rosary Parish Fairfield
description:
Our Lady of the Rosary Parish is a welcoming and diverse Roman Catholic community located on Vine Street in Fairfield, Sydney. Serving a multicultural congregation, the parish provides a peaceful and spacious sanctuary for worship, prayer, and community gathering.

Parishioners and visitors frequently praise the church for its beautiful, serene interior and the dedicated pastoral care of its priests. The parish hosts regular weekday Masses from Monday to Friday at 9:30 AM, preceded by morning Exposition of the Blessed Sacrament. Weekend worship options include a Saturday morning Mass at 8:30 AM, a Saturday evening Vigil at 6:00 PM, and multiple Sunday services in English, Polish, and Vietnamese. Crucially for the local Filipino-Australian community, the parish hosts a dedicated Tagalog Mass on the fourth Sunday of each month at 11:00 AM. Reconciliation is also available on Saturdays.

The church works closely with the adjacent Our Lady of the Rosary Catholic Primary School, sharing resources and parking facilities during major parish events like weddings and sacraments. With its doors open daily for quiet personal reflection, the parish remains a vital spiritual centre and supportive environment for families in Western Sydney.

name: Our Lady of the Rosary, Kellyville Parish
description:
Located in the rapidly growing north-western sector of Sydney, Our Lady of the Rosary Kellyville Parish is a warm and active Catholic faith community. Under the pastoral care of the Order of Friars Minor Conventual (Franciscan Friars), the parish has catered for the local community for many years. It is highly valued for celebrating unity in diversity, reflecting the multicultural growth of the area. In particular, the parish is home to a vibrant Filipino congregation, hosting a regular Filipino Mass on the first Sunday of every month at 3:00 PM. This is followed by a community fellowship in the parish centre, fostering a strong sense of connection and heritage.

Parishioners and visitors frequently praise the church's recently renovated, light-filled, and beautiful sanctuary. Key highlights include a magnificent altar, an impressive stained-glass wall featuring the Stations of the Cross, and a mosaic of Pope St John Paul II. The precinct also accommodates a primary and pre-school, making it a central hub for family and spiritual development. Regular services include daily morning Masses and weekend liturgies, with the warm, welcoming parish priests and solemn atmosphere creating a favoured place for quiet prayer and community assembly.

name: Filipino Communities Council of Australia
description:
The Filipino Communities Council of Australia Inc. (FILCCA) is the national representative body and peak umbrella organisation for Filipino-Australians. Established in 1990, it is the oldest and largest national organisation of its kind in Australia. The council acts as a key communication channel between the community and both the Australian and Philippine governments, representing the interests of the fifth-largest ethnic group in the country.

FILCCA focuses on advocating for the rights, welfare, and interests of Filipino-Australians. It works closely with state and territory councils to coordinate community initiatives, support services, and cultural events. Key activities include organising the Biennial Filipino National Conference and hosting the national Filipino-Australian Youth Excellence Awards to recognise outstanding contributions in the community.

By fostering unity and celebrating diversity, the organisation helps preserve and promote Filipino culture, heritage, and language across Australia. Whether you want to engage with local support groups or participate in national community conferences, FILCCA serves as the central hub connecting Filipino organisations nationwide.

name: Okasyon Catering Services
description: Okasyon Catering Services is a family-owned Filipino catering and event planning business based in Woodcroft, in Western Sydney. They specialise in creating authentic Filipino culinary experiences for a range of special events, including weddings, birthdays, and community celebrations. Whether organising a small family gathering or a large milestone event, the team helps clients design custom packages and party trays tailored to their needs. Their diverse menu features traditional favourites, including crowd-pleasers like pork sisig, kare-kare, dinuguan, and crispy pork lumpia shanghai. Customers highly recommend their services for the generous portion sizes, exceptional customer service, and authentic Kapampangan flavours that bring back memories of home-cooked meals. By handling all the food preparation and event details, they allow hosts to relax and enjoy the moment with their guests. Please note that the business is temporarily closed for a break from 22 June to 31 July 2026, with regular catering operations and bookings resuming on 1 August 2026.

name: PBA Originals
description:
Established in 2006, Pinoy Basketball Australia Original (PBAO) is a premier community-led sports league and organisation based at the Minto Indoor Sports Centre in South Western Sydney. Operating from 9 Redfern Road, Minto, the group uses organised basketball competitions and family-oriented social activities as a positive platform to promote physical health, social connection, and cultural inclusion among the local Filipino-Australian and broader multicultural communities.

PBAO has built a reputation that extends far beyond basketball, fostering a warm, friendly, and family-like atmosphere where members describe the group as a close-knit family. The committee prioritises welcoming mothers and children, ensuring that everyone feels valued and included. Key highlights of their calendar include popular family days, winter and summer leagues, and Saturday social games. All coordinators and committee members are highly approachable and supportive, helping participants build lifelong friendships.

For registration enquiries regarding their summer or winter competitions, contact the team at pbaoriginals@gmail.com, call 0477 555 160, or visit their website at pbaoriginals.com.au.

name: Our Lady of the Way Catholic Church
description:
Our Lady of the Way Catholic Church is a welcoming parish located in Emu Plains, Western Sydney. Serving the spiritual and community needs of the local area, the church is a central hub for the local Filipino-Australian diaspora, hosting a monthly Filipino Mass on the third Sunday of the month at 11:30 am.

The parish is highly regarded by its members for its warm, family-oriented atmosphere and strong sense of community. Visitors frequently praise the friendly congregation and the caring leadership team who support the wellbeing of the parishioners. The church building features large ceiling fans to keep attendees comfortable during warmer days. Next to the church is the parish primary school, which is well-loved by local families.

Whether you are attending a regular mass, participating in the monthly Filipino service, or seeking spiritual support, the parish provides a peaceful and supportive space. It is a wonderful community centre where people can connect with their faith and build strong friendships with their neighbours.

name: Mrs. Ube
description:
Mrs. Ube was a beloved specialty Filipino cake shop and bakery located in Marayong, in Western Sydney. Founded in 2019, it specialised in authentic, traditional Filipino-inspired desserts and pastries.

The bakery was highly regarded for its purple yam creations, particularly the signature Ube Overload and Ube Macapuno cakes. These cakes featured homemade ube halaya and airy, delicate sponge layers. Customers loved that the cakes and cupcakes were not overly sweet. Other favourites included buko pandan cupcakes, yema cakes, and ube macarons. On Fridays and Saturdays, the shop was especially popular for its freshly baked specialty doughnuts, featuring unique flavours like ube leche flan and calamansi.

The store offered a warm and welcoming atmosphere. It quickly became a favourite local gathering point for those seeking a comforting taste of home or wishing to experience traditional Filipino sweets in Sydney.

This business has permanently closed.

name: Joflow Basketball Academy Kings Park
description:
Joflow Basketball Academy is a professional sports training centre located at Unit 8/360 Vardys Rd, Kings Park, in Sydney's west. Led by a team of passionate coaches, the academy specialises in comprehensive basketball training and player pathway development. They offer structured skills training, development leagues, local competitions, private coaching sessions, and school holiday camps designed to support the growth of players at every stage of their athletic journey. They also provide opportunities for elite players to represent the academy in interstate and international tournaments.

Local families and athletes highly recommend the academy for its family-friendly and supportive environment. Customers love how the coaches foster a positive community where team members encourage one another to improve. Beyond developing on-court techniques and game understanding, the academy focuses on teaching vital life values such as teamwork, resilience, discipline, and sportsmanship. By combining high-performance training with character-driven coaching, the academy helps young players build confidence and grow into active leaders. It is a popular hub for the local Filipino-Australian community and young basketball enthusiasts across Western Sydney.

name: Our Lady of Czestochowa Queen of Poland (Polish War Memorial Chapel)
description:
Our Lady of Czestochowa Queen of Poland, widely known as the Polish War Memorial Chapel, is a Roman Catholic church located on Quakers Road in Marayong. Consecrated in 1966, the chapel was built by Sydney’s Polish community to serve families across New South Wales. The building features a striking modernist design by architect Michael Dysart. Its roof has six distinct peaks that symbolise the six million Polish lives lost during the Second World War. Inspired by the Jasna Góra Shrine and the Sydney Opera House, the structure resembles an eagle in flight when viewed from above, representing resilience and community heritage.

Parishioners and visitors cherish the chapel for its peaceful atmosphere and welcoming community. It serves as a vibrant spiritual sanctuary, hosting daily Mass services in both English and Polish. In addition to serving the Polish community, the chapel supports the local Filipino Catholic community by hosting a monthly Tagalog Mass on the third Friday of each month at 8:00 PM. The beautiful grounds also feature well-maintained outdoor spaces and a touching memorial monument. It remains a cherished landmark in Marayong, offering a serene place for quiet reflection, prayer, and cultural connection.

name: Pabico Club 55
description:
Pabico Club 55 is a popular Filipino restaurant and spacious event venue located at 1 Mount Druitt Road in Mount Druitt, Sydney. Known for its welcoming atmosphere and friendly, accommodating staff, this local favourite specialises in authentic Filipino cuisine. Diner favourites include the comforting pancit molo, as well as a selection of delicious sweet treats like fresh coffee and brownies.

The venue is particularly well-loved by the local Filipino-Australian community for hosting group gatherings, birthday parties, and anniversary celebrations. It features a large stage area where popular local singers like Lily regularly perform, adding vibrant live entertainment to the dining experience. In addition to lively weekend celebrations, the versatile space also hosts regular community gatherings and Christian services, making it a true hub for social and spiritual connection.

Pabico Club 55 is open daily from 10:40 am to 6:00 pm, offering a spacious area that is perfect for families and larger groups. Whether you are dining in for a delicious lunch, grabbing takeaway, or planning a special community event, this friendly centre offers a warm, high-energy slice of the Philippines in Western Sydney.

name: Pabico Fast Food & Asian Groceries
description:
Pabico Fast Food & Asian Groceries is a popular Filipino eatery and grocery store situated inside the Westfield Mt Druitt shopping centre on Carlisle Avenue. As a beloved hub for the local Filipino-Australian community, this venue offers both a convenience shop and a casual dining space.

For those dining in or taking away, the eatery specialises in simple, authentic Filipino dishes served in a home-cooked style. Customers highly recommend classic favourites such as pork sinigang, monggo, and beef nilaga. Many diners share that the traditional flavours remind them of home. The meals are well regarded for their affordability and generous portions.

In addition to ready-to-eat hot food, the grocery section is stocked with a wide range of Filipino ingredients, snacks, and wares. Visitors can purchase popular regional drinks like calamansi juice, soursop juice, and Sarsi soda. Beyond food and groceries, the business also assists the local community by providing cargo and freight forwarding services for balikbayan boxes. Whether you are looking to enjoy a classic comfort meal or stock up on pantry staples, this local business provides a welcoming taste of the Philippines in Western Sydney.

name: Pace Migration & Education Consultancy
description:
Pace Migration & Education Consultancy is an established migration agency located in the heart of Sydney on Bathurst Street. Registered under MARN 0745684, the team provides professional education and migration assistance. They specialise in guiding clients through the complexities of Australian visa applications, including partner visas, student visas, employer-sponsored pathways, and skilled independent visas.

Clients highly value the personalised attention and professional expertise provided by the consultancy. The resident agents, Syed and Anna, are frequently praised for their honest, upfront advice and prompt communication. They ensure that applicants understand all their pathways without any sugar-coating, making a stressful process much easier to navigate.
