Whether you are stopping by for a hearty breakfast or catching up with friends over lunch, this vibrant spot brings authentic Filipino flavours and a welcoming community vibe to the local area.

name: ProSource - Philippine & Australian Virtual Assistant Agency
description:
ProSource is a specialised recruitment and virtual assistant agency based in the heart of Sydney on Barrack Street. The agency connects Australian businesses with highly skilled virtual assistants and professionals from the Philippines. By providing vetted candidates, they help local businesses scale their operations smoothly and cost-effectively.

Client feedback highlights the agency's personalised approach. The team, led by Chris, takes the time to understand each business's unique short-term and long-term goals. Clients appreciate their ability to match businesses with reliable virtual assistants who deliver high-quality work, particularly in areas like social media management.

Contractors also speak highly of their experience with the agency. They feel valued, respected, and well-supported, receiving excellent opportunities to grow their careers. ProSource ensures a mutually beneficial partnership, fostering strong relationships between talented professionals and Australian enterprises. The agency maintains a physical presence in the Sydney Central Business District, bridging the gap between local enterprise demands and offshore talent. Their services are designed to minimise staffing overheads while maintaining high operational standards and compliance.

name: Proper Clean Australia
description:
Proper Clean Australia is a professional, eco-friendly commercial cleaning service based in Burwood, Sydney. Run by Ken and a dedicated team of experienced Filipino cleaners, they specialise in office, warehouse, retail, medical centre, and strata cleaning. They also handle tricky window cleaning jobs for both residential and commercial shopfronts across Strathfield, Marrickville, and the wider Sydney metropolitan area.

Local clients highly praise the team for their punctuality, reliability, and thorough work ethic. Business owners have noted a significant improvement in their workplace hygiene and atmosphere after partnering with this team. They consistently keep clients in the loop regarding timing and quotes, ensuring a hassle-free and transparent service from the preliminary stage to completion.

Operating seven days a week from 8:00 am to 6:00 pm, Proper Clean Australia provides flexible schedules that minimise disruption to daily business operations. For enquiries or to arrange a service, they can be reached directly via phone or email at info@propercleanaus.com.au.

name: Quakers Hill Uniting Church - Filipino Service
description:
Quakers Hill Uniting Church - Filipino Service is a warm and inclusive multicultural Christian congregation located in Quakers Hill, Sydney. Since the 1990s, the local Filipino community has played a vital role in shaping the church's diverse and welcoming fellowship. The community gathers weekly for its main Sunday worship service at 9:30 am, which features Bible readings, prayers, and inspirational messages, alongside dedicated programmes for children and youth. Once a month, the congregation shares in a community Communion service.

Visitors and regulars frequently praise the church for its down-to-earth atmosphere, friendly members, and caring leadership team. The clean, modern facilities are highly valued by the local community for hosting weddings, meetings, and educational activities.

Beyond Sunday services, the church coordinates a variety of mid-week ministries to support families and young people. These include the Noah's Ark Playgroup on Tuesday mornings for younger children, fortnightly Kids Rock sessions on Friday evenings, and the Rock On Youth group for high school students. Older youth and young adults gather on Friday nights for the Reflections Bible study to connect and grow in faith. It is a wonderful community space for anyone looking to connect with a supportive local church family.

name: Quality Dental Care (Dr. Manuelita Contemplacion)
description:
Quality Dental Care is a well-established dental practice in Regents Park, Sydney, that has been serving the local community for over twenty years. Led by principal dentist Dr. Manuelita Nocon, affectionately known as Dr. Yayie, and Dr. Nicole Contemplacion, the clinic specialises in high-quality preventative, general, and cosmetic dental treatments. Patients can access a wide range of services including fillings, deep cleaning, root canal therapy, crowns, bridges, veneers, professional teeth whitening, and Invisalign clear aligners.

The clinic is highly valued by local residents for its gentle and compassionate approach, making it an excellent choice for patients who feel anxious or nervous about visiting the dentist. Visitors frequently praise Dr. Yayie for her exceptionally light touch and precise care when administering local anaesthetic. The practice prioritises patient comfort by taking the time to explain procedures thoroughly, ensuring that individuals feel relaxed and informed at every stage of their treatment. Supported by a friendly and professional team of dental nurses, the clinic utilises modern dental technology to deliver precise and comfortable outcomes. Additionally, the practice offers bulk billing under the Child Dental Benefits Scheme to support families in maintaining excellent oral health.

name: RCG Automotive
description:
Located in Mount Druitt, RCG Automotive is a highly regarded mechanical workshop run by the father-and-son team of Raul and Arvin (also known as Alvin). This family-operated business has established a strong reputation in the local community for delivering honest, practical, and affordable automotive services.

Local customers frequently praise the team for their genuine customer service and transparent advice, ensuring car owners receive reliable recommendations without unnecessary costs. The workshop specialises in a comprehensive range of mechanical repairs, logbook servicing, and vehicle maintenance for all makes and models. They also offer quick and straightforward registration inspections to help get vehicles back on the road with minimal fuss.

Whether you need a routine service or a major repair, the friendly mechanics at RCG Automotive provide a quick turnaround and high-quality workmanship. The workshop is conveniently situated at 6/9 Belmore Ave, Mount Druitt, making it an accessible and trusted choice for the Filipino-Australian community and residents across western Sydney.

name: RS Migration Services
description:
RS Migration Services is a professional immigration and legal consultancy located in Blacktown, Sydney, specialising in comprehensive Australian visa and migration assistance. Led by Solicitor Rinku Sharma and registered migration agent Ramil Saguilon, the agency focuses on providing clear, legal pathways tailored to individual needs.

Clients frequently praise the team for their friendly, patient, and highly professional approach. They are known for providing honest advice and timely updates, which helps keep applicants informed and reassured during what can often be a stressful process. The firm has successfully managed complicated visa files, including reversing past visa refusals, assisting with skills assessments, and handling offshore applications. Their expertise covers a wide range of options such as Subclass 485 Temporary Graduate visas, Subclass 491 Family Sponsored visas, student visas, parent visitor visas, and partner visa applications.

Operating from Main Street in Blacktown, the agency offers both face-to-face appointments and virtual consultations via Zoom and Skype. This flexibility helps them support the diverse local multicultural community in Sydney as they navigate their journeys toward temporary and permanent residency in Australia.

name: Raine's Mini Mart
description:
Raine's Mini Mart is a friendly local Asian grocery shop located in the heart of Rooty Hill, Sydney. This community store specialises in providing a wide selection of convenient snacks, drinks, and everyday essential goods.

Local customers highly recommend the shop for its immaculate customer service and welcoming atmosphere. The shelves are filled with a great variety of popular Asian snacks, different types of ramen, spices, and fresh produce. Whether you are looking for specific ingredients for a home-cooked meal or just want to grab a quick snack, the helpful staff are always ready to assist you.

The shop is open from 9 am to 7 pm, Monday to Saturday, making it easy to visit after work or during the day. It is closed on Sundays. You can find this convenient grocery spot at 10 Rooty Hill Road South, right near the local transport hub, serving the Filipino and broader multicultural community in Western Sydney.

name: Reggie Natividad Hair & Makeup
description:
Reggie Natividad Hair & Makeup is a welcoming Filipino hair salon in Rooty Hill, Sydney. Under the expert care of owner Reggie and his team, the salon has served as a trusted community hub for over a decade.

Local clients highly recommend the salon for its friendly, professional service and warm atmosphere. Many visitors describe it as a little piece of home, where staff offer complimentary snacks and coffee to ensure everyone feels relaxed. The salon is a popular choice for the whole family, catering to adults and teenagers alike with clean-cut styling and quick, quality service.

Specialising in a wide range of hair treatments, the salon offers cuts, colouring, hair straightening, and makeup services. Clients are particularly impressed by their premium treatments, including keratin hair rebonding and hair botox, which keep hair soft and straight even after beach visits. 

Because the salon gets quite busy, booking an appointment in advance is recommended. Whether you are preparing for a wedding or looking for a fresh new style, Reggie and his approachable team provide expert makeovers with authentic Filipino hospitality. You can find them on the second floor of the Kamayan building.

name: Remote Staff
description:
Remote Staff is a well-established recruitment and staffing agency that specialises in connecting Australian and New Zealand businesses with skilled remote professionals from the Philippines. Established in 2007, the company acts as a dedicated bridge, managing the sourcing, compliance, and ongoing integration of offshore talent.

Clients highly praise their structured support model, highlighting the professionalism, reliability, and dedication of the remote team members. The agency's commitment to active support is a standout feature for many businesses, with dedicated account managers providing guidance and ensuring smooth, long-term operations. This supportive approach helps remote workers integrate seamlessly, functioning as valuable extensions of local teams.

Remote Staff offers flexible staffing options, including plug-and-play teams, Employer of Record (EOR) services, and tailored recruitment across diverse roles such as administration, customer support, bookkeeping, and web development. Based at the International Towers in Barangaroo, Sydney, and supported by a major operations centre in Makati City, Philippines, the company has successfully guided over 8,000 remote working relationships. They offer a compliant and transparent pathway for businesses seeking to optimise overheads while accessing high-calibre international professionals.

name: Renew Filipino Ministries
description:
Renew Filipino Ministries is a vibrant community church and outreach initiative based at Doonside Anglican Church in Western Sydney. Led by ministry worker Paolo Santillan, this organisation specialises in welcoming and supporting second-generation Filipino-Australians. The ministry operates under the Sydney Anglican Evangelism and New Churches (ENC) network, striving to share Christian hope and foster fellowship.

The congregation meets for Sunday services at 1:30 pm at the Doonside Anglican Church premises on Cameron Street. Gathering as a tight-knit community, members engage in relational outreach, group discipleship, and community service. Sharing meals and social activities form a central part of their ministry, helping people build genuine connections.

By partnering with local youth and young adult ministries, Renew Filipino Ministries aims to guide young people in their faith journey. The team hopes to replicate this supportive church model across other Western Sydney suburbs with large Filipino populations. Whether you are new to the area or seeking a welcoming faith community, you will find a warm reception here.

name: Respall Migration Australia
description:
Respall Migration Australia is a registered migration agency specialising in helping Filipino individuals, families, and professionals migrate to Australia. With over 25 years of industry experience, the agency is managed by registered migration agents, including principal Jose Respall (MARN 9901644). They provide clear migration advice, written prospects of success, and transparent fee estimates before starting any work.

Clients frequently praise the team for being highly professional, approachable, and supportive throughout their migration journey. The staff are noted for their meticulous attention to detail, guiding applicants step-by-step through complicated paperwork with no hidden fees. The agency has a strong track record of assisting with tourist visas, partner visas, and permanent residency pathways, such as subclass 190 visas. Dedicated team members like Jennifer Lancero, Lorielie, and Jessa are highly regarded for their responsive and accommodating service, keeping clients informed at every stage of the application process.

Based in Sydney, Respall Migration Australia is a trusted service for those in the Filipino-Australian community navigating the complex process of moving to Australia.

name: Reyes Group Australia (Accounting, Taxation, Mortgage and Property Solutions)
description:
Reyes Group Australia is a professional accounting, taxation, and property solutions firm located in Guildford, Sydney. This Filipino-owned practice specialises in helping clients navigate the annual tax return process with ease. Led by May and Acel, the team is highly regarded for providing friendly, dependable, and personalised services that take the stress out of managing finances.

Clients frequently praise the firm for its quick and efficient processes. Newcomers to Australia especially value the team's patience in explaining complex local tax rules, which helps them feel confident and informed. The firm focuses on clear, tailored advice to help maximise refunds and save clients both time and money.

Beyond tax preparation, Reyes Group Australia offers valuable support for property finance and mortgages. They go above and beyond to guide clients through the process of securing their first investment properties. With a reputation for being approachable and easy to talk to, this local business has built strong relationships, with many clients returning year after year. Whether you need assistance with individual tax lodgements or complex property solutions, they provide reliable support in a welcoming atmosphere.

name: Rhoshell Food Products
description:
Rhoshell Food Products was a well-regarded Filipino food manufacturer and wholesale supplier based in Blacktown, Sydney. The family-run business specialised in authentic Filipino meat products and traditional bakery items, serving as a trusted supplier for both retail outlets and local families. Long-term customers and business partners frequently praised their friendly service and the high quality of their goods, noting that doing business with them was always a pleasant experience.

Their product range was designed to bring the genuine flavours of the Philippines to the Australian table. Key meat offerings included the popular Pampanga Gold pork longanisa, which was available in both native and skinless varieties, as well as succulent pork tocino. In addition to savoury meats, they manufactured classic Filipino bakery goods such as Rhoshell ensaymada, a buttery and fluffy sweet pastry topped with cheese. These traditional delicacies were distributed through retail stores and online platforms, including Pinoy Warehouse, allowing kababayans across Sydney to enjoy a taste of home.

This business has permanently closed.

name: Ria Panlasang Pinoy Australia
description:
Ria Panlasang Pinoy Australia is a Filipino community organisation located in the suburb of Rockdale, within the greater Sydney area. This group functions as a local cultural association, with a focus on connecting the Filipino-Australian community and fostering cultural ties in New South Wales.

As a community group, the organisation aims to provide a welcoming network for individuals of Filipino heritage and those interested in Filipino culture. It serves as a helpful point of contact for local residents in Rockdale and the surrounding southern Sydney suburbs who wish to connect with the Pinoy community.

Fostering strong community support, cultural preservation, and mutual assistance is central to the group's presence in Australia. By providing a platform for communication and shared experiences, it helps Pinoy residents feel at home in Sydney. Whether you are seeking cultural connection or looking to engage with the local Filipino diaspora in Sydney, this organisation supports community integration and communication. Ria Panlasang Pinoy Australia helps maintain cultural roots, supports local initiatives, and encourages community collaboration in the local Sydney region.

name: Rizal Park Movement of Campbelltown
description:
The Rizal Park Movement of Campbelltown is a dedicated Filipino-Australian community organisation based in the Campbelltown region. Established formally in 2007 after decades of community advocacy, the group aims to promote the rich legacy and cultural heritage of the Philippine national hero, Dr. Jose Rizal.

The movement is best known for its successful campaign to create Rizal Park in the suburb of Rosemeadow. The park features a stunning five-metre bronze statue of Dr. Jose Rizal, created by the famous sculptor Eduardo Castrillo. Unveiled in 2012 by former Philippine President Benigno Aquino III, this monument serves as a central symbol of pride and connection for the local Filipino community.

Locals and visitors highly appreciate the park as a welcoming space for family outings, morning jogs, and quiet reflection. The area includes children's play equipment, walk-ways, undercover seating, barbecue facilities, and public toilets. The Rizal Park Movement of Campbelltown regularly collaborates with the Campbelltown City Council and the Philippine Consulate General in Sydney to host annual commemorative events. These events include Philippine Independence Day celebrations and wreath-laying ceremonies that bring the community together to honour their shared history and cultural roots.

name: Rj & Lhen Balloons Events Company
description:
Rj & Lhen Balloons Events Company is a family-owned event planning and balloon decoration business based in Prospect, Sydney. The company specialises in custom balloon artistry, custom backdrops, and creative party styling for birthdays, baby showers, grand openings, and surprise events. Managed by Gina, RJ, and Ate Lhen, the team is highly regarded by the local community for their friendly service, clear communication, and meticulous attention to detail.

Customers frequently praise the team's ability to bring complex themes to life, such as construction-themed birthdays and nostalgic Filipino fiesta celebrations featuring custom-made bahay kubo centrepieces, jeepneys, and sorbetes carts. Whether designing elegant backdrops with added floral touches or installing vibrant balloon garlands, they tailor every detail to the customer's vision. Guests and hosts alike appreciate their quick installation and professional service. Operating 24 hours a day, Rj & Lhen Balloons Events Company provides reliable, high-quality event styling services across the Sydney region, helping families and businesses create beautiful and unforgettable memories.

name: Roll & Refresh
description:
Roll & Refresh is an upcoming neighborhood market and cafe located at 6/24 Main St in Blacktown. This new local spot is set to specialise in a unique blend of Filipino groceries, fresh cafe treats, and healthy drinks.

For the local community and fans of Filipino cuisine, the store will offer a dedicated selection of imported pantry staples, sauces, popular snacks, and frozen favourites. Alongside the retail market, visitors can enjoy a relaxed cafe environment serving fresh coffee, pastries, and quick bites. The menu is planned to highlight delicious options, including fresh banh mi rolls with savoury fillings. Visitors can also enjoy refreshing, cold-pressed sugarcane juice and vibrant açaí bowls topped with fruit and granola.

The team is currently finalising site preparations for the official launch. This venue will be a welcoming space for Blacktown residents to shop, eat, and connect. It brings a modern, fresh approach to the local area, offering everyday essentials alongside tropical treats in a friendly setting.

name: Rolling Express
description:
Rolling Express is a dedicated freight forwarding service. They specialise in door-to-door shipping of balikbayan boxes and cargo from Australia to the Philippines. The team helps families stay connected by offering a reliable and professionally managed logistics service. They handle everything from pickup to final delivery. They carry standard boxes and larger cargo requests, including shipping containers, ensuring items are securely transported.

Customers frequently praise the service for its friendly team and safe handling. Many clients share positive experiences of their packages arriving in excellent condition. Deliveries go to various locations, including Manila, Cebu City, and Davao del Sur. The team is known for providing clear communication throughout the transit process. Sea shipments generally take between four and ten weeks.

While most clients experience a seamless service, a few have noted occasional delays. However, the majority of the community recommends them for their care and helpful customer support. For bookings, tracking, and inquiries, you can reach Rolling Express directly at 0478 500 881. You can also contact them via email at rollingexpressqld1@gmail.com.

name: Rosehill Filipino Congregation of Jehovah's Witnesses
description:
The Rosehill Filipino Congregation of Jehovah's Witnesses is a welcoming Christian fellowship that meets at the Granville Kingdom Hall, located at 85 South Street in Granville, New South Wales. This local congregation specialises in Tagalog-language worship, Bible study, and community activities, providing spiritual support for the Filipino community in Western Sydney.

Visitors and members appreciate the warm, peaceful, and family-like atmosphere where individuals from diverse cultural backgrounds gather. Weekly services focus on discussing Bible teachings with active audience participation. Foyer attendants are friendly and helpful, ensuring that everyone feels comfortable and welcome. The Kingdom Hall facility features excellent accessibility, including wheelchair-accessible entrances, wide aisles, doorways, and dedicated toilets.

The congregation holds its midweek services on Friday evenings at 7:30 PM and weekend services on Sundays at 5:00 PM. All meetings are free to attend, and no collections are ever taken. For those wishing to learn more or find resources, information is available on their official website at jw.org.

name: Royals Basketball
description: Royals Basketball is a Western Sydney-based non-profit sports organisation and basketball coaching club. The club is highly valued for its unique pathways, connecting grassroots basketball in Australia with official national programs in the Philippines through its partnership with the Samahang Basketbol ng Pilipinas (SBP). They specialise in comprehensive player development and mentorship for young athletes. Key services include weekly group trainings, one-on-one playmaker sessions, junior skill programs starting from age five, and sports conditioning at their Rouse Hill facility. The club facilitates team placements for local competitions across Hills, Stanhope, and Hornsby, and organises touring teams for national and international tournaments. Headquartered in Western Sydney, they hold weekly team training sessions at the Blacktown Leisure Centre in Stanhope Gardens, with additional sessions in Rhodes and Nirimba Precinct. They offer welcoming pathways for athletes of all skill levels to develop their game, cultivate leadership, and embrace a growth mindset. Families appreciate their focus on sportsmanship, positive coaching, and building human connections.

name: Ruby's Special Kakanin
description:
Ruby's Special Kakanin specialises in authentic, homemade Filipino desserts and traditional rice cakes based in Botany, Sydney. Bringing the rich culinary heritage of Pampanga to Australia, this local business prepares classic delicacies fresh to order. Customers can order customised platters for special celebrations, featuring popular favourites such as sapin-sapin, cassava cake, and suman malagkit. Other highly requested selections include kutsinta, palitaw, bibingkang malagkit, puto, and pichi-pichi.

Locals highly recommend these desserts for their authentic flavours that taste exactly like the traditional delicacies found back home. Reviewers frequently praise the excellent quality, reasonable pricing, and friendly, professional service. The business operates on a strict pre-order model to ensure maximum freshness for every batch. Because every item is homemade, customers need to provide at least twenty-four hours of notice for standard orders. For larger party platters, a notice period of three days is required.

The business caters to the Sydney metropolitan area and offers a convenient delivery service for community events and birthdays. Customers can place their orders and make enquiries directly by sending a direct message via the official Facebook page, which has built a loyal following of over four hundred and eighty supporters.

name: Ryan Works Mobile Mechanic
description:
Ryan Works Mobile Mechanic is a trusted mobile mechanical service based in Glendenning, Western Sydney. Operated by Hazel Legaspi and lead mechanic Ryan, they bring professional automotive repairs and maintenance directly to the customer's doorstep. They service a wide range of vehicles, including cars, motorcycles, forklifts, and golf carts.

Local clients frequently praise Ryan for his prompt response, punctuality, and clear communication. Customers appreciate his honest pricing, high-quality workmanship, and thorough diagnostics. He has extensive expertise with various car brands, including Honda and Volkswagen. Ryan is also known for handling repairs on short notice and following up with clients after a service to ensure their vehicles continue to run smoothly.

The business offers minor and major logbook servicing, brake repairs, suspension work, and specialised DPF cleaning. Their fully equipped mobile workshop covers Western Sydney, including Blacktown, Parramatta, and Penrith. Operating hours are Monday to Friday from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM, with Saturdays available by appointment. This friendly and reliable service makes vehicle maintenance stress-free for the local community.

name: SBC Foods Pty Ltd
description:
SBC Foods Pty Ltd is a dedicated importer and distributor of authentic Filipino food products, located in St Marys, Sydney. The business serves both wholesale and retail customers, making it a valuable hub for anyone looking to source genuine ingredients from the Philippines. They specialise in bringing a wide range of Filipino pantry favourites to the local Australian market.

Their extensive catalogue features popular Filipino snacks, dry goods, frozen items, and essential toiletries. Whether you are a local supermarket looking to stock your shelves or an individual customer craving familiar tastes from home, SBC Foods provides access to high-quality imports. They work closely with local convenience stores and supermarkets to distribute trusted brands across the region.

Located at 5/14 Bent Street in St Marys, the warehouse provides a convenient pickup point for bulk orders and wholesale partners. The friendly team is committed to helping the local Filipino-Australian community stay connected to their heritage through food. You can easily place orders through their official website or contact them directly to enquire about their product availability.

name: SINAG (WSU Filipino Student Society)
description:
SINAG, also known as the Filipino Student Society of Western Sydney University (WSU), is a vibrant student-led organisation based in Parramatta. The society focuses on bringing Filipino-Australian students together, promoting cultural heritage, and building a supportive student community across WSU campuses.

Members value the club for its engaging social events, cultural celebrations, and collaborative activities. A key highlight of their calendar is participating in the annual Tulong Liga, a popular Filipino student-led sports charity tournament in New South Wales where they compete alongside other local university groups to raise funds for charitable causes. The society also actively participates in campus-wide initiatives such as Diversity Fest and friendly inter-club trivia competitions.

Whether you want to connect with fellow students, celebrate Filipino traditions, or participate in exciting sports and social gatherings, SINAG offers a welcoming environment for everyone. You can easily get in touch with the team or register for membership through the university's student portal or by contacting them directly via email.

name: SINAG Filipino Student Society
description:
The Filipino Student Society at Western Sydney University (FSS WSU), also known as SINAG, is a vibrant student-run organisation that serves as a welcoming hub for Filipino and Filipino-Australian students, as well as anyone eager to explore the rich culture of the Philippines. Operating primarily across WSU campuses, including Parramatta, the society specialises in cultivating meaningful friendships and fostering a strong sense of community.

Members can participate in a diverse calendar of social events and cultural initiatives throughout the academic year. Highlights include the annual Tulong Liga charity sports tournament, which raises support for organisations like BANTU Philippines, and the popular Tambayan Tunes karaoke nights held in collaboration with other university societies. Whether joining online chats via Discord or attending on-campus gatherings, students find a supportive network to share their heritage and celebrate local traditions. The club is an excellent way for students to connect with their culture and build lasting relationships during their studies in Sydney.

name: SINAG Filipino Student Society (Western Sydney University)
description:
SINAG Filipino Student Society is a vibrant, registered student club at Western Sydney University (WSU). They foster a supportive community for students sharing a passion for Filipino culture. Operating on WSU's WesternLife platform, the society focuses on cultivating friendships and celebrating Filipino arts, traditions, and heritage.

Members enjoy a variety of social gatherings. These include the welcome event Salu-Salo, featuring traditional Filipino food and games. Fostering strong connections across the state, SINAG regularly collaborates with other New South Wales university societies. They co-host exciting joint events like Tambayan Tunes karaoke nights at the UNSW Roundhouse. They also join social cruises like Tides and Tagay departing from Man O' War Steps.

The society also encourages community service. They participate in student-led charity sports events like Tulong Liga at the Stanhope Blacktown Leisure Centre. Students can connect via their active online community on Discord or at on-campus O-Week meetups. SINAG provides a welcoming space for anyone to learn about the culture and build lasting friendships.

name: Sacred Heart Catholic Church Mount Druitt South
description:
Sacred Heart Catholic Church is a welcoming and serene parish located at 9 Ropes Creek Road in Mount Druitt South. Serving as a vital spiritual centre for the local community, the church is known for its warm and friendly parishioners who make visitors feel at home, welcomed, and understood. Parishioners appreciate the solemn homilies and peaceful environment, which provide a supportive space for personal prayer and family worship.

The church hosts regular weekday and weekend liturgies, including Saturday evening vigils and Sunday morning masses. The parish features inclusive community updates and recently updated facilities that bring a fresh feel to the congregation's worship.

For the Filipino-Australian Catholic community in Western Sydney, the church offers dedicated community liturgies. These include a Filipino Community Mass celebrated on the fourth Sunday of each month at 6:00 p.m., as well as a monthly mass on the third Friday at 8:00 p.m. Led by welcoming parish clergy and active volunteers, Sacred Heart Catholic Church provides a supportive environment for families to gather, grow in faith, and celebrate their shared cultural and spiritual heritage.

name: Sacred Heart Catholic Church, Cabramatta
description:
Sacred Heart Catholic Church is a vibrant multicultural parish located in the heart of Cabramatta, New South Wales. This welcoming community centre specialises in serving a diverse range of local groups, including a very active Filipino congregation that gathers for fellowship and a monthly Filipino Mass held on the fourth Sunday of each month. 

Parishioners and visitors frequently praise the church for its beautiful architecture, peaceful atmosphere, and uplifting choir music. The parish is known for its warm, easygoing environment where people from all walks of life come together for prayer and reflection. Families appreciate the close connection to the local primary school and the dedicated care provided by the parish sisters and helpers. 

Alongside the monthly Filipino fellowship, the church offers regular weekend Masses in English, Vietnamese, and Polish, catering to the rich cultural heritage of the Cabramatta area. The parish office is open from Tuesday to Friday to support the community. Whether you are attending a weekend Mass, a community wedding, or seeking a quiet place to pray, Sacred Heart Catholic Church provides a supportive spiritual home for everyone.

name: Saint Paul's Catholic Church (St Paul the Apostle, Parish)
description:
St Paul the Apostle Parish in Winston Hills is a welcoming Catholic church within the Diocese of Parramatta. Perched at the top of a hill, the parish offers visitors peaceful surroundings, beautifully maintained gardens, and spectacular views of the city and valley. The community is known for its warm and friendly atmosphere, guided by supportive and humble priests, including Filipino parish priest Father JP Escarlan.

Members of the congregation particularly appreciate the active parish life, which includes a wide range of ministries and devotional groups. The parish celebrates personal milestones by offering special blessings for birthdays and wedding anniversaries. Additionally, the community hosts regular morning teas and shared lunches after Mass, fostering a strong sense of connection and fellowship among parishioners.

For convenience, the church features a spacious, multi-purpose parish centre, a large parking lot, and modern tap-and-go donation options. In addition to daily and weekend liturgies, the parish supports the local Filipino-Australian community by hosting a dedicated Tagalog Mass on the second Sunday of each month at 11:00 AM.

name: Saint Therese Catholic Church
description:
Located at 15 Garrong Road in Lakemba, Saint Therese Catholic Church is a welcoming and active spiritual hub serving the local community. The church is known for its warm and friendly congregation, bringing together people from diverse cultural backgrounds, including Vietnamese, Lebanese, and Filipino-Australian families.

Attendees highly value the peaceful and clean worship environment, which is perfect for quiet prayer and Sunday family reflection. The church features beautiful grounds, a lovely garden that has undergone recent renovations, and serves as a popular venue for local weddings.

To cater to its multicultural community, Saint Therese hosts regular English services as well as specialized chaplaincy services. There is a Saturday Vietnamese Vigil Mass at 4:00 PM and an English Vigil Mass at 6:00 PM. Sunday Mass is celebrated at 9:00 AM. For the Filipino-Australian community, a dedicated Tagalog Mass is hosted on the first Sunday of each month at 3:00 PM. Regular reconciliation services are held on Tuesday and Wednesday evenings from 6:30 PM to 7:00 PM, and on Saturday mornings and afternoons.

This parish provides a wonderful space for worship, reflection, and community connection in the Canterbury-Bankstown region.

name: Salon De Anne - Hair and Beauty Studio
description:
Salon De Anne is a welcoming, family-run hair and beauty studio situated in Berala, New South Wales. Led by experienced stylist Ate Anne and her daughter Maria, the salon specialises in a wide array of hair and beauty treatments. Local clients highly recommend their expert hair cuts, styling, and professional colouring services, particularly their ability to blend grey hair seamlessly into highlighting tones.

The studio is also popular for advanced hair care solutions, including nanoplasty treatments that leave hair feeling exceptionally soft, silky, and rejuvenated. Beyond hair styling, Salon De Anne offers essential beauty services such as precise eyebrow shaping, upper lip threading, and relaxing pedicures.

Clients consistently praise Ate Anne for her warm, friendly manner and easy-going conversations. She takes the time to understand each client’s preferences, ensuring high-quality, personalised results without ever rushing. The salon is celebrated for its lovely vibes, comfortable atmosphere, and reasonable pricing. Whether you are seeking a complete style transformation or a routine trim, this Berala studio provides a reliable and pleasant experience that keeps locals returning.

name: Sans Rival Food Service
description:
Sans Rival Food Service, also affectionately known by locals as My Kababayan, is a popular Filipino grocery and takeaway eatery located in the heart of Fairfield. Operating as a classic sari-sari store and casual dining spot, it offers a warm welcome and a little taste of home to the local Filipino community. 

The shop is famous for its daily selection of hot, authentic home-style viands served from a traditional bain-marie. Customers frequently enjoy hearty dishes such as dinuguan, beef stew, and a variety of native sweets. For larger family gatherings or festive occasions, the kitchen specialises in preparing big trays of party food made to order, including special Noche Buena meals. 

Beyond the hot food counter, the store is packed with a wide range of Philippine products. Visitors can browse shelves stocked with favourite snacks, cooking ingredients, cool drinks, and frozen goods imported directly from the Philippines. The shop also serves as a convenient community hub, offering reliable overseas money transfer and package remittance services. While some diners note that the pricing is slightly high, most agree that the authentic flavours, extensive grocery range, and helpful service make it a beloved local centre for the community.

name: Santo Niño Association of Campbelltown and Macarthur
description:
The Sto Niño Association of Campbelltown and Macarthur (SNACM) is a vibrant Filipino-Australian community organisation based in the Macarthur region of New South Wales. This unincorporated association is dedicated to preserving cultural heritage and fostering religious devotion, particularly around the traditional Feast of Santo Niño.

Local members and devotees appreciate the group's active role in bringing families together and supporting their cultural identity. The association regularly participates in collaborative community initiatives across Greater Sydney, including regional events and educational competitions alongside partners such as Lakas Filipina and the Campbelltown Region Filipino Community Council. Through these diverse activities, they promote connection, dialogue, and mutual support among Filipino-Australians.

For anyone in Campbelltown and the Macarthur area looking to connect with their heritage or participate in traditional festivities, the organisation provides a welcoming and highly supportive environment. Devotees and community members can engage with their activities, stay updated on their programme of events, and keep track of upcoming gatherings through their official Facebook page.

name: Sari-Sari Store ni Tatay
description:
Sari-Sari Store ni Tatay is a local home-based Filipino grocery and convenience store located in Marsden Park, Sydney. This welcoming shop specialises in bringing a taste of the Philippines to the local community in the Greater Western Sydney region.

Visitors can find a wide range of authentic imported Filipino goods, pantry staples, and traditional snacks. The selection includes popular items such as soy sauces, vinegar, sweet delicacies, and unique cooking ingredients essential for preparing classic Pinoy meals at home. Whether you are looking to cook traditional dishes like adobo or sinigang, or simply seeking your favourite childhood snacks, this store serves as a helpful local resource.

The shop operates on weekends, opening from 9:00 am to 9:00 pm on both Saturdays and Sundays, while remaining closed during the week. This convenient weekend schedule allows local residents and families to drop by and stock up on their essential groceries. Sari-Sari Store ni Tatay provides a friendly, familiar shopping experience for the Filipino-Australian community and anyone interested in exploring Filipino flavours.

name: Saving Grace Christian Church
description:
Saving Grace Christian Church (SGCC) is a community-focused Pentecostal church located in the suburb of Glendenning, in Sydney's outer west. Dedicated to supporting the local Filipino-Australian community and the wider region, the church offers regular Sunday worship services, bible study groups, and a dedicated Sunday school program for children.

Visitors and congregation members appreciate the church for its sound biblical teaching and its warm, welcoming, and family-oriented atmosphere. SGCC is deeply committed to community welfare, running outreach and community feeding initiatives that provide essential support, such as clothing, fresh food, hygiene items, and household goods, to international students, the unemployed, and local families facing financial difficulties. Additionally, the church offers compassionate spiritual care and practical support services, including Christian wedding ceremonies and child baptisms.

Conveniently located at Unit 2, 171-173 Power Street, Glendenning, Saving Grace Christian Church provides a safe and uplifting space for spiritual growth, fellowship, and practical community support in Western Sydney.

name: Seven Hills Family Medical Centre
description:
Seven Hills Family Medical Centre was a bulk-billing general practice located in the Seven Hills Plaza Shopping Centre in Seven Hills, Sydney. The clinic offered comprehensive GP and family medicine services, specialising in caring for the local community with dedicated, multilingual care. It was particularly well-regarded by the local Filipino-Australian community for its Tagalog-speaking general practitioners, Dr Lorna Benedicto and Dr Boanerges Relativo.

Patients who attended the clinic frequently praised the medical staff for their thoroughness, compassion, and high-quality clinical care. Dr Relativo was often highlighted for going above and beyond for his patients, while Dr Benedicto was highly valued for her focused and gentle approach, ensuring that health issues were caught early and treated. The clinic also provided essential nursing support, which included administering immunisations and vaccines. While some patients noted longer waiting times and mixed administrative experiences with the reception desk, the professional care from the doctors remained a highlight for many families over the years. 

This business has permanently closed.

name: Señor Jesus Nazareno Sydney Australia Group
description:
The Señor Jesus Nazareno Sydney Australia Group is a dedicated Filipino-Australian Catholic devotion community based at the Good Shepherd Catholic Church in Hoxton Park. Established in 1995, this community group has fostered a deep spiritual connection for local devotees for over three decades. The devotion was initiated when a life-sized replica of the Poong Nazareno (the Black Nazarene) was brought to Western Sydney directly from the historic Quiapo Church in Manila, Philippines.

Each year in January, the group organises the annual Feast of the Black Nazarene, which coincides with the traditional Traslación festivities in Manila. The event is a central highlight for the local Filipino-Australian community, featuring a solemn procession, Mass, and a warm community fellowship that brings families together. This long-standing tradition has become a cherished part of the multicultural fabric of Western Sydney, with the devotion being actively passed down and sustained through generations of kababayans. It offers a welcoming space for both lifelong devotees and those wishing to experience this vibrant expression of faith and culture close to home.

name: Shellabs Filo Food
description:
Shellabs Filo Food is a home-cooked catering service specialising in authentic Filipino cuisine, based in Bankstown, Sydney. The business focuses on preparing traditional, home-style meals for local families and events. By providing personalised food orders, Shellabs Filo Food brings the rich and comforting flavours of the Philippines directly to the local community in the Canterbury-Bankstown region.

Customers appreciate the convenience of pre-ordering fresh, home-cooked dishes, which makes it easy to enjoy traditional Filipino fare. The service accommodates custom bookings for small gatherings, family meals, or community events. Typical menu offerings include classic Filipino favourites, prepared with care using traditional recipes and ingredients. 

Operating as a home-based catering business, Shellabs Filo Food allows customers to arrange orders directly. This setup ensures that each meal is cooked fresh to order. For anyone in the local Sydney area looking to experience authentic, home-cooked Pinoy hospitality, this local service offers a reliable option for delicious catering and custom food orders.

name: Sherpa Super Tax
description:
Sherpa Super Tax, situated in St Marys, is a professional accounting and tax advisory firm that has been serving the local community for over twenty years. Formerly known as L. D. Ceniza & Co., the practice is led by Gyalzen Sherpa and Leo D. Ceniza, a registered migration agent. They specialise in business advisory, superannuation funds, payroll services, and tax planning and preparation.

Clients highly recommend their services, frequently praising the team's professional, friendly, and supportive nature. Local business owners appreciate their expertise in restructuring sole traders into company formats and setting up trust funds. The practice is known for its paperless, customer-focused approach, making tax season stress-free. The office is located at 18 Queen Street and is open Monday to Friday from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM, with Saturday consultations available by appointment. For those seeking strategic business guidance or assistance with tax returns, Sherpa Super Tax provides a strategic and approachable partnership.

name: ShipLite
description:
ShipLite is a flat-rate air cargo service specialising in shipping parcels from Australia to the Philippines, designed specifically to support the Filipino-Australian community. Operating from a central Sydney warehouse located in Smithfield, ShipLite offers a regular weekly flight schedule to Manila, providing a much faster alternative to traditional sea freight.

Customers can easily drop off their packages at the Smithfield depot or send their shipments from anywhere in Australia using Australia Post or local couriers. The service features clear, flat-rate pricing tiers up to 30 kg, ensuring absolute transparency with no hidden fees or complex sign-ups. Once cargo arrives in Manila, ShipLite works with a robust local courier network to deliver packages nationwide across Metro Manila, Luzon, Visayas, and Mindanao.

Community feedback highlights ShipLite as one of the most affordable and reliable air cargo options, with customers praising its fast delivery times and safe handling of precious cargo (padala). With a friendly team ready to assist, ShipLite makes connecting with family and friends back home simple and worry-free.

name: ShipLite
description:
ShipLite is a specialised cargo and freight service based in Sydney that makes sending parcels from Australia to the Philippines simple and worry-free. Operating from their warehouse in Smithfield, they specialise in flat-rate air cargo services designed specifically for the Filipino-Australian community.

Customers appreciate their fast and reliable weekly flights to Manila, which ensure that balikbayan boxes do not sit in storage for long periods. The service is highly regarded for its transparent pricing model, which features flat rates for packages up to thirty kilograms with nationwide delivery across the Philippines included. With no hidden fees or complicated sign-up processes, sending care packages to family and friends back home is straightforward.

Senders can choose to drop off their boxes directly at the Smithfield warehouse, post them, or arrange for a local Sydney Metro pickup. A friendly, Filipino-speaking customer support team guides customers through every step, including customs clearance and logistics restrictions on items like gadgets or batteries. Whether sending gifts or small business orders, ShipLite provides a safe and cost-effective connection to Luzon, Visayas, and Mindanao.

name: ShippingCart Australia
description:
ShippingCart Australia is a reliable cargo forwarding and logistics service that helps the Filipino-Australian community send packages back home to the Philippines. Operating from their newly relocated warehouse facility in Smithfield, Western Sydney, the service specialises in both air and sea freight options. Customers can shop online across various Australian retailers and direct their purchases to the Smithfield depot, where the team provides convenient parcel consolidation and secure storage solutions.

The service is highly regarded for its easy-to-use mobile application, which allows users to track their shipments in real time and manage their consolidated boxes. While air freight offers quick transit times, sea cargo provides a more economical choice for larger boxes, though users should prepare for longer transit windows. As a service backed by the trusted logistics network of LBC, it offers peace of mind and secure handling for precious packages (known as Balikbayan boxes). The Smithfield warehouse is open for package acceptance and processing from Monday to Friday, between 9:00 AM and 5:00 PM, providing a structured schedule for managing shipments.

name: Shopinoy Merrylands
description:
SHOPinoy Merrylands was a dedicated Filipino grocery shop located at Unit 5, 10-16 Mason Street in Merrylands, New South Wales. The store specialised in importing and retailing a comprehensive variety of authentic products directly from the Philippines. Its offerings included a wide range of pantry essentials, traditional ingredients, sweet snacks, local drinks, and health and beauty items.

The business was highly regarded by the local community for its exceptionally friendly service and welcoming atmosphere. Customers frequently praised the owner for being kind and helpful during their visits. In addition to the warm customer care, shoppers appreciated the store's very affordable pricing, noting that it made authentic Filipino groceries accessible. Many visitors highlighted that the shop was a reliable place to find unique, hard-to-find items and their favourite childhood treats that other grocery stores did not stock. It served as a vital hub for community members looking to recreate the tastes of home.

Although this local sari-sari store was a favourite shopping destination for the Filipino-Australian community in the Western Sydney region, the business has permanently closed.

name: Sir Manong
description: Sir Manong is a welcoming Filipino restaurant located in the Eastern Creek Quarter shopping centre in Western Sydney. The eatery specialises in serving a modern twist on authentic, traditional Filipino cuisine. It provides a warm and relaxed dining atmosphere that is particularly popular for family celebrations. Diners highly recommend the signature crispy pata, which features perfectly crispy skin and moist, tender meat. Other favourite dishes include the flavourful pork hash sisig, rich beef kare kare, pancit palabok, and a unique torta topped with dried kale. For those wanting a comforting meal, the restaurant also serves hearty binagoongan and fresh sinuglaw. Guests can round out their dining experience with a solid selection of traditional desserts. The space is decorated with nostalgic motifs like a jeepney, capturing the lively energy of a true Pinoy gathering. Families especially appreciate the children's play area situated just outside the venue. Sir Manong is open daily from 10:30 am until 8:30 pm, making it a convenient spot for both lunch and dinner. Booking in advance is recommended on weekends due to its popularity.

name: Sir Manong Chatswood Chase
description:
Sir Manong Chatswood Chase is a modern Filipino restaurant located on the lower ground level (Level B) of the Chatswood Chase Shopping Centre in Chatswood. The eatery specialises in bringing traditional Filipino flavours into a contemporary dining setting, offering a warm and vibrant atmosphere perfect for family gatherings and casual meals.

Locals highly recommend the signature crispy pata (crispy pork hock) served with vinegar, and the rich beef kare-kare. Other popular menu choices include charcoal-grilled chicken inasal, milkfish parilla, and a unique roasted eggplant torta topped with crispy kale. The dishes are generous and designed for sharing, paired perfectly with garlic fried rice or annatto fried rice, and refreshing juices.

While the restaurant can get busy during peak hours—particularly Sunday lunchtime—the friendly staff work hard to provide warm and welcoming service. Sir Manong is open from 10:00 am to 8:30 pm from Monday to Thursday, and from 9:30 am to 8:30 pm from Friday to Sunday. It is a fantastic spot on the North Shore for anyone craving authentic and comforting Filipino food.

name: Sizzling Fillo
description:
Sizzling Fillo was a long-standing Filipino restaurant located directly in front of the Lidcombe railway station in Sydney's inner west. Operating since 2009, this popular dining spot specialised in authentic Filipino comfort food served in a colourful and cosy space. The venue was well-known for its lively atmosphere, complete with a traditional karaoke setup that made it a favourite destination for group gatherings and special celebrations.

Local diners and visitors from further afield highly praised the restaurant for its traditional culinary offerings. The signature crispy pata was particularly celebrated for its perfectly crunchy texture and flavourful preparation. Other popular menu highlights included the sizzling sisig, savoury chicken adobo, pancit, and fragrant garlic fried rice. The restaurant earned a strong reputation for its boodle fights, which offered a communal banquet experience where guests enjoyed various meats, fish, rice, and vegetables spread across banana leaves. Guests also enjoyed classic desserts such as halo-halo and leche flan, and often highlighted the warm hospitality and friendly service provided by the staff.

This business has permanently closed.

name: Skin & Beauty Studio by Mildred
description:
Skin & Beauty Studio by Mildred is a professional boutique beauty salon located in Rooty Hill, in the heart of Western Sydney. Nestled in a key hub for the local Filipino-Australian community, this studio specialises in a wide range of advanced skincare treatments, dermal therapies, and hair removal services.

Mildred offers personalised skincare solutions tailored to individual needs. The studio's comprehensive service list includes custom facial treatments, chemical peels such as the K-Glow peel, LED light therapy, nano infusion, and cellulose masks. Additionally, clients can book specialised cosmetic procedures including blemish removal, Sterex Diathermy, milia removal, and facial warts removal. The salon also provides professional laser hair removal services.

The studio operates from Tuesday to Saturday, opening from 9:00 am until 5:00 pm. It remains closed on Sundays and Mondays. Clients can easily view available times and book their appointments online through the salon's integrated Square booking system. For enquiries, customers can connect directly via phone or WhatsApp. With its welcoming atmosphere and focus on healthy, radiant skin, this studio is a fantastic choice for professional beauty care in the Rooty Hill area.

name: Skinita
description:
Skinita, recently renamed Sunny Corner Cafe, is a welcoming Filipino cafe and restaurant located in Doonside, right opposite the train station. Under its friendly new management, this cosy eatery has become a popular spot for locals looking to enjoy authentic Filipino street food and comfort dishes.

Specialising in traditional breakfast dishes, the menu features a range of hearty silog meals. These include tapsilog beef, bangsilog milkfish, and cornsilog corned beef, all served with garlic rice and fried eggs. Guests also highly recommend their mouth-watering pork sisig, classic pork barbecue skewers, and stir-fried pancit noodles. For those looking for warming comfort food, the cafe serves delicious Tagalog favourites such as authentic lomi and lugaw rice porridge.

To finish a satisfying meal, customers can choose from refreshing drinks and desserts. Popular options include calamansi juice, iced coffees, and a generously sized halo-halo. The cafe features a dog-friendly outdoor patio and a relaxed indoor dining area. It is a wonderful local venue for families and friends to gather. Whether you are stopping by for takeaway or sitting down for lunch, the service is exceptionally warm. The friendly staff make every customer feel right at home.

name: Smoky Cravings Campsie
description:
Smoky Cravings Campsie is a popular casual Filipino restaurant located on Beamish Street in Campsie, Sydney. It specialises in authentic charcoal-grilled barbecue and classic street food. Customers regularly praise the friendly and helpful staff, as well as the welcoming, family-friendly dining environment.

The menu is famous for its sweet, smoky, and perfectly caramelised pork and beef skewers. Diners also love the tender and flavourful chicken inasal, which is often served with adobo rice. Other popular menu options include the crispy pork liempo, sizzling kare-kare, Filipino-style spaghetti, and crunchy spring rolls. For adventurous food lovers, the restaurant also serves traditional street foods like balut and quail eggs. To finish the meal, patrons highly recommend the special halo-halo, a beloved traditional dessert featuring sweet shaved ice and ice cream.

Whether you are dining in or ordering takeaway, Smoky Cravings provides a comforting taste of home. It brings the vibrant flavours of the Philippines straight to Sydney's inner west.

name: Smoky Cravings Kings Cross
description: Smoky Cravings Kings Cross is a friendly Filipino barbecue restaurant located in the Kings Cross Centre at Potts Point, Sydney. It specialises in authentic Pinoy charcoal-grilled street food and traditional desserts. Local patrons highly recommend trying their signature pork, chicken, and lamb skewers, which feature a sweet and savoury marinade. Other popular menu options include the grilled pork liempo served with vinegar, chicken inasal, and flavourful adobo rice. To finish the meal, customers love ordering the classic halo-halo dessert, which features a rich and delicious ube paste. The service is warm and welcoming, with friendly staff creating a pleasant dining atmosphere. The restaurant is open daily from 11:00 am to 9:00 pm, making it a convenient spot for both lunch and dinner. Whether you are dining in or grabbing a quick takeaway, it is a favourite destination for those seeking authentic Filipino comfort food in the heart of the city.

name: Smoky Cravings Parramatta
description:
Smoky Cravings Parramatta is a popular casual diner specialising in authentic Filipino barbecue and traditional street food. Located on Victoria Road in North Parramatta, the eatery sits just above the Coles supermarket, offering convenient parking spaces for dine-in and takeaway customers.

Local diners love the restaurant for its rich charcoal-grilled flavours and generous portions. The menu features classic Filipino favourites, including tender pork skewers, crispy pork belly (liempo) combo meals, goto, and crispy pata. Regular visitors highly recommend the crispy kare-kare and the kwek-kwek. For larger groups, they offer a substantial food tower combo that comes with garlic rice and warm soup. To finish the meal, patrons can enjoy refreshing cold desserts such as special halo-halo and mais con yelo.

The diner provides a welcoming atmosphere with a sink for washing hands. Friendly staff members like Erik and Ivan frequently receive praise for their helpful, accommodating service. Smoky Cravings Parramatta is open daily from 11:00 am to 9:00 pm, offering a true taste of Philippine street food culture to Western Sydney residents.

name: Smoky Cravings Potts Point
description:
Smoky Cravings in Potts Point is a popular local spot for authentic Filipino street food and charcoal barbecue. Located inside the Kings Cross Centre, this casual eatery offers a warm and welcoming atmosphere where diners can enjoy traditional flavours at a budget-friendly price.

The menu features a variety of classic dishes, with the sweet and salty soy-glazed barbecue skewers being a clear favourite among regulars. Visitors also highly recommend the grilled pork liempo paired with vinegar, chicken inasal, and traditional adobo rice served with a side of hot soup. For those seeking authentic street food delicacies, the kitchen serves kwek kwek and isaw manok, cooked to perfection. The dining experience is rounded out by their signature halo-halo, a layered dessert that provides a refreshing end to any meal.

Run by a friendly team known for their warm hospitality, Smoky Cravings provides a quick, satisfying lunch or relaxed dinner option. Whether you are a local resident, a visitor from another city, or someone looking to explore Filipino cuisine, this cosy branch is an excellent choice for a genuine taste of the Philippines in the heart of Sydney.

name: Smoky Cravings Tempe
description: Smoky Cravings in Tempe is a popular Filipino-Australian food-truck-style eatery and restaurant situated on Princes Highway. Known for its lively street food atmosphere, the venue offers a relaxed space featuring foldout tables and chairs where diners can enjoy authentic charcoal-grilled skewers. The menu highlights classic Filipino street eats, including tender pork, beef, lamb, and chicken skewers, pig's ears, and intestines, all cooked fresh to order. Other favorites include the crispy pork knuckle (Crispy Pata), adobo rice, kwek kwek, and traditional sio mai. To top off the meal, customers highly recommend their ube-loaded halo-halo, which is generous with leche flan, though reviews of the dessert vary. Diners consistently praise the friendly, hospitable staff and the welcoming, fresh vibe of the location. While the food is packed with flavor and captures the nostalgic kanto vibes of the Philippines, some customers have noted minor inconsistencies, such as dishes occasionally not being hot enough for winter or portions being slightly small on certain offal items. Overall, it serves as a fantastic hangout spot for anyone craving quality Filipino barbecue in Sydney's Inner West.

name: Sol Eco Cleaning
description: Sol Eco Cleaning is a professional commercial cleaning service based in Darlinghurst, Sydney. Operating as a dedicated team of Filipino and Latin American professionals, they bring high standards, an exceptional work ethic, and deep community care to every project they undertake. The business specialises in preparing studio apartments and Airbnb units between guest visits, ensuring that the spaces are left completely immaculate. Clients highly value their promptness, efficient workflow, and clear communication, with services extending to deep cleaning, making beds, and delivering detailed reports after each visit. Beyond holiday letting preparation, the company also cleans luxury retail venues, hospitality spaces, and office complexes across the metropolitan area, ensuring a pristine environment for staff and customers alike. Known for their reliability, the team operates from Monday to Sunday between 9:00 AM and 5:00 PM, making it easy to accommodate busy commercial schedules. With their eco-friendly approach and focus on custom-tailored cleaning plans, they provide a reliable choice for local Sydney businesses seeking top-tier sanitation and maintenance.

name: Sol Eco Cleaning Services
description:
Sol Eco Cleaning Services is a Darlinghurst-based cleaning company specialising in eco-friendly commercial and residential cleaning across Sydney. Operated by a dedicated team of Filipino and Latin professionals, the business brings a unique blend of cultural pride, care, and attention to detail to every cleaning project.

The team specialises in preparing Airbnb properties between guest stays. Their comprehensive turnover service includes deep cleaning, fresh bed-making, and detailed status reports for hosts. Beyond short-term rentals, they also service local office buildings, restaurants, and luxury retail spaces throughout the metropolitan area.

Clients frequently praise the company for their reliable service, prompt arrival, and exceptional cleaning standards. Their commitment to using non-toxic, eco-friendly products ensures all spaces are safe, hygienic, and pristine. With clear communication and customisable cleaning plans, the business is a trusted partner for Sydney property owners seeking sustainable and meticulous property maintenance. You can contact them on 0416 883 647 or via email at info@solecocleaning.com.au to discuss your cleaning needs.

name: Southwest Sydney Filipino Australian Basketball Association
description:
The Southwest Sydney Filipino Australian Basketball Association (SSFABA) is a registered non-profit community organisation dedicated to bringing the local Filipino-Australian community together through the love of sport. As an active incorporated association in New South Wales, SSFABA organises regular basketball competitions and community tournaments designed for players of various skill levels.

The association regularly hosts its weekly games and seasonal tournaments at the Fairfield Youth and Community Centre. These events serve as a fantastic hub for physical fitness, sportsmanship, and social connection. The group focuses heavily on youth development, family participation, and cultural inclusion, creating a welcoming space for everyone in the Southwest Sydney region.

Local participants appreciate the well-structured match play and the family-friendly atmosphere at their events. By coordinating seasonal leagues sponsored by local businesses like Right Wealth, SSFABA continues to promote active lifestyles and strengthen community ties. Whether you want to play competitively, join social matches, or support the local teams, the association provides a wonderful opportunity to connect with the vibrant Filipino-Australian sporting community in Sydney.

name: Sovereign Grace Church Parramatta
description:
Sovereign Grace Church Parramatta is a welcoming, gospel-centred Christian congregation gathering weekly in North Parramatta. Led by Senior Pastor Riley Spring, this diverse community brings together people from many different cultural backgrounds, including a dedicated Tagalog-speaking Life Group fellowship that meets on Sunday afternoons.

Services are held every Sunday morning at 10:00 am at Parramatta East Public School on Gaggin Street. Attendees can expect a warm and friendly atmosphere, faithful Bible-centred preaching, and worship music that blends traditional hymns with modern instruments. The church is known for its strong community feel, where visitors are treated with kindness and can enjoy excellent coffee and tea facilities before and after the service.

Families will find a safe and nurturing environment for their children. The church runs highly regarded children and youth ministries, featuring an excellent kids program designed to support family discipleship. For those who are exploring the Christian faith or have questions about God, the community regularly hosts courses and dinners in a relaxed setting. It is a wonderful spiritual home for anyone looking to connect and grow with a genuine local church family in the Parramatta area.

name: St Aidan's Catholic Church, Rooty Hill
description:
St Aidan’s Catholic Church in Rooty Hill is a vibrant and welcoming parish within the Diocese of Parramatta. Initially established with Irish and Maltese roots, this community-centred parish has evolved to embrace a strong, majority-Filipino culture. Parishioners and visitors value the warm, friendly atmosphere and the inspirational homilies delivered by the caring priests.

The church features a well-maintained and fully air-conditioned sanctuary, complete with padded kneeling stools for comfortable prayer. Worshippers can attend weekday Masses on Mondays, Tuesdays, Thursdays, and Saturdays at 9:15 am, and on Wednesdays and Fridays at both 9:15 am and 7:00 pm. Weekend services include a Saturday Vigil at 6:00 pm and Sunday Masses at 7:30 am, 9:00 am, 10:30 am, and 5:30 pm. Sacramental programmes and ministries are run in partnership with the adjacent St Aidan’s Primary School.

Beyond worship, the parish facilitates community fellowships in a large, modern multi-function hall equipped with air conditioning, audio systems, a stage, and full kitchen facilities. Conveniently located near local shops and Rooty Hill railway station, St Aidan’s Parish offers a harmonious spiritual home where diverse cultures unite in faith.

name: St Aidan's Catholic Parish
description:
St Aidan's Catholic Parish is a vibrant, welcoming multicultural parish located in Rooty Hill. It serves as a spiritual and community hub for a diverse congregation, with a long-standing and deep connection to the local Filipino-Australian community. Originally served by the late Father Renato Paras, the first official Filipino Catholic Chaplain in Australia, the parish continues its active evangelising mission.

Worshippers highly value the warm, friendly atmosphere and the care shown by the parish priests and assisting nuns. Visitors frequently praise the inspirational homilies and the caring nature of the leadership team. The parish offers daily Holy Masses, regular weekday and weekend services, reconciliation, and adoration. Its facilities are well-equipped, featuring comfortable seating with padded kneeling stools and reverse-cycle air conditioning to ensure comfort during worship. Additionally, the parish features a fully air-conditioned multi-function hall with complete kitchen facilities, providing an ideal space for community fellowship and events.

Located on Adelaide Street, the church is easily accessible by public transport. It is situated just a short fifteen-minute walk from Rooty Hill railway station and is close to local shops and schools, including St Aidan's Primary School.

name: St Anthony of Padua Catholic Church
description:
St Anthony of Padua Catholic Church in Austral is a welcoming parish community in Sydney's south-west, led by Parish Priest Father Ronnie Maree and Assistant Priest Father Adrian Suyanto. The church features a modern design that is deceptively spacious on the inside, including a dedicated glass-enclosed room where families with young children can comfortably participate in services.

Parishioners highly value the warm and approachable nature of the clergy. Father Ronnie is particularly known for his friendly pastoral care and active presence in the community, including home visits to bless local families. The parish holds regular weekday and weekend Masses, including Saturday Vigil and multiple Sunday services, alongside dedicated times for Eucharistic Adoration and Confessions. As a key gathering point for multicultural groups in the area, the parish also serves the local Filipino-Australian community by hosting a dedicated monthly Filipino Mass on the fourth Sunday of the month at 11:00 am.

Visitors often comment on the clean, airy, and well-designed layout of the church, as well as the ample parking space available on site. Whether you are attending a standard Sunday service, a family celebration, or seeking community connection, St Anthony of Padua Catholic Church offers a peaceful and supportive spiritual home for all.

name: St Anthony of Padua Catholic Church (Toongabbie)
description: St Anthony of Padua Catholic Church in Toongabbie is a warm and inclusive parish community located near the shopping precinct and train station. Nestled in the heart of the local area, this vibrant church serves as a serene sanctuary for worship, quiet reflection, and community gatherings. Parishioners and visitors frequently praise the church for its peaceful atmosphere and the friendly nature of its congregation. The parish is known for its diverse multicultural groups, including an active Filipino community and a dedicated Filipino choir that enhances liturgical celebrations with their music. Regular Mass times are held throughout the week, with weekend services including a Saturday Vigil at 5:30 pm and Sunday Masses at 8:00 am and 10:00 am. The community also hosts a Malayalam Mass at 5:00 pm on the fourth Sunday of each month, and a Tuesday evening Novena to St Anthony at 7:30 pm following the 7:00 pm Mass. Volunteers play a vital role in parish life, offering friendly hospitality by serving hot drinks and biscuits after Sunday Mass. With its convenient location, spacious layout, and welcoming community spirit, St Anthony of Padua Catholic Church is a central hub for spiritual nourishment and local connection in the Toongabbie area.

name: St Anthony's Catholic Church
description:
St Anthony's Catholic Church in Marsfield is a warm and welcoming Catholic parish run by the Vincentian fathers under the Diocese of Broken Bay. Located at 54 Agincourt Road, the church serves as a key spiritual and cultural hub for the local Filipino-Australian community in the Ryde and Marsfield areas. A highlight of their community outreach is a dedicated Filipino Mass, held on the fourth Sunday of every month at 11:00 am.

Parishioners frequently praise the parish for its simple yet beautiful interior, featuring matching wooden furnishings and a peaceful atmosphere. To help those with international backgrounds or those unfamiliar with the service order, the church uses an overhead screen to display the Mass texts. Visitors also note the caring and compassionate nature of the parish priest, Father Greg Walsh, who provides strong support during sacramental preparation and memorial services.

The parish holds regular weekday Masses from Tuesday to Friday at 9:00 am, a Saturday Vigil at 6:00 pm, and Sunday Masses at 9:00 am and 6:00 pm. While onsite parking is limited, ample street parking is available. The church is also equipped with modern audio-visual facilities to support community events and family tribute videos.

name: St Augustine's Church (Brookvale)
description:
St Augustine's Church is a Catholic place of worship located on Alfred Street in Brookvale, New South Wales. Nestled on the Northern Beaches of Sydney, the church works in close association with the Order of St Augustine and the neighbouring St Augustine's College. The community offers a warm, welcoming spiritual home for local residents, including a vibrant Filipino-Australian congregation.

Historically, this sanctuary has served as an important community hub for diverse migrant groups in the area. Today, it continues this tradition of hospitality and spiritual care. To support the devotional life of the local community, the church hosts a regular monthly Filipino Mass in Tagalog. This service takes place on the third Sunday of each month at 11:00 am, bringing families together to share in worship and fellowship.

Beyond Sunday services, the church participates in local outreach and pastoral care. It fosters strong connections with Augustinian missions in the Philippines through solidarity programs. The church provides a peaceful environment for prayer, reflection, and community gatherings on the Northern Beaches.

name: St Brendan's Catholic Church - Filipino Community
description:
Located in the heart of Bankstown, St Brendan’s Catholic Church is a vibrant and welcoming parish serving a diverse multicultural congregation. The church is well-known for its friendly community atmosphere, offering a supportive place of worship in the middle of a bustling suburb. A key highlight for the local Filipino-Australian community is the dedicated Filipino Mass, which is held on the first Sunday of every month at 11:30 am.

Parishioners frequently praise the warm environment and the engaging, humorous homilies delivered by the parish priest, Father Toan Nguyen. The church itself is spacious and features air conditioning, ensuring a comfortable setting for weekly services. Beyond standard worship times, the parish organises various multicultural celebrations, including Lunar New Year masses, and supports a local primary school and out-of-school hours care program.

For those planning to visit, the church is situated at 54 Northam Avenue. While the parish is popular and onsite parking can occasionally be limited, arriving a little early ensures a stress-free visit. Whether you are attending the monthly Filipino Mass or joining the regular weekly services, St Brendan’s offers a friendly and spiritually enriching centre for the whole family.

name: St Felix de Valois Parish Bankstown
description:
St Felix de Valois Parish is a welcoming Catholic church located on Chapel Road in Bankstown, serving a diverse multicultural community. Local parishioners and visitors highly appreciate the parish for its beautiful church building, warm and accommodating atmosphere, and spacious on-site parking.
