The church is led by dedicated priests who support a vibrant youth group and foster an active, inclusive faith community.

The parish hosts regular weekly Masses, including morning services on weekdays and Saturdays, alongside a Saturday evening Vigil and multiple Sunday services. Special language services are also offered, such as a Sunday Polish Mass and a monthly Vietnamese Mass. The church has a strong history of engaging with the local Filipino community, previously hosting events like the Divine Mercy Bankstown Anniversary featuring the El Shaddai Sydney Gospel Choir.

Visitors often praise the beautiful stations of the cross, the inspiring choir singing, and the supportive community spirit. It is a peaceful place for worship, reflection, and community fellowship in Sydney's south-west.

name: St Joachim's Catholic Parish Lidcombe
description:
St Joachim's Catholic Parish in Lidcombe is a welcoming place of worship serving the local community in Sydney's west. Located only a short walk from Lidcombe train station, this historic church is well-known for its active congregation and diverse parish ministries.

Parishioners and visitors frequently praise the parish's perpetual adoration chapel, which offers a quiet and beautiful sanctuary for personal prayer and meditation. The parish also hosts regular events for young adults and runs active sacramental programs. For those driving to services, the church provides a convenient on-site car park, although the surrounding streets can be narrow to navigate.

A central highlight of the parish is its strong connection to the local Filipino-Australian community. St Joachim's proudly hosts major cultural and religious celebrations, most notably the annual Feast of Our Lady of Peñafrancia, which brings people together for novenas, traditional masses, and lively street processions. With its reverent liturgy and supportive community atmosphere, the parish continues to be a central spiritual hub for Lidcombe.

name: St Joachim’s Catholic Church
description:
St Joachim’s Catholic Church in Lidcombe is a welcoming and active parish that serves as a vital spiritual and cultural centre for the local Filipino-Australian community. Originally established in 1926, the parish is celebrating its centenary of service to the Lidcombe community. Parishioners and visitors highly appreciate the peaceful sanctuary, describing it as a beautiful place to meditate, pray, and find quiet reflection.

One of the church's standout features is its Eucharistic Adoration Chapel, which provides a dedicated space for quiet prayer. The parish is also known for hosting events for young adults and having warm, welcoming priests. Although the surrounding streets can be narrow, the church offers a convenient and spacious car park. It is also situated just a short five-minute walk from Lidcombe train station.

The parish hosts key Filipino cultural and religious celebrations, including the annual feast of Our Lady of Peñafrancia and traditional Simbang Gabi dawn masses. Please note that the church building is undergoing renovations. During this period, services are held at both the St Joachim's Parish Hall and nearby St Andrew’s Ukrainian Catholic Church.

name: St John Vianney Catholic Church (Greenacre)
description:
St John Vianney Catholic Church, located in Greenacre, is a welcoming and multicultural Catholic parish that serves as a central spiritual home for the local community. The parish is known for its peaceful and serene atmosphere, offering a quiet space for prayer, reflection, and worship. It is closely connected with the adjacent St John Vianney Catholic Primary School, fostering a close-knit, family-oriented parish community.

As a verified Filipino-affiliated congregation, St John Vianney is particularly cherished by the local Filipino-Australian community. The parish actively supports multicultural ministries and hosts a dedicated monthly Filipino Mass on the fourth Sunday of every month at 11:00 AM, bringing together families from Greenacre, Bankstown, and surrounding suburbs. Visitors and parishioners alike appreciate the community spirit and the opportunity to celebrate their faith and cultural heritage together. The parish office is conveniently located on Rawson Road, assisting with sacraments, community events, and pastoral care throughout the week.

name: St John Vianney Parish
description:
St John Vianney Parish is a welcoming Catholic church located on Cameron Street in Doonside. The parish serves a diverse local congregation, featuring a strong and active Filipino-Australian ministry that enlivens the parish community.

Parishioners frequently praise the church for its beautiful, well-maintained sanctuary and its peaceful, shrine-like atmosphere, which is ideal for quiet reflection and prayer. Visitors appreciate the parish's commitment to traditional Catholic worship and solemnity. Notable practices that parishioners value include the regular use of communion rails and a focus on reverent liturgical rubrics led by devoted priests.

The church holds weekday Masses at 6:30 am from Monday to Friday, alongside evening services on Tuesdays and Fridays at 7:30 pm, and morning Masses on Wednesdays and Thursdays at 9:15 am. Weekend worship includes a Saturday morning Mass at 9:00 am, a Saturday Vigil at 6:30 pm, and Sunday services at 7:30 am and 9:30 am. Reconciliation is offered on Saturdays at 4:30 pm and following weekday Masses. Additionally, a dedicated Filipino (Tagalog) Mass is hosted on the third Sunday of each month at 1:30 pm at the nearby Augustinian Recollect Sisters' Convent on Hill End Road, fostering cultural devotions such as Simbang Gabi.

name: St John of God Catholic Church
description:
St John of God Catholic Church is a welcoming and multicultural parish in Auburn, New South Wales. For over a century, this historic church has served as a peaceful sanctuary and a spiritual home. Under the pastoral care of Father Richard Ddumba, the parish is known for its warm, friendly congregation and inclusive environment.

Parishioners and visitors appreciate the calm and peaceful atmosphere, making it a beautiful place to worship. The community regularly gathers for weekly services. These include morning Masses from Tuesday to Saturday, a Saturday evening Vigil, and Sunday morning services. The parish also hosts Eucharistic Adoration on Thursdays and the first Saturday of each month.

St John of God Catholic Church actively supports its diverse congregation. The parish holds a dedicated monthly Filipino Mass on the fourth Sunday of every month at 11:00 am. This offers a vital space for the local Filipino-Australian community to celebrate their faith and culture. With active volunteer groups and a choir, this vibrant community continues to focus on care and hospitality for all.

name: St John the Baptist Catholic Parish
description:
St John the Baptist Catholic Parish is a warm and welcoming Catholic faith community located in Bonnyrigg Heights, Sydney. Serving local families and visitors, the church specialises in creating a supportive environment for spiritual growth and fellowship. The parish offers a variety of vibrant ministries to engage the community, including youth groups, a men's ministry, adult faith formation, and a charismatic prayer circle.

Parishioners frequently praise the friendly congregation and the beautiful, spacious church building, which provides a peaceful setting for worship. Mass services feature an excellent parish choir that adds a lovely atmosphere to the liturgy. After Sunday masses, the community gathers to share morning tea and snacks, fostering strong parish connections.

As a multicultural centre of worship, the parish is particularly known for hosting a dedicated Filipino and Tagalog Mass on the fourth Sunday of each month at 5:30 PM. This special service is a favourite for the local Filipino-Australian community, who come together for faith and cultural connection. The parish also maintains a close relationship with the adjacent Catholic primary school, making it a central pillar of the local community.

name: St John the Evangelist Catholic Church
description:
St John the Evangelist Catholic Church is an historic parish situated at 35 Cordeaux Street in Campbelltown. Originally established in the early nineteenth century with its foundation stone laid in 1824, the church is a heritage landmark featuring beautiful stained-glass windows and a traditional choir loft. The parish works closely with the adjacent St John the Evangelist Catholic Primary School, creating a family-friendly and welcoming atmosphere.

Parishioners and visitors appreciate the warm, inclusive community and the supportive pastoral staff. The church offers a full schedule of weekly services, including weekday morning Masses at 9:30 am, Saturday evening Vigil at 5:00 pm, and multiple Sunday Masses. Confession and Eucharistic Adoration are also held on Saturday mornings.

A central highlight for the multicultural local community is the dedicated Filipino Mass, celebrated on the second Sunday of every month at 4:00 pm. This service brings together local Filipino-Australian families to worship and celebrate their heritage with vibrant community gatherings. Whether you are visiting for quiet prayer, attending a family service, or connecting with the Filipino congregation, the parish offers a welcoming spiritual home in Campbelltown.

name: St Joseph Catholic Church Belmore
description:
St Joseph Catholic Church in Belmore is a welcoming, multicultural parish serving the local Filipino-Australian community in Sydney. Parishioners appreciate the warm and nurturing environment, particularly for young children experiencing church. The community is described as friendly and supportive, led by welcoming priests who make everyone feel comfortable. The beautiful, historic church building provides a holy and peaceful atmosphere for worship, with many families choosing it for special occasions like christenings.

St Joseph Belmore offers daily morning Mass from Monday to Saturday, alongside a Saturday Vigil Mass and regular Sunday morning services. The parish hosts a dedicated Filipino Mass on the first Sunday of each month at 3:00 PM, and a Tongan Mass on the last Sunday of the month. Other spiritual activities include Wednesday evening Adoration and Benediction, regular Confessions, and sacramental preparation programs that actively engage parents and youth. Centred on Canterbury Road in Belmore, the parish has been a cornerstone of spiritual nourishment and multicultural fellowship for over a century.

name: St Joseph's Catholic Church Filipino Group
description:
Located in Enfield, Sydney, the St Joseph's Catholic Church Filipino Group is a vibrant community ministry that plays an active role in parish life. Parishioners and visitors appreciate the warm, welcoming environment of the church community. They particularly highlight the solemnity of the services and the parish's majestic, immaculate architecture. The community frequently gathers for liturgical celebrations, including a popular nine o'clock Sunday service that features beautiful traditional choir music and inspiring sermons.

Beyond Sunday worship, the group enriches parish life by organising social and cultural activities. These gatherings celebrate Filipino heritage and Catholic faith, bringing a diverse mix of cultures together. Occasional events feature jumping castles for children and shared bring-a-plate community parties to mark church milestones and religious holidays. The parish priests, known for their kindness and professionalism, support these activities. With parking available behind the church and bus stops nearby on Liverpool Road, the church is highly accessible. This dedicated group offers a supportive spiritual home for Filipino-Australians looking to connect, worship, and share their culture.

name: St Joseph's Catholic Church Moorebank
description:
St Joseph's Catholic Church in Moorebank is a vibrant and welcoming parish community serving Chipping Norton, Moorebank, and Holsworthy. Administered by the Somascan Fathers, this bright, spacious church features high ceilings, excellent acoustics, and a simple layout that provides easy wheelchair access. The local community highly values the warm and prayerful atmosphere fostered by the active priests.

A key highlight for the local Filipino-Australian community is the regular Tagalog Mass, which is celebrated on the fourth Sunday of every month at 11:00 AM. In addition to these spiritual services, the church hosts various youth ministries, such as the MooreWorthy Somascan Youth, along with parish gatherings, processions, and cultural events. Visitors frequently note the friendly, family-oriented environment and the supportive parishioners who make everyone feel included. With a large car park and a convenient location on Newbridge Road, the parish offers an accessible, peaceful, and family-friendly spiritual home for the local community.

name: St Joseph's Catholic Church Rockdale (Filipino Community)
description:
St Joseph's Catholic Church in Rockdale is a welcoming spiritual centre for the local community and a key gathering place for Filipino Catholics in Sydney's south. As part of the St Mary MacKillop Parish, this cosy and traditional church is renowned for its friendly, inclusive atmosphere and warm congregational support.

Members of the congregation particularly appreciate the prayerful environment and the dedicated service of the local priests. The church plays a special role in the lives of many families, with many parish members having grown up attending services and serving the parish here.

Weekend services include a Saturday Vigil at 6:30 pm and Sunday morning Masses at 9:00 am (held in Italian) and 10:30 am. A Sunday evening Mass is celebrated at 6:00 pm, which is held in Filipino on the third Sunday of each month. Weekday services are also held on Tuesdays and Thursdays.

Conveniently located at 11 Walz Street in Rockdale, the church is a simple two-minute walk from Rockdale train station. Ample parking is available on the church grounds during weekend services, with entry via Parker Street or Walz Street.

name: St Joseph’s Catholic Church (Rockdale)
description:
St Joseph's Catholic Church in Rockdale is a welcoming spiritual sanctuary located near Rockdale Train Station on Walz Street. As a key part of the St Mary MacKillop Parish, the church serves as an active community centre for a diverse, multicultural congregation. Visitors and long-term parishioners frequently highlight the warm, friendly, and traditional atmosphere of the parish, as well as the dedicated support of its local priests.

The church offers regular holy mass celebrations in multiple languages to support its diverse community. Along with English services, the parish features Sunday Mass in Italian. It also offers a dedicated Filipino Mass on the third Sunday of every month at 6:00 PM, providing a vital spiritual and cultural gathering space for the local Filipino-Australian Catholic community.

Conveniently located just a two-minute walk from the station, the church grounds also offer weekend parking accessible via Parker Street or Walz Street. St Joseph’s Catholic Church remains a cherished spiritual home where multicultural families can grow in their faith and celebrate their heritage.

name: St Kevin's Catholic Church
description:
St Kevin's Catholic Church is a welcoming parish community located in the heart of Dee Why, serving the Warringah Catholic Parish. Visitors frequently admire the stunning heritage-listed architecture, beautiful statues, and peaceful atmosphere. The congregation is known for its warm and friendly welcome, and the church features an angelic choir.

A diverse range of services is offered to cater to the multicultural community. This includes a regular Tagalog Mass on the third Sunday of the month at 11:45 AM, and on the first Saturday of the month at 4:00 PM. There is also a Spanish Mass held every fortnight, and an Italian Mass every Sunday at 10:30 AM. Daily Mass is celebrated at 8:30 AM, preceded by the Rosary at 8:00 AM from Monday to Saturday. Other Sunday services include Masses at 8:00 AM, 9:15 AM, and a dedicated Youth Mass at 6:00 PM.

Conveniently situated just a five-minute walk from the town centre, the church also provides good parking facilities for attendees.

name: St Kevin's Catholic Church (Eastwood)
description:
St Kevin's Catholic Church is a welcoming and active parish located on Hillview Road in Eastwood, Sydney. Serving as a key gathering place for the local Filipino community, it proudly hosts the Epping and Carlingford Tagalog Mass fellowship. Regular Tagalog and Filipino language masses are celebrated here on the first Sunday of every month at 4:00 PM and the third Sunday of the month at 11:45 AM, offering a warm and familiar space for spiritual connection and community gathering.

Parishioners appreciate the friendly, inclusive environment and the sincere hospitality shown by the church community. The sanctuary features beautiful stained-glass windows and is equipped with modern comforts like air conditioning, making it a comfortable place to worship throughout the year. The facilities are wheelchair friendly, and convenient parking is accessible in the school playground during mass services. Visitors and long-term members describe the parish as a sacred and peaceful place to find comfort. St Kevin's Catholic Church remains a vital spiritual centre for the multicultural community in Sydney's northern suburbs.

name: St Kevin’s Catholic Church (Dee Why)
description:
Located in the heart of Dee Why, St Kevin’s Catholic Church is a vibrant and welcoming parish that serves the local Catholic community, including a strong Filipino presence. Housed in a stunning heritage-listed building, the church is known for its beautiful design, featuring numerous statues and religious art that create a peaceful space for prayer and reflection.

Parishioners and visitors frequently highlight the warm, family-like atmosphere and the friendly nature of the congregation and priests. The church features an active choir located in the upstairs loft, whose uplifting music is a favourite part of the services. For convenience, the venue offers good parking facilities and is an easy five-minute walk from the main town centre.

As part of the Warringah Catholic Parish, the church hosts a variety of services to support its multicultural community. This includes a regular Tagalog and Filipino Mass held on the first Sunday of the month at 4:00 PM and the third Sunday at 11:45 AM. The parish also offers daily Masses, Saturday Vigil services, a youth Mass on Sunday evenings, and Italian and Spanish services. It is a central hub for spiritual growth and community connection on the Northern Beaches.

name: St Luke the Evangelist Catholic Church
description:
St Luke the Evangelist Catholic Church is a vibrant and welcoming parish situated in Revesby, New South Wales. This multicultural community brings together people from diverse backgrounds, offering a supportive environment for worship and spiritual growth. The parish provides regular weekday and weekend Masses, including convenient opportunities for Adoration and Reconciliation.

The community is particularly appreciated for its warm hospitality, gentle clergy, and helpful pastoral team. Parishioners and visitors value the church's beautiful layout, which features impressive statues that enhance the prayerful atmosphere. In addition to regular services, the parish is home to active youth ministries, playgroups, and social groups that foster strong community connections.

For the Filipino community in Sydney, the parish is a central hub for devotions and cultural celebrations. It is well known for hosting the festival of Santo Niño de Cebu, where parishioners organise inclusive gatherings. The church also holds a dedicated Santo Niño votive Mass at 7:00 PM on the fourth Friday of each month. Located at 1 Beaconsfield Street, the church features wheelchair access and parking facilities to welcome all visitors.

name: St Luke’s Catholic Parish Marsden Park
description:
St Luke’s Catholic Parish is a welcoming and growing faith community located in Marsden Park, Western Sydney. The parish marked a significant milestone with the opening of its purpose-built church building on Frontier Avenue in October 2025. Previously gathering at the local Catholic college, parishioners now enjoy a dedicated worship space.

Locals and visitors praise the parish for its friendly and warm atmosphere. The community is highly supportive, making it easy for new faces to connect and feel included. A central highlight of parish life is the active engagement of the local Filipino community. They regularly participate in parish fundraisers and host community food stalls. After services, people gather to enjoy traditional Filipino favourites such as pork barbecue, pancit, and delicious desserts.

The parish office operates from Monday to Friday, between 9:30 am and 4:30 pm. Regular worship services include a Saturday evening Vigil Mass at 5:30 pm. Sunday Masses are held at 8:00 am, 10:00 am, and 5:30 pm, providing multiple opportunities for fellowship. With its inclusive spirit and strong community links, the parish continues to be a vibrant hub for spiritual growth and cultural connection in the region.

name: St Margaret Mary's Catholic Church
description:
St Margaret Mary's Catholic Church in Merrylands is a warm and inclusive parish serving a diverse, multicultural community in Western Sydney. Under the pastoral care of the Pauline Fathers within the Diocese of Parramatta, the church offers a welcoming space for worship, reflection, and community connection.

The parish is highly regarded for its active and friendly community, where regular attendees and new visitors alike feel a genuine sense of belonging. The dedicated priests provide supportive spiritual guidance through a regular schedule of weekday and weekend Masses, Adoration, and Confessions. Visitors frequently praise the uplifting music and the parish's encouraging, down-to-earth atmosphere.

For the local Filipino-Australian community, the church is a major cultural and spiritual hub. It proudly hosts the annual Simbang Gabi novena, celebrating the Christmas dawn masses with traditional devotion and festive fellowship. The church also features an active youth group, regular baptism services, family movie nights, and various seasonal activities that bring families together. Located on Chetwynd Road, the parish continues to be a vibrant centre of faith and fellowship for Merrylands and its surrounding suburbs.

name: St Margaret Mary's Parish
description:
St Margaret Mary's Parish is a welcoming Catholic church located in the heart of Merrylands, Sydney. Under the pastoral care of the Pauline Fathers (Order of St Paul the First Hermit) and within the Diocese of Parramatta, it serves a vibrant, multicultural local community. The parish specialises in bringing together diverse ethnic groups, with a strong and active Filipino-Australian congregation. It is particularly renowned for hosting the annual Simbang Gabi, a beloved series of traditional Filipino Christmas novena dawn masses that draw families from across the region.

Parishioners appreciate the warm, down-to-earth atmosphere and the dedicated priests who lead the community. The church offers a range of regular activities, including an active youth group, family movie nights, and various children's programmes. Regular baptism services are held on the first and third Sundays of each month. In addition to standard services, the parish holds Mass, Stations of the Cross, and Exposition on the first Friday of the month, with Adoration extending until midnight.

Conveniently situated at 1-5 Chetwynd Road, the centre is a focal point for spiritual growth and community support in Western Sydney, though visitors during busy Sunday services are advised to plan ahead for local parking.

name: St Mark's Anglican Church
description: St Mark's Anglican Church is a welcoming and active multicultural parish located at 39 Jamieson Street in Granville. Led by Rector Reverend Doctor Tallis Tien, this evangelical church is dedicated to serving the local community through Bible-based teaching and liturgical Anglican worship. Local visitors frequently describe the church as a beautiful and friendly centre with great preaching and an inclusive fellowship. Families particularly appreciate the local playgroup, which offers a variety of engaging activities for young children. To support the diverse local community, the church hosts services in multiple languages on Sundays. Congregants can attend the 9:00 am Prayer Book service, followed by a dedicated Filipino service at 9:30 am. There is also a Mandarin and Cantonese service held at 11:00 am. The active Filipino and English chaplaincy service is led by Chaplain Retchie Salvador, supporting a vibrant Filipino congregation that celebrated its first anniversary in June 2026. Whether you are looking to join a service or participate in community classes, St Mark's provides a supportive environment to connect with others.

name: St Mark's Granville Filipino Congregation
description:
St Mark's Granville Filipino Congregation is a vibrant and welcoming Christian community located at 39 Jamieson Street, Granville. Established in June 2025 under the guidance of Reverend Deacon Richie Salvador and the broader parish leadership, the congregation recently celebrated its first anniversary in June 2026. This growing ministry specialises in providing a welcoming space for the local Filipino-Australian community to gather, praise, and connect.

The congregation hosts a dedicated Sunday service at 9:30 am, featuring joyful worship and authentic fellowship tailored to the spiritual needs of its members. Visitors and regular attendees alike are welcomed with warm hospitality and a focus on building deep community connections. Beyond Sunday worship, the congregation engages in regular outreach activities, social events, and community support groups, fostering a strong sense of home and belonging. As a vital part of the St Mark's Granville parish family, this congregation continues the rich, decades-long heritage of Filipino Anglican ministry in Sydney. Whether you are seeking a place to pray, connect with your cultural roots, or join in community activities, the team at St Mark's Granville Filipino Congregation is ready to welcome you with open arms.

name: St Mary Immaculate Catholic Church (Manly Freshwater Parish)
description:
St Mary Immaculate Catholic Church is a welcoming Catholic community situated in Manly, serving as a peaceful sanctuary for the local parish. As part of the Manly Freshwater Parish, this beautiful church features the Shrine of Hope for Priests and Vocations, making it a highly meaningful site for local and visiting pilgrims.

Parishioners and visitors frequently praise the warm, inviting atmosphere and the friendly, forward-thinking clergy who lead the community. The spotless chapel provides a serene and quiet space for personal prayer, meditation, and weekly Eucharistic Adoration. The parish is also active in local charity work and community outreach, providing support to those in need. For convenience, the property features its own on-site parking.

The church holds a strong, long-standing connection to the local Filipino-Australian community, hosting a dedicated Filipino Family Group. This group gathers on the second Sunday of the month for fellowship and morning tea, offering a wonderful way for families to connect and share support. Located on Raglan Street, the church is a supportive and beautiful centre of faith on the Northern Beaches.

name: St Mary MacKillop Catholic Parish
description:
St Mary MacKillop Catholic Parish is a welcoming, multicultural faith community located in Oran Park, within the Catholic Diocese of Wollongong. Established to serve the growing population of South West Sydney, this modern parish regularly hosts parishioners from diverse nationalities, including a vibrant Filipino-Australian community.

Parishioners appreciate the warm, inclusive atmosphere and the active involvement of families. The local clergy, including Father David, Deacon Sam, and Father Feleki, are highly regarded for their engaging liturgies and supportive approach, particularly during baptisms and family services. The community focuses on building strong connections with local schools like St Justin’s Primary School, fostering active participation in music, youth ministries, and sacramental preparation.

The parish holds weekly services, with the Saturday Vigil Mass scheduled at 5:00 pm, preceded by Reconciliation from 4:00 pm to 4:30 pm. Sunday Masses are celebrated at 8:00 am and 10:00 am, and a weekday Mass is offered on Fridays at 9:30 am. The parish office is open Tuesday through Friday from 9:00 am to 1:00 pm to support the administrative needs of the local area. Whether visiting for regular prayer or special community events, the parish provides a solemn and nurturing centre for spiritual growth.

name: St Mary Mackillop Parish, Rockdale City
description:
St Mary MacKillop Parish, located in Rockdale City, is a warm and welcoming Catholic community serving the southern suburbs of Sydney. Combining the congregations of Rockdale, Arncliffe, and Bexley, the parish has established itself as a central hub of spiritual and communal support. It is particularly known for its dedication to the local Filipino-Australian community, hosting a regular Filipino Community Mass on the third Sunday of each month at 6:00 pm.

Parishioners frequently describe the church as a peaceful and prayerful sanctuary. Visitors appreciate the inspiring sermons delivered by the parish priest and the warm, friendly nature of the congregation. The parish also maintains close ties with local Catholic schools, including St Gabriel's and St Joseph's, creating a lively environment where staff, students, and families come together for worship. Services are often enhanced by the parish choir, making mass celebrations a joyful and uplifting experience.

Whether you are looking to attend regular parish mass, participate in sacraments, or connect with fellow kababayans, the parish offers a supportive spiritual home. Located at 13 Parker Street, Rockdale, the church is a convenient two-minute walk from Rockdale train station, with parking facilities available on the church grounds during weekend services.

name: St Mary's Catholic Church
description:
St Mary's Catholic Church, also known as Mary Immaculate Church, is located in the coastal suburb of Manly on Sydney's Northern Beaches. Operating under the Manly Freshwater Catholic Parish, this welcoming church serves as a key spiritual and community hub for local residents and visitors alike. It holds regular weekday Masses from Tuesday to Friday at 9:15 AM, a Saturday Vigil Mass at 5:00 PM, and a Sunday morning Mass at 9:00 AM.

For the local Filipino-Australian community, the parish offers a warm and supportive environment. A dedicated Filipino Family Group plays an active role in parish life by organising regular social gatherings. This includes a monthly morning tea held on the second Sunday of the month, which follows the morning Mass. These events help to foster cultural connections and build a strong sense of fellowship.

Visitors and parishioners frequently praise the church for its peaceful, holy atmosphere and spotless chapel, making it a comfortable place for prayer and quiet reflection. The welcoming nature of the priests and the friendly, inviting congregation are also highly valued by the community. Additionally, the parish provides convenient on-site parking for attendees.

name: St Michael's Catholic Church (West Ryde)
description:
St Michael's Catholic Church in West Ryde is a warm and welcoming parish community serving Meadowbank, West Ryde, and surrounding areas. Led by Father Emmanuel Seo, the parish is highly regarded for its friendly atmosphere and inspiring, thought-provoking homilies. Visitors frequently highlight the beautiful music provided by the weekend choir and local musicians. The spacious church building includes a loft designed for discreet live streaming of services. For personal reflection and prayer, the sanctuary remains open to visitors during the day.

St Michael's hosts a regular Filipino (Tagalog) Mass on the fourth Sunday of each month at 11:00 am. This service provides a wonderful opportunity for the local Filipino-Australian community to gather and enjoy fellowship. St Michael's also offers daily Mass, Eucharistic Adoration, and Reconciliation. Active parish groups like the Legion of Mary provide further opportunities for community involvement. Whether you want to join a weekend liturgy or find a quiet space for prayer, this community welcomes you.

name: St Michael's Catholic Church Blacktown
description:
St Michael's Catholic Church in Blacktown is a welcoming, family-oriented faith community and a key part of the Mary, Queen of the Family Parish. Worshippers cherish this holy place for its prayerful, cosy atmosphere and the warm welcome extended by the parish priests and team. The church features convenient parking and a peaceful ambience that invites quiet prayer and reflection.

A core highlight for the Filipino-Australian community is the regular Filipino/Tagalog Mass, celebrated on the fourth Sunday of every month at 11:00 am. Worshippers also gather for Syro-Malabar Masses in Malayalam, offered on Saturdays at 9:00 am and Sundays at 3:45 pm.

Located behind St Michael’s Primary School on Orwell Street, the church is easily accessible via public transport. Visitors can take the 724 bus to the St Hedwig Village stop or walk from Reservoir Road up Kurrajong Crescent via a connecting footpath. Whether you are attending for regular sacraments, a special mass, or quiet devotion, St Michael's offers a friendly environment and a strong sense of community fellowship.

name: St Nicholas of Myra Catholic Parish
description:
St Nicholas of Myra Catholic Parish is a historic and active faith community located in the heart of Penrith on High Street. Active since 1835, the parish serves over 3,000 local Catholic families and offers a welcoming spiritual home for everyone. Visitors frequently admire the unique circular architecture of the church, which features an art-deco style, a raised dome ceiling adorned with stained glass windows, and a prominent altar in the centre of the church. The building is fully air-conditioned, making it a comfortable place to worship throughout the year.

The local community appreciates the warm and vibrant atmosphere of the parish, highlighting the helpful pastoral team and the beautiful singing of the choir. The parish is known for organising smooth and supportive sacramental programs, including baptisms and Paschal services. Beyond standard mass times, the church hosts monthly devotions to Our Lady of Peñafrancia, as well as annual celebrations like Flores de Mayo and Simbang Gabi, which bring together the Filipino-Australian community in Western Sydney. The parish grounds also accommodate community support groups, such as local AA meetings on Tuesdays. Ample parking is available at the rear of the property, accessible via Higgins Street.

name: St Patrick's Catholic Church (Summer Hill)
description:
St Patrick's Catholic Church in Summer Hill is a welcoming parish that serves as a spiritual centre for the local community. Housed in a beautiful building characterised by its classic architecture and warm wooden floors, the church is widely appreciated for its friendly priest and supportive community of parishioners. It is a wonderful faith community where locals and visitors alike feel welcome to participate in worship and reflection.

The church offers a regular schedule of liturgical services, including a Saturday Vigil at 6:00 pm, preceded by the Rosary at 5:00 pm. Sunday Mass is held at 8:00 am, with weekday Masses celebrated on Wednesdays and Fridays at 9:00 am. For those seeking quiet contemplation, the parish hosts Eucharistic Adoration on Tuesday evenings at 7:30 pm.

A key highlight for the multicultural community is the dedicated monthly Filipino Mass, celebrated on the fourth Sunday of each month at 11:00 am. This special service brings together Filipino-Australian families from Summer Hill and surrounding Sydney suburbs, fostering cultural connection and shared faith. Located on Drynan Street, St Patrick's Catholic Church continues to be a vibrant and inclusive space for spiritual growth and community fellowship.

name: St Patrick's Catholic Church Blacktown (Mary Queen of the Family Parish)
description:
St Patrick's Catholic Church in Blacktown is a vital spiritual hub and a key part of the Mary, Queen of the Family Parish. The church is well-known for hosting the Filipino Chaplaincy of the Catholic Diocese of Parramatta, offering a welcoming space for the local Filipino-Australian community. A dedicated monthly Filipino Mass is celebrated here on the fourth Sunday of each month at 11:00 am, bringing together congregation members for worship and cultural fellowship.

Beyond its specialised chaplaincy services, the church provides a robust weekly schedule of services. These include regular weekday morning Masses, Friday evening Masses accompanied by Reconciliation and Holy Hour, and Saturday morning and Vigil services. Members of the parish appreciate the church's spacious interior, ample seating, and beautifully presented surroundings, which make it suitable for large family gatherings such as baptisms, christenings, and funerals. Visitors frequently highlight the welcoming and compassionate parish leadership, as well as the accommodating staff who assist community videographers and organisers. Whether attending a solemn Sunday liturgy or participating in community events, St Patrick's offers a peaceful sanctuary for faith and fellowship in Western Sydney.

name: St Paul of the Cross Catholic Parish Dulwich Hill
description:
St Paul of the Cross Catholic Parish is a vibrant and historic church community located in Dulwich Hill, Sydney. Known for its striking dark brick exterior and a beautifully light, airy interior, the church provides a welcoming space for prayer and quiet reflection. Visitors and regular parishioners highly appreciate the active parish life, which features lovely choirs, great acoustics, and an exquisite devotional garden surrounding the building.

The parish is exceptionally family-friendly, offering an accommodating children’s play area during services. The community is also known for its outreach programs, such as providing fresh bread during the Saturday morning service.

For sacramental celebrations and weekly worship, the parish offers regular Mass times from Monday to Friday at 9:00 am, a Saturday morning service at 9:30 am, and a Saturday Vigil Mass at 5:30 pm. Sunday Masses are held at 8:00 am and 10:00 am. Notably, the active local Filipino community group hosts a monthly Filipino Mass on the first Sunday of every month at 11:30 am, fostering strong cultural and spiritual connections in the Sydney area.

name: St Paul the Apostle Catholic Church (Winston Hills)
description:
St Paul the Apostle Catholic Church is a welcoming parish community located in Winston Hills, New South Wales. Perched on a hill, the church is known for its peaceful surroundings, lovely gardens, and spectacular views toward Parramatta. It serves as a vital spiritual hub in the local area, offering regular weekday and weekend Masses alongside reconciliation services on Thursday evenings and Saturday afternoons.

The parish is particularly appreciated for its warm and friendly atmosphere. Visitors and parishioners love the active community life, which includes morning teas and special blessings for birthdays and wedding anniversaries. Under the guidance of dedicated priests, including Filipino parish priest Father JP Escarlan, the church hosts various active ministries and prayer groups. In partnership with the Filipino Chaplaincy of the Diocese of Parramatta, the parish hosts a regular Tagalog and Filipino Mass on the second Sunday of each month at 11:00 AM. This service brings together community members for worship and fellowship, often followed by shared meals, making it a wonderful centre of faith and connection.

name: St Paul the Apostle Parish Winston Hills
description:
St Paul the Apostle Parish in Winston Hills is a welcoming and lively Catholic church community situated in the Diocese of Parramatta. Perched on a hill, the parish is highly regarded by locals for its warm and friendly atmosphere. Parishioners and visitors appreciate the care and spiritual guidance provided by the local priests, including the Filipino Parish Priest, Father JP Escarlan.

The parish offers a comprehensive schedule of services, including weekday Mass at 9:10 am from Monday to Saturday, and weekend Masses on Saturday at 5:00 pm (Vigil) and Sunday at 8:00 am, 9:30 am, and 5:00 pm. Community life is vibrant, featuring regular morning teas and a special practice of blessing anniversaries and birthdays. Notably, the church offers a shared lunch after Mass, fostering a strong sense of fellowship.

Set in a peaceful residential area, the parish features beautiful gardens and spectacular views of the Parramatta district. It includes a spacious church interior, a multi-purpose parish centre, and a large parking lot with wheelchair-accessible entrances, making it easily accessible for all worshippers.

name: St Peter Julian's Church
description:
St Peter Julian's Catholic Church is a welcoming spiritual sanctuary located in the heart of Sydney's bustling Chinatown. Established by the Congregation of the Blessed Sacrament, this church serves as a peaceful retreat from the busy city streets. From the outside, the building blends seamlessly with the surrounding George Street shops, featuring a modern glass sound barrier. Once inside, visitors discover a beautiful, quiet, and reverent space designed for prayer and reflection.

The church is well-known for its welcoming community and friendly clergy. Priests are frequently praised for their uplifting homilies. St Peter Julian's offers a wide range of accessible services. These include a popular daily midday Mass at 12:10 pm, adoration sessions, and a small gift shop.

The church supports multicultural congregations by hosting regular English services and fortnightly Indonesian Masses. It also holds a dedicated Tagalog Mass for the local Filipino community. This monthly Filipino Mass takes place on the first Sunday of every month at 1:30 pm.

Conveniently located close to public transport, including the Chinatown light rail and Town Hall station, the church remains a favourite spot for anyone seeking a quiet moment of solace.

name: St Therese Catholic Church
description:
St Therese Catholic Church is a vibrant parish located in the Rosebery and Mascot area of Sydney. Run by the Scalabrinian Missionaries, this parish specialises in welcoming migrants, families, and young people into a caring and supportive community environment.

The church features beautiful, traditional architecture that is popular with locals. Visitors regularly praise the peaceful atmosphere and the warm hospitality of the priest and volunteers. The parish offers a diverse range of liturgical services. These include regular English services, a Spanish Mass at 6:30 PM on Saturdays, and an Italian Mass at 11:30 AM on the second Sunday of each month.

For the local Filipino community, St Therese is a favourite spiritual home. The parish hosts a dedicated Filipino Mass on the fourth Sunday of each month at 12:00 PM. This mass is followed by a warm fellowship lunch that brings the community together.

Due to major refurbishment work at the main church site, parish services are temporarily operating out of the Parish Hall on the corner of Harris and Coward Streets, as well as the nearby Parish Pastoral Centre chapel at 39 Sutherland Street.

name: St Therese Catholic Church (Mascot)
description: St Therese Catholic Church is a vibrant and welcoming parish centre located in Mascot, Sydney. For many local parishioners, the church serves as a warm spiritual refuge and a home away from home. Visitors regularly praise the beautiful brick architecture and the peaceful, respectful atmosphere during services. The parish features a diverse and multicultural congregation, offering regular liturgical services in multiple languages to support the local community. Notably, the church hosts a dedicated Filipino Mass on the fourth Sunday of each month at 12:00 PM, which is highly anticipated for its welcoming environment, uplifting sermons, and the lively fellowship lunch that follows. The community also organises weekly Masses, Saturday Vigil services, Spanish-language Masses on Saturday evenings, and Italian-language services on the second Sunday of the month. To keep the parish facilities in excellent condition, the church has recently undergone major renovations, with services temporarily held in the nearby Church Hall. With its friendly volunteers, active youth group, and inclusive programs like the Alpha course, St Therese Catholic Church provides a wonderful space for worship, friendship, and community connection in Mascot.

name: St Therese's Catholic Church
description:
St Thérèse's Catholic Church in Sadleir is a welcoming and close-knit place of worship within the Sadleir-Miller parish. The church provides a warm, intimate setting that helps parishioners feel focused and connected during mass. Members of the diverse congregation frequently praise the friendly and supportive nature of the community, which welcomes people of all backgrounds.

The parish offers a comprehensive schedule of services to cater to its multicultural congregation. Regular weekday masses run from Tuesday to Friday at 8:00 am, with Saturday morning mass at 9:00 am and a Saturday Vigil mass at 5:00 pm. On Sundays, the main mass takes place at 9:00 am, followed by a Vietnamese language mass at 11:00 am. Notably, the church also supports the local Filipino-Australian community by hosting a dedicated Tagalog or Filipino Mass on the fourth Sunday of each month at 11:00 am.

Conveniently located at 125 Cartwright Avenue, adjacent to St Thérèse Catholic Primary School, the church serves as an important spiritual centre for families in South West Sydney. Whether attending weekly services, baptisms, or monthly community devotions, visitors can expect a warm reception and a peaceful atmosphere.

name: St. Elias Speleota’s Spanish Catholic Church of Australia
description:
St. Elias Speleota’s Spanish Catholic Church of Australia in Ashcroft is a warm and welcoming parish community under the Archdiocese of Sydney. The church caters to a diverse multicultural congregation, serving as a key spiritual hub for the local Spanish-speaking and Filipino-Australian communities in the Ashcroft and Liverpool regions. It is particularly known for hosting a dedicated Filipino community Mass on the fourth Sunday of every month at 5:30 p.m., bringing families and friends together for worship and fellowship.

Parishioners and visitors appreciate the comfortable and friendly atmosphere of the church, which features helpful amenities like air conditioning, ceiling fans, and ample on-site parking. The church grounds include a large undercover veranda and a concrete stage, which are used for outdoor community events and performances. There is also a spacious hall that supports community gatherings. With a strong focus on community life, the parish welcomes everyone warmly, offering a place to build real friendships and feel at home. In addition to Spanish and English services, the church supports local catechism classes, prayer groups, and youth programs, making it a lively centre of faith and culture in South West Sydney.

name: St. Elias' Catholic Church
description:
St. Elias Speleota’s Spanish Catholic Church is a cosy and welcoming parish located in Ashcroft, New South Wales. The church primarily serves the Spanish-speaking Catholic chaplaincy of Sydney, which spans the Ashcroft, Fairfield, and Hurstville communities. It also serves the local Filipino-Australian community by hosting a regular monthly Filipino/Tagalog Mass on the fourth Sunday of the month.

Members of the congregation and visitors frequently praise the church for its warm, family-oriented atmosphere, describing it as a place where everyone is made to feel welcome. The property features comfortable facilities, including air conditioning, ceiling fans, and a large undercover veranda. There is also an outdoor concrete stage for community performances, a parish hall, and plenty of car parking space. The church staff are known to be highly helpful, accommodating special events such as family live streams and community gatherings.

Regular Sunday services are held from 9:00 AM to 10:00 AM, often followed by community breakfast fundraisers featuring traditional food, which helps to foster a strong sense of fellowship. Additional prayer meetings take place on Monday evenings from 7:00 PM to 9:00 PM.

name: St. Ezekiel Moreno Convent - Filipino Mass, Doonside
description:
The St Ezekiel Moreno Convent in Doonside is a peaceful spiritual sanctuary and community hub for the local Filipino-Australian community in western Sydney. Operated by the Congregation of the Augustinian Recollect Sisters, this welcoming convent is named after Saint Ezekiel Moreno, a Spanish missionary who served in the Philippines and is venerated as the patron saint of cancer patients.

The convent plays an important role in fostering Catholic faith, prayer, and cultural connection. A central highlight of its community outreach is the monthly Filipino Mass, which is celebrated on the third Sunday of every month at 1:30 pm. This regular Eucharist offers a warm space for families and individuals to gather in fellowship and celebrate their shared heritage.

Set within serene surroundings featuring a garden and a grotto, the convent provides a quiet place for reflection and prayer. In addition to the monthly Mass, the convent hosts various spiritual activities and community events throughout the year, especially around the liturgical feast of St Ezekiel Moreno on August 19. It remains a vital pillar of religious and social support for the Filipino diaspora in the Parramatta Diocese.

name: St. Martha's Catholic Church
description:
St. Martha's Catholic Church is a welcoming parish community situated on the corner of Churchill Avenue and Homebush Road in Strathfield. The church is known for its traditional, serene, and beautiful worship space, making it a peaceful sanctuary for the local community. Visitors and regular parishioners appreciate the warm, inclusive atmosphere, with many praising the gentle, thoughtful, and personal homilies delivered by the parish priest.

The parish features active community groups and is closely integrated with the adjacent primary school. For those holding special services or live streams, the church features a convenient choir loft that allows discreet filming. Regular English services include a Saturday Vigil Mass at 5:00 pm and Sunday Masses at 8:00 am, 10:00 am, and 6:00 pm. Additionally, the parish proudly supports its Filipino congregation by hosting a dedicated Tagalog Mass on the fourth Sunday of every month at 11:00 am. The church office is open Monday to Friday from 9:00 am to 3:00 pm, providing a central point for general enquiries and parish administration.

name: St. Michael's Catholic Church
description:
St Michael’s Catholic Church in Hurstville is a vibrant and welcoming parish community serving the Hurstville and Bexley areas of Sydney. Managed by the Michaelites priests, the church is known for its warm, family-like atmosphere and dedicated volunteers who support the congregation's spiritual journey.

The parish holds a regular mass schedule, including Sunday masses at 8:00 am, 10:00 am, and 6:00 pm, alongside a Saturday Vigil mass at 5:00 pm. It also hosts a Malayalam Syro-Malabar Catholic Mass on Sundays from 12:00 pm to 1:00 pm, catering to the multicultural local community. Parishioners actively participate in devotions such as the St Michael devotion on Tuesdays at 7:30 pm, the Novena to Our Lady of Perpetual Help on Saturdays at 10:00 am, and the Legion of Mary every Wednesday at 7:30 pm.

Visitors and regulars alike appreciate the parish's serene environment, the inspiring homilies, and the beautifully sung liturgies led by a talented choir. Additionally, the church features an active Filipino community Cenacle group that coordinates devotions, fostering strong fellowship and community hospitality.

name: St. Michael's Catholic Parish, Lane Cove
description:
St Michael’s Catholic Parish is a warm and welcoming church community located in Lane Cove, near Lane Cove Village. The parish is known for its beautiful modern building that retains a traditional, prayerful atmosphere. Parishioners appreciate the holy and reverent atmosphere during services, with many highlighting the clear sound system and the dedicated service of Parish Priest Fr. Mark Anderson and Assistant Priest Fr. Jorge Mairena.

The parish offers a comprehensive schedule of services, including weekday Masses from Tuesday to Saturday, weekend Vigil Masses, regular Adoration, and Reconciliation. For the local Filipino-Australian community, the parish hosts a monthly Filipino Mass on the fourth Sunday of every month at 11:00 am, providing a wonderful opportunity for worship and fellowship.

Beyond religious services, St Michael’s serves as a hub for local events, such as the parish’s popular annual Monster Garage Sale and trivia nights. The church is easily accessible, though visitors should note that the onsite car park fills up quickly. Pairing a visit with a trip to the nearby Lane Cove shops is highly recommended.

name: St. Patrick's Catholic Church, Mortlake
description:
St Patrick's Catholic Church in Mortlake is a welcoming parish located in the Inner West of Sydney, serving the communities of Mortlake, Breakfast Point, Cabarita, and Concord. The parish is highly regarded by visitors and locals for its calm, peaceful atmosphere, modern architecture with a glass-enclosed porch, and warm, supportive community spirit.

Father Thomas Stevens provides compassionate leadership that is highly appreciated by parishioners, who frequently describe him as approachable and caring. The parish offers a variety of active ministries, including children's after-school activities, youth groups, and a Saturday morning organic market, which foster strong connections and fellowship. Regular services include weekday Masses, Saturday Vigil, and Sunday services. The parish also hosts a dedicated monthly Filipino Mass on the fourth Sunday of the month at 11:00 am, which serves as a key gathering point for the local Filipino-Australian community. Whether attending for regular Mass, sacramental celebrations, or community events, visitors consistently find a friendly, inclusive environment.

name: Staffhouse Australia
description:
Staffhouse Australia, based in Macquarie Park, Sydney, serves as the local affiliate office of Staffhouse International Resources. The organisation specialises in ethical and transparent international recruitment, helping Australian employers connect with highly skilled Filipino professionals across various industries, including healthcare, engineering, construction, and hospitality.

Under the leadership of Director Rowel Mercado, who has over two decades of regional migration and advisory experience, the office provides comprehensive recruitment support. Staffhouse Australia features an on-site licensed immigration lawyer to ensure all visa and placement processes comply fully with both Australian and Philippine labour regulations.

The agency is widely recognised for its commitment to ethical recruitment, operating under a strict non-fee charging policy where candidates face no placement fees, processing fees, or salary deductions. Clients value their professional volume recruitment capabilities, while employees praise the organisation for offering a supportive, collaborative, and productive work environment that focuses on skill development and career growth. Located at 7 Eden Park Drive, the office provides local businesses with reliable expertise to navigate global talent sourcing smoothly.

name: Starlight Bakery
description: Starlight Bakery is a beloved Filipino bakery and café located in Doonside, Western Sydney. Serving the local community for over 25 years, it is a favourite destination for authentic Filipino breads, sweets, and pastries. Customers frequently queue for their soft, fluffy pandesal, which is baked fresh daily. Other popular specialities include sweet Spanish bread, cheese ensaymada, ube buns, custard cake, and a decadent Basque cheesecake. The bakery also offers a selection of savoury options, such as chicken empanadas with a crumbly shortcrust. To accompany the baked treats, visitors can order hot and cold beverages, including flat whites and matcha lattes. A recent renovation has transformed the space, giving it a clean, modern look with a subtle Japandi aesthetic. It now features a cozy indoor seating area that can accommodate up to 20 people. Whether you are tasting Filipino treats for the first time or looking for a comforting taste of home, this family-run bakery provides a warm and welcoming experience in the heart of the west.

name: Sto. Nino Oriental Mini Mart
description:
Sto. Nino Oriental Mini Mart is a local Filipino and Asian grocery store located on Queen Street in St Marys, Sydney. The shop specialises in importing a wide variety of groceries, spices, specialty sauces, and frozen goods from Southeast Asian countries, with a strong focus on popular Filipino products. In addition to East and Southeast Asian items, they also stock a select range of African and Indian products to serve the multicultural local community.

Customers appreciate the convenience and friendly atmosphere of the store, often highlighting the welcoming and helpful nature of the owners. Regulars value the personalised service, noting that they can phone ahead to check product availability or have their grocery lists gathered for quick payment and pick-up. The shop is highly regarded as a favourite spot for the local Filipino community in the St Marys area. Located next to a popular local banh mi bakery, it offers easy access and convenient street parking for shoppers looking to stock up on authentic ingredients.

name: Stojanovic & Associates
description:
Stojanovic & Associates is a registered migration agency that specialises in helping Australian and Filipino couples navigate the Australian visa system. Led by Danijela Stojanovic (MARN 0958278), the firm has been assisting families and individuals since 2009. They are based in North Sydney at 100 Mount Street, but they support clients right across Australia and globally through remote video and phone consultations.

The agency focuses heavily on partner visa pathways, including onshore and offshore partner visas and prospective marriage visas. They also manage skilled migration, skills assessments, and appeals for visa refusals. Clients frequently praise the team for their warm, professional, and personalised care. Reviewers share how Danijela treats every case with absolute dedication, making a complex and stressful process feel clear and manageable. Couples like Josip and Melinda have highlighted the exceptional service and guidance they received during their successful application.

Stojanovic & Associates is open from 9:00 am to 5:00 pm, Monday to Friday. If you need expert guidance for your migration journey, you can reach out to Danijela and her team via phone or email.

name: Suki Kart
description:
Suki Kart is a dedicated Filipino grocery store located in the heart of Hornsby, Sydney, at Shop 3, Ground Floor, 25-31 Florence Street. It specialises in providing the local community with easy access to a wide range of authentic Filipino products, including frozen and dry ingredients, traditional breads, cakes, desserts, and popular Pinoy delicacies. Beyond groceries, Suki Kart supports the Filipino-Australian community by offering convenient money remittance and Balikbayan box services.

Customers highly appreciate Suki Kart's efficient online ordering system and reliable home delivery service, which extends across Australia. The store's packaging for frozen food is particularly well-regarded, utilising double silver wrapping and disposable freezer packs to ensure items arrive in excellent condition. Customers also frequently highlight the friendly, responsive, and respectful customer service team, who are proactive in communicating about order updates and out-of-stock items. Recognised for its positive impact and high-quality service, Suki Kart was named a finalist in the Hornsby Ku-ring-gai Local Business Awards. Whether you are shopping in-store at The Piazza or ordering from afar, Suki Kart brings a welcome taste of home to Australia.

name: Suki Kart & Linalyn's Rooty Hill
description:
Suki Kart & Linalyn's Rooty Hill is a welcoming Filipino and Asian grocery store located at 10 Rooty Hill Road South in Rooty Hill. It serves as a popular hub for the local Filipino community. The store is clean, tidy, and well-organised, making it easy to find everything you need.

Local shoppers appreciate the store for its great product range and fresh produce. It offers a wide variety of quality Filipino pantry staples, snacks, and frozen items. The business also hosts Linalyn's Fresh Specials, featuring hot meals and traditional desserts like mango float, buko pandan, buko pie, and ube macapuno cake. If you are sending items home, they can help arrange balikbayan boxes and padala money remittance.

Customer service is a major highlight, with staff praised for being warm, friendly, and helpful. Visitors often mention how staff members go out of their way to offer honest recommendations. The store provides excellent value and includes convenient parking spaces right outside. Whether you are looking for authentic ingredients or a sweet treat, it is a fantastic one-stop shop.

name: Summer Vibe
description:
Summer Vibe is a vibrant beverage and dessert bar specialising in refreshing fruit juices, bubble teas, and traditional Filipino treats. Located in the basement level of Westpoint Blacktown near the food court, this locally loved spot offers a taste of the Philippines right in Western Sydney.

The menu features a diverse range of fresh fruit juices and thick crushes, including popular combinations like watermelon lychee and mango sago. Customers particularly appreciate the use of fresh ingredients and the friendly service from the staff. A signature highlight is their warm Taho, a traditional comfort food made of silken tofu, chewy pearls, and sweet arnibal syrup, which makes for a perfect treat during the cooler winter months. For those looking for classic Filipino desserts, Summer Vibe also serves a refreshing Halo-Halo, packed with ube, shaved ice, and gulaman.

Whether you are craving a nostalgic taste of home-style Philippine refreshments or looking for a refreshing fruit crush, Summer Vibe provides an authentic and affordable option in the heart of Blacktown.

name: Sunny Corner Cafe
description:
Sunny Corner Cafe, formerly known as Eskinita Café, is a welcoming Filipino breakfast and lunch spot situated conveniently across from the Doonside railway station in Sydney. Under its new ownership and management, this cosy venue has become a local favourite for authentic Filipino comfort foods and friendly service.

The cafe specialises in generous portions of traditional breakfast silog dishes, including flavourful tapsilog and tocilog. Guests also rave about the savoury sisig, comforting pancit, and rich lomi or lugaw, which provide a nostalgic taste of home. For those seeking lighter snacks, the menu features simple yet delicious options like tomato and cheese rolls with caramelised onion. To complete the dining experience, the cafe serves top-notch coffees, iced mochas, rich hot chocolates, and refreshing halo-halo, alongside decadent Basque cheesecakes.

With its relaxed atmosphere and dog-friendly outdoor terrace, it is an ideal spot to stop by after a morning walk at Nurragingy Reserve or for a satisfying meal when travelling through the area.

name: Super Pinoy Sari-Sari Store
description: Super Pinoy Sari-Sari Store is a welcoming Filipino grocery and convenience store located on Queen Street in St Marys, Sydney. The shop specialises in bringing a taste of the Philippines to the local community, offering an extensive and growing range of authentic Pinoy food products. Visitors can browse a variety of pantry essentials, popular snacks, imported ingredients, and fresh Filipino delicacies, including local favourites like chicken chorizo and traditional sweets. In addition to specialty groceries, the store serves as a key community hub by providing convenient services such as Ria Money Transfer and Western Union remittance to help customers send support back home. They also showcase beautiful, modern Filipiniana clothing, celebrating traditional heritage with a contemporary touch. Local customers frequently highlight the friendly and accommodating service provided by the owners, who create a warm and inviting shopping environment. With competitive prices and unique products that can be difficult to find elsewhere, it is a favourite spot for the Filipino-Australian community in Western Sydney. The store is open daily, making it easy for shoppers to stock up on their favourite items.

name: Superior Bakeshop & More
description:
Superior Bakeshop & More is a popular family-run Filipino bakery located on the first floor of Westfield Mount Druitt, just a short walk from the station. The bakeshop specialise in authentic Filipino-style breads, traditional pastries, and customisable cakes, making it a local favourite in the community.

Customers frequently praise the bakery for its warm hospitality and speedy service. Among the standout treats are the soft and sweet pandesal, Spanish bread, and sugar-dusted ensaymada, which often sell out early in the day. The savoury pork buns are another crowd favourite, alongside their range of traditional egg pies, coconut pies, and soft sponge cakes.

Whether you are looking for a quick morning snack or planning to order a custom cake for a special occasion, this welcoming shop offers a delightful taste of classic Filipino baking. The bakery is open daily from 6:00 am to 7:00 pm, providing plenty of opportunities to pick up fresh treats.

name: Supersave Convenience Store
description:
Supersave Convenience Store is a welcoming local Asian grocery shop and Filipino convenience store situated on Woodburn Road in Berala, Sydney. Serving the local community in the Greater Western Sydney region, this store specialises in providing a wide selection of authentic Filipino snacks, pantry groceries, and daily fresh food options.

Local customers highly recommend the store for its exceptional convenience and friendly service, often highlighting the warm hospitality of 'Kuya' and the staff who make every visitor feel valued. Reviewers consistently praise the quality and variety of the snacks, describing the venue as a go-to spot for both quick bites and essential grocery items. The daily selection of fresh food is a particular favourite among locals, making it a reliable choice for those looking to enjoy authentic tastes of home.

Conveniently located near Berala, Supersave Convenience Store is a central hub for the local Filipino community and anyone seeking quality Asian imports. Whether you are popping in for your favourite snacks or picking up fresh food, the store offers a pleasant and reliable shopping experience.

name: Sydney Cebu Lechon
description:
Sydney Cebu Lechon was a highly regarded Filipino eatery that specialised in authentic, Cebuano-style charcoal-roasted suckling pig. Originally established in the Newtown and Enmore area, the family-run business later relocated to Blacktown to continue serving the community.

Locals and food enthusiasts loved visiting the casual, home-style dining space for its warm, cheery hospitality. The star of the menu was the traditional Cebu lechon, celebrated for its tender, juicy meat infused with aromatics like lemongrass, garlic, star anise, and shallots, paired with an exceptionally crispy skin. Beyond the signature roast pork, the eatery offered a range of classic Filipino favourites. These included lechon kawali, chorizo de Cebu, beef nilaga, pinakbet, Bicol express, and smoky pork barbecue skewers. Guests could also enjoy sweet desserts such as halo-halo, ube cheesecake, and frozen brazo de mercedes.

Although the business has consolidated its operations over the years, it remains a fond part of Sydney’s Filipino culinary history. This business has permanently closed.

name: Sydney Eco Cleaning
description:
Sydney Eco Cleaning is a professional, locally owned cleaning service specialising in eco-friendly and organic cleaning solutions for residential and commercial properties across Sydney. Established in September 2011 by Rob and Joco, the team has built a strong reputation for their meticulous attention to detail and reliable service.

Clients frequently highlight their exceptional communication, strong work ethic, and the peace of mind that comes from using non-toxic, biodegradable products that do not harm the environment. The company offers a wide range of services, including regular weekly or monthly home maintenance, spring cleaning, office and studio care, and strata building upkeep. They are also highly regarded for their comprehensive end-of-lease bond cleans. For move-out cleans, the team provides fair quotes, works thoroughly to satisfy real estate inspection requirements, and even coordinates key handovers directly with property managers.

Operating from bases in Ultimo and Potts Point, Sydney Eco Cleaning is easily accessible for one-off tasks or ongoing cleaning partnerships. Whether preparing a home for a new tenant or keeping a local business looking immaculate, this professional team provides high-quality results with a green, sustainable focus.

name: Sydney Elshaddai Church
description:
Sydney Elshaddai Church is a welcoming community group and local chapter of El Shaddai DWXI PPFI Australia, a prominent Filipino Catholic Charismatic renewal movement. Conveniently based at the Senior Citizen Centre in Blacktown, this vibrant fellowship provides a warm, supportive, and nurturing environment for the Filipino-Australian community to gather, pray, and grow together in their faith. 

The group specialises in hosting regular prayer fellowships, charismatic worship services, and spiritual healing gatherings. These meetings offer community members a meaningful space for spiritual renewal, pastoral support, and mutual encouragement. Beyond their weekly services, the Sydney chapter is highly active in Western Sydney, organising local outreach programmes, family support initiatives, and youth ministry activities that foster cultural connection and social cohesion. Fellowships are regularly conducted on Sunday evenings from 5:30 pm, providing a consistent and welcoming time for families and individuals to participate, share testimonials, and strengthen their community bonds.

name: Sydney FilOz Badminton
description:
Sydney FilOz Badminton (SFB) is a friendly community-based social sports group established in 2017. Originally founded by four Filipino expats who wanted to play recreationally, the group has grown into a large network of over 1,000 members. The group specialises in bringing people together, promoting a healthy lifestyle, and helping new arrivals settle into life in Australia through a shared love of badminton.

The group welcomes players of all skill levels, from beginners to advanced. Regular social badminton sessions are held on Wednesdays from 7:00 PM to 10:00 PM and Saturdays from 1:00 PM to 4:00 PM. These games take place at the Alpha Badminton Centre, utilising venues in Auburn and Silverwater.

To keep court queueing organised, the club uses the DoublesTeam mobile app. Members appreciate this digital queue system, noting that it makes organising matches faster and more convenient than traditional whiteboards. Beyond the sport, participants value the warm, inclusive atmosphere that makes it easy to form lasting friendships. Those interested in joining can contact the organisers via their social media profiles.

name: Sydney Filipino Social Club
description:
The Sydney Filipino Social Club is a vibrant community and social group that brings together Filipino Australians, expats, and friends across Sydney. Organised by Mark Serdina, the club focuses on strengthening cultural ties and helping members form lasting friendships in a welcoming environment.

The group hosts a diverse range of activities tailored to different interests. Members regularly gather for popular social events such as dining out at local restaurants, casual drinks nights, and lively karaoke sessions. For those who enjoy the outdoors, the club organises scenic hikes, road trips, beach visits, and picnics. Sports are also a major highlight of the group, with basketball being a clear favourite among members.

With nearly one hundred members, the club welcomes anyone of Filipino heritage, as well as those who are interested in learning more about the culture and making new friends. By organising regular, relaxed catch-ups, the Sydney Filipino Social Club serves as an excellent support network and a hub of community connection in the heart of Sydney.

name: Sydney Jokers Social Golf Club
description:
Sydney Jokers Social Golf Club is a lively community organisation based in Sydney, New South Wales. This friendly group primarily brings together Filipino-Australian golf enthusiasts, but it warmly welcomes players of all skill levels, from beginners to experienced golfers.

The club focuses on promoting active lifestyles, friendly competition, and social connection. Members gather for weekly golf games at various local golf courses across the Sydney metropolitan area. In addition to these regular rounds, the club organises annual major tournaments and special social events throughout the year. These events provide great opportunities for members and their families to socialise and build lasting friendships within the community.

Whether you are looking to improve your golf swing, enjoy a relaxed game on the weekend, or connect with other Filipino-Australian sports fans, Sydney Jokers Social Golf Club offers a welcoming and supportive environment. It is a fantastic way to stay active and enjoy the beautiful golf courses that Sydney has to offer while sharing stories and laughs with a wonderful group of people.

name: Sydney Kali Academy
description:
The Sydney Kali Academy is a dedicated martial arts school situated in Hurstville, Sydney, that has been teaching local students since 2003. Led by head instructor Alf Cocco, who is certified under the Inosanto Academy, the school provides training in a relaxed and welcoming environment.

The academy specialises in three main martial arts disciplines. Students can learn Bruce Lee's Jeet Kune Do, Filipino Martial Arts (also known as Kali or Eskrima), and Maphilindo Silat. Training focuses on empty-hand combat techniques, stick and knife defence, and functional self-defence systems. All classes are structured to accommodate different skill levels, from beginners to advanced practitioners.

Instructors at the academy are government-accredited through the National Coaching Accreditation Scheme and the Martial Arts Industry Association. The school is also a proud member of the Australian Jeet Kune Do Association, ensuring high standards of training and safety.

Training sessions run from 7:00 pm to 8:30 pm every Wednesday and Thursday evening at their central Hurstville location. The academy offers a friendly community atmosphere for anyone looking to improve their fitness, learn self-defence, or study traditional Southeast Asian martial arts.

name: Sydney Lawyers and Associates
description:
Sydney Lawyers and Associates is a professional legal practice based in Parramatta, specialising in property law, conveyancing, family law, and criminal law. Led by principal solicitor Angelita Manlangit, a Tagalog-speaking professional, the firm provides accessible and highly supportive legal assistance to the local community, particularly assisting first-home buyers through the conveyancing process.

Clients regularly praise the team for their exceptional communication, responsiveness, and clear guidance. Reviewers appreciate that the solicitors are always approachable and proactive, keeping clients informed at every step of a transaction without requiring follow-ups. The firm is known for its patient and stress-free approach, with staff members going above and beyond to review documents at short notice, even on weekends.

In addition to property and conveyancing services, the practice is highly regarded for its compassionate support during challenging personal matters, including family law disputes and academic misconduct allegations. Clients value the firm's methodical thinking, transparency, and dedication to achieving positive outcomes at affordable rates. Located in the heart of Parramatta, Sydney Lawyers and Associates offers a welcoming and professional service for individuals and families navigating the Australian legal system.

name: TERESA'S Siopao & Catering
description:
Teresa's Siopao & Catering is a family-owned Filipino takeaway and catering service located in Doonside, New South Wales. The business specialises in providing home-style catering for various local events and gatherings. It is particularly renowned in the local community for its freshly prepared traditional Filipino steamed buns, known as siopao, which are a popular choice for regular takeaway orders.

The establishment is positioned in a convenient location near Doonside railway station, making it easy for customers to visit and pick up their favourite meals. The business offers outdoor seating for visitors who wish to enjoy their food on-site. Customers appreciate the friendly service provided by the team, though experiences regarding the freshness of the dishes can sometimes vary.

Operating seven days a week, the shop opens daily from 8:00 AM until 6:00 PM. This consistent schedule ensures that local families and community groups can plan their event catering and meal pick-ups throughout the entire week.

name: TFC Traditional Filipino Catering
description:
TFC Traditional Filipino Catering is a family-owned business specialising in authentic Kapampangan Filipino cuisine, catering services, and event food trays. Originally serving the community near Mount Druitt station, this popular local eatery has relocated to a fresh space at Shop 8, 88 Stonecutters Drive in Colebee.

The business offers a daily rotating selection of traditional hot dishes, freshly baked breads, pastries, and classic desserts. Customers particularly enjoy their crispy pork sisig, which is prepared in the classic Kapampangan style, as well as sweet treats like turon and creamy ube halaya. For special occasions and family gatherings, they prepare generous party trays and offer custom catering packages.
