With a welcoming family atmosphere and friendly service, TFC is a favourite spot for the local Filipino-Australian community to enjoy home-style meals. The physical store is open from Tuesday to Sunday, between 10:00 AM and 6:00 PM, providing both dine-in options and takeaway. Customers can also order online or contact the team directly to arrange catering services.

name: TNB Cafe
description:
TNB Cafe, also known as The Naked Barista Cafe, is a welcoming speciality coffee and brunch spot located right next to Rooty Hill station in Western Sydney. Founded by Adrian Duquilla, a former IT specialist who turned his lockdown coffee hobby into a beloved local business, the cafe brings the vibrant feel of a Surry Hills eatery to the heart of the community.

The cafe specialises in high-quality brews using Paradox Coffee Roasters beans, prepared with professional equipment such as the Slayer Steam. Customers can choose from espresso, batch brew, or hand drip methods, alongside unique drinks like Spanish lattes and iced strawberry matcha.

The menu offers a delightful fusion of classic Australian brunch and modern Filipino flavours. Favourite dishes include the cornflakes-crusted brioche French toast, pork longganisa brekky burger, slow-cooked pork belly scramble, and chicken sisig pasta.

Locals highly appreciate the warm, family-run atmosphere, friendly service, and generous portions, with thoughtful touches like warmed plates to keep meals hot. The minimal yet inviting space is open daily from 6:00 am to 3:00 pm on weekdays, and 7:00 am to 3:00 pm on weekends, making it a perfect neighbourhood hub for coffee lovers and families.

name: Tagalog Association of Australia Inc.
description:
The Tagalog Association of Australia Incorporated (TAA) is a vibrant community organisation based in Mount Druitt, New South Wales. Established in July 2012, TAA dedicatedly promotes Tagalog heritage, the Filipino language, and traditional cultural values across the greater Sydney area.

The organisation is well known for hosting cultural events that bring the local Filipino-Australian community together. Each August, they celebrate Buwan ng Wika (National Language Month) with their signature FiesTAA festival. A key highlight of their calendar is the Balagtasan, a traditional form of Filipino poetic debate in verse. TAA also organises spiritual and traditional performances, including the Good Friday Pasyon Mahal at St Mary's Cathedral.

Beyond cultural preservation, the association actively supports humanitarian efforts. They coordinate and provide essential disaster relief, goods, and services to communities in the Tagalog region of the Philippines affected by floods and other natural calamities.

Led by community figures, TAA offers a supportive network for both new arrivals and long-term residents seeking to connect with their culture and kababayans (fellow countrymen) in Australia.

name: Taguan Cafe
description:
Taguan Cafe is a cosy, community-focused Filipino café located on Abercrombie Street in Darlington. The name 'Taguan' translates to hideaway, reflecting its homey layout which features both indoor and alfresco seating, alongside an upstairs space for private gatherings. Local patrons love the warm, family-like hospitality of the staff and the café’s dog-friendly atmosphere, making it a popular neighbourhood hub.

The menu specialises in traditional Filipino comfort foods reimagined with contemporary twists. Guests can enjoy authentic silog breakfasts, including the popular milkfish (bangus) and the highly recommended Tatay's chicken or pork adobo. Other favourites include slow-cooked beef pares toasties, fresh house-baked pandesal, and indulgent ube cheese toasties. For drinks, the café serves specialty coffees brewed from Philippine beans, alongside signature ube matcha lattes and mango cold brews.

Dedicated to elevating the local Filipino-Australian community, the owners actively partner with nearby businesses and artists. Whether stopping by for a quick morning brew or enjoying a relaxed Sunday brunch with karaoke, visitors will find a warm space that feels just like a family home.

name: Takam
description:
Located on Victoria Street in Darlinghurst, Takam is an intimate restaurant that specialises in progressive, modern Filipino cuisine. Founded by chefs Francis Dela Cruz and Aileen Aguirre, the venue offers a refined and thoughtful take on classic dishes, moving away from traditional counter-style dining to offer a stylish, welcoming space with real character.

Diners consistently praise the warm hospitality and knowledgeable service, where staff take the time to explain the ingredients and origin of each offering. The menu highlights include a popular coal-roasted chicken inasal with a perfect char, tender beef tapa, and fresh swordfish. Other favourite dishes feature spanner crab fat fried rice, empanadas, and king prawn palabok. For dessert, the rich leche flan and ube thick shakes are highly popular choices among locals. For those wanting a complete culinary journey, Takam offers a structured set menu that beautifully showcases their creative flavour pairings.

Whether you are enjoying a casual lunch or celebrating a special birthday, this Darlinghurst venue provides a cosy and comfortable atmosphere to experience authentic Filipino flavours reimagined with contemporary flair. It is a fantastic spot to share a meal with family and friends.

name: Takam, Filipino Food Forward
description:
Takam, Filipino Food Forward is a modern Filipino dining venue located on Victoria Street in Darlinghurst, Sydney. Founded by passionate chefs Aileen and Francis Dela Cruz, the restaurant specialises in elevating traditional Pinoy flavours with creative, contemporary twists.

Diners are welcomed into a warm and intimate space featuring a cosy indoor dining room and an outdoor terrace. The service is highly professional and welcoming, with friendly floor staff taking the time to explain the menu details.

The menu offers both brunch and dinner selections, including a popular set menu experience. Standout dishes include the perfectly charred lemongrass chicken inasal, spanner crab fried rice, and a refined palabok. Guests also love the Byron Bay pork cutlet, fresh calamari, and swordfish. For drinks and dessert, the calamansi soda and a delightful leche flan are highly recommended.

Takam offers a unique dining experience that showcases Filipino culinary culture with pride. It is a fantastic destination for special occasions, relaxed group dinners, or a first-time introduction to authentic Filipino food.

name: Tambayan Pinoy Variety Store
description:
Tambayan Pinoy Variety Store is a local Filipino grocery store located at 513 Burwood Road in Belmore, Sydney. The shop specialises in bringing authentic Filipino products, regional delicacies, and imported pantry essentials to the local community. Customers can find a variety of favourite ingredients, snacks, sauces, and household items from the Philippines.

The store is open from Saturday through Thursday between 9:00 am and 8:30 pm, providing a convenient window for local shoppers to drop in after work or on weekends. Please note that the shop is closed on Fridays. With a dedicated focus on friendly customer service, this variety store serves as a helpful neighbourhood hub for the Filipino-Australian community in the Canterbury-Bankstown region. Whether you are looking for specific ingredients for a home-cooked family feast, native Filipino treats, or simply want to browse their range of variety items, the store offers a welcoming environment and reliable phone service for all visitors.

name: Tarro Foods
description:
Tarro Foods is a popular, family-owned Filipino food truck operating at the Liberty Service Station in Doonside. This local gem specialises in serving authentic Filipino street food and comforting delicacies to the Sydney community.

Customers rave about the freshly made Vigan empanadas, which are prepared in real time on the day and never frozen overnight to maintain their quality. Due to their high demand, locals recommend reserving these crispy, golden pastries in advance via social media to avoid the wait. Other standout favourites include flavourful pork and chicken isaw, grilled chicken skewers, beef pares, and classic arroz caldo. For a refreshing finish, visitors can enjoy their signature gulaman or ube and pandan drinks.

The team behind Tarro Foods is well known for bringing genuine Filipino hospitality, offering a warm and welcoming service even during busy hours. While the food truck is located at a petrol station with limited on-site parking, plenty of street parking is available nearby. There is also cosy outdoor seating where you can sit and enjoy your meal straight from the grill. It is a fantastic spot to satisfy your cravings for authentic Pinoy street food in Western Sydney.

name: Tata Rods
description:
Tata Rods is a popular Sydney-based food stall and delivery service specialising in authentic Filipino-style roast pork belly, known as lechon belly. Located in Carlingford, this family-run venture is inspired by the patriarch Tata Rod and his deep love for the traditional spit-roasted delicacy.

Customers regularly praise the exceptional quality of their signature lechon belly, highlighting the perfectly crunchy, flavourful crackle and the tender, moist meat inside. Beyond their classic pork belly, they also craft seasoned sausages infused with traditional Philippine spices and ingredients.

Operating primarily as a mobile food stall and catering service, Tata Rods is a favourite fixture at local market events across Sydney and the Central Coast, such as the Greedy Guts market in Terrigal. They also offer direct delivery options for those looking to enjoy these traditional flavours at home. Whether you are hosting a family gathering or catching them at a local community event, Tata Rods brings the comforting taste of authentic Filipino fiesta food to New South Wales.

name: Tatak Pinoy Land Down Under
description:
Tatak Pinoy Land Down Under was a popular Filipino and Asian grocery store situated on Main Street in Blacktown, New South Wales. Serving one of the largest Filipino populations in the state, this local shop functioned as a welcoming hub for kababayans seeking authentic products and a sense of community in Western Sydney.

The store specialised in a comprehensive range of imported groceries, packaged goods, and daily necessities from the Philippines. Local shoppers visited regularly on weekends to purchase popular items like Filipino hotdogs, sweet treats, and fresh produce. In addition to pantry staples, Tatak Pinoy stocked unique cultural items, including beautiful hand-crafted native handbags and traditional garments such as the Barong Tagalog and Baro’t saya. Customers frequently praised the friendly, welcoming staff and the helpful store manager who made everyone feel at home. The business also featured a special Filipino ice cream cart that was available for hire at local community events and celebrations.

This business has permanently closed.

name: The 3 Bears Bakeshop
description:
The 3 Bears Bakeshop is a beloved, family-run home bakeshop based in Baulkham Hills that specialises in authentic Filipino cakes and pastries. Managed with heart by Elena, this bakery has built a loyal following across Sydney for its high-quality treats that offer a nostalgic taste of Manila.

Customers particularly rave about their signature ube cake, which is celebrated for being incredibly moist and perfectly sweet. Other highly recommended offerings include their rich chocolate cake—which can be ordered with a luscious dulce de leche filling—fluffy cheese ensaymada, and a classic sans rival featuring crunchy meringue and mocha buttercream. For those who love smaller treats, their buttery lengua de gato, fudgy crinkles coated in powdered sugar, and soft cheese rolls are always a hit.

The bakery is well-known for its exceptionally warm, friendly customer service and generous approach, often offering free taste samples of their bakes. While based in Baulkham Hills for pre-orders, they frequently connect with the community through lively pop-up stalls at Westpoint Blacktown and local events like the Burwood Winter Market.

name: The Bangketa Grocer
description:
The Bangketa Grocer is a spacious and well-organised Filipino specialty store located in the Penrith CBD. The shop specialises in bringing a taste of home to the local community. It offers an extensive selection of favourite Pinoy snacks, essential pantry items, and quality frozen goods.

Customers can find popular items like Mama Lors bread and hopia, Nagaraya cracker nuts, and specialty delicacies such as Delimondo corned beef. There is also a variety of local sauces, spicy garlic condiments, and unique spicy vinegars. For dessert, they stock traditional Filipino kakanin including leche flan, pitchi-pitchi, sapin-sapin, and kutchinta.

The shop also provides helpful community services. It acts as a RIA money transfer agent and a drop-off point for sending balikbayan boxes to the Philippines. Visitors appreciate the convenient parking, clean layout, and the exceptionally friendly staff who are always happy to help. For convenience, they offer reliable home delivery via DoorDash and Menulog. Orders are carefully packed to ensure all goods arrive safely.

name: The Brown Beat Band
description: The Brown Beat Band is a vibrant Filipino-Australian live music and cover band based in Sydney, New South Wales. Consisting of dedicated musicians including Jon Padilla, Mark Malana, Martin Van Ferrer, and Peter Franz Floyd, the group specialises in bringing high-energy performances and positive vibes to every stage they grace. Their diverse repertoire spans classic rock, pop, beat, and contemporary live music covers, making them a favourite addition to local entertainment venues. The band is a regular performer at popular community hubs, such as the Kowboy Bar & Grill in Blacktown, where they are known for engaging local crowds and creating a lively, dance-filled atmosphere. Whether entertaining patrons at local pubs, performing at cultural community gatherings, or playing at private events, The Brown Beat Band brings a professional yet warm presence that resonates with the Filipino-Australian community and music lovers alike. If you are looking to experience a night of great tunes, dancing, and fantastic local talent, keeping an eye on their upcoming gig schedule is highly recommended.

name: The Filipino Saturday School
description:
The Filipino Saturday School is a volunteer-run, non-profit community organisation based in Rooty Hill, in the Western suburbs of Sydney. Founded in 2016 by Jade Cadelina, the school dedicates itself to promoting, teaching, and preserving Filipino culture, history, and heritage for the younger generation, adult learners, and the wider Australian community.

The school offers structured language classes, specialising in basic Tagalog lessons for primary and high school students from Year 1 to Year 12. Adult learners can also enrol in dedicated introductory Tagalog courses, which typically run online on Saturday mornings. Beyond language education, the organisation provides cultural enrichment through unique hands-on programs. These include traditional music classes, such as beginner lessons for the stringed bandurria instrument, as well as educational workshops exploring the ancient Philippine writing system, Baybayin.

Classes and music lessons are hosted in community spaces, including the Rooty Hill Senior Citizens Centre on Rooty Hill Road South, creating a welcoming and accessible environment for all participants. As a volunteer-run group that does not receive regular government funding, the school relies on the dedication of community coordinators and local educators to foster cultural pride and connection in Western Sydney.

name: The Filoz Restaurant
description:
The Filoz Restaurant is a warm and family-friendly dining spot that specialises in authentic Filipino food. Previously located in Campbelltown, this popular eatery is well known for its welcoming community atmosphere and lively weekend karaoke nights.

Local diners appreciate the restaurant for its rich, traditional flavours and very generous portions. Some of the most popular mains include the classic tender pork adobo, sizzling pork sisig, crispy palabok noodles, and a hearty beef soup served with a marrow bone. For starters, visitors regularly recommend the crispy pork spring rolls and savoury buffalo wings. To finish the meal, many patrons choose the refreshing halo-halo, which is a colourful shaved-ice dessert filled with sweet toppings. Other dessert options include sweet sago't gulaman and traditional leche flan.

The staff are highly regarded for their friendly, accommodating service and home-style hospitality. This makes it a favoured destination for large family gatherings and celebratory parties. Please note that the restaurant is currently in the process of relocating and is temporarily closed at its Queen Street premises.

name: The Gospel Church of Jesus
description:
The Gospel Church of Jesus is a warm and welcoming Christian church fellowship located in Mount Druitt, New South Wales. Serving the local Filipino-Australian community, the church features a lively and faith-filled environment where people of all ages can connect and grow in their spiritual journeys. Visitors and regular members are greeted with genuine warmth and compassion by the church leadership and congregation.

The church specialises in vibrant Sunday worship services filled with great singing, uplifting worship music, and friendly fellowship. Beyond the weekly Sunday services, the church community stays connected through midweek prayer meetings and home-based Bible study groups that provide a supportive space for deeper learning and personal encouragement.

With a strong focus on pastoral care, the church leadership is dedicated to supporting individuals and families during both joyful celebrations and difficult times. They conduct essential religious ceremonies, including baptisms and marriages, and provide compassionate outreach and support for youth, adults, and the wider community. Located at Kurrajong Avenue, this caring congregation offers a peaceful and respectful centre for anyone seeking to experience faith, love, and genuine community spirit in Western Sydney.

name: The Handsome Mechanic (Andre Automotive PL)
description:
The Handsome Mechanic (operating as Andre Automotive PL) is a highly regarded, Filipino-owned mechanical workshop located in Mount Druitt, Western Sydney. For over ten years, this family-operated business has served the local community with professional car repairs, logbook servicing, and reliable tyre services. They specialise in rego safety checks, including pink slips, blue slips, defect clearances, and advanced brake testing.

Customers consistently praise the workshop for its spotless cleanliness and organised environment. The team is known for transparent communication, providing clear pricing, and sharing before-and-after photos of parts and repairs. Clients appreciate their warm, welcoming hospitality, which makes them feel like family. The mechanics are highly efficient, often accommodating urgent after-hours tyre punctures or Saturday bookings to keep drivers safe on the road.

As an active supporter of local Filipino community events and festivals, The Handsome Mechanic has earned several local small business award nominations. Whether you need a simple battery replacement, a new set of tyres, or complex engine repairs, they offer trusted and honest car care.

name: The Kamayan
description:
The Kamayan is a cherished Filipino restaurant located in Rooty Hill, Sydney. Serving the community since 1995, this family-run institution offers a warm, welcoming atmosphere reminiscent of a traditional neighbourhood eatery. The venue features a cozy, unpretentious dining space that makes visitors feel right at home.

Patrons highly recommend their extensive selection of authentic, home-style dishes. Daily hot food offerings change regularly, featuring popular classics such as peanut stew (kare-kare), deep-fried pork belly (lechon kawali), and pork blood stew (dinuguan). Other favourite dishes include sweet-style pork skewers, garlic rice, and pancit palabok. Customers frequently praise the balanced flavours and generous portions of the meals.

To complete the dining experience, the adjoining bakery and coffee shop, PanaDarna, offers popular sweet treats such as their famous buko pie alongside iced lattes. With friendly service from the local family and staff, it is an excellent destination for comforting Filipino cuisine in Western Sydney.

name: The Love of Jesus Christian Ministries
description:
The Love of Jesus Christian Ministries is a welcoming, Bible-based Christian church located in Glendenning, Western Sydney. Led by Senior Minister Orlando Borja, this congregation has deep roots in the Filipino-Australian community and actively supports missions in the Philippines.

Members and visitors value the church as a warm, friendly space that feels like a second home. Attendees describe the fellowships as theologically sound and spiritually enriching, offering a supportive environment to build faith through community service. The ministry caters to all age groups with various activities. Children from kindergarten to year six can join the Kidz in Action program during services, while youth and young adults meet for Bible studies on Tuesday and Friday evenings. There are also active Connect Groups meeting throughout the week.

The main Sunday worship service is held from 9:30 am at the Glendenning Rd venue. Additionally, the community gathers online for its weekly PrayerWatch meeting every Wednesday from 8:00 pm to 9:00 pm. Whether you are looking for a local church family or a place to serve, this ministry welcomes everyone to experience fellowship and support.

name: The Word Christian Mission
description:
The Word Christian Mission is a warm and welcoming non-denominational Christian church located in North St Marys, in Western Sydney. Established in 1993 by Filipino migrants Pastor Rene and Sister Nancy, this Bible-based fellowship has spent over thirty years supporting local families. The church is affiliated with the Word International Network and welcomes people from all walks of life.

Visitors and regular members frequently praise the congregation for its exceptionally friendly and embracing atmosphere. It is widely recognised as a sweet, open-hearted community where everyone feels valued and included. Whether you are attending for the first time or are a long-term member, you can expect genuine hospitality and kindness.

The church focuses on worship, discipleship, and sharing the message of Jesus Christ. Weekly Sunday worship services are held at 10:00 am, offering a vibrant environment for prayer, fellowship, and celebration. They also host various seminars and community events throughout the year. Conveniently situated on Forthorn Place, the centre provides a supportive environment for spiritual growth and community connection in the local St Marys area.

name: Tindahan Filipino-Asian Grocery
description: Tindahan Filipino-Asian Grocery is a clean and welcoming retail store located on Junction Street in the heart of Nowra, offering a wide selection of products to the local community. Customers highly appreciate the friendly, family-owned atmosphere, often noting the warm hospitality of the owners who make visitors feel right at home. The grocery specialises in importing a rich variety of authentic Filipino ingredients, popular snacks, and pantry staples. Locals particularly love the excellent range of sweet snacks and flavours, with classic Filipino favourites like Fudgee Barrs being highly sought after. Beyond packaged groceries, the store serves as an essential community hub for Filipino and Asian residents in the South Coast region of New South Wales, providing convenient access to beloved tastes of home and familiar brands. Whether you are looking to cook a traditional Pinoy meal or simply craving a sweet snack, this clean and well-organised shop offers a lovely shopping experience. With its friendly service, tidy shelves, and diverse product range, it has quickly become a favourite spot for anyone looking to source genuine Filipino foods and household items in the local area.

name: Tindahan Ni Juan
description:
Tindahan Ni Juan is a local Filipino grocery and variety shop located in Bexley, in Sydney's southern suburbs. Specialising in imported goods and products from the Philippines, this retail store serves the local community by providing easy access to a wide range of popular Filipino groceries, pantry staples, and nostalgic snacks.

Visitors can find classic Filipino brand-name products, ranging from essential condiments and traditional sauces to sweet treats and savoury snacks. It operates as a local sari-sari style grocery store, making it a convenient spot for the Filipino-Australian community and locals to pick up their favourite household cooking ingredients, drinks, and daily essentials.

Conveniently situated on Forest Road in Bexley, the store is easily accessible to residents in the surrounding St George area looking to cook authentic dishes at home or simply enjoy a taste of the Philippines. Whether you are searching for specific cooking ingredients for an adobo or popular dessert mixes, this local shop aims to offer a friendly and welcoming service to all its visitors.

name: Tindahan ni Marekoy
description:
Tindahan ni Marekoy is a well-loved Filipino grocery store located in Kelmscott, Western Australia. Specialising in bringing a true taste of home to the local community, this store offers a wide selection of authentic Filipino ingredients, snacks, condiments, and frozen products. For customers located in Sydney and other parts of Australia, they provide a reliable online ordering and nationwide delivery service. Every bulk order is carefully packaged to ensure items arrive in excellent condition.

Customers frequently praise the shop for its welcoming atmosphere and friendly, accommodating customer service. Owner Lyzel and the staff go out of their way to assist shoppers, even sharing product photos before shipping to confirm freshness and details. Whether you are looking for classic pantry staples or traditional Filipino street food treats like bilo-bilo and taho, this grocery store is a fantastic local resource. It makes sourcing your favourite Pinoy brands and ingredients easy and convenient, no matter where you reside in Australia.

name: Tita Carinderia
description:
Tita Carinderia was a beloved Filipino restaurant and café located in Marrickville, Sydney. The eatery specialised in serving authentic, home-style Filipino comfort food in a cosy, welcoming space designed to remind visitors of the Philippines.

Locals and visitors loved the nostalgic decor and exceptionally warm hospitality that made them feel like they were dining at a relative's house. The kitchen offered popular morning favourites like tapsilog, busugsilog, and gutomsilog breakfast plates. These dishes featured garlic rice, fried egg, house-made atchara, and savoury meats like pork tocino and longganisa. Customers also enjoyed unique creations such as longganisa sausage rolls, pork adobo, and traditional sweets including chocobutternut donuts, ube bannofee, and ube basque cheesecake. For drinks, the ube matcha float, Manila matcha sweetened with condensed milk, and refreshing calamansi juice were crowd favourites.

The friendly service and vibrant community atmosphere established Tita Carinderia as a popular destination for the Filipino-Australian community in Sydney's inner west. This business has permanently closed.

name: Tita's Cakes
description:
Tita's Cakes is a boutique Filipino bakery and dessert shop located in Rooty Hill, Western Sydney. Starting as a humble home-based lockdown side hustle by a mother and daughter team, it has grown into a beloved storefront and active pop-up destination. They specialise in premium, handcrafted treats that offer a contemporary take on classic Filipino flavours.

Local customers rave about the soft and fluffy cupcakes, particularly the signature ube overload. This favourite is made with rich, earthy homemade ube halaya and offers a balanced sweetness. Other popular cupcake varieties include pandan with cheese, banana turon, buko pandan, and mango royale. Beyond cupcakes, the menu features indulgent ube cakes, cathedral jelly, and ube gelato. The loaded halo-halo dessert drink is also a massive hit, often called the best in Sydney.

The shop is known for its warm, family-oriented atmosphere and exceptionally helpful customer service. Whether picking up online orders or building a custom variety box at a local shopping centre pop-up, visitors are greeted with genuine hospitality. Tita's Cakes provides a comforting taste of home for the Filipino-Australian community while introducing unique artisanal desserts to the wider Sydney region.

name: Tong Li Supermarket Cabramatta
description:
Tong Li Supermarket in Cabramatta is a popular Asian grocery store that offers a wide variety of specialty ingredients and products. Local shoppers visit the shop to find a comprehensive range of items imported from China, Japan, Korea, Thailand, Vietnam, and the Philippines. In particular, members of the local Filipino community appreciate that the store stocks traditional Pinoy sorbetes (Filipino ice cream) in classic flavours such as avocado, ube (purple yam), ube macapuno, and cheese (keso).

Customers enjoy the convenience of the store's central location near local transport links. The shop features a large selection of fresh produce, tofu, sauces, instant noodles, and frozen products, making it a reliable destination for weekly grocery shopping. While some visitors have noted that certain specialty items are slightly higher in price, most shoppers value the store for stocking hard-to-find ingredients that are difficult to locate in mainstream supermarkets. The clean and well-organised shop floor allows for easy navigation, though cashiers can sometimes rush customers at the checkout during busy periods. Located in the heart of Cabramatta, this supermarket serves as a helpful hub for locals looking to specialise in authentic Asian and Filipino cooking at home.

name: Tourism Philippines - Australia & New Zealand
description:
The Philippine Department of Tourism in Australia and New Zealand is the official government office dedicated to promoting travel, tourism, and cultural experiences in the Philippines. Located on Clarence Street in the heart of Sydney, this dedicated centre serves as a vital bridge for Australians and New Zealanders planning their next tropical getaway.

The office assists local travel agents and travellers by providing authentic resources, official travel updates, and promotional campaigns. Through their specialised training portal, agents can become certified experts to help clients design their ideal itineraries. They also promote the custom TravelPH mobile app, which allows users to easily discover hidden gems, beaches, and food spots across the Philippine islands.

Whether you are looking to explore the historic streets of Manila, dive in Cebu, or surf the pristine waves of Siargao, this office provides helpful guidance. Travellers can access official resources online or get in touch with their Sydney-based team for official travel requirements and industry collaborations.

name: Trixie Freed Weddings
description:
Trixie Freed Weddings is a professional wedding planning and coordination service based in Sydney. Founded by Trixie, a Filipino-Australian project manager with extensive experience in financial services and hospitality, the business specialises in bespoke on-the-day coordination, styling, and planning for local, multicultural, and destination weddings. Trixie established the venture in 2024 after managing her own wedding in Italy, bringing a wealth of project management expertise and a calm, structured approach to event coordination.

Couples highly appreciate Trixie’s warm, organised, and reassuring presence, which allows them to fully enjoy their special day without managing logistics. Clients frequently commend her attention to detail in setting up venues, coordinating with vendors, and keeping events running smoothly. Families also love the personal touches she integrates to keep guests engaged, such as champagne during photo shoots and Polaroid cameras. Operating throughout the Sydney region, Trixie Freed Weddings helps couples turn their unique visions into memorable, stress-free celebrations. The office is open Monday to Friday from 9:00 am to 5:00 pm, and enquiries are welcome via email.

name: UNSW Filipino Student Society (FiloSoc)
description:
The UNSW Filipino Student Society, popularly known as FiloSoc, is a vibrant student-run organisation at the University of New South Wales in Kensington, Sydney. Dedicated to fostering a spirited community, the society bridges the Filipino-Australian student experience while celebrating the rich heritage of the Philippines. FiloSoc welcomes local and international students from all backgrounds who wish to engage with Filipino culture.

Members and visitors appreciate the society's warm, inclusive atmosphere, which offers a comforting home away from home. The group was recognised for its outstanding contribution to campus life when it was named the Arc UNSW Club of the Year in 2018.

FiloSoc hosts a diverse range of social, cultural, and food-centred events throughout the academic term. Key activities include traditional boodle fights, karaoke nights such as Tambayan Tunes, local night markets, and coffee gatherings known as kapihan. The society also organises study sessions and inter-university sports socials, helping students build lasting friendships and support networks while balancing their academic life in Sydney.

name: UTS Filipino Student Association
description:
The UTS Filipino Student Association (also known as FSA UTS or FiloSoc) is a vibrant, student-led community group at the University of Technology Sydney. Registered with ActivateUTS, this welcoming club is open to all university students, staff, and alumni who share a passion for Filipino culture, heritage, and community. The society focuses on creating a strong support network for Filipino-Australian students while sharing the warmth of Filipino hospitality with the wider campus.

Members can participate in a variety of social and cultural activities throughout the academic year. The club regularly organises exciting community-building events, such as the popular 'Tides and Tagay' Vivid Sydney boat cruise, co-hosted with Filipino societies from other regional universities. Other favourite activities include the 'Kainan Kulitan' park picnics, active sports days, traditional boodle fights, and lively karaoke sessions. During university orientation weeks, the association hosts a 'sari-sari' style stall to welcome newcomers with free snacks and info.

Located at the UTS campus in Ultimo, the group is a wonderful way to build lifelong friendships and stay connected to cultural roots. Whether you want to practice Tagalog, share delicious food, or participate in campus life, the association offers a home away from home.

name: United Architects of the Philippines - Australia Chapter
description:
The United Architects of the Philippines (UAP) Australia Chapter is a professional organisation that supports Filipino architects living and working across Australia and New Zealand. Officially inaugurated on 6 March 2021 to mark the 75th anniversary of Philippines-Australia relations, the chapter is accredited by the United Architects of the Philippines and the Integrated and Accredited Professional Organization of Architects (IAPOA).

The organisation serves as a vital professional network and support system. It helps migrated architects navigate local registration pathways, share knowledge, and engage with the wider building design and construction industry. Members benefit from continuing professional development (CPD) programs, seminars, and networking opportunities.

Beyond career development, the chapter organises conferences and events to foster camaraderie and cultural connections. These events include the annual architectural conference (such as BRISCON) and Technocon webinars, which bring together members and industry partners from different states. By encouraging collaboration, the chapter helps Filipino professionals succeed in the Australian built environment while maintaining strong ties to their cultural heritage. The group is registered as an incorporated association in Australia, offering a welcoming community for architects to connect, learn, and grow together.

name: University of the Philippines Alumni Association of Australia - NSW Chapter
description:
Established on 1 March 1980, the University of the Philippines Alumni Association of Australia – New South Wales Chapter, Inc. (UPAAA-NSW) is one of the oldest and most active UP alumni organisations outside the Philippines. Headquartered in Sydney, this vibrant community group specialises in fostering strong bonds of fellowship among alumni while actively promoting Filipino culture and heritage across the region.

Beneficiaries and members highly value the chapter’s dedicated support network. Scholars describe the association’s assistance as a life-changing support system that goes far beyond simple financial aid, providing meaningful personal encouragement and guidance. Key initiatives include the UPAAA-NSW Scholarship Grant, which supports academically excellent but financially disadvantaged undergraduate students back in the Philippines, alongside local community outreach and social fundraisers. The group hosts annual events, such as cultural gatherings and the Philippine Alumni Extravaganza, to connect alumni from various Philippine educational institutions. UPAAA-NSW represents a welcoming centre for the Filipino-Australian community, championing education and cultural pride.

name: University of the Philippines Alumni Association of Australia - New South Wales
description:
The University of the Philippines Alumni Association of Australia - New South Wales Chapter, Inc. (UPAAA-NSW) is a dedicated community organisation established in 1980 to support alumni of the University of the Philippines residing in New South Wales. Operating under the motto of unity, community, and loyalty, the association focuses on fostering strong local connections while staying connected to their alma mater.

A central highlight of the organisation is its impactful Scholarship Grant Project, which assists academically gifted but disadvantaged students in the Philippines. Scholars share that this support is an answered prayer that provides not only crucial financial aid but also a welcoming community and encouragement. To fund these initiatives, the association organises popular community and fundraising events, including golf tournaments like the Oblation Cup, musical concerts, and social soirées. UPAAA-NSW also actively engages in local community service initiatives in the Sydney region, such as environmental projects for National Tree Day and information sessions for the elderly. Through these efforts, the group helps to enrich the local Filipino-Australian community, champion qualifications recognition for migrants, and provide a supportive network for alumni and students.

name: VUNO Physiotherapy & Rehabilitation
description:
Vuno Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation is a dedicated allied health practice that provides clinic-based, mobile, and telehealth physiotherapy and occupational therapy services across Sydney. With physical clinics located next to Seven Hills Plaza and inside Castle Mall Shopping Centre in Castle Hill, they also offer mobile home-visit treatments throughout greater Sydney, including Blacktown, Parramatta, and Liverpool. The practice is a registered NDIS provider and offers services in both English and Tagalog, making it highly accessible to the local Filipino community.

Clients frequently praise the welcoming, modern environment of the clinics and the highly supportive team. Senior physiotherapists like Brendon Arizala and therapists like Shinal are highly regarded for their expertise and encouraging approach. The team specialises in creating progressive, personalised exercise programs to manage persistent pain, recover from knee or back injuries, and improve overall strength and balance. They also provide compassionate support for children with special needs and offer manual handling training for families and carers. Whether visiting a clinic or receiving care at home, patients can expect friendly, professional guidance tailored to helping them gain independence in their everyday activities.

name: Valencia's Kitchenette
description:
Valencia’s Kitchenette is a dedicated catering service located in Sydney, specialising in authentic Filipino cuisine. They offer high-quality traditional dishes that are perfect for any gathering or special event.

Local clients highly recommend their services for the delicious, home-style food that brings a taste of the Philippines to Australia. Many appreciate the affordable prices and the excellent value, noting that the quality of the dishes matches the price perfectly. The menu features a variety of classic dishes, making it a popular choice for those who miss traditional Filipino cooking.

Whether you are hosting a small family gathering or a larger community celebration, Valencia’s Kitchenette can cater to your needs. They operate seven days a week, opening early from Monday to Saturday to prepare fresh meals, with shorter operating hours on Sundays. If you want to organise catering for your next event or discuss their menu options, you can contact them directly by phone.

name: Victory Life Christian Church
description:
Victory Life Christian Church is a welcoming Christian fellowship located in Blacktown, Western Sydney. Situated at the corner of Mort and Cardiff Streets, this church is affiliated with Foursquare Church Australia and serves the local community through regular worship, prayer, and ministries.

The congregation is known for its warm hospitality, inviting atmosphere, and vibrant praise and worship. Attendees appreciate the biblically grounded, balanced teaching that focuses on spiritual growth and faith. It is a place where people of all ages and backgrounds can find fellowship and support.

Reflecting the multicultural character of the local area, the church plays an active role in supporting different community groups. It hosts a dedicated Filipino community service and fellowship on the first Sunday of every month. Additionally, the church venue is shared with a Korean congregation that holds services on Sunday afternoons at 1:00 pm.

Regular Sunday morning services for Victory Life Christian Church begin at 10:00 am. Whether you are looking for a local place of worship, supportive community groups, or solid biblical teaching, this centre offers a friendly environment to connect and grow.

name: Visa Hub Tech
description:
Visa Hub Tech is a professional education and migration agency located in Belmore, Sydney. The agency is operated by registered migration agent Aubrey Pia Estacio (MARN: 2418377), who is also a member of the Migration Institute of Australia. As former international students and immigrants, the team understands the unique challenges of moving to Australia and specialises in supporting the Filipino community.

Clients frequently praise Aubrey and her team for their highly supportive, patient, and thorough approach. They take the time to understand complex individual cases and guide applicants step-by-step through every requirement. The agency assists with a wide range of pathways, including Temporary Graduate visas (subclass 485), Skilled Independent visas (subclass 189), dependent visas, and tourist visas. They also offer detailed assistance for skills assessments like the ACS process, helping with customised document formats and cover letters.

Known for being responsive and approachable, Visa Hub Tech organises regular check-ins to make the application journey as smooth and stress-free as possible. The agency serves clients from its office on Burwood Road in Belmore and assists applicants across the greater Sydney area.

name: Visayan Association of Australia Inc.
description:
The Visayan Association of Australia Inc. is a welcoming community organisation based in Liverpool, New South Wales. Established in 2003, the group focuses on preserving and celebrating the rich cultural heritage of the Visayas region of the Philippines. Local members gather to foster camaraderie, share their distinct dialects, and promote Filipino culture within the multicultural Sydney community.

The association is well-known for hosting vibrant cultural events throughout the year. Key celebrations include the annual Flores de Mayo, Santacruzan, and the traditional fiesta of the Virgin of the Rule. They also organise the Mrs VAA-Australia pageant, which serves as a creative fundraiser to support humanitarian outreach projects.

Beyond cultural preservation, the organisation is deeply committed to charitable work. They actively run donation drives to support underprivileged children and assist communities affected by natural disasters in the Philippines. As a proud affiliate of the Philippine Community Council of New South Wales, the association serves as an inspiring hub for cultural connection and social support in Sydney's south-west.

name: West Sydney Community Church
description:
West Sydney Community Church is a welcoming, Filipino-connected Christian congregation located at 1/9 Kilto Crescent in Glendenning. Led by Pastor Rey Luciano and Pastor Arnold 'Dong' De Leon, this vibrant church focuses on helping the Western Sydney community discover, grow, and belong together in their faith journey.

Members of the congregation frequently praise the church for its warm, supportive environment, describing it as a glorious community centred on faith. To make visitors feel at home, the church hosts the Selah Cafe, which opens on Sundays from 9:45 am to 10:30 am, and again after the service from 12:00 pm to 12:30 pm. This cafe provides a wonderful space for fellowship and connection.

Regular Sunday services are held at 10:30 am. For those who cannot attend in person, the church offers weekly online livestreams via Vimeo. Additionally, the ministry produces 'Good Morning Devotions' with Pastor Rey Luciano, alongside various podcasts to support members throughout the week. Outside of Sunday services, access to the church centre is available by appointment. Whether you are seeking a place to belong or wishing to participate in community-focused service, this Glendenning-based fellowship offers a friendly, active space for everyone.

name: Wow Filipino Food
description:
Wow Filipino Food is a casual Sydney dining stall that specialises in authentic Filipino street food. Located in the bustling Burwood Chinatown food district, this eatery is popular for bringing the vibrant flavours of Manila to the local community. It is operated by passionate owners Mae Manio and Richard Rodillas, who are dedicated to sharing traditional Pinoy street food culture.

Customers highly recommend their signature sizzling pork sisig, charcoal-grilled pork belly (liempo), and sweet-and-tangy barbecue skewers. Other popular items on the menu include tasty chicken Maryland, kwek-kwek, and refreshing gulaman. Patrons frequently praise the friendly and welcoming customer service, highlighting the warm hospitality of the staff. While some diners mention that the pricing is slightly higher than other food court options, most agree that the authentic taste, generous portions, and quality packaging make it a worthwhile dining experience.

The stall operates primarily from Thursday to Sunday during evening night market hours. Whether you are craving a comforting taste of home or eager to explore traditional grilled delicacies, Wow Filipino Food offers a lively and authentic taste of the Philippines in Sydney.

name: Woy woy Sari sari Filipino store
description:
Woy Woy Sari Sari Filipino Store is a charming local grocery store situated at 32 Blackwall Rd in Woy Woy, New South Wales. This welcoming shop serves as a fantastic hub for the Central Coast’s Filipino-Australian community, offering an extensive selection of essential products, snacks, and cooking ingredients imported directly from the Philippines. Under the warm management of new owners Thess and Greg, the store has become a favourite stop for locals seeking authentic tastes of home.

Visitors consistently praise the friendly, helpful service and the clean, well-organised layout of the shop. The shelves are stocked with a diverse range of items, including popular pantry staples, delicious Filipino desserts, and snacks. A particular highlight for many customers is the availability of fresh pandesal and delicious ube ice cream.

Whether you are looking to cook a traditional family meal or simply want to satisfy a sweet craving, this central store makes it easy to find what you need without a long trip. Thess and Greg are always happy to help guide you through their stock or recommend the perfect ingredient for your next recipe, making every visit a pleasant experience.

name: Yayin Serrano Hair and MakeUp Studio
description: Yayin Serrano Hair and MakeUp Studio is a welcoming, Filipino-owned boutique hair and beauty salon located in Hornsby, Sydney. Situated in The Piazza building on Florence Street, right opposite Westfield Hornsby, the studio specialises in professional hair care and formal makeup artistry. Under the expert care of Irene and her dedicated team, clients enjoy a highly personalised experience in a relaxing and welcoming atmosphere. The salon specialises in advanced hair smoothing treatments such as nanoplasty and vegan keratin treatments, designed to transform and tame coarse or frizzy hair into silky, glossy locks. They also offer a full range of services including custom colouring, highlights, balayage, modern haircuts, updos, and blow dries. Visitors regularly praise Irene's meticulous attention to detail, her professional consultations, and the gentle care she brings to every service. What makes the salon truly special is the friendly community feel, with the team often making guests feel right at home with complimentary refreshments during long treatments. The studio is conveniently located less than a five-minute walk from Hornsby Station, with easy parking available at the nearby Westfield shopping centre. It is open throughout the week, including Sundays, making it a perfect spot for busy individuals looking to treat themselves to a pampering session.

name: Yummylicious Cafe
description:
Yummylicious Cafe is a warm and welcoming Filipino-influenced cafe located in Campbelltown, Sydney. Situated conveniently across from Campbelltown station with plenty of parking, this hidden gem is run by the friendly owner Aileen, who specialises in freshly prepared traditional dishes and handcrafted desserts.

Local diners love the cafe's cosy atmosphere and affordable prices. The menu features classic all-day Filipino breakfasts, including tapsilog with marinated beef, garlic rice, and over-easy eggs, alongside longsilog and bangsilog options. A standout favourite is the authentic Ilocos empanada, which is prepared fresh to order with a delicate, thin dough. For sweet cravings, the cafe offers a delightful range of treats such as ube cake, ube leche flan, sylvanas, mango cake, and halo-halo loaded with toppings. These pair perfectly with their iced ube halaya coffee, chai, or summer juices.

Whether you want to wind down with a relaxing breakfast before a busy day or enjoy a casual lunch with family, this Campbelltown spot offers an authentic taste of the Philippines with warm hospitality.

name: Zarate Mechanics
description:
Zarate Mechanics is a family-owned and operated automotive workshop situated in Arndell Park. Run by John and his father, this local business has built an exceptional reputation across Western Sydney for its honesty, transparency, and high-quality workmanship. The professional team specialising in both Japanese and European makes offers a comprehensive suite of services. These include logbook servicing under novated leases, pink and blue slip safety inspections, wheel balancing and alignment, brakes, suspension work, tyre replacements, and advanced diagnostic testing.

Customers frequently praise the team for going above and beyond. From providing a loan vehicle to assisting with after-hours requests, they prioritise building long-term community relationships. The workshop is highly regarded for its clean, modern facilities equipped with safe, state-of-the-art tools. Rather than pushing unnecessary dealer upsells, they provide transparent quotes and explain repairs in plain language. Whether you require a routine service or complex repairs for a modified vehicle, they deliver reliable care at fair prices. They even take the extra step to follow up and ensure your vehicle runs perfectly.

name: bypau
description:
Based in Granville, Sydney, byPau is a beloved boutique cake shop specialising in classic Filipino celebration cakes and customised buttercream creations. The bakery is particularly renowned for its signature ube leche flan cake, which locals praise as the benchmark in Sydney for its light, fluffy texture, delicate aroma, and rich, creamy leche flan centre. 

Beyond their famous ube treats, byPau serves a delicious variety of traditional Filipino desserts including Mango Bravo—often described as a delightful twist on the classic Conti's style—as well as Sansrival, Yema, Polvoron, and Ube Macapuno chiffon cakes. Customers highly appreciate the shop's friendly and accommodating service, noting that Paula is helpful in answering inquiries and can customise orders to suit special family events like birthdays. While operating primarily on a pre-order basis for local pickup in Granville or delivery across the Sydney metropolitan area, byPau offers a sweet, authentic taste of home for the local Filipino-Australian community.

name: mydentist Fairfield
description:
mydentist Fairfield is a family-owned and operated dental practice in Fairfield, Sydney. Led by the husband-and-wife team of Dr. Dennis Francisco and Dr. Maria Victoria Francisco, this clinic has provided friendly and reliable dental services to the local community for over twenty years.

The practice specialises in general and preventative care, offering treatments such as thorough cleanings, teeth whitening, extractions, crowns, bridges, and denture fittings. Patients appreciate the clean, well-maintained facilities and the support of highly capable dental technicians like Krystal and Linda. The practice is known for its honest approach, with the team prioritising genuine patient care and affordable, reasonable pricing rather than recommending unnecessary treatments.

Conveniently located at Suite 7, 14 Court Road, the clinic is right next to Fairfield train station and bus terminals, making it simple to access via public transport. Drivers will also find ample street parking nearby. The clinic is open Monday to Friday from 9:00 am to 5:30 pm, and Saturdays from 9:00 am to 1:00 pm, offering flexible options for busy families.

name: Filipino Community Cooperative Ltd
description: The Filipino Community Cooperative is a registered not-for-profit organisation established in 1981 in Mount Pritchard. For over four decades, this community-based group has supported local families through welfare services, community development, and the active promotion of Filipino culture in Sydney. The cooperative is particularly renowned for owning and managing the Hiyas ng Bayan Child Care Centre, located at 32 Anderson Avenue on the border of Mount Pritchard and Chipping Norton. This unique childcare facility serves as a vital community hub. It offers quality long day care and preschool programmes tailored for children from diverse cultural and linguistic backgrounds. A dedicated committee of local professionals and community members manages the centre. They focus on providing a nurturing space that celebrates multicultural heritage. The cooperative also coordinates charity events, cultural programmes, and community support groups across south-west Sydney. Through its educational services and social initiatives, the Filipino Community Cooperative continues to foster strong connections and support the needs of Filipino-Australian families.

name: Filipino Food Movement Australia Festival
description:
The Filipino Food Movement Australia Festival, widely known as FiloFomoFest, is the premier cultural and culinary takeover in Western Sydney. Organised by the Filipino Food Movement Australia (FFMA), this registered not-for-profit group champions Filipino heritage by supporting local food entrepreneurs and showcasing traditional flavours to a broader audience.

Held at Centenary Square in Parramatta to celebrate Philippine Independence Month, the festival brings the community together in the spirit of Bayanihan. Visitors love the vibrant atmosphere, live music, and the authentic celebration of culture. The event features over fifteen legendary food stalls serving street food, classic desserts, and innovative fusion dishes. Key highlights include the competitive Lumpia Legends Challenge, live band performances from groups like Platform Band, hip-hop sets, and traditional routines by the AusMin Dancers. Acoustic buskers also fill the square with music, making it a lively, family-friendly space.

Supported by the City of Parramatta and sponsors like SBS Audio, the festival attracts large, diverse crowds from across the region. It provides a valuable platform for local businesses to integrate classic Filipino flavours into the mainstream Australian food scene.

name: Iglesia Ni Cristo Locale of Penrith
description:
Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church of Christ) Locale of Penrith served as a welcoming Christian congregation dedicated to supporting the Filipino-Australian community in Western Sydney. Located at Kingswood High School along Bringelly Road, this local place of worship specialised in providing a comfortable and welcoming space for congregational prayer, worship, and fellowship. The congregation offered regular mid-week and weekend services, including weekly worship sessions held on Tuesdays at 7:30 PM and Saturdays at 10:00 AM and 12:30 PM.

Under the active local leadership of Destinado Raleigh Sanchez and Head Deacon Jesse Baylon, the church supported various community gatherings, prayer meetings, and spiritual activities. It focused on fostering Christian values, personal faith, and strong community connections among local families and individuals. For years, the locale provided a supportive, family-like environment for members seeking spiritual growth and connection with their cultural heritage. Community members appreciated the warm fellowship and dedication of the congregation. This congregation has permanently closed.

name: Lugaw Queen Restaurant
description: Lugaw Queen Restaurant is a warm, family-run Filipino eatery located in Ashfield, Sydney. The restaurant specialises in authentic Filipino comfort food, bringing a genuine taste of the Philippines to the local community. Customers particularly love their comforting rice porridge and noodle dishes, such as their rich Lomi overload, Mami overload, and hearty Goto, which are especially popular on chilly days. Another standout is the loaded beef pares, served with crispy lechon kawali, chicharon bulaklak, and a deeply flavourful beef soup. For groups, the generous portion sizes of their sharing options, like the popular Barkada Supreme Bundle, offer excellent value. The interior is beautifully decorated to evoke a cosy, home-style dining experience, complemented by the exceptionally friendly and welcoming hospitality of the owners. Lugaw Queen Restaurant is open from Tuesday to Friday for lunch between 11:30 am and 2:30 pm, and dinner from 5:00 pm to 9:00 pm. On weekends, they offer continuous dining from 11:30 am to 9:00 pm, while the venue is closed on Mondays. It is a fantastic spot for anyone seeking satisfying, traditional Filipino flavours in a hospitable neighbourhood setting.

name: Martial Arts Research Academy - MARA Prospect
description:
Martial Arts Research Academy (MARA) in Prospect is a family-run martial arts school and fitness centre. Led by experienced coaches Blake and Jenny, the academy specialises in a wide array of disciplines. These include Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu (BJJ), Muay Thai, Kickboxing, Boxing, Mixed Martial Arts (MMA), Jeet Kune Do (JKD), and Filipino Martial Arts such as Armas Kali and Panantukan-Silat. 

The academy is highly regarded by the local community for its friendly and supportive environment. Instructors focus on making classes accessible for everyone, from children in the specialised kids' programs to seasoned competitors. Students frequently praise the coaches for their welcoming nature, which helps beginners feel comfortable on the mats rather than intimidated. Training sessions are structured to help members build self-defence skills, physical fitness, confidence, and discipline. 

The facility is clean, spacious, and equipped with quality training gear. With flexible classes held throughout weekdays and on Saturday mornings, members can easily fit sessions into their schedules. Located on Rowood Road, MARA provides a positive space for families and individuals in Western Sydney to grow as martial artists.

name: PAGASA Inc
description:
PAGASA Inc, or the Philippine Australian Global Alliance for Service & Advocacy, is a welcoming non-profit community organisation based in Liverpool, Western Sydney. Established in 2023, this dedicated alliance works hard to support the local Filipino-Australian community. The group focuses on bringing people together, celebrating rich cultural traditions, and helping those in need.

Local community members value PAGASA Inc for its active role in organising engaging events, such as cultural festivals, info sessions, and local charity drives. The alliance also carries out important humanitarian work, including disaster relief efforts for communities in the Philippines and support programs closer to home. By building strong partnerships, they help create a supportive network for individuals and families across New South Wales.

PAGASA Inc is passionate about helping vulnerable community sectors and raising awareness for local needs. Led by a devoted team, including Jhun Salazar and Larry Perez, the association provides an invaluable voice and advocacy for the Filipino community in the region. Whether you want to volunteer, participate in community events, or seek support, this organisation offers a wonderful way to connect with others and give back.

name: PICPA Australia
description:
The Philippine Institute of Certified Public Accountants (PICPA) Australia Chapter is a registered professional non-profit organisation established to support Filipino Certified Public Accountants (CPAs) living and working in Australia. Officially incorporated in New South Wales in August 2023, the chapter serves as an international extension of the national body in the Philippines.

Members highly value the supportive network and the professional development opportunities provided. The chapter actively runs continuing professional development (CPD) programmes, helping members stay competent with local accounting standards and tax laws. A key highlight is the support for the Member Pathway Agreement (MPA) with CPA Australia, which assists qualified Filipino CPAs to gain local recognition and transition smoothly into the Australian workforce.

Led by President Mr Ricardo De Vera and Vice-President Dr Jaime Lopez, the chapter provides mentorship, networking events, and career guidance. The founding officers were officially inducted in January 2024 at Merrylands RSL in Sydney by the Philippine Consul General. The organisation serves as a vital bridge, blending cultural connection with career growth for accounting professionals across Sydney and broader Australia.

name: PLUS Cargo Australia
description:
Plus Cargo Australia, based in Claremont Meadows, Sydney, is a dedicated cargo forwarding service specialising in transporting balikbayan boxes from Australia to the Philippines. With over a decade of industry presence, the company offers door-to-door sea and air freight options to help the Filipino-Australian community send gifts and essentials to their families.

Customers appreciate the reliable service and the helpful support team, particularly praising coordinators like Gert Blanco, Sir Loel, and Mira for their professional assistance and regular updates. Loyal patrons, some of whom have used the service for nine years, value the convenience of tracking shipments through the website and receiving SMS notifications from the owner when deliveries are close to arrival. While some clients have experienced occasional transit delays and communication challenges, particularly during peak shipping seasons, many highlight that their parcels arrive safely and in good condition.

Operating Monday to Friday, Plus Cargo remains a popular choice for families looking for secure logistics solutions to connect with their loved ones overseas.

name: St. Francis Xavier Catholic Church
description:
St Francis Xavier Catholic Church in Lurnea is a warm, caring, and active parish serving the local community since 1965. Following a beautiful refurbishment in 2018, the church features a bright, modern, and peaceful sanctuary with cosy pine wood details and lovely stained glass windows. Parishioners and visitors highly appreciate the peaceful atmosphere, describing the church as a beautiful place to pray.

The parish is particularly loved for its family-friendly environment and welcoming community. Many families value the close connection with the local primary school. The parish priest is frequently praised for his gentle, welcoming nature and dedicated service. The church also has a vibrant youth group and talented singers who enhance the worship experience. Regular services include weekday Masses from Tuesday to Friday at 9:00 am, Saturday Vigil at 5:00 pm, and Sunday Masses at 8:00 am, 9:30 am, and 6:00 pm. Reconciliation is held on Saturdays from 4:00 pm to 4:30 pm. Reflecting Lurnea's diverse multicultural community, the parish hosts a dedicated Filipino Mass on the fourth Sunday of each month at 11:00 am. The church provides ample parking on site, making it convenient and accessible for everyone.

name: Sydney Australian-Filipino Seniors Inc. (SAFSI)
description:
Sydney Australian-Filipino Seniors Inc. (SAFSI) is a registered charity and community organisation dedicated to supporting senior citizens within the Filipino-Australian community in Western Sydney. Established in 1987 by community elders in the Blacktown district, the group has spent nearly four decades promoting well-being, advocacy, and social connection for its members.

The organisation is best known for its monthly social meetings, which take place on the third Sunday of each month from 9:00 am to 1:00 pm at the Marayong Community Centre. These gatherings blend Filipino and Australian traditions, featuring birthday celebrations, cultural performances, and health updates. To promote active and healthy lifestyles, the group organises fitness activities like dancercise. They also run popular performance groups, including the Staying Alive Dancers and the Sampaguita Dancers.

In addition to their regular socials, SAFSI members are highly active in the broader community. They regularly participate in public events such as the annual Blacktown Streets Alive Parade and the Filipino Christmas Festival at Tumbalong Park. The group is also committed to local charity work, including raising funds for the Cancer Council's Biggest Morning Tea. Offering a welcoming, family-like support network, SAFSI remains a vital pillar for Filipino-Australian seniors across the region.
